## 7125: elect, the (id: 7125)

Those chosen by God, entirely of his grace, for the special privilege of experiencing salvation.

Israel as the elect
-------------------

**[DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6)** *See also* [EXO 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:4-Exod19:6); [PSA 105:6](https://ref.ly/Ps105:6), [PSA 105:43](https://ref.ly/Ps105:43), [PSA 135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:4); [ISA 41:8–41:9](https://ref.ly/Isa41:8-Isa41:9), [ISA 43:10](https://ref.ly/Isa43:10), [ISA 44:1–44:2](https://ref.ly/Isa44:1-Isa44:2), [ISA 45:4](https://ref.ly/Isa45:4); [AMO 3:2](https://ref.ly/Amos3:2); [ROM 11:1–11:18](https://ref.ly/Rom11:1-Rom11:18)

The church as the elect
-----------------------

**[EPH 1:4–1:13](https://ref.ly/Eph1:4-Eph1:13)**; **[1PE 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1-1Pet1:2)** *See also* [JHN 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/John10:4-John10:5); [1TH 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:4-1Thess1:5); [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9), [1PE 5:132](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:132)JN [1:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1), [2JN 1:13](https://ref.ly/2John1:13)

Individuals chosen by God as his elect
--------------------------------------

### Abraham

[GEN 18:18–18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:18-Gen18:19)

### Moses

[PSA 106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:23)

### David

[1KI 8:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:16); ; [PSA 89:3–89:4](https://ref.ly/Ps89:3-Ps89:4)

### Rufus

[ROM 16:13](https://ref.ly/Rom16:13)

God's elect enjoy his presence and favour
-----------------------------------------

### The elect are asssured of the presence of God

**[HEB 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5-Heb13:6)** *See also* [JOS 1:1–1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:1-Josh1:9); [PSA 23:1–23:6](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1-Ps23:6)

### The elect are assured of the protection of God

**[MAT 24:22](https://ref.ly/Matt24:22)** parallel [MRK 13:20](https://ref.ly/Mark13:20) *See also* [ROM 8:33–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:33-Rom8:39); [REV 17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:14)

### The elect are assured of a place in the world to come

**[MRK 13:26–13:27](https://ref.ly/Mark13:26-Mark13:27)** parallel [MAT 24:30–24:31](https://ref.ly/Matt24:30-Matt24:31) *See also* [ISA 65:17–65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17-Isa65:25); [JHN 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/John14:1-John14:3); [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5); [2PE 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:10-2Pet1:11)

### The elect are assured of justice

**[LUK 18:7–18:8](https://ref.ly/Luke18:7-Luke18:8)**

Responsibilities of the elect
-----------------------------

**[COL 3:12–3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:12-Col3:17)** *See also* [DEU 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1-Deut14:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 7:6; Exodus 19:4-6; Psalms 105:6; Psalms 105:43; Psalms 135:4; Isaiah 41:8-9; Isaiah 43:10; Isaiah 44:1-2; Isaiah 45:4; Amos 3:2; Romans 11:1-18; Ephesians 1:4-13; 1 Peter 1:1-2; John 10:4-5; 1 Thessalonians 1:4-5; 1 Peter 2:9; 1 Peter 5:132; 1 Peter 1:1; 2 John 1:13; Genesis 18:18-19; Psalms 106:23; 1 Kings 8:16; Psalms 89:3-4; Romans 16:13; Hebrews 13:5-6; Joshua 1:1-9; Psalms 23:1-6; Matthew 24:22; Mark 13:20; Romans 8:33-39; Revelation 17:14; Mark 13:26-27; Matthew 24:30-31; Isaiah 65:17-25; John 14:1-3; James 2:5; 2 Peter 1:10-11; Luke 18:7-8; Colossians 3:12-17; Deuteronomy 14:1-2
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6638: election (ID: `6638@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6708: predestination (ID: `6708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7135: Israel, as the people of God (ID: `7135@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7130: flock, God's (id: 7130)

God's people are frequently portrayed as a flock of sheep, for whom he cares with the tenderness and concern of a loving shepherd.

The divine shepherd
-------------------

### God portrayed as a shepherd

**[PSA 23:1](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1)** *See also* [PSA 80:1](https://ref.ly/Ps80:1)

### Jesus Christ portrayed as a shepherd

**[JHN 10:11](https://ref.ly/John10:11)** *See also* [MAT 26:31](https://ref.ly/Matt26:31); [ZEC 13:7](https://ref.ly/Zech13:7)

God's people are his flock
--------------------------

### Israel and Judah depicted as God's flock

**[PSA 77:20](https://ref.ly/Ps77:20)** *See also* [PSA 78:52](https://ref.ly/Ps78:52); [ISA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Isa63:11); [JER 13:17](https://ref.ly/Jer13:17), [JER 31:10](https://ref.ly/Jer31:10), [JER 50:17](https://ref.ly/Jer50:17); [MIC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mic2:12), [MIC 7:14](https://ref.ly/Mic7:14)

### NT believers depicted as God's flock

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *See also* [LUK 12:32](https://ref.ly/Luke12:32); [JHN 10:14–10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:14-John10:16)

God cares for his flock
-----------------------

**[ISA 40:11](https://ref.ly/Isa40:11)** *See also* [PSA 95:6–95:7](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6-Ps95:7); [EZK 34:11–34:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11-Ezek34:16); [MIC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Mic5:4); [ZEC 9:16](https://ref.ly/Zech9:16)

God judges his flock
--------------------

**[EZK 34:17–34:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:17-Ezek34:24)** *See also* [ZEC 11:7–11:16](https://ref.ly/Zech11:7-Zech11:16)

The under\-shepherds of the flock
---------------------------------

### Many of Israel's leaders exploited God's flock

**[EZK 34:2–34:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:2-Ezek34:3)** *See also* [JER 10:21](https://ref.ly/Jer10:21); [ZEC 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/Zech11:4-Zech11:5); [JHN 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/John10:12-John10:13)

### God will judge the corrupt shepherds

**[EZK 34:7–34:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:7-Ezek34:10)** *See also* [JER 23:1–23:5](https://ref.ly/Jer23:1-Jer23:5), [JER 25:34–25:36](https://ref.ly/Jer25:34-Jer25:36); [ZEC 10:3](https://ref.ly/Zech10:3), [ZEC 11:17](https://ref.ly/Zech11:17)

### Church leaders are to care for God's flock

**[1PE 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2-1Pet5:3)** *See also* [ACT 20:28–20:29](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28-Acts20:29)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 23:1; Psalms 80:1; John 10:11; Matthew 26:31; Zechariah 13:7; Psalms 77:20; Psalms 78:52; Isaiah 63:11; Jeremiah 13:17; Jeremiah 31:10; Jeremiah 50:17; Micah 2:12; Micah 7:14; Acts 20:28; Luke 12:32; John 10:14-16; Isaiah 40:11; Psalms 95:6-7; Ezekiel 34:11-16; Micah 5:4; Zechariah 9:16; Ezekiel 34:17-24; Zechariah 11:7-16; Ezekiel 34:2-3; Jeremiah 10:21; Zechariah 11:4-5; John 10:12-13; Ezekiel 34:7-10; Jeremiah 23:1-5; Jeremiah 25:34-36; Zechariah 10:3; Zechariah 11:17; 1 Peter 5:2-3; Acts 20:28-29
* **Associated Articles:** 1220: God, as shepherd (ID: `1220@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2330: Jesus Christ, as shepherd (ID: `2330@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4684: sheep (ID: `4684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5293: defence, human (ID: `5293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5611: watchman (ID: `5611@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7140: people of God (ID: `7140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7784: shepherd (ID: `7784@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7135: Israel, as the people of God (id: 7135)

God called Israel out of Egypt to be his own treasured possession and his covenant faithfulness with them is maintained, despite their persistent disobedience.

God's election of Israel
------------------------

### The nation was founded upon God's promises to the patriarchs

**[GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3)** *See also* [GEN 17:1–17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1-Gen17:8), [GEN 35:9–35:13](https://ref.ly/Gen35:9-Gen35:13)

### Israel as God's chosen possession

**[1KI 8:53](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:53)** *See also* [EXO 19:3–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:3-Exod19:6); [DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6), [DEU 32:7–32:12](https://ref.ly/Deut32:7-Deut32:12); [PSA 135:3–135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:3-Ps135:4)

### God redeemed Israel from Egypt

**[EXO 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6-Exod6:7)** *See also* [EXO 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:10); [2SA 7:22–7:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:22-2Sam7:24) parallel [1CH 17:20–17:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:20-1Chr17:22)

### God enters into a special covenant relationship with Israel

[EXO 24:3–24:8](https://ref.ly/Exod24:3-Exod24:8)

### Israel's privileged status within the covenant

**[ROM 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Rom9:4-Rom9:5)** *See also* [ROM 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Rom3:1-Rom3:2); [PHP 3:4–3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:6)

Israel's disobedience to the covenant
-------------------------------------

### Israel provoked God in the wilderness

**[PSA 78:40–78:41](https://ref.ly/Ps78:40-Ps78:41)** *See also* [EXO 32:1–32:10](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:10); [NUM 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/Num11:1-Num11:6), [NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4)

### Israel's unfaithfulness in the promised land

**[ISA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Isa1:4)** *See also* [JDG 2:10–2:13](https://ref.ly/Judg2:10-Judg2:13); [JER 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Jer11:9-Jer11:10); [EZK 20:27–20:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:27-Ezek20:28)

### Israel exiled for her sins

**[2KI 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:11-2Kgs18:12)** *See also* [2KI 25:8–25:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:8-2Kgs25:12) parallels [JER 39:8–39:10](https://ref.ly/Jer39:8-Jer39:10), [JER 52:12–52:16](https://ref.ly/Jer52:12-Jer52:16)

God's promise to restore Israel
-------------------------------

### God's promise not to abandon Israel completely

**[DEU 4:31](https://ref.ly/Deut4:31)** *See also* [LEV 26:44–26:45](https://ref.ly/Lev26:44-Lev26:45); [ISA 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/Isa6:11-Isa6:13) *Though Israel will be destroyed, a holy remnant will be left*; [JER 31:35–31:37](https://ref.ly/Jer31:35-Jer31:37)

### God's promise to bring Israel back to the land

**[JER 30:3](https://ref.ly/Jer30:3)** *See also* [ISA 43:5–43:7](https://ref.ly/Isa43:5-Isa43:7); [EZK 37:11–37:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:11-Ezek37:14)

### God's promise to reunite Israel

**[JER 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jer3:18)** *See also* [EZK 37:15–37:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:15-Ezek37:23); [HOS 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hos1:10-Hos1:11)

### The promise of a restored relationship between Israel and God

**[JER 31:31–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:31-Jer31:34)** *See also* [ISA 40:1–40:2](https://ref.ly/Isa40:1-Isa40:2); [EZK 36:24–36:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:24-Ezek36:28)

### Israel returns from exile

[EZR 1:1–1:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:1-Ezra1:5) parallel [2CH 36:22–36:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:22-2Chr36:23)

Jesus Christ, the Saviour of Israel
-----------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's ministry was specifically to the nation of Israel

**[MAT 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:6)** *See also* [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30)

### Jesus accepts the title \`\`Christ'' (Messiah), God's anointed deliverer for Israel

**[MAT 16:16–16:17](https://ref.ly/Matt16:16-Matt16:17)** parallels [MRK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Mark8:29), [LUK 9:20](https://ref.ly/Luke9:20) *Although Jesus accepted the title “Messiah” (Christ), he did not fulfil contemporary Israelite hopes of a political deliverer.*

### Jesus Christ declares to Israel the good news of God's reign

**[MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15)** *Jesus Christ takes up Isaiah's message of good news, that God is coming in sovereign power to restore Israel (see [Isa 40:9–10](https://ref.ly/Isa40:9-Isa40:10), [52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7)).* *See also* [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23), [MAT 9:35](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35); [LUK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1)

Israel's rejection of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------

### Israel warned that rejection of Christ means exclusion from the kingdom

**[MAT 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11-Matt8:12)** *See also* [MAT 21:33–21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:46) parallels [MRK 12:1–12:12](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:12), [LUK 20:9–20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:19); [MAT 22:1–22:10](https://ref.ly/Matt22:1-Matt22:10) parallel [LUK 14:16–14:24](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16-Luke14:24); [LUK 13:28–13:30](https://ref.ly/Luke13:28-Luke13:30)

### Israel's rejection of the gospel

**[ROM 9:30–10:4](https://ref.ly/Rom9:30-Rom10:4)** *See also* [ACT 28:24–28:28](https://ref.ly/Acts28:24-Acts28:28); [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); [1TH 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:14-1Thess2:16)

God's faithfulness to Israel maintained
---------------------------------------

### Membership of the true Israel is based on God's election, not merely on physical descent from Abraham

[ROM 9:6–9:18](https://ref.ly/Rom9:6-Rom9:18)

### A remnant of Israel has been elected by grace

[ROM 11:1–11:12](https://ref.ly/Rom11:1-Rom11:12)

### Paul declares that \`\`all Israel'' will be saved

[ISA 59:20–59:21](https://ref.ly/Isa59:20-Isa59:21); ; [ROM 11:25–11:32](https://ref.ly/Rom11:25-Rom11:32) *The phrase “all Israel” is interpreted as either: (1\) the total number of the elect (either Jews, or Jews and Gentiles) of every generation, or (2\) the great majority of Jews of the final generation who will be saved when Christ returns.*

The new Israel
--------------

### The church as the new Israel

**[GAL 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/Gal6:15-Gal6:16)**; **[PHP 3:3](https://ref.ly/Phil3:3)** *See also* [1PE 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9-1Pet2:10)

### The church described as the 12 tribes of Israel

**[JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1)** *See also* [REV 7:4–7:8](https://ref.ly/Rev7:4-Rev7:8) *Some see this as a reference to members of actual Jewish tribes, but others see it as symbolic of all the faithful believers who live through the time of tribulation*; [REV 21:12](https://ref.ly/Rev21:12) *The number 12 emphasises the continuity of the NT church and the OT people of God.*

### The apostles as the 12 leaders of the new Israel

**[MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28)** *See also* [LUK 22:29–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:29-Luke22:30); [REV 21:14](https://ref.ly/Rev21:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 12:1-3; Genesis 17:1-8; Genesis 35:9-13; 1 Kings 8:53; Exodus 19:3-6; Deuteronomy 7:6; Deuteronomy 32:7-12; Psalms 135:3-4; Exodus 6:6-7; Exodus 3:7-10; 2 Samuel 7:22-24; 1 Chronicles 17:20-22; Exodus 24:3-8; Romans 9:4-5; Romans 3:1-2; Philippians 3:4-6; Psalms 78:40-41; Exodus 32:1-10; Numbers 11:1-6; Numbers 14:1-4; Isaiah 1:4; Judges 2:10-13; Jeremiah 11:9-10; Ezekiel 20:27-28; 2 Kings 18:11-12; 2 Kings 25:8-12; Jeremiah 39:8-10; Jeremiah 52:12-16; Deuteronomy 4:31; Leviticus 26:44-45; Isaiah 6:11-13; Jeremiah 31:35-37; Jeremiah 30:3; Isaiah 43:5-7; Ezekiel 37:11-14; Jeremiah 3:18; Ezekiel 37:15-23; Hosea 1:10-11; Jeremiah 31:31-34; Isaiah 40:1-2; Ezekiel 36:24-28; Ezra 1:1-5; 2 Chronicles 36:22-23; Matthew 10:5-6; Matthew 15:21-28; Mark 7:24-30; Matthew 16:16-17; Mark 8:29; Luke 9:20; Mark 1:14-15; Isaiah 40:9-10; Isaiah 52:7; Matthew 4:23; Matthew 9:35; Luke 8:1; Matthew 8:11-12; Matthew 21:33-46; Mark 12:1-12; Luke 20:9-19; Matthew 22:1-10; Luke 14:16-24; Luke 13:28-30; Romans 9:30-10:4; Acts 28:24-28; Isaiah 6:9-10; 1 Thessalonians 2:14-16; Romans 9:6-18; Romans 11:1-12; Isaiah 59:20-21; Romans 11:25-32; Galatians 6:15-16; Philippians 3:3; 1 Peter 2:9-10; James 1:1; Revelation 7:4-8; Revelation 21:12; Matthew 19:28; Luke 22:29-30; Revelation 21:14

## 7140: people of God (id: 7140)

Those called out to belong to God and serve him in his world. God called Israel to be his people in the OT, anticicpating the calling of the church in the NT.

* **Associated Articles:** 7141: people of God, in OT (ID: `7141@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7142: people of God, in NT (ID: `7142@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7141: people of God, in OT (id: 7141)

The OT relates how God called Israel to be his people.

Chosen by God
-------------

**[DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6)** *See also* [DEU 4:37](https://ref.ly/Deut4:37); [1KI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:8); [ISA 41:8–41:9](https://ref.ly/Isa41:8-Isa41:9), [ISA 43:1](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1), [ISA 44:1–44:2](https://ref.ly/Isa44:1-Isa44:2); [EZK 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:5)

Loved by God
------------

**[DEU 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Deut7:7-Deut7:8)** *See also* [DEU 4:37](https://ref.ly/Deut4:37), [DEU 23:5](https://ref.ly/Deut23:5); [1KI 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:9); [PSA 44:3](https://ref.ly/Ps44:3)

Sanctified by God
-----------------

**[DEU 28:9](https://ref.ly/Deut28:9)** *See also* [EXO 19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:6), [EXO 22:31](https://ref.ly/Exod22:31); [LEV 11:44–11:45](https://ref.ly/Lev11:44-Lev11:45); [DEU 14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:2); [ISA 62:12](https://ref.ly/Isa62:12)

God's possession
----------------

**[EXO 19:5](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5)** *See also* [DEU 14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:2), [DEU 26:18](https://ref.ly/Deut26:18); [PSA 135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:4); [ISA 43:1](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1); [MAL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Mal3:17)

God's inheritance
-----------------

**[DEU 4:20](https://ref.ly/Deut4:20)** *See also* [DEU 9:26](https://ref.ly/Deut9:26), [DEU 9:29](https://ref.ly/Deut9:29); [PSA 28:9](https://ref.ly/Ps28:9), [PSA 33:12](https://ref.ly/Ps33:12), [PSA 68:9](https://ref.ly/Ps68:9), [PSA 74:2](https://ref.ly/Ps74:2); [JER 10:16](https://ref.ly/Jer10:16); [JOL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Joel3:2); [MIC 7:14](https://ref.ly/Mic7:14)

God's servants
--------------

**[DEU 32:36](https://ref.ly/Deut32:36)** *See also* [NEH 1:6](https://ref.ly/Neh1:6), [NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11); [PSA 135:14](https://ref.ly/Ps135:14); [ISA 41:8–41:9](https://ref.ly/Isa41:8-Isa41:9)

God's children
--------------

**[EXO 4:22](https://ref.ly/Exod4:22)** *See also* [DEU 32:6](https://ref.ly/Deut32:6); [ISA 63:16](https://ref.ly/Isa63:16), [ISA 64:8](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8); [JER 3:19](https://ref.ly/Jer3:19), [JER 31:9](https://ref.ly/Jer31:9); [HOS 11:1](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1)

God's sheep
-----------

**[PSA 95:6–95:7](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6-Ps95:7)**; **[EZK 34:11–34:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11-Ezek34:16)** *See also* [PSA 28:9](https://ref.ly/Ps28:9), [PSA 74:1](https://ref.ly/Ps74:1), [PSA 79:13](https://ref.ly/Ps79:13), [PSA 80:1](https://ref.ly/Ps80:1), [PSA 100:3](https://ref.ly/Ps100:3); [ISA 40:11](https://ref.ly/Isa40:11); [JER 23:1](https://ref.ly/Jer23:1), [JER 31:10](https://ref.ly/Jer31:10)

## 7142: people of God, in NT (id: 7142)

The NT relates how the Christian church is the new people of God, assuming all the privileges and responsibilities of Israel in the OT, but with a universal mission to all the world, rather than any one nation.

NT believers are God's people
-----------------------------

**[ROM 9:22–9:26](https://ref.ly/Rom9:22-Rom9:26)** *See also* [1PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:10); [2CO 9:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:12); [EPH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3); [REV 22:21](https://ref.ly/Rev22:21)

Believers are the true children of Abraham
------------------------------------------

**[GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:29)** *See also* [MAT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Matt3:9) parallel [LUK 3:8](https://ref.ly/Luke3:8); [ROM 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/Rom4:16-Rom4:18); [GAL 3:6–3:9](https://ref.ly/Gal3:6-Gal3:9), [GAL 4:22–4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:22-Gal4:31); [1PE 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:6)

The church is the people of God
-------------------------------

### God's chosen people

**[1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)** *See also* [COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12); [1TH 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:4); [2TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:13)

### God's children

**[JHN 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:12-John1:13)** *See also* [GAL 3:26](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26), [GAL 4:4–4:6](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:6); [EPH 1:5](https://ref.ly/Eph1:5); [PHP 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14-Phil2:15)

### God's possession

[EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14); ; [TIT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:14); ; [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)

### God's servants

[1PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:16); ; [REV 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rev1:1), [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

### The temple of God

**[1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5)** *See also* [EPH 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19-Eph2:22); [HEB 3:6](https://ref.ly/Heb3:6)

### The body of Christ

**[EPH 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22-Eph1:23)** *See also* [ROM 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4-Rom12:5); [1CO 12:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27); [EPH 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:13)

Jesus Christ sends believers into all the world
-----------------------------------------------

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)** *See also* [MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19); [MRK 13:10](https://ref.ly/Mark13:10), [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15); [LUK 24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:47); [JHN 17:20–17:24](https://ref.ly/John17:20-John17:24)

## 7145: remnant (id: 7145)

A small group of people who remain faithful to God, despite widespread unbelief or faithlessness around them. God preserves a remnant in order to ensure a renewed and restored people in the future.

God preserves a remnant of his people
-------------------------------------

### Noah and his family

**[GEN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Gen7:23)** *See also* [GEN 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Gen6:7-Gen6:8), [GEN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Gen6:18), [GEN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Gen7:1), [GEN 7:7](https://ref.ly/Gen7:7)

### Joseph and the sons of Israel

**[GEN 45:7](https://ref.ly/Gen45:7)** *See also* [GEN 50:20](https://ref.ly/Gen50:20)

### Seven thousand faithful in Israel

**[1KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:18)**

### Survivors of Assyria's invasion of Judah

**[2KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:4)** parallel [ISA 37:4](https://ref.ly/Isa37:4) *See also* [2KI 18:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:13)

### Survivors of the destruction of Israel

People from the northern kingdom of Israel who had moved to Judah and so escaped its destruction by Assyria: [2CH 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:14), [2CH 15:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:9), [2CH 31:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:6), [2CH 34:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:9)

### Judah as the remnant of God's people

[2KI 17:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:18); [2KI 21:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:14); ; [2CH 34:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:21)

### Survivors of the fall of Jerusalem

Those taken as exiles into Babylon: [2KI 25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:11); ; [2CH 36:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:20); [2KI 25:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:12) parallel [JER 39:10](https://ref.ly/Jer39:10); ; [2KI 25:22–25:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:22-2Kgs25:23); ; [JER 40:11–40:12](https://ref.ly/Jer40:11-Jer40:12), [JER 40:15](https://ref.ly/Jer40:15), [JER 42:2](https://ref.ly/Jer42:2), [JER 43:5](https://ref.ly/Jer43:5)

### Those who returned after the exile

**[EZR 9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:8)** *See also* [EZR 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:1-Ezra2:2), [EZR 9:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:13); [NEH 1:2](https://ref.ly/Neh1:2); [HAG 1:12–1:14](https://ref.ly/Hag1:12-Hag1:14)

### Those waiting for Jesus Christ's coming

[LUK 2:25](https://ref.ly/Luke2:25), [LUK 2:38](https://ref.ly/Luke2:38); ; [JHN 1:12](https://ref.ly/John1:12)

The remnant as the few who escape judgment
------------------------------------------

**[DEU 4:27](https://ref.ly/Deut4:27)** *See also* [ISA 10:22](https://ref.ly/Isa10:22); [ROM 9:27](https://ref.ly/Rom9:27); [JER 6:9](https://ref.ly/Jer6:9), [JER 44:14](https://ref.ly/Jer44:14), [JER 44:28](https://ref.ly/Jer44:28); [EZK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:12)EZK [9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:8), [EZK 11:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:13)[EZK 17:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:21); [AMO 3:12](https://ref.ly/Amos3:12), [AMO 5:3](https://ref.ly/Amos5:3)

God's promise of hope and restoration
-------------------------------------

### Hope for a remnant seen in the coming of the Messiah

**[ISA 11:1](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1)** *See also* [ISA 6:13](https://ref.ly/Isa6:13); [JER 23:5](https://ref.ly/Jer23:5); [ZEC 3:8](https://ref.ly/Zech3:8)

### God's promise that survivors will remain

**[ISA 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Isa4:2-Isa4:3)** *See also* [ISA 1:9](https://ref.ly/Isa1:9); [ROM 9:29](https://ref.ly/Rom9:29); [ISA 17:6](https://ref.ly/Isa17:6), [ISA 37:31–37:32](https://ref.ly/Isa37:31-Isa37:32) parallel [2KI 19:30–19:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:30-2Kgs19:31); [EZK 14:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:22); [JOL 2:32](https://ref.ly/Joel2:32)

Themes associated with the remnant
----------------------------------

### The land restored to the remnant of God's people

[ISA 27:12–27:13](https://ref.ly/Isa27:12-Isa27:13); ; [ZEP 2:7–2:10](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:7-Zeph2:10); ; [ZEC 8:6–8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:6-Zech8:12), [ZEC 10:9](https://ref.ly/Zech10:9)

### A second exodus

[ISA 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Isa4:5-Isa4:6), [ISA 10:26](https://ref.ly/Isa10:26), [ISA 11:11](https://ref.ly/Isa11:11), [ISA 11:15–11:16](https://ref.ly/Isa11:15-Isa11:16), [ISA 31:5](https://ref.ly/Isa31:5); ; [JER 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Jer16:14-Jer16:15)

### Restoration of the people

**[ISA 10:20–10:21](https://ref.ly/Isa10:20-Isa10:21)** *See also* [ISA 11:12](https://ref.ly/Isa11:12), [ISA 28:5](https://ref.ly/Isa28:5), [ISA 35:8–35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:8-Isa35:10), [ISA 46:3–46:4](https://ref.ly/Isa46:3-Isa46:4); [JER 31:7](https://ref.ly/Jer31:7)**[JER 23:3](https://ref.ly/Jer23:3)** *See also* [MIC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mic2:12)

Further promises associated with the remnant
--------------------------------------------

### The promise of future hope

[ISA 60:21–60:22](https://ref.ly/Isa60:21-Isa60:22), [ISA 61:4–61:6](https://ref.ly/Isa61:4-Isa61:6); ; [JOL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Joel3:1); ; [MIC 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Mic4:6-Mic4:7), [MIC 5:7–5:9](https://ref.ly/Mic5:7-Mic5:9)

### God's promise of forgiveness to the remnant

**[JER 50:20](https://ref.ly/Jer50:20)** *See also* [ISA 33:24](https://ref.ly/Isa33:24); [JER 31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:34), [JER 33:7–33:8](https://ref.ly/Jer33:7-Jer33:8); [MIC 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mic7:18)

### A renewed relationship with God

[JER 24:5–24:7](https://ref.ly/Jer24:5-Jer24:7), [JER 32:37–32:40](https://ref.ly/Jer32:37-Jer32:40); ; [ZEP 3:12–3:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:12-Zeph3:17)

### The need for faithful obedience

[ISA 1:19](https://ref.ly/Isa1:19); ; [JER 42:6–42:10](https://ref.ly/Jer42:6-Jer42:10), [JER 44:7–44:8](https://ref.ly/Jer44:7-Jer44:8); ; [EZR 9:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:14)

The remnant as heirs of God's new covenant
------------------------------------------

[JER 31:2](https://ref.ly/Jer31:2) *The promise of the new covenant is made to the surviving remnant of Israel*; ; [JER 31:31–31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:31-Jer31:33)

### A faithful remnant among the Jews

**[ROM 11:5](https://ref.ly/Rom11:5)** *See also* [LUK 1:16](https://ref.ly/Luke1:16), [LUK 12:32](https://ref.ly/Luke12:32); [ROM 11:7](https://ref.ly/Rom11:7), [ROM 11:15](https://ref.ly/Rom11:15), [ROM 11:23](https://ref.ly/Rom11:23), [ROM 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/Rom11:25-Rom11:26); [JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1)

### A faithful remnant from among the Gentiles

[ISA 56:6–56:8](https://ref.ly/Isa56:6-Isa56:8); ; [ZEC 14:16](https://ref.ly/Zech14:16); ; [JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:16) *Jesus Christ's other sheep are believing Gentiles who will be gathered with the believers of Israel*; ; [ACT 15:17](https://ref.ly/Acts15:17); ; [AMO 9:12](https://ref.ly/Amos9:12); ; [ROM 11:17](https://ref.ly/Rom11:17); ; [EPH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:6)

### God's new Israel includes all believers

[MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28) parallel [LUK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:30); ; [GAL 6:16](https://ref.ly/Gal6:16); ; [1PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1), [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)

Other references to a surviving remnant
---------------------------------------

Og, king of Bashan, only survivor of the Rephaites: [DEU 3:11](https://ref.ly/Deut3:11); ; [JOS 13:12](https://ref.ly/Josh13:12); [JDG 20:46–20:47](https://ref.ly/Judg20:46-Judg20:47) *survivors from the tribe of Benjamin*; ; [2CH 18:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:16) *the scattered remains of Israel's army under Ahab*; ; [ISA 16:4](https://ref.ly/Isa16:4) *fugitives from Moab*; ; [ISA 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/Isa21:16-Isa21:17) *few survivors from Kedar*

## 7150: righteous, the (id: 7150)

The designation, especially in the OT, of those who live and act in accordance with God's commands.

Characteristics of the righteous
--------------------------------

**[PRO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Prov20:7)** *See also* [PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21); [PRO 10:11](https://ref.ly/Prov10:11), [PRO 10:20–10:21](https://ref.ly/Prov10:20-Prov10:21), [PRO 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Prov10:31-Prov10:32), [PRO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Prov12:5), [PRO 13:5](https://ref.ly/Prov13:5), [PRO 15:28](https://ref.ly/Prov15:28), [PRO 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/Prov21:25-Prov21:26), [PRO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Prov29:7), [PRO 29:27](https://ref.ly/Prov29:27); [MAT 25:31–25:40](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31-Matt25:40)

God's dealings with the righteous
---------------------------------

### He loves them

**[PSA 146:8](https://ref.ly/Ps146:8)** *See also* [GEN 18:20–18:32](https://ref.ly/Gen18:20-Gen18:32)

### He protects them

**[PRO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Prov18:10)** *See also* [PSA 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Ps1:5-Ps1:6), [PSA 37:16–37:17](https://ref.ly/Ps37:16-Ps37:17), [PSA 37:39](https://ref.ly/Ps37:39), [PSA 55:22](https://ref.ly/Ps55:22); [PRO 10:29](https://ref.ly/Prov10:29), [PRO 11:8](https://ref.ly/Prov11:8), [PRO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Prov12:21)

### He provides for them

**[PRO 10:3](https://ref.ly/Prov10:3)** *See also* [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25); [PRO 13:25](https://ref.ly/Prov13:25)

### He blesses them

**[PSA 5:12](https://ref.ly/Ps5:12)** *See also* [JOB 36:7](https://ref.ly/Job36:7); [PRO 3:33](https://ref.ly/Prov3:33), [PRO 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/Prov10:6-Prov10:7)

### He hears their petitions

**[1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12)** *See also* [PSA 34:17](https://ref.ly/Ps34:17); [PRO 15:29](https://ref.ly/Prov15:29)

The faith of the righteous
--------------------------

**[HAB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4)** *See also* [ROM 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rom1:17); [GAL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Gal3:11); [HEB 10:38](https://ref.ly/Heb10:38)

The righteous are secure
------------------------

**[PRO 12:3](https://ref.ly/Prov12:3)** *See also* [PRO 10:30](https://ref.ly/Prov10:30), [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28), [PRO 12:7](https://ref.ly/Prov12:7), [PRO 28:1](https://ref.ly/Prov28:1)

The righteous can be corrupted
------------------------------

**[EZK 18:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:24)** *See also* [EXO 23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:8); [DEU 16:19](https://ref.ly/Deut16:19); [PSA 125:3](https://ref.ly/Ps125:3); [EZK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:20), [EZK 33:12–33:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:12-Ezek33:13)

The oppression of the righteous
-------------------------------

**[PSA 37:12–37:13](https://ref.ly/Ps37:12-Ps37:13)** *See also* [PSA 31:17–31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:17-Ps31:18), [PSA 94:21](https://ref.ly/Ps94:21); [LAM 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Lam4:11-Lam4:13); [AMO 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:7), [AMO 5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:12); [HAB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:4)

The equality, in human terms, of the righteous and the wicked
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)** *See also* [ECC 8:12–8:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:12-Eccl8:14), [ECC 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:1-Eccl9:2)

The reward of the righteous
---------------------------

**[PRO 10:16](https://ref.ly/Prov10:16)** *See also* [PSA 37:29](https://ref.ly/Ps37:29), [PSA 58:10–58:11](https://ref.ly/Ps58:10-Ps58:11), [PSA 92:12–92:15](https://ref.ly/Ps92:12-Ps92:15); [PRO 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/Prov10:6-Prov10:7), [PRO 10:28](https://ref.ly/Prov10:28), [PRO 11:23](https://ref.ly/Prov11:23), [PRO 11:30–11:31](https://ref.ly/Prov11:30-Prov11:31), [PRO 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/Prov13:21-Prov13:22), [PRO 15:6](https://ref.ly/Prov15:6); [ISA 3:10](https://ref.ly/Isa3:10)

The death of the righteous
--------------------------

**[ISA 57:1](https://ref.ly/Isa57:1)** *See also* [PSA 116:15](https://ref.ly/Ps116:15); [PRO 14:32](https://ref.ly/Prov14:32)

The hope of the righteous
-------------------------

**[LUK 14:12–14:14](https://ref.ly/Luke14:12-Luke14:14)** *See also* [PSA 17:14–17:15](https://ref.ly/Ps17:14-Ps17:15); [PRO 14:32](https://ref.ly/Prov14:32); [MAL 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Mal3:17-Mal3:18); [ACT 24:14–24:16](https://ref.ly/Acts24:14-Acts24:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 20:7; Psalms 37:21; Proverbs 10:11; Proverbs 10:20-21; Proverbs 10:31-32; Proverbs 12:5; Proverbs 13:5; Proverbs 15:28; Proverbs 21:25-26; Proverbs 29:7; Proverbs 29:27; Matthew 25:31-40; Psalms 146:8; Genesis 18:20-32; Proverbs 18:10; Psalms 1:5-6; Psalms 37:16-17; Psalms 37:39; Psalms 55:22; Proverbs 10:29; Proverbs 11:8; Proverbs 12:21; Proverbs 10:3; Psalms 37:25; Proverbs 13:25; Psalms 5:12; Job 36:7; Proverbs 3:33; Proverbs 10:6-7; 1 Peter 3:12; Psalms 34:17; Proverbs 15:29; Habakkuk 2:4; Romans 1:17; Galatians 3:11; Hebrews 10:38; Proverbs 12:3; Proverbs 10:30; Proverbs 11:28; Proverbs 12:7; Proverbs 28:1; Ezekiel 18:24; Exodus 23:8; Deuteronomy 16:19; Psalms 125:3; Ezekiel 3:20; Ezekiel 33:12-13; Psalms 37:12-13; Psalms 31:17-18; Psalms 94:21; Lamentations 4:11-13; Amos 2:6-7; Amos 5:12; Habakkuk 1:4; Matthew 5:45; Ecclesiastes 8:12-14; Ecclesiastes 9:1-2; Proverbs 10:16; Psalms 37:29; Psalms 58:10-11; Psalms 92:12-15; Proverbs 10:28; Proverbs 11:23; Proverbs 11:30-31; Proverbs 13:21-22; Proverbs 15:6; Isaiah 3:10; Isaiah 57:1; Psalms 116:15; Proverbs 14:32; Luke 14:12-14; Psalms 17:14-15; Malachi 3:17-18; Acts 24:14-16

## 7155: saints (id: 7155)

The people of God, especially in relation to their being set apart from the world to serve him. Believers are called upon to lead holy lives worthy of their calling.

Saints are set apart for God
----------------------------

**[1CO 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:2)** *To be holy is to be set apart. The same Greek word is used in the NT for both “holy” and, in the plural, “saints”.* *See also* [DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6), [DEU 26:19](https://ref.ly/Deut26:19); [PSA 4:3](https://ref.ly/Ps4:3); [ROM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Rom1:7); [HEB 10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:10)

### They are members of God's covenant people

**[PSA 85:8](https://ref.ly/Ps85:8)** *See also* [2CH 6:41](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:41) parallel [PSA 132:9](https://ref.ly/Ps132:9); [PSA 30:4](https://ref.ly/Ps30:4), [PSA 34:9](https://ref.ly/Ps34:9), [PSA 50:5](https://ref.ly/Ps50:5), [PSA 79:1–79:2](https://ref.ly/Ps79:1-Ps79:2), [PSA 132:16](https://ref.ly/Ps132:16)

### They are believers

**[ROM 16:15](https://ref.ly/Rom16:15)** *See also* [ROM 8:27](https://ref.ly/Rom8:27), [ROM 15:31](https://ref.ly/Rom15:31); [PHP 4:21–4:22](https://ref.ly/Phil4:21-Phil4:22); [COL 1:26](https://ref.ly/Col1:26); [1TI 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:10)

### They make up the church

**[1CO 14:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:33)** *See also* [ACT 9:32](https://ref.ly/Acts9:32); [EPH 1:1](https://ref.ly/Eph1:1); [COL 1:2](https://ref.ly/Col1:2)

Saints are to live lives worthy of their calling
------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3-Eph5:4)** *See also* [ROM 16:2](https://ref.ly/Rom16:2); [EPH 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:1-Eph4:3); [2PE 3:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:11); [REV 13:10](https://ref.ly/Rev13:10), [REV 14:12](https://ref.ly/Rev14:12)

Saints have responsibility for one another
------------------------------------------

**[ROM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Rom12:13)** *See also* [ROM 15:25–15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:25-Rom15:26); [1CO 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1), [1CO 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:1-1Cor16:2), [1CO 16:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15); [2CO 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:1-2Cor9:2), [2CO 9:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:12)

Saints have spiritual union with Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

**[HEB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Heb2:11)** *See also* [JHN 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:4-John15:5); [1CO 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:2); [EPH 1:1](https://ref.ly/Eph1:1); [PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1), [PHP 4:21](https://ref.ly/Phil4:21); [COL 1:2](https://ref.ly/Col1:2); [2TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:10)

Saints may be persecuted
------------------------

**[JHN 15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:19)** *See also* [DAN 7:21](https://ref.ly/Dan7:21), [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25), [DAN 8:24](https://ref.ly/Dan8:24); [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14); [ACT 9:13](https://ref.ly/Acts9:13), [ACT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10); [REV 13:7](https://ref.ly/Rev13:7), [REV 16:6](https://ref.ly/Rev16:6)

Saints are precious in God's sight
----------------------------------

**[PSA 16:3](https://ref.ly/Ps16:3)** *See also* [PSA 116:15](https://ref.ly/Ps116:15), [PSA 147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:11), [PSA 149:4](https://ref.ly/Ps149:4); [EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eph1:18)

### They enjoy God's protection

**[1SA 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9)** *See also* [PSA 31:23](https://ref.ly/Ps31:23), [PSA 37:28](https://ref.ly/Ps37:28), [PSA 97:10](https://ref.ly/Ps97:10); [PRO 2:8](https://ref.ly/Prov2:8)

### They experience exaltation

**[DAN 7:18](https://ref.ly/Dan7:18)** *See also* [PSA 149:6–149:9](https://ref.ly/Ps149:6-Ps149:9); [DAN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:22), [DAN 7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:27); [MAT 27:52](https://ref.ly/Matt27:52); [1CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:2); [COL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Col1:12)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 1:2; Deuteronomy 7:6; Deuteronomy 26:19; Psalms 4:3; Romans 1:7; Hebrews 10:10; Psalms 85:8; 2 Chronicles 6:41; Psalms 132:9; Psalms 30:4; Psalms 34:9; Psalms 50:5; Psalms 79:1-2; Psalms 132:16; Romans 16:15; Romans 8:27; Romans 15:31; Philippians 4:21-22; Colossians 1:26; 1 Timothy 5:10; 1 Corinthians 14:33; Acts 9:32; Ephesians 1:1; Colossians 1:2; Ephesians 5:3-4; Romans 16:2; Ephesians 4:1-3; 2 Peter 3:11; Revelation 13:10; Revelation 14:12; Romans 12:13; Romans 15:25-26; 1 Corinthians 6:1; 1 Corinthians 16:1-2; 1 Corinthians 16:15; 2 Corinthians 9:1-2; 2 Corinthians 9:12; Hebrews 2:11; John 15:4-5; Philippians 1:1; Philippians 4:21; 2 Thessalonians 1:10; John 15:19; Daniel 7:21; Daniel 7:25; Daniel 8:24; John 17:14; Acts 9:13; Acts 26:10; Revelation 13:7; Revelation 16:6; Psalms 16:3; Psalms 116:15; Psalms 147:11; Psalms 149:4; Ephesians 1:18; 1 Samuel 2:9; Psalms 31:23; Psalms 37:28; Psalms 97:10; Proverbs 2:8; Daniel 7:18; Psalms 149:6-9; Daniel 7:22; Daniel 7:27; Matthew 27:52; 1 Corinthians 6:2; Colossians 1:12

## 7160: servants of the Lord (id: 7160)

A description of individuals, groups, and the nation of Israel. Isaiah portrays a unique servant whom the NT identifies as Jesus Christ.

Individuals as servants of the Lord
-----------------------------------

### The patriarchs as servants of the Lord

**[EXO 32:13](https://ref.ly/Exod32:13)** *See also* [GEN 18:3](https://ref.ly/Gen18:3), [GEN 32:10](https://ref.ly/Gen32:10); [DEU 9:27](https://ref.ly/Deut9:27); [1CH 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:13); [PSA 105:6](https://ref.ly/Ps105:6)

### Moses as the servant of the Lord

**[EXO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Exod14:31)** *See also* [NUM 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Num12:7-Num12:8); [DEU 34:5](https://ref.ly/Deut34:5); [JOS 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Josh1:1-Josh1:2); [NEH 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Neh1:7-Neh1:8); [MAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mal4:4)

### Other leaders as servants of the Lord

[NUM 14:24](https://ref.ly/Num14:24); ; [JOS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Josh5:14), [JOS 24:29](https://ref.ly/Josh24:29); ; [JDG 2:8](https://ref.ly/Judg2:8), [JDG 15:18](https://ref.ly/Judg15:18)

### David as the servant of the Lord

**[PSA 89:20](https://ref.ly/Ps89:20)** *See also* [1SA 23:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:11); [2SA 7:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:5) parallel [1CH 17:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:4); [1KI 14:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:8)

### Other kings as servants of the Lord

[1KI 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:7-1Kgs3:9); ; [2CH 32:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:16)

### Individual prophets as servants of the Lord

[1KI 14:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:18), [1KI 18:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:36); ; [2KI 14:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:25); ; [ISA 20:3](https://ref.ly/Isa20:3)

### Paul as the servant of the Lord

**[ACT 27:23](https://ref.ly/Acts27:23)** *See also* [ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); [EPH 3:1](https://ref.ly/Eph3:1)

### Other individual believers as servants of the Lord

[1SA 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11); ; [JOB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Job1:8), [JOB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Job2:3), [JOB 42:7–42:8](https://ref.ly/Job42:7-Job42:8); ; [HAG 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hag2:23); ; [LUK 1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:38), [LUK 1:48](https://ref.ly/Luke1:48), [LUK 2:29](https://ref.ly/Luke2:29); ; [JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1); ; [2PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:1); ; [JUD 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jude1:1); ; [REV 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rev1:1)

### Pagan kings as servants of the Lord

**[JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9)** *See also* [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1); [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10)

Groups of servants of the Lord
------------------------------

### The people of Israel as the Lord's servants

**[ISA 41:8–41:9](https://ref.ly/Isa41:8-Isa41:9)** *See also* [LEV 25:42](https://ref.ly/Lev25:42), [LEV 25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:55); [NEH 1:6](https://ref.ly/Neh1:6), [NEH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Neh1:10); [ISA 43:10](https://ref.ly/Isa43:10); [JER 30:10–30:11](https://ref.ly/Jer30:10-Jer30:11) parallel [JER 46:27–46:28](https://ref.ly/Jer46:27-Jer46:28); [LUK 1:54](https://ref.ly/Luke1:54)

### Failure of the people of Israel to serve the Lord

**[ISA 42:19](https://ref.ly/Isa42:19)** *See also* [JDG 10:6](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6); [1SA 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:10); [NEH 9:35](https://ref.ly/Neh9:35); [MAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mal3:14)

### Priests as the Lord's servants

[EXO 28:1](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1), [EXO 28:41](https://ref.ly/Exod28:41); ; [LEV 7:35](https://ref.ly/Lev7:35); ; [NUM 18:7](https://ref.ly/Num18:7); ; [HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Heb8:5)

### Levites as the Lord's servants

[DEU 18:7](https://ref.ly/Deut18:7); ; [1CH 23:28–23:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:28-1Chr23:31); ; [EZR 6:18](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:18); ; [EZK 44:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:11)

### Prophets as the Lord's servants

**[JER 7:25](https://ref.ly/Jer7:25)** *See also* [JER 29:19](https://ref.ly/Jer29:19), [JER 44:4](https://ref.ly/Jer44:4); [EZK 38:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:17); [DAN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Dan9:6); [AMO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Amos3:7); [ZEC 1:6](https://ref.ly/Zech1:6); [MAT 21:34–21:36](https://ref.ly/Matt21:34-Matt21:36) parallels [MRK 12:2–12:5](https://ref.ly/Mark12:2-Mark12:5), [LUK 20:10–20:12](https://ref.ly/Luke20:10-Luke20:12); [REV 10:7](https://ref.ly/Rev10:7)

### Other nations as the Lord's servants

**[PSA 72:11](https://ref.ly/Ps72:11)** *See also* [ISA 56:6](https://ref.ly/Isa56:6); [ZEP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:9)

The Lord's servants recognised as such by pagans
------------------------------------------------

**[DAN 3:26](https://ref.ly/Dan3:26)** *See also* [DAN 6:16](https://ref.ly/Dan6:16), [DAN 6:20](https://ref.ly/Dan6:20); [ACT 16:17](https://ref.ly/Acts16:17)

The unique servant of the Lord
------------------------------

### Isaiah's vision of this servant

### Serving the Lord as he requires

## 7203: ark, Noah's (id: 7203)

The craft built at God's command by Noah and his family as a means of escape from the flood. The ark is seen as corresponding to the redemption offered to believers through Jesus Christ.

The building of the ark
-----------------------

### The command to build

**[GEN 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gen6:14)** *See also* [GEN 6:1–6:13](https://ref.ly/Gen6:1-Gen6:13), [GEN 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/Gen6:15-Gen6:16)

### Its purpose for Noah and his family

**[GEN 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/Gen6:17-Gen6:18)**

### Its purpose for the animals

**[GEN 6:19](https://ref.ly/Gen6:19)** *See also* [GEN 6:20–6:22](https://ref.ly/Gen6:20-Gen6:22)

The ark and the flood
---------------------

### Noah obeyed God

**[GEN 7:5](https://ref.ly/Gen7:5)** *See also* [GEN 7:1–7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:1-Gen7:4), [GEN 7:6–7:16](https://ref.ly/Gen7:6-Gen7:16)

### The ark served its purpose

**[GEN 7:17](https://ref.ly/Gen7:17)** *See also* [GEN 7:18–7:24](https://ref.ly/Gen7:18-Gen7:24), [GEN 8:1–8:19](https://ref.ly/Gen8:1-Gen8:19), [GEN 9:1–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:1-Gen9:17)

The significance of the ark elsewhere in Scripture
--------------------------------------------------

### An instance of judgment

**[MAT 24:38–24:39](https://ref.ly/Matt24:38-Matt24:39)** parallel [LUK 17:26–17:27](https://ref.ly/Luke17:26-Luke17:27) *Judgment clearly divides humanity into the saved and the lost.* *See also* [2PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:5)

### An example of faith

**[HEB 11:7](https://ref.ly/Heb11:7)** *Noah took God at his word in circumstances which must have appeared ridiculous.*

### A symbol of salvation

**[1PE 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:20-1Pet3:21)** *The link between the ark and baptism is in the twin ideas of water and salvation.*

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 6:14; Genesis 6:1-13; Genesis 6:15-16; Genesis 6:17-18; Genesis 6:19; Genesis 6:20-22; Genesis 7:5; Genesis 7:1-4; Genesis 7:6-16; Genesis 7:17; Genesis 7:18-24; Genesis 8:1-19; Genesis 9:1-17; Matthew 24:38-39; Luke 17:26-27; 2 Peter 2:5; Hebrews 11:7; 1 Peter 3:20-21
* **Associated Articles:** 1347: covenant, God's with Noah (ID: `1347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1680: types (ID: `1680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4845: rainbow (ID: `4845@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5106: Noah (ID: `5106@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6510: salvation (ID: `6510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7227: flood, the (ID: `7227@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7206: community (id: 7206)

A group, small or large, of people. In Scripture it denotes the people of Israel, especially in pre\-monarchic times and particularly during the period of the wilderness wanderings.

The community of Israel in the wilderness
-----------------------------------------

**[EXO 16:1](https://ref.ly/Exod16:1)** *See also* [EXO 17:1](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1); [NUM 13:26](https://ref.ly/Num13:26), [NUM 20:22](https://ref.ly/Num20:22), [NUM 20:29](https://ref.ly/Num20:29)

### Moses and Aaron communicate God's instructions to the wilderness community

### Responsibilities towards one another within the wilderness community

[LEV 10:6](https://ref.ly/Lev10:6); ; [NUM 1:53](https://ref.ly/Num1:53)

### Leaders and officials, under Moses, of the wilderness community

**[EXO 16:22](https://ref.ly/Exod16:22)** *See also* [EXO 34:31](https://ref.ly/Exod34:31); [NUM 26:9](https://ref.ly/Num26:9), [NUM 31:13](https://ref.ly/Num31:13), [NUM 31:26–31:27](https://ref.ly/Num31:26-Num31:27), [NUM 32:1–32:5](https://ref.ly/Num32:1-Num32:5)

### Censuses of the wilderness community

**[NUM 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:1-Num1:3)** *See also* [EXO 38:25](https://ref.ly/Exod38:25); [NUM 1:17–1:19](https://ref.ly/Num1:17-Num1:19), [NUM 4:34](https://ref.ly/Num4:34)

-----------------------------------------------

## 7209: congregation (id: 7209)

A group of people gathered or called together for a purpose. In Scripture a congregation refers particularly to the Israelites gathered for worship; it is also used of Christians in the NT.

The congregation of the Israelites
----------------------------------

### For the giving of the Ten Commandments

*See also* [EXO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Exod19:17); [DEU 5:22](https://ref.ly/Deut5:22)

### For hearing God's word

**[NEH 8:1–8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:1-Neh8:9)** *See also* [DEU 5:1](https://ref.ly/Deut5:1); [NEH 8:18](https://ref.ly/Neh8:18), [NEH 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/Neh9:1-Neh9:3)

### For praising God

**[PSA 22:22](https://ref.ly/Ps22:22)** *See also* [PSA 40:9–40:10](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9-Ps40:10), [PSA 68:26](https://ref.ly/Ps68:26); [HEB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Heb2:12)

### To make a sin offering for the sin of the community

[LEV 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13-Lev4:14); ; [NUM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Num15:24)

### For the ordination of priests and Levites

[LEV 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Lev8:3-Lev8:4); ; [NUM 8:9](https://ref.ly/Num8:9)

### For census, retribution and major events

## 7212: exile (id: 7212)

A period of absence from one's homeland, often imposed as a form of punishment. The OT regards Israel's exile in Babylon as a form of divine punishment for past sin and as an opportunity for renewal and rededication.

Exile commanded by God for his purposes
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 12:1](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1)** *See also* [GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4), [GEN 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:8-Gen11:9); [PSA 44:11](https://ref.ly/Ps44:11); [ZEC 2:6](https://ref.ly/Zech2:6); [MAT 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13-Matt2:15)

Exile imposed as a form of punishment
-------------------------------------

### On individuals

**[GEN 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gen3:23)** *See also* [GEN 17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:14); [LEV 7:20](https://ref.ly/Lev7:20), [LEV 18:29](https://ref.ly/Lev18:29), [LEV 19:8](https://ref.ly/Lev19:8), [LEV 20:17](https://ref.ly/Lev20:17); [DEU 28:36](https://ref.ly/Deut28:36); [1SA 12:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:25); [2KI 24:15–24:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:15-2Kgs24:16); [2CH 36:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:4), [2CH 36:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:10); [ZEC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3)

-----------------------------

### To escape danger

**[EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15)** *See also* [GEN 16:6–16:8](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6-Gen16:8), [GEN 35:6–35:7](https://ref.ly/Gen35:6-Gen35:7); [1SA 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:18), [1SA 21:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:10); [2SA 13:38–13:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:38-2Sam13:39); [REV 1:9](https://ref.ly/Rev1:9)

### At the advice of others

[GEN 27:42–27:45](https://ref.ly/Gen27:42-Gen27:45); ; [1SA 20:12–20:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:12-1Sam20:13)

-----------------------------------

## 7215: exile, the (id: 7215)

The period of Israel's captivity in foreign lands, under Assyria in the eighth century B.C., and under Babylon in the sixth. Such exile is regarded by biblical writers as a judgment upon Israel for her folly and unfaithfulness. It is also seen as a time of repentance and renewal leading eventually to restoration.

* **Associated Articles:** 7216: exile, in Assyria (ID: `7216@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7217: exile, in Babylon (ID: `7217@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7216: exile, in Assyria (id: 7216)

People of the northern kingdom of Israel were taken into exile after their defeat by the Assyrians and the fall of Samaria. The Israelites were transported to Assyria in stages, starting with the upper strata of society. They were replaced by immigrants from other parts of the Assyrian empire. The Israelites had to share their land with these people when they returned from exile after the downfall of the Assyrians. This was the origin of the Samaritan people.

Israel was warned of coming punishment for her misconduct
---------------------------------------------------------

### God warned his people

**[DEU 28:62–28:66](https://ref.ly/Deut28:62-Deut28:66)**; **[ISA 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/Isa5:12-Isa5:13)** *See also* [LEV 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Lev26:27-Lev26:28); [DEU 28:49–28:57](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49-Deut28:57); [ISA 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Isa10:5-Isa10:6); [HOS 8:1–8:8](https://ref.ly/Hos8:1-Hos8:8), [HOS 13:16](https://ref.ly/Hos13:16); [AMO 3:2](https://ref.ly/Amos3:2), [AMO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Amos3:11), [AMO 5:27](https://ref.ly/Amos5:27), [AMO 9:4](https://ref.ly/Amos9:4), [AMO 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/Amos9:7-Amos9:9); [MIC 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Mic1:6-Mic1:7)

## 7217: exile, in Babylon (id: 7217)

The southern kingdom of Judah was conquered by Babylon under Nebuchadnezzar. Before and after the destruction of Jerusalem in 587 B.C. the population was taken into exile in three stages.

The entire episode was within the purposes of God and under his control
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7221: exodus, the (id: 7221)

The escape of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt under Moses' leadership. Despite their great deliverance, hardness of heart and disobedience towards God persisted. Divine deliverance in the exodus foreshadowed the act of salvation through Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 7222: exodus, events of (ID: `7222@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7223: exodus, spiritual significance of (ID: `7223@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7222: exodus, events of (id: 7222)

The escape of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt and their deliverance in the crossing of the Red Sea. The beginning of a long and difficult journey under Moses' leadership to Canaan, the promised land.

----------------------------

**[EXO 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:1-Exod6:8)** *See also* [EXO 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:10)

----------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------

## 7223: exodus, spiritual significance of (id: 7223)

Scripture records the many instances of praise being given to God on account of the exodus. The events of the exodus, however, reveal the lack of faith, the hardness of heart and the spiritual stubbornness that can persist among believers. Scripture warns of the consequences of these things. In addition the exodus is seen as an act of divine deliverance which foreshadows the greater act of salvation through Jesus Christ.

-------------------------------------------

## 7227: flood, the (id: 7227)

A universal catastrophe by means of which the world was overwhelmed by water as the judgment of God on sinful humanity. Only Noah, his family and the animals were saved.

Details of the flood
--------------------

### Its source

*See also* [GEN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:4), [GEN 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Gen7:11-Gen7:12), [GEN 8:2](https://ref.ly/Gen8:2)

### Its extent

**[GEN 7:17–7:20](https://ref.ly/Gen7:17-Gen7:20)**

### Its duration

**[GEN 7:24](https://ref.ly/Gen7:24)**

### Its effect

*See also* [GEN 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Gen7:21-Gen7:23)

The flood was God's judgment on sinful humanity
-----------------------------------------------

### Wickedness had become universal

[GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5), [GEN 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/Gen6:11-Gen6:13)

### Judgment would be universal

[GEN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Gen6:7), [GEN 6:13](https://ref.ly/Gen6:13), [GEN 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gen6:17), [GEN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:4)

### Judgment was universal

[GEN 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Gen7:21-Gen7:22)

Those not destroyed by the flood
--------------------------------

### Noah, who was blameless in the midst of wickedness

[GEN 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Gen6:8-Gen6:9)

### Noah and his family kept safe in the ark

**[GEN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Gen7:23)** *See also* [GEN 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gen6:14), [GEN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Gen6:18), [GEN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Gen7:1), [GEN 7:7](https://ref.ly/Gen7:7), [GEN 7:13](https://ref.ly/Gen7:13), [GEN 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Gen8:15-Gen8:16), [GEN 8:18](https://ref.ly/Gen8:18); [2PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:5), [2PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:9)

### God kept the animals safe in the ark

[GEN 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Gen6:19-Gen6:21), [GEN 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Gen7:2-Gen7:3), [GEN 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Gen7:8-Gen7:9), [GEN 7:14–7:16](https://ref.ly/Gen7:14-Gen7:16), [GEN 8:1](https://ref.ly/Gen8:1), [GEN 8:17](https://ref.ly/Gen8:17), [GEN 8:19](https://ref.ly/Gen8:19)

God promised never to send another universal flood
--------------------------------------------------

### God decided this in his heart

**[GEN 8:21–8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:21-Gen8:22)** *See also* [ISA 54:9](https://ref.ly/Isa54:9)

### God established his covenant with Noah

[GEN 9:8–9:11](https://ref.ly/Gen9:8-Gen9:11)

### The rainbow as the sign and reminder of God's covenant

[GEN 9:12–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:12-Gen9:17)

The story of the flood used figuratively
----------------------------------------

### Of the judgment to come

[DAN 9:26](https://ref.ly/Dan9:26); ; [2PE 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:6-2Pet3:7)

### Of Jesus Christ's second coming

[MAT 24:36–24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:36-Matt24:44) parallel [LUK 17:26–17:27](https://ref.ly/Luke17:26-Luke17:27)

### Of baptism

[1PE 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:20-1Pet3:21)

## 7230: genealogies (id: 7230)

In Scripture, lists of the descendants or forebears of an individual, or a record of the names of particular groups of people.

Genealogies as records of ancestors and lines of descent
--------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 1:1–1:17](https://ref.ly/Matt1:1-Matt1:17)** parallel [LUK 3:23–3:38](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23-Luke3:38) *See also* [GEN 10:1–10:32](https://ref.ly/Gen10:1-Gen10:32), [GEN 11:10–11:32](https://ref.ly/Gen11:10-Gen11:32), [GEN 36:10–36:43](https://ref.ly/Gen36:10-Gen36:43); [1CH 1:1–6:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:1-1Chr6:30), [1CH 9:35–9:44](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:35-1Chr9:44); [HEB 7:3](https://ref.ly/Heb7:3)

Kinds of genealogies
--------------------

### Population lists

**[1CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:1)** *See also* [GEN 46:8–46:27](https://ref.ly/Gen46:8-Gen46:27); [1CH 9:3–9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:3-1Chr9:22); [2CH 31:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:16); [EZR 2:1–2:67](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:1-Ezra2:67) parallel [NEH 7:5–7:69](https://ref.ly/Neh7:5-Neh7:69)

### Heads of families

**[1CH 9:34](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:34)** *See also* [1CH 8:1–8:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr8:1-1Chr8:28), [1CH 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:7-1Chr9:9), [1CH 26:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:31)

### Men eligible for military service

**[1CH 7:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:2)** *See also* [NUM 26:1–26:62](https://ref.ly/Num26:1-Num26:62); [1CH 7:4–7:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:4-1Chr7:11), [1CH 7:30–7:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:30-1Chr7:40)

Some prophets may have been responsible for recording or maintaining genealogies
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[2CH 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:15)**

Christians are urged to avoid fruitless discussions concerning genealogies
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[TIT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9)** *See also* [1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4)

## 7233: Israel, as northern kingdom (id: 7233)

The northernmost region of the two kingdoms, comprising the ten northern tribes which broke away from Judah in 930 B.C. and remained independent until the Assyrian conquest of 722 B.C., when the nation was exiled because of its sinfulness.

Origins of the division between Judah and Israel
------------------------------------------------

### Israelite settlement in Canaan had developed around two distinct regions in the north and south

**[JOS 18:5](https://ref.ly/Josh18:5)**

### The northern Israelite tribes were less ready to transfer allegiance from Saul to David

**[2SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:10)** *See also* [2SA 19:41–20:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:41-2Sam20:2) *Israel seems to have been ready to follow Sheba, because he came from the same tribe as Saul.*

### Israel's separation from Judah was the result of disobedience under Solomon

[1KI 11:26–11:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:26-1Kgs11:33)

### Rehoboam's folly provoked Israel's rebellion

[1KI 12:1–12:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:1-1Kgs12:17) parallel [2CH 10:1–10:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:1-2Chr10:17)

Israel's foreign affairs
------------------------

### Israel at war with Judah (c. 930–886 B.C.)

[1KI 14:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:30) parallel [2CH 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:15); ; [2CH 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:2-2Chr13:3), [2CH 16:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:1) parallel [1KI 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:16-1Kgs15:17)

### Israel's wars with Aram (c. 886–753 B.C.)

[1KI 15:18–15:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:18-1Kgs15:20) parallel [2CH 16:2–16:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:2-2Chr16:4); ; [1KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:1), [1KI 20:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:26); ; [2KI 6:24–6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:24-2Kgs6:25), [2KI 10:32–10:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:32-2Kgs10:33), [2KI 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:3), [2KI 13:24–13:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:24-2Kgs13:25), [2KI 14:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:28)

### Israel's alliance with Judah against Aram and Moab (853 B.C.)

[1KI 22:1–22:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:1-1Kgs22:4) parallel [2CH 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:1-2Chr18:3); ; [2KI 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:4-2Kgs3:7)

### Israel attacked by Assyria (c. 743–734 B.C.)

[2KI 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:19-2Kgs15:20), [2KI 15:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:29)

### Israel's alliance with Aram against Judah (c. 735/734 B.C.)

[2KI 16:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:5); ; [ISA 7:1](https://ref.ly/Isa7:1)

### Israel conquered by Assyria, and her inhabitants exiled (722 B.C.)

[2KI 17:3–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:3-2Kgs17:6) parallel [2KI 18:9–18:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:9-2Kgs18:11)

Centres of worship in Israel
----------------------------

### The calf idols in Bethel and Dan

**[1KI 12:26–12:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26-1Kgs12:33)** *See also* [2KI 10:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:29)

### Worship of Baal and Asherah in the capital, Samaria

**[1KI 16:32–16:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:32-1Kgs16:33)**

### Gilgal was also a centre of worship

*See also* [HOS 12:11](https://ref.ly/Hos12:11)

Condemnation of Israel's way of life
------------------------------------

### Israel's worship condemned

**[AMO 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Amos4:4-Amos4:5)** *Amos' language here is full of irony.* *See also* [HOS 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Hos8:11-Hos8:13); [AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24)

### The corruption of Israel's priests condemned

**[HOS 6:9](https://ref.ly/Hos6:9)** *See also* [HOS 4:6–4:9](https://ref.ly/Hos4:6-Hos4:9)

### Israel's unfaithfulness to God condemned

**[HOS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Hos5:7)** *See also* [2KI 17:7–17:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:7-2Kgs17:23); [HOS 4:10–4:13](https://ref.ly/Hos4:10-Hos4:13)

### Prophetic condemnation of sin in Israel

**[HOS 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Hos4:1-Hos4:2)** *See also* [HOS 6:11–7:2](https://ref.ly/Hos6:11-Hos7:2); [AMO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:8), [AMO 5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:12)

### Israel condemned for looking to foreign nations for help instead of to God

**[HOS 11:12–12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos11:12-Hos12:1)** *See also* [HOS 5:13–5:15](https://ref.ly/Hos5:13-Hos5:15), [HOS 7:8–7:11](https://ref.ly/Hos7:8-Hos7:11), [HOS 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Hos8:9-Hos8:10)

God's attitude to the northern kingdom of Israel
------------------------------------------------

### God's call to Israel to repent

**[HOS 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Hos14:1-Hos14:2)** *See also* [HOS 10:12](https://ref.ly/Hos10:12); [AMO 5:4–5:6](https://ref.ly/Amos5:4-Amos5:6)

### God's attempt to call Israel back through hardship

**[1KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:1)** *See also* [AMO 4:6–4:11](https://ref.ly/Amos4:6-Amos4:11)

### God's rejection of Israel

**[HOS 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hos1:6)** *See also* [2CH 25:5–25:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5-2Chr25:7); [HOS 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Hos1:8-Hos1:9)

### God's punishment of Israel

**[AMO 5:26–5:27](https://ref.ly/Amos5:26-Amos5:27)** *See also* [HOS 10:5–10:8](https://ref.ly/Hos10:5-Hos10:8); [AMO 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Amos3:11-Amos3:12)

### God's intention to restore Israel

**[HOS 1:10](https://ref.ly/Hos1:10)** *See also* [HOS 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hos2:23), [HOS 14:4–14:8](https://ref.ly/Hos14:4-Hos14:8); [AMO 9:11–9:15](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11-Amos9:15)

## 7236: Israel, as united kingdom (id: 7236)

The 12 tribes proclaimed Saul as their first king in 1050 B.C., and remained united under David and Solomon, until the rebellion of the ten northern tribes in 930 B.C.

Israel's request for a king
---------------------------

### Israel requested a king because it had been governed by corrupt judges

[1SA 8:1–8:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:1-1Sam8:5)

### Israel requested a king to be like the other nations

[1SA 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:19-1Sam8:20)

### Israel requested a king because of the threat from Ammon

[1SA 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12)

### Israel's request for a king seen as a rejection of God

**[1SA 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:6-1Sam8:7)** *See also* [1SA 10:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:19), [1SA 12:16–12:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:16-1Sam12:19)

----------------------------------

### The popularity of David in Israel

**[1SA 18:30](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:30)** *See also* [1SA 18:6–18:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:9)

### Israel defeated by the Philistines, and the death of Saul

[1SA 31:1–31:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:1-1Sam31:10) parallel [1CH 10:1–10:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:1-1Chr10:10)

### Israel victorious under David

*See also* [2SA 8:1–8:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:1-2Sam8:14) parallel [1CH 18:1–18:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:1-1Chr18:13)

## 7239: Jerusalem (id: 7239)

The city in southern Palestine, situated in the Judean hills, around 20 miles west of the Dead Sea. Established as the Israelite capital by David, it remained the capital of Judah from the reign of Rehoboam until its destruction by the Romans in A.D. 70\.

* **Associated Articles:** 7240: Jerusalem, history of (ID: `7240@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7241: Jerusalem, significance of (ID: `7241@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7240: Jerusalem, history of (id: 7240)

Jerusalem was originally a Jebusite city, occupied by Israel at the time of David. At this stage, it became the capital city of Israel. Following the disintegration of the united kingdom, the city became the capital of the southern kindom of Judah.

Pre\-Israelite Jerusalem
------------------------

### Inhabited by Jebusites

**[JDG 19:9–19:12](https://ref.ly/Judg19:9-Judg19:12)** *See also* [GEN 10:15–10:16](https://ref.ly/Gen10:15-Gen10:16), [GEN 15:18–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:18-Gen15:21); [EXO 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:8), [EXO 23:23](https://ref.ly/Exod23:23), [EXO 34:11](https://ref.ly/Exod34:11); [NUM 13:29](https://ref.ly/Num13:29); [DEU 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:2); [JOS 3:10](https://ref.ly/Josh3:10), [JOS 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Josh9:1-Josh9:2), [JOS 10:1–10:7](https://ref.ly/Josh10:1-Josh10:7), [JOS 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Josh15:7-Josh15:8), [JOS 18:28](https://ref.ly/Josh18:28)

## 7241: Jerusalem, significance of (id: 7241)

Chosen by God as the location for his sanctuary, Jerusalem was his symbolic dwelling and became the only acceptable place for sacrificial worship. It retained its symbolic significance in NT times when it came to represent the hope of restoration and renewal for the people of God.

Jerusalem chosen by God
-----------------------

**[1KI 11:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:32)** *See also* [1KI 8:44–8:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:44-1Kgs8:51) parallel [2CH 6:34–6:39](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:34-2Chr6:39); [1KI 11:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:36), [1KI 14:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:21) parallel [2CH 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:13); [2KI 23:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:27); [NEH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Neh1:8-Neh1:9)

## 7245: Judah, kingdom of (id: 7245)

The southernmost of the two kingdoms into which Israel split after the death of Solomon. It was ruled by a series of Davidic kings until the sixth century B.C., when it became a province of the Babylonian empire.

Origin of the kingdom of Judah
------------------------------

**[1KI 12:1–12:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:1-1Kgs12:24)** parallel [2CH 10:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:1-2Chr11:4)

Loss of the wealth amassed in Judah
-----------------------------------

**[1KI 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:25-1Kgs14:26)** parallel [2CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:9) *See also* [1KI 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:18-1Kgs15:19) parallel [2CH 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:2-2Chr16:3); [2KI 12:17–12:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:17-2Kgs12:18), [2KI 14:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:14) parallel [2CH 25:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:24); [2KI 16:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:8) parallel [2CH 28:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:21); [2KI 18:14–18:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:14-2Kgs18:16); [2CH 24:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:23)

Apostasy in Judah
-----------------

**[1KI 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:22-1Kgs14:24)** *See also* [2KI 8:16–8:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:16-2Kgs8:19) parallel [2CH 21:5–21:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:5-2Chr21:7); [2KI 16:10–16:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10-2Kgs16:16), [2KI 21:2–21:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:2-2Kgs21:16) parallel [2CH 33:2–33:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:2-2Chr33:10); [2CH 28:22–28:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:22-2Chr28:23)

Religious revival in Judah
--------------------------

**[2KI 18:2–18:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:2-2Kgs18:7)** parallels [2CH 29:1–29:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:1-2Chr29:2), [2CH 31:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:1), [2CH 31:20–31:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:20-2Chr31:21) *See also* [2KI 12:1–12:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:1-2Kgs12:16) parallel [2CH 24:1–24:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:1-2Chr24:14); [2KI 23:1–23:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:1-2Kgs23:25); [2CH 34:1–34:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:1-2Chr34:13), [2CH 34:29–34:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:29-2Chr34:33), [2CH 29:1–31:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:1-2Chr31:1), [2CH 35:1–35:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:1-2Chr35:19)

The reign of Athaliah in Judah
------------------------------

**[2KI 11:1–11:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:1-2Kgs11:16)** parallel [2CH 22:10–23:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:10-2Chr23:21)

Coalitions between Judah and Israel
-----------------------------------

**[2KI 8:28–8:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:28-2Kgs8:29)** parallel [2CH 22:5–22:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:5-2Chr22:6) *See also* [1KI 22:1–22:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:1-1Kgs22:5) parallel [2CH 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:1-2Chr18:3); [2KI 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:4-2Kgs3:7)

War in Judah
------------

### With Israel

### With other nations

**[2KI 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:7)** *See also* [2KI 8:20–8:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:20-2Kgs8:22) parallel [2CH 21:8–21:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:8-2Chr21:10); [2KI 18:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:8), [2KI 18:13–19:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:13-2Kgs19:36) parallels [2CH 32:9–32:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:9-2Chr32:19), [ISA 36:1–36:22](https://ref.ly/Isa36:1-Isa36:22); [2CH 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:16-2Chr21:17), [2CH 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:11-2Chr25:12), [2CH 28:17–28:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:17-2Chr28:18)

Judah's loss of independence and change of status from kingdom to province
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7251: pillar of cloud and fire (id: 7251)

A physical manifestation of the presence of God among his people; a special form of theophany particularly associated with Israel being offered guidance on the exodus journey.

Functions of the pillar of cloud and fire
-----------------------------------------

### To lead Israel

**[EXO 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/Exod13:21-Exod13:22)** *See also* [EXO 40:36–40:37](https://ref.ly/Exod40:36-Exod40:37); [NUM 9:17](https://ref.ly/Num9:17), [NUM 14:14](https://ref.ly/Num14:14); [NEH 9:12](https://ref.ly/Neh9:12), [NEH 9:19](https://ref.ly/Neh9:19)

### To separate and protect Israel from the armies of Egypt

**[EXO 14:19–14:20](https://ref.ly/Exod14:19-Exod14:20)** *This was just before the crossing of the Red Sea.*

### To represent God's presence, especially to Moses

**[EXO 33:9](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9)** *See also* [NUM 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Num12:5-Num12:6); [DEU 31:15–31:16](https://ref.ly/Deut31:15-Deut31:16); [PSA 99:7](https://ref.ly/Ps99:7)

Effects of the pillar of cloud and fire
---------------------------------------

### It evoked worship

[EXO 33:10](https://ref.ly/Exod33:10)

### It caused the nations to speak of God

[NUM 14:14](https://ref.ly/Num14:14)

### Experiencing the cloud did not guarantee acceptance by God

[1CO 10:1–10:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:1-1Cor10:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 13:21-22; Exodus 40:36-37; Numbers 9:17; Numbers 14:14; Nehemiah 9:12; Nehemiah 9:19; Exodus 14:19-20; Exodus 33:9; Numbers 12:5-6; Deuteronomy 31:15-16; Psalms 99:7; Exodus 33:10; 1 Corinthians 10:1-5
* **Associated Articles:** 1454: theophany (ID: `1454@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7222: exodus, events of (ID: `7222@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7474: Tent of Meeting (ID: `7474@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8124: guidance (ID: `8124@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7254: plumb-line (id: 7254)

A cord weighted with lead that is used in building to check that vertical structures are true. It is used symbolically to refer to the divine standard against which God, the builder of his people, tests and judges them. It also symbolises the standards by which God will rebuild his people.

The plumb\-line as the measuring tool of a structure
----------------------------------------------------

**[ZEC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Zech1:16), [ZEC 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Zech4:9-Zech4:10)** *Using the plumb\-line Zerubbabel will carry out his God\-given task of building the temple.* *See also* [JER 31:38–31:40](https://ref.ly/Jer31:38-Jer31:40); [ZEC 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Zech2:1-Zech2:2)

God's plumb\-line reveals the nation's failure
----------------------------------------------

### Although built true, Israel has become corrupt

**[AMO 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Amos7:7-Amos7:8)** *See also* [ISA 1:21–1:24](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21-Isa1:24), [ISA 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Isa5:1-Isa5:2), [ISA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:7), [ISA 59:14](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14)

### God's testing brings judgment

**[2KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:13)** *See also* [ISA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Isa34:11); [LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8)

God will rebuild his people
---------------------------

### God's promise to rebuild Israel

**[JER 31:4](https://ref.ly/Jer31:4)** *See also* [PSA 69:35–69:36](https://ref.ly/Ps69:35-Ps69:36), [PSA 147:2](https://ref.ly/Ps147:2); [ISA 54:11–54:12](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11-Isa54:12)

### They will be rebuilt true to God's plumb\-line

**[ISA 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16-Isa28:17)** *See also* [PSA 51:18–51:19](https://ref.ly/Ps51:18-Ps51:19); [ISA 1:25–1:26](https://ref.ly/Isa1:25-Isa1:26); [PSA 89:14](https://ref.ly/Ps89:14), [PSA 97:2](https://ref.ly/Ps97:2), [PSA 99:4](https://ref.ly/Ps99:4); [ISA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Isa16:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Zechariah 1:16; Zechariah 4:9-10; Jeremiah 31:38-40; Zechariah 2:1-2; Amos 7:7-8; Isaiah 1:21-24; Isaiah 5:1-2; Isaiah 5:7; Isaiah 59:14; 2 Kings 21:13; Isaiah 34:11; Lamentations 2:8; Jeremiah 31:4; Psalms 69:35-36; Psalms 147:2; Isaiah 54:11-12; Isaiah 28:16-17; Psalms 51:18-19; Isaiah 1:25-26; Psalms 89:14; Psalms 97:2; Psalms 99:4; Isaiah 16:5
