## 7257: promised land, the (id: 7257)

The territory of the Canaanite nations given by God to Abraham and his descendants as part of his covenant with them.

* **Associated Articles:** 7258: promised land, early history of (ID: `7258@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7259: promised land, later history of (ID: `7259@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7258: promised land, early history of (id: 7258)

The promise of a homeland, made to Abraham, began to unfold through Moses and Joshua. Moses taught that the land was to be held in trust by God's people, obedience to God's ways bringing blessing in the land and disobedience bringing his judgment.

The location of the promised land
---------------------------------

**[GEN 15:18–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:18-Gen15:21)** *See also* [NUM 34:1–34:12](https://ref.ly/Num34:1-Num34:12); [DEU 7:1](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1)

Traditional descriptions of the promised land
---------------------------------------------

**[EXO 3:17](https://ref.ly/Exod3:17)** *See also* [NUM 13:26–13:27](https://ref.ly/Num13:26-Num13:27); [DEU 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:8); [JER 32:22](https://ref.ly/Jer32:22); [EZK 20:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:6)

The promised land and Abraham
-----------------------------

### The covenant with Abraham

**[GEN 15:7–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:7-Gen15:21)** *See also* [GEN 13:14–13:17](https://ref.ly/Gen13:14-Gen13:17), [GEN 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:7-Gen17:8)

### The promised land given to Abraham's descendants

**[GEN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Gen12:7)** *See also* [GEN 17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:8), [GEN 24:7](https://ref.ly/Gen24:7); [EXO 33:1](https://ref.ly/Exod33:1)

### A burial plot was Abraham's only earthly possession in the promised land

**[GEN 25:9–25:10](https://ref.ly/Gen25:9-Gen25:10)** *See also* [GEN 23:3–23:20](https://ref.ly/Gen23:3-Gen23:20)

The promised land and the patriarchs
------------------------------------

**[GEN 26:2–26:5](https://ref.ly/Gen26:2-Gen26:5)** *See also* [GEN 28:12–28:15](https://ref.ly/Gen28:12-Gen28:15), [GEN 35:11–35:12](https://ref.ly/Gen35:11-Gen35:12), [GEN 50:24–50:25](https://ref.ly/Gen50:24-Gen50:25)

Later generations look back to the promise made to the patriarchs
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 1:8](https://ref.ly/Deut1:8)** *See also* [EXO 32:13–32:14](https://ref.ly/Exod32:13-Exod32:14); [DEU 6:20–6:23](https://ref.ly/Deut6:20-Deut6:23); [NEH 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Neh9:7-Neh9:8); [PSA 105:8–105:11](https://ref.ly/Ps105:8-Ps105:11) parallel [1CH 16:15–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:15-1Chr16:18)

The promised land and Moses
---------------------------

### Moses is called to lead Israel into the promised land

**[DEU 10:11](https://ref.ly/Deut10:11)** *See also* [EXO 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Exod3:16-Exod3:17), [EXO 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:1-Exod6:8); [NUM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:12)

### A whole generation is not permitted to enter the promised land

**[JOS 5:6](https://ref.ly/Josh5:6)** *See also* [NUM 14:20–14:38](https://ref.ly/Num14:20-Num14:38), [NUM 32:9–32:13](https://ref.ly/Num32:9-Num32:13); [PSA 106:24–106:27](https://ref.ly/Ps106:24-Ps106:27)

### Moses and Aaron are not permitted to enter the promised land

**[NUM 20:12](https://ref.ly/Num20:12)** *See also* [NUM 20:23–20:29](https://ref.ly/Num20:23-Num20:29), [NUM 27:12–27:14](https://ref.ly/Num27:12-Num27:14); [DEU 32:48–32:52](https://ref.ly/Deut32:48-Deut32:52), [DEU 34:1–34:4](https://ref.ly/Deut34:1-Deut34:4)

### Moses' instructions to destroy the inhabitants of the land and their practices

**[DEU 7:16](https://ref.ly/Deut7:16)** *See also* [NUM 33:50–33:53](https://ref.ly/Num33:50-Num33:53); [DEU 7:1–7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:6), [DEU 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2-Deut12:3), [DEU 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:9-Deut18:12)

### The instructions for distributing the promised land

**[NUM 33:54](https://ref.ly/Num33:54)** *See also* [NUM 34:13–34:15](https://ref.ly/Num34:13-Num34:15), [NUM 36:5–36:9](https://ref.ly/Num36:5-Num36:9); [JOS 14:1–14:5](https://ref.ly/Josh14:1-Josh14:5); [PSA 78:55](https://ref.ly/Ps78:55)

### Obedience to the law was a condition for enjoying the blessings of the promised land

**[DEU 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Deut6:1-Deut6:3)** *See also* [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12) parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16); [LEV 26:3–26:12](https://ref.ly/Lev26:3-Lev26:12); [DEU 6:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/Deut6:17-Deut6:19), [DEU 7:12–7:15](https://ref.ly/Deut7:12-Deut7:15), [DEU 11:8–11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:8-Deut11:15), [DEU 28:1–28:8](https://ref.ly/Deut28:1-Deut28:8); [JER 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Jer11:1-Jer11:5)

### Warnings not to forget God and follow Canaanite ways

**[DEU 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Deut11:16-Deut11:17)** *See also* [EXO 34:12–34:16](https://ref.ly/Exod34:12-Exod34:16); [LEV 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/Lev18:1-Lev18:5); [NUM 33:55–33:56](https://ref.ly/Num33:55-Num33:56); [DEU 8:10–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10-Deut8:14), [DEU 28:15–28:19](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:19); [JOS 23:14–23:16](https://ref.ly/Josh23:14-Josh23:16)

### Laws concerning the land

**[LEV 25:23–25:24](https://ref.ly/Lev25:23-Lev25:24)** *The promised land is seen as belonging to God.* *See also* [EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11); [LEV 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/Lev25:1-Lev25:7), [LEV 25:8–25:13](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:13); [NUM 35:33–35:34](https://ref.ly/Num35:33-Num35:34)

The promised land and Joshua
----------------------------

### Joshua is called to lead the Israelites into the promised land

**[DEU 31:7–31:8](https://ref.ly/Deut31:7-Deut31:8)** *See also* [DEU 3:28](https://ref.ly/Deut3:28); [JOS 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Josh1:1-Josh1:6)

### The Israelites enter the promised land and begin to possess it

[JOS 3:14–3:17](https://ref.ly/Josh3:14-Josh3:17), [JOS 11:16–11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:16-Josh11:23), [JOS 21:43](https://ref.ly/Josh21:43)

### God's people are given rest

**[JOS 21:44–21:45](https://ref.ly/Josh21:44-Josh21:45)** *See also* [DEU 12:10](https://ref.ly/Deut12:10); [JOS 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Josh1:13-Josh1:15)

## 7259: promised land, later history of (id: 7259)

Established firmly in the land under David and Solomon, God's people ultimately began to neglect the terms of the covenant and were warned by the prophets of the consequent judgment and exile.

Israel's failure to take full possession of the promised land under the judges
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JDG 1:19](https://ref.ly/Judg1:19)** *See also* [JDG 1:27–1:35](https://ref.ly/Judg1:27-Judg1:35), [JDG 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Judg2:1-Judg2:3), [JDG 18:1](https://ref.ly/Judg18:1)

Israel was finally established in the promised land under David and Solomon
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CH 22:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:18)** *See also* [2SA 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:10-2Sam7:11) parallel [1CH 17:9–17:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:9-1Chr17:10); [1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21), [1KI 4:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:25)

The promised land and the prophets
----------------------------------

### The prophetic condemnation of the life of God's people in the promised land

**[JER 2:7](https://ref.ly/Jer2:7)** *See also* [JER 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Jer3:1-Jer3:3); [EZK 22:23–22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:23-Ezek22:29); [HOS 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:1-Hos4:3)

### Prophetic warning of coming judgment on the land and exile from it

**[ISA 13:9](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9)** *See also* [ISA 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/Isa8:6-Isa8:8); [JER 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Jer10:17-Jer10:18), [JER 16:10–16:13](https://ref.ly/Jer16:10-Jer16:13); [EZK 7:2–7:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:2-Ezek7:4); [AMO 7:17](https://ref.ly/Amos7:17)

### Prophetic assurance of ultimate deliverance and restoration to the promised land

**[JER 30:3](https://ref.ly/Jer30:3)** *See also* [ISA 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Isa14:1-Isa14:2), [ISA 60:15–60:22](https://ref.ly/Isa60:15-Isa60:22); [JER 24:5–24:7](https://ref.ly/Jer24:5-Jer24:7); [EZK 11:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:17), [EZK 37:11–37:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:11-Ezek37:14); [AMO 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Amos9:14-Amos9:15); [ZEC 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/Zech2:10-Zech2:12)

The desolation of the promised land
-----------------------------------

### By Antiochus Epiphanes

**[DAN 8:9–8:14](https://ref.ly/Dan8:9-Dan8:14)** *The growing horn is Antiochus Epiphanes who tried to wipe out the Jewish faith in 168 B.C., forbidding Jewish practices and defiling the temple with pagan sacrifices and prostitution.* *See also* [DAN 11:29–11:35](https://ref.ly/Dan11:29-Dan11:35) *The help that would be received by God's people probably refers to the Maccabean revolt and the cleansing of the temple in 165 B.C., although by NT times the land would be occupied by Rome.*

### By Rome

**[LUK 21:20–21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20-Luke21:24)** parallels [MAT 24:15–24:21](https://ref.ly/Matt24:15-Matt24:21), [MRK 13:14–13:19](https://ref.ly/Mark13:14-Mark13:19) *Jesus Christ prophesies the desolation of the nation by Rome in A.D. 70\.*

The promised land in the NT
---------------------------

### Referred to by Stephen

*See also* [ACT 7:3–7:7](https://ref.ly/Acts7:3-Acts7:7), [ACT 7:45–7:47](https://ref.ly/Acts7:45-Acts7:47)

### Referred to by Paul

[ACT 13:16–13:19](https://ref.ly/Acts13:16-Acts13:19)

### Referred to by Hebrews

[HEB 11:8–11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:10) *Abraham was looking for a more permanent city than the promised land could ever provide*; ; [HEB 11:13–11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13-Heb11:16)

### The concept of spiritual rest

**[HEB 3:18–4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb3:18-Heb4:11)**

### The church looks beyond the promised land

**[ACT 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:6-Acts1:8)** *The apostles' concern was for the deliverance of the promised land from Roman domination, but Jesus Christ shows God's concern to be far wider, to all nations.* *See also* [REV 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9-Rev5:10), [REV 7:9–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9-Rev7:17), [REV 21:9–21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:9-Rev21:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 1:19; Judges 1:27-35; Judges 2:1-3; Judges 18:1; 1 Chronicles 22:18; 2 Samuel 7:10-11; 1 Chronicles 17:9-10; 1 Kings 4:21; 1 Kings 4:25; Jeremiah 2:7; Jeremiah 3:1-3; Ezekiel 22:23-29; Hosea 4:1-3; Isaiah 13:9; Isaiah 8:6-8; Jeremiah 10:17-18; Jeremiah 16:10-13; Ezekiel 7:2-4; Amos 7:17; Jeremiah 30:3; Isaiah 14:1-2; Isaiah 60:15-22; Jeremiah 24:5-7; Ezekiel 11:17; Ezekiel 37:11-14; Amos 9:14-15; Zechariah 2:10-12; Daniel 8:9-14; Daniel 11:29-35; Luke 21:20-24; Matthew 24:15-21; Mark 13:14-19; Acts 7:3-7; Acts 7:45-47; Acts 13:16-19; Hebrews 11:8-10; Hebrews 11:13-16; Hebrews 3:18-4:11; Acts 1:6-8; Revelation 5:9-10; Revelation 7:9-17; Revelation 21:9-27
* **Associated Articles:** 5056: rest (ID: `5056@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5085: David (ID: `5085@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5118: Solomon (ID: `5118@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7145: remnant (ID: `7145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7215: exile, the (ID: `7215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7233: Israel, as northern kingdom (ID: `7233@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7236: Israel, as united kingdom (ID: `7236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7239: Jerusalem (ID: `7239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7245: Judah, kingdom of (ID: `7245@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7269: Zion (ID: `7269@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7505: Jews, the (ID: `7505@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7772: prophets (ID: `7772@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7263: theocracy (id: 7263)

The form of government among the early Israelites in which God was their supreme ruler, and his laws their laws.

The covenant at Sinai established the theocracy
-----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5-Exod19:6)** *See also* [EXO 19:8](https://ref.ly/Exod19:8), [EXO 24:3](https://ref.ly/Exod24:3), [EXO 24:7–24:8](https://ref.ly/Exod24:7-Exod24:8); [DEU 5:27](https://ref.ly/Deut5:27)

Moses and Joshua renewed the theocratic covenant
------------------------------------------------

[DEU 26:16–26:19](https://ref.ly/Deut26:16-Deut26:19), [DEU 29:9–29:15](https://ref.ly/Deut29:9-Deut29:15); ; [JOS 24:24–24:25](https://ref.ly/Josh24:24-Josh24:25)

Under the theocracy God was the Israelites' supreme ruler
---------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 33:5](https://ref.ly/Deut33:5)** *“Jeshurun” means “the upright one”, i.e., Israel.* *See also* [NUM 23:21](https://ref.ly/Num23:21); [DEU 33:26](https://ref.ly/Deut33:26); [JDG 8:23](https://ref.ly/Judg8:23); [PSA 145:1](https://ref.ly/Ps145:1)

In the theocracy Israel was governed by God's laws
--------------------------------------------------

[EXO 20:1–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:1-Exod20:17) parallel [DEU 5:6–5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:21); ; [EXO 21:1](https://ref.ly/Exod21:1); ; [DEU 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Deut4:1-Deut4:2), [DEU 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Deut4:13-Deut4:14), [DEU 17:18–17:19](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18-Deut17:19), [DEU 31:12–31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12-Deut31:13); ; [JOS 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:7-Josh1:8)

Israel rejected the theocracy
-----------------------------

**[1SA 8:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:7)** *See also* [1SA 8:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:19), [1SA 10:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:19), [1SA 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 19:5-6; Exodus 19:8; Exodus 24:3; Exodus 24:7-8; Deuteronomy 5:27; Deuteronomy 26:16-19; Deuteronomy 29:9-15; Joshua 24:24-25; Deuteronomy 33:5; Numbers 23:21; Deuteronomy 33:26; Judges 8:23; Psalms 145:1; Exodus 20:1-17; Deuteronomy 5:6-21; Exodus 21:1; Deuteronomy 4:1-2; Deuteronomy 4:13-14; Deuteronomy 17:18-19; Deuteronomy 31:12-13; Joshua 1:7-8; 1 Samuel 8:7; 1 Samuel 8:19; 1 Samuel 10:19; 1 Samuel 12:12
* **Associated Articles:** 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1349: covenant, God's at Sinai (ID: `1349@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5326: government (ID: `5326@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5358: judges (ID: `5358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5368: kingship (ID: `5368@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7266: tribes of Israel (id: 7266)

The divisions of Israel on lines of kinship, according to descent from the twelve sons of Jacob. These divisions were most pronounced in the period before Saul became king of Israel but were maintained throughout Israel's history.

Origin of the tribes of Israel
------------------------------

### Twelve sons of Jacob

**[GEN 35:22–35:26](https://ref.ly/Gen35:22-Gen35:26)** *See also* [GEN 29:31–29:35](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31-Gen29:35), [GEN 30:3–30:13](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3-Gen30:13), [GEN 30:17–30:24](https://ref.ly/Gen30:17-Gen30:24), [GEN 35:16–35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:16-Gen35:18)

### Increased in number while living in Egypt

**[GEN 46:5–46:27](https://ref.ly/Gen46:5-Gen46:27)** *See also* [EXO 1:1–1:7](https://ref.ly/Exod1:1-Exod1:7)

Blessings on the tribes of Israel
---------------------------------

[GEN 49:1–49:28](https://ref.ly/Gen49:1-Gen49:28); ; [DEU 33:1–33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:1-Deut33:29)

Association of the twelve apostles with the tribes of Israel
------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28)** *See also* [MAT 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1-Matt10:4) parallels [MRK 3:13–3:19](https://ref.ly/Mark3:13-Mark3:19), [LUK 6:12–6:16](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12-Luke6:16); [LUK 22:28–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:28-Luke22:30); [ACT 1:12–1:26](https://ref.ly/Acts1:12-Acts1:26); [REV 21:12–21:14](https://ref.ly/Rev21:12-Rev21:14)

Other NT references to the tribes of Israel
-------------------------------------------

**[JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1)** *See also* [ACT 26:6–26:7](https://ref.ly/Acts26:6-Acts26:7)

The tribes of Israel in end\-time prophecy
------------------------------------------

**[REV 7:1–7:8](https://ref.ly/Rev7:1-Rev7:8)** *See also* [EZK 47:13–47:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:13-Ezek47:14), [EZK 48:1–48:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek48:1-Ezek48:35); [REV 21:9–21:14](https://ref.ly/Rev21:9-Rev21:14)

In the wilderness: [NUM 1:40–1:41](https://ref.ly/Num1:40-Num1:41), [NUM 10:25–10:28](https://ref.ly/Num10:25-Num10:28); [DEU 27:12–27:13](https://ref.ly/Deut27:12-Deut27:13); ; [JOS 19:24–19:31](https://ref.ly/Josh19:24-Josh19:31); ; [JDG 7:22–7:23](https://ref.ly/Judg7:22-Judg7:23); [2CH 30:6–30:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:6-2Chr30:11); ; [LUK 2:36–2:38](https://ref.ly/Luke2:36-Luke2:38)

The tribe of Benjamin
---------------------

**[GEN 46:21](https://ref.ly/Gen46:21)** *See also* [NUM 26:38–26:41](https://ref.ly/Num26:38-Num26:41); [1CH 7:6–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:6-1Chr7:12), [1CH 8:1–8:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr8:1-1Chr8:40), [1CH 9:1–9:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:1-1Chr9:9)

### The history of Benjamin

In the wilderness: [NUM 1:36–1:37](https://ref.ly/Num1:36-Num1:37), [NUM 2:18–2:23](https://ref.ly/Num2:18-Num2:23); [DEU 27:12](https://ref.ly/Deut27:12); ; [JOS 18:11–18:28](https://ref.ly/Josh18:11-Josh18:28); ; [1CH 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:1-1Chr12:2); [JDG 19:1–21:23](https://ref.ly/Judg19:1-Judg21:23) *the war against Benjamin*; [1KI 12:21–12:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:21-1Kgs12:24) parallel [2CH 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:1-2Chr11:4); ; [2CH 11:5–11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:5-2Chr11:12); ; [EZR 1:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:5)

----------------

### The genealogy of Dan

**[NUM 26:42–26:43](https://ref.ly/Num26:42-Num26:43)**

In the wilderness: [NUM 1:38–1:39](https://ref.ly/Num1:38-Num1:39), [NUM 2:25](https://ref.ly/Num2:25), [NUM 10:25](https://ref.ly/Num10:25); [DEU 27:12–27:13](https://ref.ly/Deut27:12-Deut27:13); ; [JOS 19:40–19:47](https://ref.ly/Josh19:40-Josh19:47); ; [JDG 18:1–18:31](https://ref.ly/Judg18:1-Judg18:31)

The tribe of Ephraim
--------------------

### The tribe of Ephraim was descended from Joseph's younger son

**[JOS 14:4](https://ref.ly/Josh14:4)** *See also* [GEN 48:1–48:20](https://ref.ly/Gen48:1-Gen48:20)

### The genealogy of Ephraim

**[NUM 26:35–26:37](https://ref.ly/Num26:35-Num26:37)** *See also* [1CH 7:20–7:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:20-1Chr7:29)

### The history of Ephraim

In the wilderness: [NUM 1:32–1:33](https://ref.ly/Num1:32-Num1:33), [NUM 2:18](https://ref.ly/Num2:18); [JOS 16:1–16:10](https://ref.ly/Josh16:1-Josh16:10), [JOS 17:14–17:18](https://ref.ly/Josh17:14-Josh17:18); ; [JDG 1:29](https://ref.ly/Judg1:29), [JDG 7:22–8:3](https://ref.ly/Judg7:22-Judg8:3); [JDG 12:1–12:6](https://ref.ly/Judg12:1-Judg12:6) *the war against Ephraim*

The tribe of Gad
----------------

### The genealogy of Gad

**[GEN 46:16](https://ref.ly/Gen46:16)** *See also* [NUM 26:15–26:18](https://ref.ly/Num26:15-Num26:18)

### The history of Gad

In the wilderness: [NUM 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Num1:24-Num1:25), [NUM 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Num2:10-Num2:14); [NUM 32:28–32:36](https://ref.ly/Num32:28-Num32:36); ; [DEU 3:12–3:17](https://ref.ly/Deut3:12-Deut3:17), [DEU 29:7–29:8](https://ref.ly/Deut29:7-Deut29:8); ; [JOS 13:24–13:28](https://ref.ly/Josh13:24-Josh13:28); [JOS 1:12–1:18](https://ref.ly/Josh1:12-Josh1:18), [JOS 4:10–4:13](https://ref.ly/Josh4:10-Josh4:13), [JOS 22:1–22:34](https://ref.ly/Josh22:1-Josh22:34); ; [1CH 5:18–5:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:18-1Chr5:22); [1CH 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:25-1Chr5:26), [1CH 12:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:37)

The tribe of Issachar
---------------------

### The genealogy of Issachar

**[GEN 46:13](https://ref.ly/Gen46:13)** *See also* [NUM 26:23–26:25](https://ref.ly/Num26:23-Num26:25); [1CH 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:1-1Chr7:5)

### The history of Issachar

In the wilderness: [NUM 1:28–1:29](https://ref.ly/Num1:28-Num1:29), [NUM 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/Num2:3-Num2:6); [DEU 27:12](https://ref.ly/Deut27:12); ; [JOS 19:17–19:23](https://ref.ly/Josh19:17-Josh19:23); ; [JDG 10:1](https://ref.ly/Judg10:1); [1CH 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:38-1Chr12:40); ; [2CH 30:10–30:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:10-2Chr30:20)

The tribe of Judah
------------------

### The genealogy of Judah

**[NUM 26:19–26:22](https://ref.ly/Num26:19-Num26:22)** *See also* [GEN 46:8–46:12](https://ref.ly/Gen46:8-Gen46:12); [1CH 2:1–4:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:1-1Chr4:23)

### The tribe of Judah in the wilderness

**[NUM 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Num1:26-Num1:27)** *See also* [NUM 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/Num2:3-Num2:4), [NUM 13:1–13:6](https://ref.ly/Num13:1-Num13:6), [NUM 34:16–34:19](https://ref.ly/Num34:16-Num34:19); [DEU 27:11–27:12](https://ref.ly/Deut27:11-Deut27:12), [DEU 33:7](https://ref.ly/Deut33:7)

### Judah was prominent among the tribes

**[GEN 49:8–49:12](https://ref.ly/Gen49:8-Gen49:12)** *See also* [NUM 2:1–2:9](https://ref.ly/Num2:1-Num2:9), [NUM 7:10–7:17](https://ref.ly/Num7:10-Num7:17), [NUM 10:11–10:14](https://ref.ly/Num10:11-Num10:14); [JDG 1:1–1:20](https://ref.ly/Judg1:1-Judg1:20)

### The territory of the tribe of Judah

**[JOS 18:5](https://ref.ly/Josh18:5)** *See also* [JOS 11:21](https://ref.ly/Josh11:21), [JOS 15:1–15:63](https://ref.ly/Josh15:1-Josh15:63) *the extent of the allotment for Judah*; [JOS 20:1–20:7](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:7) *cities of refuge in Judah*[JOS 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Josh21:1-Josh21:4), [JOS 21:9–21:16](https://ref.ly/Josh21:9-Josh21:16)[1SA 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:1), [1SA 22:3–22:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:3-1Sam22:5), [1SA 23:21–23:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:21-1Sam23:23), [1SA 27:8–27:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:8-1Sam27:10), [1SA 30:11–30:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:11-1Sam30:16); [2SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:1), [2SA 24:5–24:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:5-2Sam24:7)

### Early evidence of the distinction between Judah and the other tribes

**[2SA 24:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:9)** parallel [1CH 21:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:5) *See also* [1SA 11:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:8), [1SA 17:52](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:52), [1SA 18:162](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:162)SA [2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8-1Sam2:11), [2SA 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:9-2Sam3:10), [2SA 5:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:5)[2SA 11:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:11), [2SA 12:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:8), [2SA 19:40–20:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:40-2Sam20:2) *Judah remained loyal to David when Israel rebelled*; [2SA 21:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:2), [2SA 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:11)KI [1:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:35), [1KI 4:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:20), [1KI 4:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:25)

### The separation of Judah from the northern tribes

**[1KI 12:1–12:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:1-1Kgs12:24)** parallel [2CH 10:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:1-2Chr11:4)

### The continuing tribal identity of Judah

**[2CH 19:8–19:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:8-2Chr19:11)** *See also* [2KI 17:18–17:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:18-2Kgs17:20); [PSA 78:67–78:68](https://ref.ly/Ps78:67-Ps78:68); [EZK 37:15–37:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:15-Ezek37:28), [EZK 48:1–48:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek48:1-Ezek48:8), [EZK 48:30–48:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek48:30-Ezek48:31); [REV 7:1–7:8](https://ref.ly/Rev7:1-Rev7:8)

The tribe of Levi
-----------------

### The genealogy of Levi

[EXO 6:16–6:25](https://ref.ly/Exod6:16-Exod6:25); ; [NUM 3:17–3:20](https://ref.ly/Num3:17-Num3:20), [NUM 26:57–26:62](https://ref.ly/Num26:57-Num26:62)

### The tribe of Levi is set apart to God

[EXO 32:25–32:29](https://ref.ly/Exod32:25-Exod32:29) *Their loyalty to the LORD set them apart.*; [NUM 1:47–1:53](https://ref.ly/Num1:47-Num1:53), [NUM 3:38](https://ref.ly/Num3:38)

### They were claimed by God as a substitute for the firstborn males of Israel

**[NUM 3:11–3:13](https://ref.ly/Num3:11-Num3:13)** *See also* [NUM 3:40–3:45](https://ref.ly/Num3:40-Num3:45), [NUM 8:15–8:18](https://ref.ly/Num8:15-Num8:18)

### The tribe of Levi received no allotment of land

**[DEU 18:1–18:2](https://ref.ly/Deut18:1-Deut18:2)** *See also* [NUM 18:23](https://ref.ly/Num18:23); [JOS 13:32–13:33](https://ref.ly/Josh13:32-Josh13:33), [JOS 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Josh14:3-Josh14:4)

### The tribe of Levi lived in designated towns

**[NUM 35:1–35:8](https://ref.ly/Num35:1-Num35:8)** *See also* [LEV 25:32–25:34](https://ref.ly/Lev25:32-Lev25:34); [JOS 21:1–21:42](https://ref.ly/Josh21:1-Josh21:42) parallel [1CH 6:54–6:80](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:54-1Chr6:80)

### Tithes and offerings given by the rest of Israel supported the tribe of Levi

**[NUM 18:21–18:24](https://ref.ly/Num18:21-Num18:24)** *See also* [DEU 14:22–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:22-Deut14:29); [NEH 10:37–10:38](https://ref.ly/Neh10:37-Neh10:38), [NEH 13:4–13:13](https://ref.ly/Neh13:4-Neh13:13)

### Moses blesses the tribe of Levi

[DEU 33:8–33:11](https://ref.ly/Deut33:8-Deut33:11)

### The tribe of Levi in the period of the monarchy

[1CH 12:26–12:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:26-1Chr12:28) *Their fighting men join David at Hebron*; ; [1CH 23:2–23:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:2-1Chr23:24) *David divides them into three groups and organises their responsibilities*; ; [1CH 27:16–27:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:16-1Chr27:17) *David sets an army officer over them.*

The tribe of Manasseh
---------------------

### The tribe of Manasseh was descended from Joseph's older son

**[JOS 14:4](https://ref.ly/Josh14:4)** *See also* [GEN 48:3–48:20](https://ref.ly/Gen48:3-Gen48:20); [NUM 26:28–26:37](https://ref.ly/Num26:28-Num26:37); [1CH 7:14–7:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:14-1Chr7:19)

### Manasseh in the wilderness

## 7269: Zion (id: 7269)

The geographical and spiritual centre of life among God's people.

* **Associated Articles:** 7270: Zion, as a place (ID: `7270@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7271: Zion, as a symbol (ID: `7271@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7270: Zion, as a place (id: 7270)

Originally the name of the southernmost hill on which the Canaanite fortress\-city of Jebus was located. It was conquered by David around 1000 B.C. and renamed Jerusalem. Situated on the borders of Judah and Israel, it became David's capital. As the city expanded, the name Zion came to be applied to the whole city.

----------------

**[1CH 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:4-1Chr11:5)** parallel [2SA 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:7)

## 7271: Zion, as a symbol (id: 7271)

Central to the life of the nation of Israel, it became the symbol of the whole nation and was seen, especially by the prophets, as the focus of God's promises and his final victory. In the NT Zion is a symbol of the new Israel, the church.

Zion as the dwelling\-place of God
----------------------------------

**[PSA 135:21](https://ref.ly/Ps135:21)** *See also* [PSA 76:2](https://ref.ly/Ps76:2), [PSA 132:13–132:14](https://ref.ly/Ps132:13-Ps132:14); [JOL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Joel3:17)

## 7302: altar (id: 7302)

A construction, usually of wood, stone or metal, for the offering of sacrifice. The conflict between true and false religion often focused on altars, which were of key significance in biblical times.

Early altars built to the LORD
------------------------------

**[GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20)** *These early altars were erected at sites where God's presence or power had been experienced.* [GEN 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:7-Gen12:8), [GEN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Gen13:18), [GEN 22:9](https://ref.ly/Gen22:9); [GEN 26:25](https://ref.ly/Gen26:25) *by Isaac*; [GEN 33:20](https://ref.ly/Gen33:20), [GEN 35:7](https://ref.ly/Gen35:7); [EXO 17:15](https://ref.ly/Exod17:15), [EXO 24:4](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4); [JOS 8:30](https://ref.ly/Josh8:30) *by Joshua*; ; [JDG 6:24](https://ref.ly/Judg6:24) *by Gideon*; ; [JDG 21:4](https://ref.ly/Judg21:4) *by unnamed Israelites*; ; [1SA 7:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:17) *by Samuel*; ; [1SA 14:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:35) *by Saul*; ; [2SA 24:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:25) parallel [1CH 21:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:26) *by David*

Solomon's temple altar
----------------------

It was preceded by the tabernacle altar of burnt offering: [EXO 27:1–27:8](https://ref.ly/Exod27:1-Exod27:8), [EXO 30:1–30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:1-Exod30:10), [EXO 37:25–37:28](https://ref.ly/Exod37:25-Exod37:28), [EXO 38:1–38:7](https://ref.ly/Exod38:1-Exod38:7); ; [HEB 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:3-Heb9:4); [1KI 8:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:22), [1KI 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:20)

OT pagan altars
---------------

### A symptom of decline in the northern kingdom

[1KI 16:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:32); ; [2KI 23:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:15); ; [HOS 8:11](https://ref.ly/Hos8:11), [HOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Hos10:1)

### A symptom of decline in the southern kingdom

[2KI 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10) *ignoring the command in [Dt 12:2–3](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2-Deut12:3)*; ; [2KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3); ; [ISA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Isa17:8); ; [JER 11:13](https://ref.ly/Jer11:13), [JER 19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:13); ; [ZEP 1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:5)

### Josiah's reform targeted illicit altars

**[2KI 23:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:12)** *See also* [DEU 7:5](https://ref.ly/Deut7:5), [DEU 12:8–12:14](https://ref.ly/Deut12:8-Deut12:14) *Josiah was implementing the law requiring sacrifice to be offered only on the altar at the sanctuary of God's choice*; [2KI 23:15–23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:15-2Kgs23:20)

The altar in the second temple
------------------------------

**[EZR 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:2-Ezra3:3)** *See also* [EZR 7:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:17)

Laws concerning the altar of burnt offering
-------------------------------------------

### Its construction

**[EXO 20:24–20:26](https://ref.ly/Exod20:24-Exod20:26)** *An earlier more basic form than in the tabernacle. No human hand was to shape the stones.* *See also* [EXO 27:1–27:8](https://ref.ly/Exod27:1-Exod27:8), [EXO 38:1–38:7](https://ref.ly/Exod38:1-Exod38:7)

### Its use for the daily sacrifice

**[EXO 29:38–29:39](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:39)** *See also* [EXO 30:28](https://ref.ly/Exod30:28); [NUM 4:13](https://ref.ly/Num4:13), [NUM 7:10](https://ref.ly/Num7:10), [NUM 7:84](https://ref.ly/Num7:84)

### Its use for asylum

**[1KI 1:50–1:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:50-1Kgs1:51)** *See also* [EXO 21:14](https://ref.ly/Exod21:14); [1KI 2:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:28); [AMO 3:14](https://ref.ly/Amos3:14)

Laws concerning the incense altar
---------------------------------

### Its construction

**[EXO 30:1–30:5](https://ref.ly/Exod30:1-Exod30:5)** *See also* [EXO 37:25–37:28](https://ref.ly/Exod37:25-Exod37:28)

### Its use

**[EXO 30:7](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7)** *See also* [EXO 30:8–30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:8-Exod30:10); [NUM 4:11](https://ref.ly/Num4:11)

Altars in the NT
----------------

### The Jerusalem temple altar

**[MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24)** *See also* [MAT 23:18–23:20](https://ref.ly/Matt23:18-Matt23:20); [1CO 9:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:13), [1CO 10:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:18); [HEB 7:13](https://ref.ly/Heb7:13), [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4)

### Pagan altars

**[ACT 17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:23)**

### The cross as an \`\`altar''

**[HEB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Heb13:10)**

### Altars in heaven

**[REV 6:9](https://ref.ly/Rev6:9)** *The lives of martyred saints are compared to the blood of offerings poured out at the base of the altar*; **[REV 8:3](https://ref.ly/Rev8:3)** *The prayers of God's people are like incense, offered to him on the heavenly altar.* *See also* [REV 9:13](https://ref.ly/Rev9:13), [REV 11:1](https://ref.ly/Rev11:1), [REV 14:18](https://ref.ly/Rev14:18), [REV 16:7](https://ref.ly/Rev16:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 8:20; Genesis 12:7-8; Genesis 13:18; Genesis 22:9; Genesis 26:25; Genesis 33:20; Genesis 35:7; Exodus 17:15; Exodus 24:4; Joshua 8:30; Judges 6:24; Judges 21:4; 1 Samuel 7:17; 1 Samuel 14:35; 2 Samuel 24:25; 1 Chronicles 21:26; Exodus 27:1-8; Exodus 30:1-10; Exodus 37:25-28; Exodus 38:1-7; Hebrews 9:3-4; 1 Kings 8:22; 1 Kings 6:20; 1 Kings 16:32; 2 Kings 23:15; Hosea 8:11; Hosea 10:1; 2 Kings 16:10; Deuteronomy 12:2-3; 2 Kings 21:3; Isaiah 17:8; Jeremiah 11:13; Jeremiah 19:13; Zephaniah 1:5; 2 Kings 23:12; Deuteronomy 7:5; Deuteronomy 12:8-14; 2 Kings 23:15-20; Ezra 3:2-3; Ezra 7:17; Exodus 20:24-26; Exodus 29:38-39; Exodus 30:28; Numbers 4:13; Numbers 7:10; Numbers 7:84; 1 Kings 1:50-51; Exodus 21:14; 1 Kings 2:28; Amos 3:14; Exodus 30:1-5; Exodus 30:7; Exodus 30:8-10; Numbers 4:11; Matthew 5:23-24; Matthew 23:18-20; 1 Corinthians 9:13; 1 Corinthians 10:18; Hebrews 7:13; Hebrews 9:4; Acts 17:23; Hebrews 13:10; Revelation 6:9; Revelation 8:3; Revelation 9:13; Revelation 11:1; Revelation 14:18; Revelation 16:7
* **Associated Articles:** 2315: Jesus Christ, as Lamb (ID: `2315@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6614: atonement (ID: `6614@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7314: blood (ID: `7314@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7386: incense (ID: `7386@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7412: priesthood (ID: `7412@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7454: sprinkling (ID: `7454@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7458: tabernacle, the (ID: `7458@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7466: temples (ID: `7466@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8626: worship, places of (ID: `8626@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7304: anointing (id: 7304)

The application of oil was associated with times of rejoicing and celebration. It also possessed a deeper significance, including that of being singled out by God for special favour or responsibilities.

Anointing as a social custom
----------------------------

### In personal grooming

**[ECC 9:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:8)** *The associations here are of joy and well\-being.* *See also* [RUT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:3); [PSA 92:10](https://ref.ly/Ps92:10); [ISA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Isa57:9); [AMO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:6); [MAT 6:17](https://ref.ly/Matt6:17)

### Anointing guests as a mark of honour

**[PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5)** *See also* [LUK 7:36–7:39](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36-Luke7:39), [LUK 7:44–7:47](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44-Luke7:47); [JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3)

### Anointing corpses as a burial preparation

**[MRK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Mark16:1)** *See also* [MAT 26:6–26:12](https://ref.ly/Matt26:6-Matt26:12); [JHN 19:38–19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:38-John19:40)

Anointing religious objects
---------------------------

**[EXO 40:9–40:11](https://ref.ly/Exod40:9-Exod40:11)** *Anointed objects are set apart (consecrated), to be used only in the performance of religious ceremonies.* *See also* [GEN 28:18](https://ref.ly/Gen28:18); [EXO 30:22–30:33](https://ref.ly/Exod30:22-Exod30:33)

Anointing people for office
---------------------------

### Priests

**[EXO 40:12–40:15](https://ref.ly/Exod40:12-Exod40:15)** *See also* [LEV 4:16](https://ref.ly/Lev4:16), [LEV 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:12); [1CH 29:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:22)

### Kings

**[1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39)** *See also* [1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1), [1SA 16:12–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:12-1Sam16:13); [2SA 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:3); [2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)

### A prophet

[1KI 19:15–19:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:15-1Kgs19:16) *the only reference to the anointing of a prophet*

Anointing people for other purposes
-----------------------------------

### For purification

[LEV 14:15–14:18](https://ref.ly/Lev14:15-Lev14:18)

### For healing

**[MRK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:13)** *It has been suggested that anointing in a healing context may be related to the use of oil for medicinal purposes.* *See also* [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)

The figurative use of anointing
-------------------------------

### Anointing by God

**[1SA 26:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:9)** *Israel's king is frequently referred to as “the LORD's anointed”. His physical anointing is seen as symbolising a divine anointing. The word “Messiah” literally means “the anointed one”*; **[ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1)** *Cyrus, as God's agent for a specific task, is referred to as “his anointed”.* *See also* [2SA 23:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:1); [PSA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2), [PSA 45:2](https://ref.ly/Ps45:2), [PSA 89:20](https://ref.ly/Ps89:20); [EZK 28:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:14)

### God's people in the OT

[1CH 16:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:22) parallel [PSA 105:15](https://ref.ly/Ps105:15)

### Christian believers

**[2CO 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:21-2Cor1:22)** *See also* [1JN 2:20](https://ref.ly/1John2:20), [1JN 2:27](https://ref.ly/1John2:27) *This anointing is not received in an outward ceremony but by sharing in the Holy Spirit's anointing of Jesus Christ.*

Jesus Christ as God's Anointed One (the Messiah)
------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 4:26–4:27](https://ref.ly/Acts4:26-Acts4:27)** *Jesus Christ's receiving of the Holy Spirit at his baptism was his “anointing” for his Messianic work*; **[ACT 10:38](https://ref.ly/Acts10:38)** *Note the link between anointing and the Holy Spirit.* *See also* [DAN 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/Dan9:25-Dan9:26); [LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18); [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [HEB 1:9](https://ref.ly/Heb1:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Ecclesiastes 9:8; Ruth 3:3; Psalms 92:10; Isaiah 57:9; Amos 6:6; Matthew 6:17; Psalms 23:5; Luke 7:36-39; Luke 7:44-47; John 12:3; Mark 16:1; Matthew 26:6-12; John 19:38-40; Exodus 40:9-11; Genesis 28:18; Exodus 30:22-33; Exodus 40:12-15; Leviticus 4:16; Leviticus 21:10-12; 1 Chronicles 29:22; 1 Kings 1:39; 1 Samuel 10:1; 1 Samuel 16:12-13; 2 Samuel 5:3; 2 Kings 11:12; 1 Kings 19:15-16; Leviticus 14:15-18; Mark 6:13; James 5:14; 1 Samuel 26:9; Isaiah 45:1; 2 Samuel 23:1; Psalms 2:2; Psalms 45:2; Psalms 89:20; Ezekiel 28:14; 1 Chronicles 16:22; Psalms 105:15; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22; 1 John 2:20; 1 John 2:27; Acts 4:26-27; Acts 10:38; Daniel 9:25-26; Luke 4:18; Isaiah 61:1; Hebrews 1:9
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2206: Jesus, the Christ (ID: `2206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2230: Messiah, coming of (ID: `2230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3120: Holy Spirit, descriptions of (ID: `3120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3239: Holy Spirit, anointing of (ID: `3239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4488: oil (ID: `4488@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5368: kingship (ID: `5368@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7413: priesthood, in OT (ID: `7413@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7745: ordination (ID: `7745@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8218: consecration (ID: `8218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8266: holiness (ID: `8266@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7306: ark of the covenant (id: 7306)

A rectangular wooden box, overlaid with gold, measuring 3\.75 x 2\.25 x 2\.25 feet (1\.1 x 0\.7 x 0\.7 metres). It contains the Law tablets and symbolises God's presence with his people.

Descriptions of the ark
-----------------------

[EXO 25:22](https://ref.ly/Exod25:22); ; [LEV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2); ; [NUM 10:33](https://ref.ly/Num10:33); ; [JOS 3:6](https://ref.ly/Josh3:6), [JOS 4:5](https://ref.ly/Josh4:5), [JOS 4:9](https://ref.ly/Josh4:9); ; [1SA 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:11)

### The construction of the ark

[EXO 25:10–25:16](https://ref.ly/Exod25:10-Exod25:16); ; [DEU 10:1](https://ref.ly/Deut10:1); ; [EXO 31:1–31:7](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:7), [EXO 35:10–35:12](https://ref.ly/Exod35:10-Exod35:12), [EXO 37:1–37:5](https://ref.ly/Exod37:1-Exod37:5); ; [DEU 10:3](https://ref.ly/Deut10:3); ; [EXO 39:33](https://ref.ly/Exod39:33), [EXO 39:35](https://ref.ly/Exod39:35), [EXO 40:1–40:5](https://ref.ly/Exod40:1-Exod40:5), [EXO 40:20–40:21](https://ref.ly/Exod40:20-Exod40:21)

### The construction of the atonement cover

[EXO 25:17–25:21](https://ref.ly/Exod25:17-Exod25:21), [EXO 26:34](https://ref.ly/Exod26:34), [EXO 30:6](https://ref.ly/Exod30:6), [EXO 37:6–37:9](https://ref.ly/Exod37:6-Exod37:9), [EXO 39:35](https://ref.ly/Exod39:35), [EXO 40:20](https://ref.ly/Exod40:20)

The care of the ark
-------------------

[NUM 3:30–3:32](https://ref.ly/Num3:30-Num3:32), [NUM 4:5](https://ref.ly/Num4:5); ; [DEU 10:8](https://ref.ly/Deut10:8), [DEU 31:9](https://ref.ly/Deut31:9)

Major events in the history of the ark
--------------------------------------

### The ark is taken to the promised land

[JOS 3:3](https://ref.ly/Josh3:3), [JOS 3:6](https://ref.ly/Josh3:6), [JOS 3:15–3:16](https://ref.ly/Josh3:15-Josh3:16), [JOS 6:4–6:16](https://ref.ly/Josh6:4-Josh6:16), [JOS 8:33](https://ref.ly/Josh8:33)

### The ark is the focus of the covenant renewal at Mount Ebal

[JDG 20:26–20:28](https://ref.ly/Judg20:26-Judg20:28)

### The ark is captured by the Philistines

[1SA 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:1-1Sam4:11), [1SA 4:17–4:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:17-1Sam4:22), [1SA 5:1–5:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:1-1Sam5:12)

### The ark is returned to Israel

[1SA 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:1-1Sam6:3), [1SA 6:10–6:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:10-1Sam6:15), [1SA 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:19), [1SA 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:1-1Sam7:2)

### The ark is brought to Jerusalem

[2SA 6:1–6:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:1-2Sam6:12) parallel [1CH 13:3–13:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:3-1Chr13:14); ; [2SA 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:17) parallel [1CH 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:1); ; [1CH 15:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:1), [1CH 16:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:37); ; [2SA 7:2–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:2-2Sam7:7) parallel [1CH 17:1–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:1-1Chr17:6); ; [2SA 15:24–15:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:24-2Sam15:25), [2SA 15:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:29) *David is fleeing from Absalom.*

### The ark is placed in the temple

[1KI 8:1–8:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:1-1Kgs8:6) parallel [2CH 5:2–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:2-2Chr5:7)

### The ark in later Israelite history

[2CH 35:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:3) *The ark must have been moved from the temple.*

The function of the ark
-----------------------

### To contain the tablets of the Law

**[DEU 10:5](https://ref.ly/Deut10:5)** *See also* [EXO 25:16](https://ref.ly/Exod25:16), [EXO 25:21](https://ref.ly/Exod25:21), [EXO 40:20](https://ref.ly/Exod40:20); [DEU 31:24–31:26](https://ref.ly/Deut31:24-Deut31:26); [1KI 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:9); [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4) *The jar of manna and Aaron's rod were originally placed beside the ark ([Ex 16:34](https://ref.ly/Exod16:34); [Nu 17:10](https://ref.ly/Num17:10)), and were probably put inside it at a later date.*

### As the place where God reveals his commands to Moses

**[NUM 7:89](https://ref.ly/Num7:89)** *See also* [EXO 25:22](https://ref.ly/Exod25:22), [EXO 30:6](https://ref.ly/Exod30:6), [EXO 30:36](https://ref.ly/Exod30:36)

### As a symbol of the presence of God

**[LEV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2)** *See also* [NUM 10:33–10:36](https://ref.ly/Num10:33-Num10:36); [JOS 7:6](https://ref.ly/Josh7:6); [JDG 20:27](https://ref.ly/Judg20:27); [2SA 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:2) parallel [1CH 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:1); [2CH 6:41](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:41) parallel [PSA 132:8](https://ref.ly/Ps132:8)

### Used on the Day of Atonement

**[LEV 16:13–16:15](https://ref.ly/Lev16:13-Lev16:15)**

The ark's holiness and effect
-----------------------------

### Irreverent treament of the ark brings judgment

**[2SA 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:6-2Sam6:7)** parallel [1CH 13:9–13:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:9-1Chr13:10) *See also* [JOS 3:4](https://ref.ly/Josh3:4); [1SA 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:1-1Sam5:4), [1SA 5:8–5:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:8-1Sam5:12), [1SA 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:19)

### The presence of the ark brings blessing

**[2SA 6:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:11)** parallel [1CH 13:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:14)

A restored Israel in which the ark has no place
-----------------------------------------------

**[JER 3:16](https://ref.ly/Jer3:16)**

The ark in John's revelation
----------------------------

**[REV 11:19](https://ref.ly/Rev11:19)** *The ark here symbolises the presence of God and is open and visible, not hidden in the Most Holy Place.*

## 7308: Atonement, Day of (id: 7308)

The most holy day of Israel's year (the tenth day of the seventh month) on which the high priest entered the Most Holy Place to offer sacrifices for the sins of the nation. Hebrews sees this day as symbolic of the achievements of Jesus Christ on the cross.

The priestly rituals on the Day of Atonement
--------------------------------------------

### The priest's own preparations

*See also* [LEV 16:4](https://ref.ly/Lev16:4), [LEV 16:6](https://ref.ly/Lev16:6), [LEV 16:11–16:14](https://ref.ly/Lev16:11-Lev16:14)

### The preparation of two male goats

[LEV 16:7–16:10](https://ref.ly/Lev16:7-Lev16:10); [EXO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:10); ; [LEV 16:15–16:19](https://ref.ly/Lev16:15-Lev16:19); [LEV 16:10](https://ref.ly/Lev16:10), [LEV 16:20–16:22](https://ref.ly/Lev16:20-Lev16:22)

### Concluding procedures

[LEV 16:23–16:28](https://ref.ly/Lev16:23-Lev16:28)

### Additional offerings on the Day of Atonement

[NUM 29:8–29:11](https://ref.ly/Num29:8-Num29:11)

Features unique to the Day of Atonement
---------------------------------------

### The priest entered the Most Holy Place

**[HEB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Heb9:7)** *See also* [LEV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2)

### Sacrifice was made for all the sins of all the people

**[LEV 16:30](https://ref.ly/Lev16:30)** *See also* [EXO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:10); [LEV 16:16–16:17](https://ref.ly/Lev16:16-Lev16:17), [LEV 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/Lev16:21-Lev16:22), [LEV 16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:34)

### It was a festival requiring humility and fasting

**[NUM 29:7](https://ref.ly/Num29:7)** *This provides a contrast to all other religious festivals which were times of joyous celebration and feasting. “deny yourselves” includes the idea of fasting.* *See also* [LEV 16:29](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29), [LEV 16:31](https://ref.ly/Lev16:31), [LEV 23:27–23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27-Lev23:32); [ACT 27:9](https://ref.ly/Acts27:9)

The Day of Atonement is paralleled and contrasted with the achievements of Jesus Christ in his death
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ entered the Most Holy Place

**[HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Heb9:24)** *See also* [HEB 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19-Heb6:20), [HEB 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Heb9:11-Heb9:12)

### Jesus Christ's blood was offered in sacrifice

**[HEB 9:12](https://ref.ly/Heb9:12)** *See also* [ROM 3:25](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25)

### Jesus Christ's sacrifice was outside the city gates

**[HEB 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/Heb13:11-Heb13:12)**

### Jesus Christ's sacrifice was once for all

**[HEB 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/Heb9:25-Heb9:26)** *See also* [HEB 10:12](https://ref.ly/Heb10:12)

### Jesus Christ's sacrifice gives inner rather than ritual cleansing

**[HEB 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:13-Heb9:14)** *See also* [HEB 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Heb9:9-Heb9:10)

### Jesus Christ's sacrifice gives access to God

**[HEB 10:19–10:20](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:20)** *On the Day of Atonement no\-one else was allowed to be in the Tent of Meeting, let alone in the Most Holy Place. Access into the intimate presence of God is now the right of all believers.* *See also* [LEV 16:17](https://ref.ly/Lev16:17); [MAT 27:51](https://ref.ly/Matt27:51); [EPH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Eph2:18), [EPH 3:12](https://ref.ly/Eph3:12)

## 7310: avenger of blood (id: 7310)

The one responsible in OT law for carrying out the death penalty in the case of murder, usually, the murdered person's next of kin.

The responsibility of the avenger of blood
------------------------------------------

**[NUM 35:16–35:21](https://ref.ly/Num35:16-Num35:21)** *See also* [NUM 35:26–35:27](https://ref.ly/Num35:26-Num35:27); [DEU 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:12)

Protection from the avenger of blood
------------------------------------

**[NUM 35:10–35:15](https://ref.ly/Num35:10-Num35:15)** *See also* [NUM 35:22–35:25](https://ref.ly/Num35:22-Num35:25); [DEU 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Deut19:4-Deut19:6); [JOS 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:6)

Examples of avenging of blood
-----------------------------

[GEN 4:24](https://ref.ly/Gen4:24); ; [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27), [2SA 14:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:11)

God as an avenger of blood
--------------------------

**[DEU 32:43](https://ref.ly/Deut32:43)** *See also* [JDG 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Judg9:23-Judg9:24); [2KI 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:7); [PSA 9:12](https://ref.ly/Ps9:12), [PSA 79:10](https://ref.ly/Ps79:10); [REV 6:10](https://ref.ly/Rev6:10), [REV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Rev19:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 35:16-21; Numbers 35:26-27; Deuteronomy 19:11-12; Numbers 35:10-15; Numbers 35:22-25; Deuteronomy 19:4-6; Joshua 20:1-6; Genesis 4:24; 2 Samuel 3:27; 2 Samuel 14:11; Deuteronomy 32:43; Judges 9:23-24; 2 Kings 9:7; Psalms 9:12; Psalms 79:10; Revelation 6:10; Revelation 19:2

## 7312: Baal (id: 7312)

Literally “master”, used to refer to a variety of local gods in the Near East, encountered by Israel in Canaan. In the later period, the word comes to refer especially to the local god of the city of Tyre (Baal Melgart), on account of the increasing importance of that city in the region.

The history of Baal worship
---------------------------

### In the exodus period

**[NUM 25:3–25:5](https://ref.ly/Num25:3-Num25:5)** *A Moabite deity whose worship involved sexual immorality.* *See also* [NUM 22:41](https://ref.ly/Num22:41); [DEU 4:3](https://ref.ly/Deut4:3); [PSA 106:28](https://ref.ly/Ps106:28); [HOS 9:10](https://ref.ly/Hos9:10)

### In the time of the judges

**[JDG 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Judg2:12-Judg2:13)** *Ashtoreth was the consort of Baal.* *See also* [JDG 3:7](https://ref.ly/Judg3:7), [JDG 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/Judg6:31-Judg6:32), [JDG 8:33](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33), [JDG 10:6](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6); [1SA 7:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:4), [1SA 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:10)

### In the time of Elijah

**[1KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:1)** *The proclamation of a drought and the contest on Carmel challenged the supposed power of Baal.* *See also* [1KI 16:30–16:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:30-1Kgs16:33), [1KI 18:18–19:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:18-1Kgs19:1), [1KI 22:53](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:53); [2KI 10:18–10:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:18-2Kgs10:29), [2KI 11:17–11:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:17-2Kgs11:18)

### During the later monarchy in the north

**[HOS 2:2–2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2-Hos2:13)** *Lovers are gods such as Baal.* *See also* [HOS 11:2](https://ref.ly/Hos11:2), [HOS 13:1](https://ref.ly/Hos13:1)

### During the later monarchy in the south

**[2CH 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:1-2Chr28:2)** *The Judean kingdom copied the idolatry in the north.* *See also* [JER 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jer2:8), [JER 7:9](https://ref.ly/Jer7:9); [HOS 3:3](https://ref.ly/Hos3:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 25:3-5; Numbers 22:41; Deuteronomy 4:3; Psalms 106:28; Hosea 9:10; Judges 2:12-13; Judges 3:7; Judges 6:31-32; Judges 8:33; Judges 10:6; 1 Samuel 7:4; 1 Samuel 12:10; 1 Kings 17:1; 1 Kings 16:30-33; 1 Kings 18:18-19:1; 1 Kings 22:53; 2 Kings 10:18-29; 2 Kings 11:17-18; Hosea 2:2-13; Hosea 11:2; Hosea 13:1; 2 Chronicles 28:1-2; Jeremiah 2:8; Jeremiah 7:9; Hosea 3:3
* **Associated Articles:** 5092: Elijah (ID: `5092@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7774: prophets, false (ID: `7774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8237: doctrine, false (ID: `8237@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7314: blood (id: 7314)

The symbol of life, which thus plays an especially important role in the sacrificial system of the OT. The shedding of the blood of a sacrificial animal represents the giving up of its life. The “blood of Christ” refers to Jesus Christ's obedient giving of his life, in order to achieve redemption and forgiveness.

* **Associated Articles:** 7315: blood, as basis of life (ID: `7315@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7316: blood, and OT sacrifices (ID: `7316@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7317: blood, of Jesus Christ (ID: `7317@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7318: blood, as symbol of guilt (ID: `7318@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7315: blood, as basis of life (id: 7315)

Scripture treats blood as the basis of life, and regards the shedding of blood as representing the end of life.

Blood symbolises life
---------------------

**[LEV 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Lev17:10-Lev17:14)** *See also* [GEN 9:4–9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:4-Gen9:6); [DEU 12:20–12:25](https://ref.ly/Deut12:20-Deut12:25); [2SA 23:15–23:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:15-2Sam23:17); [PSA 72:14](https://ref.ly/Ps72:14); [JER 2:34](https://ref.ly/Jer2:34); [EZK 33:1–33:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:1-Ezek33:6); [MAT 27:3–27:4](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:4); [LUK 11:50–11:51](https://ref.ly/Luke11:50-Luke11:51) parallel [MAT 23:35–23:36](https://ref.ly/Matt23:35-Matt23:36)

------------------------------

**[GEN 4:8–4:11](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8-Gen4:11)** *See also* [GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6); [JDG 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/Judg9:22-Judg9:24); [1KI 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:5-1Kgs2:6), [1KI 2:28–2:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:28-1Kgs2:34); [2KI 9:30–9:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:30-2Kgs9:33)

Shedding blood as a sin
-----------------------

The eating of blood forbidden
-----------------------------

**[LEV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Lev3:17)** *See also* [LEV 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Lev7:26-Lev7:27), [LEV 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Lev17:10-Lev17:14); [DEU 12:23](https://ref.ly/Deut12:23); [1SA 14:31–14:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:31-1Sam14:34); [ACT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:20), [ACT 15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:29)

## 7316: blood, and OT sacrifices (id: 7316)

The pouring out of animals' blood in sacrifice was God's provision, under the old covenant, for the atonement of sin.

God's provision of blood sacrifice to establish the old covenant
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 24:4–24:8](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4-Exod24:8)** *See also* [HEB 9:18–9:20](https://ref.ly/Heb9:18-Heb9:20)

God's provision of blood sacrifices within the old covenant
-----------------------------------------------------------

### Blood sacrifices and the priesthood

[EXO 29:10–29:21](https://ref.ly/Exod29:10-Exod29:21) parallel [LEV 8:14–8:24](https://ref.ly/Lev8:14-Lev8:24); ; [LEV 9:8–9:14](https://ref.ly/Lev9:8-Lev9:14), [LEV 16:1–16:14](https://ref.ly/Lev16:1-Lev16:14)

### Blood sacrifices and various offerings

[LEV 1:1–1:17](https://ref.ly/Lev1:1-Lev1:17) *the burnt offering, to gain favour or express devotion*; ; [LEV 3:1–3:17](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1-Lev3:17) *the fellowship, or peace, offering, to express gratitude*; ; [LEV 4:1–4:35](https://ref.ly/Lev4:1-Lev4:35) *the sin offering, to gain forgiveness*; ; [LEV 5:14–5:19](https://ref.ly/Lev5:14-Lev5:19) *the guilt offering, where restitution was required*; ; [LEV 16:15–16:22](https://ref.ly/Lev16:15-Lev16:22) *the Day of Atonement, for the cleansing of the nation from unintentional sin*

### Blood sacrifices and the Passover

**[EXO 12:1–12:14](https://ref.ly/Exod12:1-Exod12:14)** *See also* [2CH 30:15–30:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:15-2Chr30:20), [2CH 35:1–35:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:1-2Chr35:19); [EZR 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:19-Ezra6:21); [MRK 14:12](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12) parallel [LUK 22:7–22:8](https://ref.ly/Luke22:7-Luke22:8)

Blood sacrifices must come from animals without defect
------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 22:17–22:25](https://ref.ly/Lev22:17-Lev22:25)** *See also* [EXO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Exod12:5); [LEV 1:3](https://ref.ly/Lev1:3), [LEV 3:1](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1), [LEV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:3), [LEV 5:15](https://ref.ly/Lev5:15); [NUM 28:9](https://ref.ly/Num28:9), [NUM 28:11](https://ref.ly/Num28:11); [DEU 15:21](https://ref.ly/Deut15:21); [EZK 43:22–43:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:22-Ezek43:27)

Blood sacrifices must not come from human beings
------------------------------------------------

**[2KI 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:10)** *See also* [2KI 17:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:17), [2KI 21:1–21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:1-2Kgs21:6); [EZK 20:25–20:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:25-Ezek20:26); [MIC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mic6:7)

The limited effect of blood sacrifices
--------------------------------------

### They can become mere external ritual

**[ISA 1:11–1:13](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:13)** *See also* [ISA 66:2–66:4](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2-Isa66:4)

### They are worthless without obedience to God

**[HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6)** *See also* [1SA 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22-1Sam15:23); [PSA 40:6–40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:6-Ps40:8); [AMO 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Amos4:4-Amos4:5), [AMO 5:21–5:27](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:27)

### They are unable to cleanse the conscience

**[HEB 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Heb9:9-Heb9:10)** *See also* [HEB 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1-Heb10:4)

## 7317: blood, of Jesus Christ (id: 7317)

The shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ is seen as representing the giving of his life as an atoning sacrifice for the sins of humanity.

The blood of Jesus Christ as part of his humanity
-------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:33-John19:34)** *See also* [LUK 22:44](https://ref.ly/Luke22:44); [ACT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Acts5:28); [1JN 5:6](https://ref.ly/1John5:6)

The blood of Jesus Christ as a sacrifice
----------------------------------------

[HEB 9:12–9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:12-Heb9:14), [HEB 9:23–9:26](https://ref.ly/Heb9:23-Heb9:26), [HEB 10:3–10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:3-Heb10:14), [HEB 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/Heb13:11-Heb13:12)

The blood of Jesus Christ as a symbol of atonement
--------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:25](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25)** *See also* [EPH 1:7](https://ref.ly/Eph1:7); [REV 7:14](https://ref.ly/Rev7:14)

The effects of the blood of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------

### The institution of the new covenant

**[1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25)** parallels [MAT 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:27-Matt26:28), [MRK 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:23-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20) *See also* [HEB 9:11–9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:11-Heb9:15), [HEB 12:24](https://ref.ly/Heb12:24), [HEB 13:20](https://ref.ly/Heb13:20)

### Redemption

[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28); ; [1PE 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1-1Pet1:2), [1PE 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18-1Pet1:19); ; [REV 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9-Rev5:10)

### Forgiveness and justification

**[ROM 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rom5:9)** *See also* [MAT 26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:28) parallels [MRK 14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20); [ROM 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25-Rom3:26)

### Victory over evil and Satan

[REV 7:14–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:14-Rev7:17), [REV 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Rev12:10-Rev12:11)

### Liberation from sin

[REV 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Rev1:5-Rev1:6)

### The promise of total restoration

[COL 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Col1:19-Col1:20)

The blood of Jesus Christ and believers
---------------------------------------

### Believers are cleansed from all sin

[HEB 9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:14), [HEB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:22), [HEB 13:12](https://ref.ly/Heb13:12); ; [1JN 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:6-1John1:9)

### Believers have a new confidence before God

[EPH 2:13](https://ref.ly/Eph2:13); ; [COL 1:19–1:22](https://ref.ly/Col1:19-Col1:22); ; [HEB 10:19–10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:22)

The blood of Jesus Christ and the Lord's Supper
-----------------------------------------------

### Invitations to share in the blood of Jesus Christ

**[1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16)** *See also* [JHN 6:53–6:57](https://ref.ly/John6:53-John6:57)

### Warnings about sinning against the blood of Jesus Christ

**[1CO 11:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:27)** *See also* [HEB 10:28–10:31](https://ref.ly/Heb10:28-Heb10:31)

## 7318: blood, as symbol of guilt (id: 7318)

Blood is often used as an image of people's sin and guilt, and the judgment which follows. Blood\-guilt is ascribed to those who are responsible for the shedding of innocent blood.

Blood as an image of sin and guilt
----------------------------------

**[ISA 59:2–59:3](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2-Isa59:3)** *See also* [LEV 20:9–20:13](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9-Lev20:13), [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27); [2SA 1:14–1:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:14-2Sam1:16); [ISA 1:15–1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:15-Isa1:18); [NAM 3:1](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1); [ACT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Acts18:6)

Blood as an image of judgment
-----------------------------

**[ISA 34:5–34:6](https://ref.ly/Isa34:5-Isa34:6)** *See also* [EXO 7:14–7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:14-Exod7:21); [PSA 78:44](https://ref.ly/Ps78:44), [PSA 105:29](https://ref.ly/Ps105:29); [ACT 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:19-Acts2:20); [JOL 2:30–2:31](https://ref.ly/Joel2:30-Joel2:31)

Blood as a sign of the end times
--------------------------------

**[ACT 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:19-Acts2:20)** *See also* [JOL 2:30–2:31](https://ref.ly/Joel2:30-Joel2:31); [REV 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:15), [REV 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Rev8:7-Rev8:9), [REV 16:3–16:6](https://ref.ly/Rev16:3-Rev16:6)

Blood\-guilt, the result of shedding innocent blood
---------------------------------------------------

### Examples of blood\-guilt

**[GEN 4:8–4:11](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8-Gen4:11)** *See also* [2SA 1:14–1:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:14-2Sam1:16), [2SA 4:5–4:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:5-2Sam4:12); [EZK 35:5–35:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek35:5-Ezek35:9); [HAB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Hab2:12); [MAT 27:3–27:8](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:8), [MAT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:24); [ACT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Acts5:28)

### The right to avenge blood\-guilt under the law

**[DEU 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:13)** *See also* [NUM 35:16–35:28](https://ref.ly/Num35:16-Num35:28)

### Restraint to avoid further blood\-guilt

**[DEU 4:41–4:42](https://ref.ly/Deut4:41-Deut4:42)** *See also* [GEN 4:15](https://ref.ly/Gen4:15); [NUM 35:6–35:34](https://ref.ly/Num35:6-Num35:34); [DEU 19:4–19:10](https://ref.ly/Deut19:4-Deut19:10); [JOS 20:1–20:9](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:9)

### Blood\-guilt cannot be forgotten

**[GEN 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:5-Gen9:6)** *See also* [GEN 4:10–4:16](https://ref.ly/Gen4:10-Gen4:16), [GEN 42:22](https://ref.ly/Gen42:22); [1KI 2:28–2:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:28-1Kgs2:33); [ISA 26:21](https://ref.ly/Isa26:21); [MAT 23:30–23:31](https://ref.ly/Matt23:30-Matt23:31)

## 7320: breastpiece (id: 7320)

A pouch worn on the chest of the high priest.

The breastpiece was worn by the high priest
-------------------------------------------

**[LEV 8:5–8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:5-Lev8:9)** *See also* [EXO 28:15–28:16](https://ref.ly/Exod28:15-Exod28:16), [EXO 28:29](https://ref.ly/Exod28:29), [EXO 39:8–39:9](https://ref.ly/Exod39:8-Exod39:9)

## 7322: burnt offering (id: 7322)

Probably the earliest and most basic form of sacrifice. It is seen fundamentally as a gift to God, either in thanksgiving for his goodness or for atonement for sin.

Hebrew names for the burnt offering
-----------------------------------

**[DEU 33:10](https://ref.ly/Deut33:10)** *The rarer Hebrew expression means something whole or complete*; **[PSA 66:13](https://ref.ly/Ps66:13)** *The commoner expression in Hebrew means that which “goes up” to God.* *See also* [1SA 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:9); [PSA 51:19](https://ref.ly/Ps51:19)

Early examples of burnt offerings
---------------------------------

**[GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20)** [GEN 22:2–22:8](https://ref.ly/Gen22:2-Gen22:8), [GEN 22:13](https://ref.ly/Gen22:13); ; [EXO 10:25](https://ref.ly/Exod10:25), [EXO 18:12](https://ref.ly/Exod18:12), [EXO 20:24](https://ref.ly/Exod20:24), [EXO 24:5](https://ref.ly/Exod24:5); ; [JOB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Job1:5)

Regulations concerning the burnt offering
-----------------------------------------

### How the offering is to be made

[LEV 1:1–1:17](https://ref.ly/Lev1:1-Lev1:17), [LEV 6:8–6:13](https://ref.ly/Lev6:8-Lev6:13), [LEV 7:8](https://ref.ly/Lev7:8) *the entitlement of the priest*; ; [MAL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:8)

### Occasions for making the offering

**[NUM 28:9–28:10](https://ref.ly/Num28:9-Num28:10)** *It was an additional offering on the Sabbath.* [EXO 29:38–29:42](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:42); ; [EZR 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:3); [LEV 8:18–8:21](https://ref.ly/Lev8:18-Lev8:21) *at the ordination of priests*; [LEV 16:3](https://ref.ly/Lev16:3), [LEV 16:24](https://ref.ly/Lev16:24); [NUM 28:11–28:14](https://ref.ly/Num28:11-Num28:14) *on the first of the month, the new moon*; [NUM 28:19](https://ref.ly/Num28:19), [NUM 28:23](https://ref.ly/Num28:23); [LEV 23:12](https://ref.ly/Lev23:12), [LEV 23:18](https://ref.ly/Lev23:18); ; [NUM 28:27](https://ref.ly/Num28:27); [NUM 29:2](https://ref.ly/Num29:2), [NUM 29:6](https://ref.ly/Num29:6); [NUM 29:13](https://ref.ly/Num29:13); [LUK 2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:24) *for purification after childbirth*

Burnt offerings in idolatrous worship
-------------------------------------

[EXO 32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:6); ; [2KI 10:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:25)

Warnings about misplaced confidence in the burnt offering
---------------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22)** *See also* [ISA 1:11–1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:15); [JER 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Jer7:21-Jer7:22); [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [AMO 5:25](https://ref.ly/Amos5:25); [MIC 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:6-Mic6:8)

NT references to the burnt offering
-----------------------------------

### Jesus Christ endorsed the prophets' teaching

**[MRK 12:33–12:34](https://ref.ly/Mark12:33-Mark12:34)** *See also* [MAT 9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:13), [MAT 12:7](https://ref.ly/Matt12:7)

### The holy life of Jesus Christ is the perfect burnt offering

**[HEB 10:5–10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:5-Heb10:10)** *See also* [HEB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:14)

## 7324: calf worship (id: 7324)

A heathen (notably Egyptian and Canaanite) practice, in which statues of bull calves were worshipped as symbols of fertility and physical strength. Contrary to the second commandment this was copied by the Israelites and by Jeroboam after the division of the kingdom, in both cases incurring God's wrath.

Aaron made a golden calf for the Israelites to worship
------------------------------------------------------

### This act was born partly of impatience

**[EXO 32:1–32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:4)** *See also* [EXO 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Exod32:7-Exod32:8); [PSA 106:19–106:22](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19-Ps106:22); [ACT 7:41](https://ref.ly/Acts7:41)

### It was swiftly condemned

**[EXO 32:19–32:20](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19-Exod32:20)** *See also* [EXO 32:35](https://ref.ly/Exod32:35); [DEU 9:16–9:21](https://ref.ly/Deut9:16-Deut9:21); [PSA 106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:23)

King Jeroboam initiated calf worship
------------------------------------

### As a matter of expediency

**[1KI 12:26–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26-1Kgs12:30)** *See also* [2KI 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:15-2Kgs17:16); [2CH 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:14-2Chr11:15), [2CH 13:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:8)

### Jeroboam's sin was one of the main reasons for the Assyrian captivity

**[2KI 17:18–17:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:18-2Kgs17:28)** *See also* [HOS 8:4–8:9](https://ref.ly/Hos8:4-Hos8:9), [HOS 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Hos10:5-Hos10:6)

Calf worship is detestable and useless
--------------------------------------

[DEU 7:25](https://ref.ly/Deut7:25); ; [ISA 44:9–44:20](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9-Isa44:20), [ISA 46:6–46:7](https://ref.ly/Isa46:6-Isa46:7); ; [ROM 1:21–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21-Rom1:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 32:1-4; Exodus 32:7-8; Psalms 106:19-22; Acts 7:41; Exodus 32:19-20; Exodus 32:35; Deuteronomy 9:16-21; Psalms 106:23; 1 Kings 12:26-30; 2 Kings 17:15-16; 2 Chronicles 11:14-15; 2 Chronicles 13:8; 2 Kings 17:18-28; Hosea 8:4-9; Hosea 10:5-6; Deuteronomy 7:25; Isaiah 44:9-20; Isaiah 46:6-7; Romans 1:21-23

## 7328: ceremonies (id: 7328)

Formal outward practices and rituals symbolising or marking events of importance or spiritual significance. Although they had their place in biblical cultural life, they may become empty and hypocritical, losing their deep spiritual meaning. Ceremonies thus become symbolic of empty legalism which should be rejected.

Ceremonies marking important events
-----------------------------------

### Different stages in life

[GEN 50:7](https://ref.ly/Gen50:7), [GEN 50:10–50:11](https://ref.ly/Gen50:10-Gen50:11); ; [EXO 13:2](https://ref.ly/Exod13:2); ; [JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10); ; [JER 34:5](https://ref.ly/Jer34:5); ; [JHN 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/John2:1-John2:2), [JHN 20:4](https://ref.ly/John20:4)

### Temple worship

### Appointing leaders

**[ACT 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:2-Acts13:3)** *See also* [LEV 8:30](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30); [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13); [1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39); [ACT 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:5-Acts6:6), [ACT 9:17](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17); [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14)

### Historical events

**[RUT 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:7-Ruth4:8)** *See also* [GEN 35:14](https://ref.ly/Gen35:14); [EXO 12:14](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14), [EXO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Exod13:9); [JOS 4:4–4:9](https://ref.ly/Josh4:4-Josh4:9); [1SA 7:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:12); [LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19) parallel [1CO 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24-1Cor11:25)

Ceremonies as spiritual symbols of God's presence
-------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:20)** *There is a sense in which any gathering of believers is ceremonially symbolic of God's presence.* *See also* [EXO 30:25–30:29](https://ref.ly/Exod30:25-Exod30:29); [NUM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Num4:4), [NUM 10:35–10:36](https://ref.ly/Num10:35-Num10:36) *The ark of the covenant was a symbol of God's presence among his people. It, and its location, the tabernacle and later, the Most Holy Place, were therefore centres of the most elaborate ceremonies*; [1KI 8:3–8:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:3-1Kgs8:6) parallel [2CH 5:4–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:4-2Chr5:7); [1KI 8:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:29) parallel [2CH 6:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:20); [1CH 6:49](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:49); [HEB 9:1–9:7](https://ref.ly/Heb9:1-Heb9:7)

Initiation ceremonies
---------------------

### Circumcision

**[LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3)** *See also* [GEN 17:10](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10); [JOS 5:2](https://ref.ly/Josh5:2); [LUK 2:21–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:21-Luke2:24)

### Baptism

Ceremonies relating to ritual cleansing
---------------------------------------

**[EXO 30:18–30:20](https://ref.ly/Exod30:18-Exod30:20)** *See also* [LEV 14:14–14:18](https://ref.ly/Lev14:14-Lev14:18), [LEV 16:26](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26), [LEV 22:6](https://ref.ly/Lev22:6); [NUM 19:7](https://ref.ly/Num19:7); [HEB 9:10](https://ref.ly/Heb9:10) *Many aspects of ritual activity were added to the biblical ones in Jewish laws and regulations.*

Ceremonies relating to atonement
--------------------------------

**[LEV 17:11](https://ref.ly/Lev17:11)** *See also* [LEV 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lev4:20), [LEV 8:30–8:35](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30-Lev8:35), [LEV 16:10](https://ref.ly/Lev16:10); [HEB 5:1](https://ref.ly/Heb5:1)

Ceremonies conveying spiritual realities
----------------------------------------

### Passover: a ceremony symbolising salvation history

**[LUK 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/Luke22:15-Luke22:16)** *See also* [2KI 23:21–23:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:21-2Kgs23:22); [EZR 6:21–6:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:21-Ezra6:22); [MAT 26:17](https://ref.ly/Matt26:17) parallels [MRK 14:12](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12), [LUK 22:7–22:9](https://ref.ly/Luke22:7-Luke22:9)

### Baptism: a ceremony symbolising new birth

**[JHN 3:5](https://ref.ly/John3:5)** *See also* [ACT 10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:48), [ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:16); [GAL 3:27](https://ref.ly/Gal3:27); [COL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:12)

### Marriage: a ceremony symbolising faithful partnership

[MAT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:9); ; [EPH 5:25–5:28](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:28); ; [HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4); ; [REV 19:7](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7)

Ceremonies which lapsed into hypocrisy
--------------------------------------

### Washing ceremonies without accompanying inner cleanliness

**[MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25)** *See also* [MAT 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27-Matt23:28); [MRK 7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:4)

### Sacrificial ceremonies without inner repentance

**[AMO 5:21–5:22](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:22)** *See also* [1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22); [PSA 51:16–51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:16-Ps51:17); [ISA 1:11–1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:17); [HOS 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Hos8:11-Hos8:13); [MAL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:8)

### Initiation ceremonies without real commitment

**[1CO 7:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:19)** *See also* [ACT 15:1](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1), [ACT 15:8–15:10](https://ref.ly/Acts15:8-Acts15:10); [ROM 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:28-Rom2:29); [GAL 5:6](https://ref.ly/Gal5:6)

Jesus Christ brings freedom from meaningless ceremonies
-------------------------------------------------------

## 7330: chief priests (id: 7330)

A group of individuals in charge of temple worship in Jerusalem, and regarded as leading representatives of the Jewish people, who came into conflict with Jesus Christ and plotted his death.

OT references to the chief priest
---------------------------------

**[2CH 19:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:11)** *See also* [2KI 25:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:18) parallel [JER 52:24](https://ref.ly/Jer52:24) *Seraiah*[2CH 24:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:6), [2CH 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11)[2CH 31:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:10) *Azariah*; [EZR 7:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:5) *Ezra was descended from Aaron the chief priest*

The chief priests were in charge of temple worship in Jerusalem
---------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Matt2:4) *They were expected to pronounce on matters of Jewish law and interpretation of Scripture*; ; [MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15) *Temple discipline was their responsibility.*

Jesus Christ predicted his suffering at the hands of the chief priests and elders
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)** parallels [MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31), [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22) *See also* [MAT 20:18](https://ref.ly/Matt20:18) parallel [MRK 10:33](https://ref.ly/Mark10:33)

The response of the chief priests to Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------

### They were puzzled by Jesus Christ's teaching

**[MAT 21:23](https://ref.ly/Matt21:23)** parallels [MRK 11:27–11:28](https://ref.ly/Mark11:27-Mark11:28), [LUK 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Luke20:1-Luke20:2) *See also* [MAT 21:45–21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:45-Matt21:46) parallel [LUK 20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:19); [JHN 7:32](https://ref.ly/John7:32), [JHN 7:45](https://ref.ly/John7:45), [JHN 11:47](https://ref.ly/John11:47) *As official representatives of the Jewish people, they feared the political repercussions of Jesus Christ's popularity.*

### They and the elders plotted Jesus Christ's death

**[MAT 26:3–26:5](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:5)** parallel [MRK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1) *See also* [MAT 26:59](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59) parallel [MRK 14:55](https://ref.ly/Mark14:55); [MAT 27:1](https://ref.ly/Matt27:1)

### They collaborated with Judas

[MAT 26:14](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14) parallels [MRK 14:10](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10), [LUK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Luke22:4); ; [MAT 26:47](https://ref.ly/Matt26:47) parallels [MRK 14:43](https://ref.ly/Mark14:43), [LUK 22:52](https://ref.ly/Luke22:52), [JHN 18:3](https://ref.ly/John18:3); ; [MAT 27:3–27:6](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:6)

The role of the chief priests in the trial of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------------------------

### They made accusations against Jesus Christ

[MAT 27:12](https://ref.ly/Matt27:12) parallel [MRK 15:3](https://ref.ly/Mark15:3); ; [LUK 22:66](https://ref.ly/Luke22:66), [LUK 23:10](https://ref.ly/Luke23:10)

### They persuaded the crowd against Jesus Christ

[MAT 27:20](https://ref.ly/Matt27:20); ; [LUK 23:4](https://ref.ly/Luke23:4), [LUK 23:13](https://ref.ly/Luke23:13); ; [JHN 19:6](https://ref.ly/John19:6)

### They mocked Jesus Christ

[MAT 27:41](https://ref.ly/Matt27:41) parallel [MRK 15:31](https://ref.ly/Mark15:31)

### They handed Jesus Christ over to Pilate

[MRK 15:10](https://ref.ly/Mark15:10); ; [LUK 24:20](https://ref.ly/Luke24:20); ; [JHN 18:35](https://ref.ly/John18:35)

### They persuaded Pilate to have the tomb guarded

[MAT 27:62–27:66](https://ref.ly/Matt27:62-Matt27:66)

### They bribed the soldiers

[MAT 28:11–28:15](https://ref.ly/Matt28:11-Matt28:15)

### They rejected Jesus Christ as king

[JHN 19:15](https://ref.ly/John19:15), [JHN 19:21](https://ref.ly/John19:21)

The chief priests' attempt to prevent the preaching of the gospel
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### By silencing the apostles

[ACT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Acts4:23), [ACT 5:24](https://ref.ly/Acts5:24)

### By authorising Paul to arrest believers

[ACT 9:14](https://ref.ly/Acts9:14), [ACT 9:21](https://ref.ly/Acts9:21), [ACT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10), [ACT 26:12](https://ref.ly/Acts26:12)

The involvement of the chief priests in the arrest and trial of Paul
--------------------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 22:30](https://ref.ly/Acts22:30), [ACT 23:14](https://ref.ly/Acts23:14), [ACT 25:2](https://ref.ly/Acts25:2), [ACT 25:15](https://ref.ly/Acts25:15)
