## 7332: child sacrifice (id: 7332)

Child sacrifice is unconditionally condemned in Scripture. The few cases in which it is known to have taken place are probably due to the influence of Israel's pagan neighbours.

Laws forbidding child sacrifice
-------------------------------

**[LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21)** *Molech was the god of the Ammonites.* *See also* [EXO 22:29](https://ref.ly/Exod22:29) *to be interpreted with reference to [Ex 13:12–16](https://ref.ly/Exod13:12-Exod13:16) and [Ex 34:19,22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:19,Exod34:22)*; [DEU 18:10](https://ref.ly/Deut18:10)

Instances of child sacrifice
----------------------------

[2KI 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:27), [2KI 16:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:3) parallel [2CH 28:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:3); ; [2KI 17:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:17), [2KI 17:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:31), [2KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:6) parallel [2CH 33:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:6); ; [2KI 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:10) *Josiah's reforms involved desecrating Topheth, where child sacrifices were offered*; ; [ISA 57:5](https://ref.ly/Isa57:5); ; [JER 7:31](https://ref.ly/Jer7:31)

Arguments against child sacrifice
---------------------------------

### Child sacrifice is the idolatrous practice of the nations

**[DEU 12:31](https://ref.ly/Deut12:31)** *See also* [LEV 20:2–20:5](https://ref.ly/Lev20:2-Lev20:5); [PSA 106:35–106:38](https://ref.ly/Ps106:35-Ps106:38); [JER 32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:35); [HOS 13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:2)

### It defiles God's people

**[EZK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:26)** *See also* [EZK 20:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:31)

### It defiles God's sanctuary

[EZK 23:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:37), [EZK 23:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:39)

### It incurs God's anger

[JER 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/Jer19:4-Jer19:5); ; [EZK 16:20–16:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:20-Ezek16:21), [EZK 16:36–16:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:36-Ezek16:37); ; [HOS 13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:2)

God's unique command to Abraham to sacrifice Isaac
--------------------------------------------------

[GEN 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:2) *This was a test of obedience for Abraham. The following verses dispel any idea that God desires human sacrifice*; ; [HEB 11:17](https://ref.ly/Heb11:17); [JHN 3:16](https://ref.ly/John3:16); ; [ROM 8:32](https://ref.ly/Rom8:32)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 18:21; Exodus 22:29; Exodus 13:12-16; Exodus 34:19; Exodus 34:22; Deuteronomy 18:10; 2 Kings 3:27; 2 Kings 16:3; 2 Chronicles 28:3; 2 Kings 17:17; 2 Kings 17:31; 2 Kings 21:6; 2 Chronicles 33:6; 2 Kings 23:10; Isaiah 57:5; Jeremiah 7:31; Deuteronomy 12:31; Leviticus 20:2-5; Psalms 106:35-38; Jeremiah 32:35; Hosea 13:2; Ezekiel 20:26; Ezekiel 20:31; Ezekiel 23:37; Ezekiel 23:39; Jeremiah 19:4-5; Ezekiel 16:20-21; Ezekiel 16:36-37; Genesis 22:1-2; Hebrews 11:17; John 3:16; Romans 8:32

## 7334: circumcision (id: 7334)

The practice of cutting away the foreskin of male children, usually within a short time of birth. In the OT, the practice is seen as a sign of membership of the people of God. The NT makes it clear that Christians are under no obligation to be circumcised.

* **Associated Articles:** 7335: circumcision, physical (ID: `7335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7336: circumcision, spiritual (ID: `7336@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7335: circumcision, physical (id: 7335)

In the OT, circumcision is seen as an outward sign of membership of Israel, the people of God.

The significance of circumcision in God's covenant with Abraham
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10-Gen17:14)** *See also* [ACT 7:8](https://ref.ly/Acts7:8)

### Circumcision is also for Abraham's descendants

[GEN 17:7](https://ref.ly/Gen17:7)

Circumcision as a sign of national identity
-------------------------------------------

Dinah's brothers and the Shechemites: [GEN 34:8–34:9](https://ref.ly/Gen34:8-Gen34:9), [GEN 34:14–34:17](https://ref.ly/Gen34:14-Gen34:17); [EXO 4:24–4:26](https://ref.ly/Exod4:24-Exod4:26); ; [JOS 5:4–5:8](https://ref.ly/Josh5:4-Josh5:8)

Circumcision is integrated into the Mosaic law
----------------------------------------------

[LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3); ; [JHN 7:22](https://ref.ly/John7:22) *It was later sometimes forgotten that circumcision predated the Mosaic law.*

Circumcision as an important Passover restriction
-------------------------------------------------

[EXO 12:44](https://ref.ly/Exod12:44), [EXO 12:48–12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48-Exod12:49); ; [JDG 14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:3) *Some of Israel's neighbours practised circumcision, but not the Philistines.*

Circumcision was not necessarily a sign of consecration to God
--------------------------------------------------------------

[JER 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/Jer9:25-Jer9:26)

NT accounts of circumcision practised in obedience to the law
-------------------------------------------------------------

[LUK 1:59](https://ref.ly/Luke1:59) *John*; ; [LUK 2:21](https://ref.ly/Luke2:21) *Jesus Christ*; ; [JHN 7:23](https://ref.ly/John7:23) *Circumcision was permitted on the Sabbath*; ; [PHP 3:5](https://ref.ly/Phil3:5) *Paul refers to his own circumcision*; ; [ACT 16:3](https://ref.ly/Acts16:3) *Timothy*

The subject of circumcision debated by the first Christians
-----------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 10:45](https://ref.ly/Acts10:45), [ACT 11:2](https://ref.ly/Acts11:2), [ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5); ; [GAL 2:3](https://ref.ly/Gal2:3) *That Titus, a Greek, was not required to be circumcised illustrates the change in the attitude of the church concerning the relevance of the law to Gentile believers*; ; [EPH 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11); ; [COL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Col3:11); ; [TIT 1:10](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 17:10-14; Acts 7:8; Genesis 17:7; Genesis 34:8-9; Genesis 34:14-17; Exodus 4:24-26; Joshua 5:4-8; Leviticus 12:3; John 7:22; Exodus 12:44; Exodus 12:48-49; Judges 14:3; Jeremiah 9:25-26; Luke 1:59; Luke 2:21; John 7:23; Philippians 3:5; Acts 16:3; Acts 10:45; Acts 11:2; Acts 15:5; Galatians 2:3; Ephesians 2:11; Colossians 3:11; Titus 1:10

## 7336: circumcision, spiritual (id: 7336)

On its own, the physical sign of circumcision is no guarantee of finding favour in the sight of God. It was intended to be the outward sign of inward consecration and should be accompanied by repentance, faith and obedience. Paul stresses that circumcision confers no special privileges upon individuals; it is faith in God, rather than any outward sign, that ensures that believers stand in a right relationship to God.

Physical circumcision has no value without the obedience of the heart
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 30:6](https://ref.ly/Deut30:6)** *See also* [DEU 10:14–10:16](https://ref.ly/Deut10:14-Deut10:16); [JER 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jer4:4)**[ROM 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:28-Rom2:29)** *See also* [ROM 2:25–2:27](https://ref.ly/Rom2:25-Rom2:27); [1CO 7:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:19)

Circumcision and faith
----------------------

**[GAL 5:6](https://ref.ly/Gal5:6)** *See also* [ROM 3:30](https://ref.ly/Rom3:30)

### Abraham's righteousness depended on his faith, not on the covenant of circumcision

[GAL 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gal3:6); ; [GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6); ; [ROM 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Rom4:9-Rom4:12)

### Paul values the law as part of God's plan

[ROM 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Rom3:1-Rom3:2)

The sharp debate about circumcision for Christians
--------------------------------------------------

### The Council of Jerusalem

[ACT 15:1–15:19](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1-Acts15:19)

### Jewish Christians in Jerusalem criticise Peter for accepting hospitality from Cornelius and his (uncircumcised) friends

[ACT 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/Acts11:1-Acts11:3)

### God's acceptance of uncircumcised Gentiles is clear from the outpouring of the Holy Spirit upon them

**[ACT 10:44–10:46](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:46)** *See also* [ACT 10:34](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34)

### Peter's misgivings about Gentile believers and the law

[GAL 2:11–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:16) *the dispute between Paul and Peter*

Imposing circumcision upon Gentile Christians denies the freedom of the gospel
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1-Gal5:6)** *To insist on circumcision is to nullify the work of Jesus Christ on the cross.* *See also* [GAL 2:3–2:5](https://ref.ly/Gal2:3-Gal2:5), [GAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:14)

\`\`Judaisers'' should be resisted
----------------------------------

[GAL 5:7–5:12](https://ref.ly/Gal5:7-Gal5:12), [GAL 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12-Gal6:13), [GAL 6:15](https://ref.ly/Gal6:15); ; [PHP 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Phil3:2-Phil3:3); ; [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11)

### To be \`\`in Christ'' is to be spiritually circumcised

**[COL 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:11-Col2:12)** *See also* [COL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Col3:11)

Paul's confidence in Jesus Christ
---------------------------------

[PHP 3:4–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:9)

The relationship between baptism and circumcision in the NT
-----------------------------------------------------------

[COL 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Col2:11-Col2:13) *Both circumcision and baptism may each be described as a “seal”. Baptism signifies entry into the new covenant in the same way that circumcision marked initiation into the old*; ; [ROM 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rom4:11) *Abraham the father of all believers*; ; [2CO 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:21-2Cor1:22); ; [EPH 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13)

## 7340: clean and unclean (id: 7340)

The distinction between things which were ritually acceptable and unacceptable to God. Animals were classified as either clean or unclean, denoting their suitability or unsuitability for sacrifice and food. The motives and intentions of the heart were also sometimes judged to be clean or unclean, as uncleanness was often linked with sin.

The OT distinction between clean and unclean
--------------------------------------------

### The command to distinguish between clean and unclean

**[LEV 15:31](https://ref.ly/Lev15:31)** *See also* [LEV 10:8–10:11](https://ref.ly/Lev10:8-Lev10:11), [LEV 20:22–20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:22-Lev20:26); [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26), [EZK 44:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:23)

### The distinction between clean and unclean animals

**[GEN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Gen7:2)** *See also* [GEN 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Gen7:8-Gen7:9), [GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20); [LEV 11:1–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:47) parallel [DEU 14:3–14:20](https://ref.ly/Deut14:3-Deut14:20); [LEV 27:11–27:13](https://ref.ly/Lev27:11-Lev27:13)

### The NT attitude to clean and unclean animals

[MRK 7:14–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14-Mark7:23) parallel [MAT 15:10–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10-Matt15:20); ; [ACT 10:11–10:15](https://ref.ly/Acts10:11-Acts10:15), [ACT 11:5–11:9](https://ref.ly/Acts11:5-Acts11:9)

### Places and inanimate objects could be clean or unclean

**[LEV 10:14](https://ref.ly/Lev10:14)** *See also* [LEV 4:12](https://ref.ly/Lev4:12), [LEV 6:11](https://ref.ly/Lev6:11), [LEV 11:32–11:38](https://ref.ly/Lev11:32-Lev11:38), [LEV 14:33–14:57](https://ref.ly/Lev14:33-Lev14:57); [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11)

Causes of uncleanness
---------------------

### Physical contact with anything unclean

**[LEV 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Lev5:2-Lev5:3)** *See also* [LEV 7:19](https://ref.ly/Lev7:19); [HOS 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/Hos9:3-Hos9:4); [HAG 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Hag2:10-Hag2:14)

### Contact with corpses

**[NUM 19:11](https://ref.ly/Num19:11)** *See also* [LEV 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:4), [LEV 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:12); [NUM 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Num6:5-Num6:8)

### Infectious skin diseases

**[LEV 13:1–13:46](https://ref.ly/Lev13:1-Lev13:46)** *See also* [LEV 14:1–14:32](https://ref.ly/Lev14:1-Lev14:32); [MAT 8:2–8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:4) parallels [MRK 1:40–1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40-Mark1:44), [LUK 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12-Luke5:14)

### Certain bodily functions

**[LEV 12:2](https://ref.ly/Lev12:2)** *See also* [LEV 12:5](https://ref.ly/Lev12:5), [LEV 15:1–15:33](https://ref.ly/Lev15:1-Lev15:33)

Consequences of uncleanness
---------------------------

### Exclusion from worship

**[LEV 22:3–22:5](https://ref.ly/Lev22:3-Lev22:5)** *See also* [LEV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Lev12:4); [NUM 9:6–9:12](https://ref.ly/Num9:6-Num9:12); [1SA 20:24–20:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:24-1Sam20:26); [2CH 23:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:19), [2CH 30:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:17)

### Isolation from God's people

**[NUM 5:2](https://ref.ly/Num5:2)** *See also* [LEV 7:20–7:21](https://ref.ly/Lev7:20-Lev7:21); [DEU 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Deut23:10-Deut23:11)

--------------------------

**[LEV 16:29–16:30](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29-Lev16:30)** *See also* [LEV 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:6-Lev12:8), [LEV 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/Lev16:15-Lev16:16), [LEV 22:4–22:7](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4-Lev22:7); [NUM 6:9–6:12](https://ref.ly/Num6:9-Num6:12), [NUM 19:1–19:22](https://ref.ly/Num19:1-Num19:22), [NUM 31:21–31:24](https://ref.ly/Num31:21-Num31:24); [HEB 9:13](https://ref.ly/Heb9:13)

## 7342: cleanliness (id: 7342)

Careful, fastidious attention to personal hygiene, particularly important in the climate of the Near East. In Scripture, sin is associated with lack of cleanliness, whereas redemption and baptism are linked with washing.

Cleanliness through bathing
---------------------------

### As part of general personal hygiene

**[RUT 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:1-Ruth3:3)** *See also* [JHN 13:10](https://ref.ly/John13:10)

### For cleansing from uncleanness

**[LEV 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15-Lev17:16)** *See also* [LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9), [LEV 15:13](https://ref.ly/Lev15:13); [2KI 5:9–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:9-2Kgs5:14)

Cleanliness through washing feet
--------------------------------

### A customary practice after a journey

**[GEN 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:2)** *See also* [GEN 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1-Gen18:5), [GEN 24:32](https://ref.ly/Gen24:32), [GEN 43:24](https://ref.ly/Gen43:24); [1SA 25:40–25:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:40-1Sam25:42); [2SA 11:6–11:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:6-2Sam11:8); [LUK 7:36–7:47](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36-Luke7:47)

### As a sign of humility and love

**[JHN 13:2–13:17](https://ref.ly/John13:2-John13:17)** *See also* [1TI 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:9-1Tim5:10)

**[ISA 7:3](https://ref.ly/Isa7:3)** *See also* [2KI 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:17) parallel [ISA 36:2](https://ref.ly/Isa36:2)

### For cleansing from uncleanness

**[LEV 11:40](https://ref.ly/Lev11:40)** *See also* [EXO 19:10–19:11](https://ref.ly/Exod19:10-Exod19:11), [EXO 19:14–19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:14-Exod19:15); [LEV 13:6](https://ref.ly/Lev13:6), [LEV 16:26](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26); [NUM 8:7](https://ref.ly/Num8:7), [NUM 19:21](https://ref.ly/Num19:21)

## 7344: curtain (id: 7344)

Mentioned chiefly in relation to the construction of the tabernacle. The curtain which separated off the Most Holy Place symbolised the separation between God and humanity. The tearing of the curtain when Jesus Christ was crucified symbolised the end of this separation and gave access into God's presence for believers.

Curtains for the construction of the tabernacle
-----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 26:1–26:6](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1-Exod26:6)** *See also* [EXO 36:8–36:13](https://ref.ly/Exod36:8-Exod36:13)**[EXO 26:7–26:13](https://ref.ly/Exod26:7-Exod26:13)** *See also* [EXO 36:14–36:18](https://ref.ly/Exod36:14-Exod36:18)**[EXO 26:36–26:37](https://ref.ly/Exod26:36-Exod26:37)** *See also* [EXO 36:37–36:38](https://ref.ly/Exod36:37-Exod36:38)**[EXO 27:9–27:18](https://ref.ly/Exod27:9-Exod27:18)** *See also* [EXO 38:9–38:19](https://ref.ly/Exod38:9-Exod38:19), [EXO 35:10–35:17](https://ref.ly/Exod35:10-Exod35:17) parallel [EXO 39:33–39:40](https://ref.ly/Exod39:33-Exod39:40); [NUM 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/Num3:25-Num3:26)

**[HEB 9:2–9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:2-Heb9:4)** *See also* [EXO 26:31–26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:31-Exod26:33), [EXO 40:2–40:3](https://ref.ly/Exod40:2-Exod40:3), [EXO 40:21](https://ref.ly/Exod40:21); [NUM 4:5](https://ref.ly/Num4:5); [2CH 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:14) *As in the tabernacle, the Most Holy Place in Solomon's temple was shut off with a curtain.*

### The curtain symbolises separation from God

**[LEV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2)** *See also* [NUM 18:7](https://ref.ly/Num18:7); [HEB 9:6–9:9](https://ref.ly/Heb9:6-Heb9:9)

### The curtain indicates the hiddenness of God

**[PSA 18:11](https://ref.ly/Ps18:11)** parallel [2SA 22:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:12) *See also* [EXO 20:21](https://ref.ly/Exod20:21); [DEU 4:11](https://ref.ly/Deut4:11); [JOB 22:14](https://ref.ly/Job22:14); [PSA 97:2](https://ref.ly/Ps97:2); [1TI 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:16)

## 7346: death penalty (id: 7346)

The lawful taking of human life by the civil or military authorities. The death penalty was inflicted by all nations in the ancient world. In Israel, the main method was stoning, amongst other nations, other forms such as hanging, beheading and crucifixion were used.

The ultimate authority for the death penalty lies with God alone
----------------------------------------------------------------

### Examples of God taking human life

**[EXO 12:29](https://ref.ly/Exod12:29)** *See also* [NUM 16:35](https://ref.ly/Num16:35); [1SA 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:19), [1SA 25:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:38); [2SA 6:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:7) parallel [1CH 13:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:10); [ACT 12:23](https://ref.ly/Acts12:23)

### God may delegate his authority to human beings

## 7348: defilement (id: 7348)

The result of making oneself or someone else unclean in God's sight. Outward, physical defilement comes from contact with someone or something unclean, whereas sin produces inner, spiritual defilement.

Defilement through contact with anything unclean
------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 20:25](https://ref.ly/Lev20:25)** *See also* [LEV 7:19–7:21](https://ref.ly/Lev7:19-Lev7:21), [LEV 11:41–11:45](https://ref.ly/Lev11:41-Lev11:45), [LEV 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:4), [LEV 22:4–22:5](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4-Lev22:5); [NUM 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Num5:1-Num5:3), [NUM 6:9](https://ref.ly/Num6:9), [NUM 6:12](https://ref.ly/Num6:12); [EZK 4:9–4:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:9-Ezek4:15), [EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25); [DAN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Dan1:8); [HAG 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Hag2:10-Hag2:14); [MAL 1:7](https://ref.ly/Mal1:7)

Defilement through bodily emissions
-----------------------------------

[LEV 12:2–12:7](https://ref.ly/Lev12:2-Lev12:7), [LEV 15:1–15:17](https://ref.ly/Lev15:1-Lev15:17)

Defilement by sin
-----------------

**[EZK 14:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:11)** *See also* [LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31); [PSA 106:34–106:39](https://ref.ly/Ps106:34-Ps106:39); [JER 2:23](https://ref.ly/Jer2:23); [LAM 4:14](https://ref.ly/Lam4:14); [EZK 20:1–20:44](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:1-Ezek20:44), [EZK 22:1–22:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:1-Ezek22:4), [EZK 23:28–23:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:28-Ezek23:30), [EZK 37:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:23)

Defilement as a result of illicit sexual relations
--------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 18:20](https://ref.ly/Lev18:20)** *See also* [GEN 34:1–34:5](https://ref.ly/Gen34:1-Gen34:5) *rape*; [GEN 34:27](https://ref.ly/Gen34:27), [GEN 49:3–49:4](https://ref.ly/Gen49:3-Gen49:4); [LEV 18:23–18:30](https://ref.ly/Lev18:23-Lev18:30) *bestiality*[LEV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Lev21:7), [LEV 21:9](https://ref.ly/Lev21:9), [LEV 21:13–21:15](https://ref.ly/Lev21:13-Lev21:15)[NUM 5:19–5:22](https://ref.ly/Num5:19-Num5:22), [NUM 5:27–5:30](https://ref.ly/Num5:27-Num5:30); [DEU 24:1–24:4](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1-Deut24:4); [1CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1); [EZK 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:6), [EZK 22:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:11) *incest*; [EZK 23:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:7), [EZK 23:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:17), [EZK 33:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:26) *adultery*; [REV 14:4](https://ref.ly/Rev14:4)

Defilement of God's house
-------------------------

**[LEV 15:31](https://ref.ly/Lev15:31)** [NUM 19:13](https://ref.ly/Num19:13), [NUM 19:20](https://ref.ly/Num19:20); [2CH 29:4–29:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:4-2Chr29:5), [2CH 36:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:14); ; [PSA 74:4–74:7](https://ref.ly/Ps74:4-Ps74:7), [PSA 79:1](https://ref.ly/Ps79:1); [JER 7:30](https://ref.ly/Jer7:30), [JER 32:32–32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:32-Jer32:35); [EZK 5:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:11), [EZK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:7), [EZK 23:38](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:38); ; [ACT 21:27–21:28](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:28) *Paul was falsely accused of bringing Gentiles into the temple area.*

------------------------

**[EZK 43:7–43:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:7-Ezek43:8)**

----------------------------------

**[EXO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Exod20:25)** *See also* [NUM 18:30–18:32](https://ref.ly/Num18:30-Num18:32); [2KI 23:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:16), [2KI 23:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:19)

## 7350: drink offering (id: 7350)

A sacrificial offering of wine poured out at the foot of the altar, to accompany a burnt, fellowship or grain offering.

The drink offering was an ancient custom
----------------------------------------

**[GEN 35:14](https://ref.ly/Gen35:14)**

Drink offerings were proportionate to the size of animal sacrificed
-------------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [NUM 15:5](https://ref.ly/Num15:5), [NUM 15:7](https://ref.ly/Num15:7), [NUM 15:10](https://ref.ly/Num15:10)

Drink offerings were required on feasts and sacred days
-------------------------------------------------------

As part of the daily offerings: [EXO 29:41](https://ref.ly/Exod29:41); ; [NUM 28:7–28:8](https://ref.ly/Num28:7-Num28:8); [LEV 23:13](https://ref.ly/Lev23:13) *Firstfruits*; [LEV 23:18](https://ref.ly/Lev23:18); ; [NUM 28:31](https://ref.ly/Num28:31); [LEV 23:37](https://ref.ly/Lev23:37); ; [NUM 29:30–29:31](https://ref.ly/Num29:30-Num29:31); [NUM 28:24](https://ref.ly/Num28:24) *Passover*; ; [NUM 28:9–28:10](https://ref.ly/Num28:9-Num28:10) *Sabbath offerings*; ; [NUM 28:14](https://ref.ly/Num28:14) *monthly offerings*; ; [NUM 29:6](https://ref.ly/Num29:6) *Feast of Trumpets*; ; [NUM 29:11](https://ref.ly/Num29:11) *Day of Atonement*

Occasions for making a drink offering
-------------------------------------

### To accompany sacrifices for unintentional sins

[NUM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Num15:24)

### To mark special occasions

[NUM 6:17](https://ref.ly/Num6:17) *after taking a Nazirite vow*; ; [1CH 29:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:21) *at the accession of a king*; ; [2CH 29:35](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:35) *during a religious reformation*; ; [EZK 45:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:17) *in Ezekiel's vision of restored worship*

God's displeasure when drink offerings were poured out to idols
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:18)** *See also* [ISA 57:6](https://ref.ly/Isa57:6); [JER 19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:13), [JER 32:29](https://ref.ly/Jer32:29), [JER 44:17–44:19](https://ref.ly/Jer44:17-Jer44:19), [JER 44:25](https://ref.ly/Jer44:25); [EZK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:28)

Drink offerings as a symbol
---------------------------

### Of restoration after a locust plague

[JOL 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Joel2:13-Joel2:14)

### Of devotion and sacrifice

**[PHP 2:17](https://ref.ly/Phil2:17)** *See also* [2SA 23:15–23:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:15-2Sam23:17); [2TI 4:6–4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:6-2Tim4:8)

## 7352: ephod (id: 7352)

------------------------------------------

## 7354: feasts and festivals (id: 7354)

The OT makes reference to a number of feasts and festivals, which generally commemorate an event in Israel's history (e.g., the Feast of Passover) or some season of the year (e.g., the Feast of Weeks). In the NT, some of these festivals are given new meanings, such as Pentecost, which was originally a harvest festival, but which came to celebrate the coming of the Holy Spirit.

* **Associated Articles:** 7355: feasts and festivals, nature of (ID: `7355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7356: Feast of Dedication (ID: `7356@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7357: Feast of Firstfruits (ID: `7357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7358: Feast of Tabernacles (ID: `7358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7359: Feast of Trumpets (ID: `7359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7360: Feast of Unleavened Bread (ID: `7360@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7361: Feast of Weeks (ID: `7361@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7355: feasts and festivals, nature of (id: 7355)

Israel's feasts and festivals were to be times of religious and community celebration. Ordained by God, they were to be the outward expression of a right spirital attitude.

Feasts and festivals were appointed by God
------------------------------------------

**[EXO 5:1](https://ref.ly/Exod5:1)** *See also* [EXO 10:9](https://ref.ly/Exod10:9); [HOS 9:5](https://ref.ly/Hos9:5)

Israel's three annual festivals
-------------------------------

### The three pilgrim festivals

**[EXO 23:14–23:19](https://ref.ly/Exod23:14-Exod23:19)**; **[2CH 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:12-2Chr8:13)** [EXO 12:14](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14); ; [NUM 28:17](https://ref.ly/Num28:17); [LEV 23:15–23:21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:21); ; [NUM 28:26](https://ref.ly/Num28:26); [LEV 23:39–23:41](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39-Lev23:41); ; [NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12); ; [JDG 21:19](https://ref.ly/Judg21:19); ; [1KI 8:65](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:65) parallel [2CH 7:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:8)

### They were a joyful response to God's blessings

**[ZEC 8:19](https://ref.ly/Zech8:19)** *Contrasting with fasts.* *See also* [NAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Nah1:15)

### They were celebrated with sacrifices and offerings

[NUM 29:39](https://ref.ly/Num29:39); ; [EZK 46:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:11)

Israel's other festivals
------------------------

New Moon festivals: [NUM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Num10:10); ; [1SA 20:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:18); ; [1CH 23:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:31); ; [HOS 2:11](https://ref.ly/Hos2:11); [EXO 32:5](https://ref.ly/Exod32:5); ; [1KI 12:32–12:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:32-1Kgs12:33)

Festivals should be the outward expression of a right spiritual attitude
------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ISA 1:14](https://ref.ly/Isa1:14), [ISA 29:1](https://ref.ly/Isa29:1); ; [HOS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Hos5:7); ; [MAL 2:3](https://ref.ly/Mal2:3); ; [1CO 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:8)

OT festivals find their fulfilment in Jesus Christ
--------------------------------------------------

[COL 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:16-Col2:17)

## 7356: Feast of Dedication (id: 7356)

This feast began on 25th Kislev (November/December) and lasted seven days. It celebrated the cleansing of the temple and altar by Judas Maccabeus after they had been defiled by Antiochus Epiphanes.

Jesus Christ was challenged to declare his messiahship at the Feast of Dedication
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[JHN 10:22–10:24](https://ref.ly/John10:22-John10:24)

* **Associated Passages:** John 10:22-24


## 7357: Feast of Firstfruits (id: 7357)

The date of the offering of firstfruits, a celebration of God's gift of the harvest, is not clear; it was probably held on 16th Abib.

Offerings made at the celebration of the firstfruits
----------------------------------------------------

### A burnt offering and a grain offering

[LEV 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Lev23:12-Lev23:13)

### Other offerings

[LEV 23:19–23:20](https://ref.ly/Lev23:19-Lev23:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 23:12-13; Leviticus 23:19-20
* **Associated Articles:** 4442: firstfruits (ID: `4442@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7322: burnt offering (ID: `7322@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7368: grain offering (ID: `7368@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7480: wave offering (ID: `7480@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7358: Feast of Tabernacles (id: 7358)

This feast began on 15th Tishri (September/October) and lasted seven days. It celebrated the first gathering of the fruit and grain harvests and also God's provision for his people during their wilderness journey from Egypt to the promised land.

Instructions for celebrating the Feast of Tabernacles
-----------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)** *See also* [EXO 23:14](https://ref.ly/Exod23:14), [EXO 34:22–34:23](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22-Exod34:23); [LEV 23:33–23:34](https://ref.ly/Lev23:33-Lev23:34), [LEV 23:39](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39); [NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12); [DEU 16:13](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13), [DEU 16:14](https://ref.ly/Deut16:14), [DEU 16:16](https://ref.ly/Deut16:16)

People lived in booths for the duration of the Feast of Tabernacles
-------------------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 23:40](https://ref.ly/Lev23:40), [LEV 23:42–23:43](https://ref.ly/Lev23:42-Lev23:43)

The offerings to be made on each day of the Feast of Tabernacles
----------------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 23:37](https://ref.ly/Lev23:37); ; [NUM 29:13–29:38](https://ref.ly/Num29:13-Num29:38)

The Feast of Tabernacles was celebrated throughout Israel's history
-------------------------------------------------------------------

[2CH 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:12-2Chr8:13) *under Solomon*; [EZR 3:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:4); ; [NEH 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Neh8:17-Neh8:18); [JHN 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/John7:2-John7:3), [JHN 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/John7:37-John7:38)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 23:16; Exodus 23:14; Exodus 34:22-23; Leviticus 23:33-34; Leviticus 23:39; Numbers 29:12; Deuteronomy 16:13; Deuteronomy 16:14; Deuteronomy 16:16; Leviticus 23:40; Leviticus 23:42-43; Leviticus 23:37; Numbers 29:13-38; 2 Chronicles 8:12-13; Ezra 3:4; Nehemiah 8:17-18; John 7:2-3; John 7:37-38
* **Associated Articles:** 7322: burnt offering (ID: `7322@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7368: grain offering (ID: `7368@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7444: sin offering (ID: `7444@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7359: Feast of Trumpets (id: 7359)

This feast, which is not specifically mentioned as such in the OT, took place at the observance of the new moon in the month of Tishri (September/October). It signalled the end of the agricultural year.

It was to be a day of rest, commemorated with the sounding of trumpets
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 23:24–23:25](https://ref.ly/Lev23:24-Lev23:25); ; [NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1)

Offerings were made to God
--------------------------

[LEV 23:25](https://ref.ly/Lev23:25); ; [NUM 29:2–29:5](https://ref.ly/Num29:2-Num29:5)

Celebrated after the exile
--------------------------

[NEH 8:2–8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2-Neh8:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 23:24-25; Numbers 29:1; Leviticus 23:25; Numbers 29:2-5; Nehemiah 8:2-6
* **Associated Articles:** 4406: agriculture (ID: `4406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5056: rest (ID: `5056@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7360: Feast of Unleavened Bread (id: 7360)

The feast was kept from 15th\-21st Abib (March/April), to commemorate the haste with which the Israelites left Egypt. It was always celebrated in the calendar of feasts with the Feast of Passover.

Celebrating the Feast of Unleavened Bread
-----------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:15–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:15-Exod12:20)** *See also* [EXO 12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:39), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15); [LEV 23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:6); [NUM 28:17](https://ref.ly/Num28:17); [DEU 16:3](https://ref.ly/Deut16:3)

All use of yeast was forbidden during the Feast of Unleavened Bread
-------------------------------------------------------------------

### All leaven was to be removed from the home before the feast began

[EXO 12:19](https://ref.ly/Exod12:19), [EXO 13:7](https://ref.ly/Exod13:7); ; [DEU 16:4](https://ref.ly/Deut16:4)

### No yeast was to be used in the preparation of food

[EXO 12:18–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:18-Exod12:20), [EXO 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Exod13:6-Exod13:7), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15)

Sacrifices made at the Feast of Unleavened Bread
------------------------------------------------

[LEV 23:8](https://ref.ly/Lev23:8); ; [NUM 28:19–28:24](https://ref.ly/Num28:19-Num28:24)

The Feast of Unleavened Bread in NT times
-----------------------------------------

[MRK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1), [MRK 14:12](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12); ; [ACT 12:3](https://ref.ly/Acts12:3); ; [1CO 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:6-1Cor5:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 12:15-20; Exodus 12:39; Exodus 23:15; Leviticus 23:6; Numbers 28:17; Deuteronomy 16:3; Exodus 12:19; Exodus 13:7; Deuteronomy 16:4; Exodus 12:18-20; Exodus 13:6-7; Leviticus 23:8; Numbers 28:19-24; Mark 14:1; Mark 14:12; Acts 12:3; 1 Corinthians 5:6-8
* **Associated Articles:** 4404: food (ID: `4404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4554: yeast (ID: `4554@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7222: exodus, events of (ID: `7222@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7406: Passover (ID: `7406@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7361: Feast of Weeks (id: 7361)

This feast, which took place seven weeks after Passover in Sivan (May/June), was also known as the Feast of Pentecost. It expressed joy and thankfulness to God for the harvest.

The Feast of Weeks was one of three compulsory feasts
-----------------------------------------------------

[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16), [EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22)

Offerings made at the Feast of Weeks
------------------------------------

### The firstfruits of the wheat harvest were offered to God

[NUM 28:26](https://ref.ly/Num28:26); ; [DEU 16:10](https://ref.ly/Deut16:10) *The offerings brought were to be in proportion to the harvest.*

### Two loaves of bread were also offered

[LEV 23:17](https://ref.ly/Lev23:17)

### Burnt offerings and sin offerings were made

[LEV 23:18–23:20](https://ref.ly/Lev23:18-Lev23:20) parallel [NUM 28:27–28:30](https://ref.ly/Num28:27-Num28:30)

The Feast of Weeks (Pentecost) in the NT
----------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:4)** *The term “Pentecost” refers to the 50 days that separate this feast from the Passover Sabbath.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 23:16; Exodus 34:22; Numbers 28:26; Deuteronomy 16:10; Leviticus 23:17; Leviticus 23:18-20; Numbers 28:27-30; Acts 2:1-4
* **Associated Articles:** 4442: firstfruits (ID: `4442@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7322: burnt offering (ID: `7322@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7408: Pentecost (ID: `7408@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7444: sin offering (ID: `7444@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8352: thankfulness (ID: `8352@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7364: fellowship offering (id: 7364)

Sacrificial meals shared by offerer, people and priests. The name of these offerings is related to the Hebrew “shalom”, meaning “wholeness” or “peace”, and they are therefore also known as “peace offerings”.

Fellowship offerings were required by God
-----------------------------------------

*See also* [EXO 20:24](https://ref.ly/Exod20:24)

### Their purpose was thanksgiving, vow fulfilment or freewill offering

[LEV 7:11–7:18](https://ref.ly/Lev7:11-Lev7:18)

Regulations for making a fellowship offering
--------------------------------------------

### The animal

[LEV 3:1](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1), [LEV 3:6](https://ref.ly/Lev3:6), [LEV 3:12](https://ref.ly/Lev3:12)

### Its slaughter

[LEV 3:2](https://ref.ly/Lev3:2), [LEV 3:8](https://ref.ly/Lev3:8), [LEV 3:13](https://ref.ly/Lev3:13), [LEV 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/Lev17:5-Lev17:6)

### All requirements must be fulfilled for the offering to be acceptable

[LEV 19:5](https://ref.ly/Lev19:5), [LEV 22:21](https://ref.ly/Lev22:21); ; [JOS 22:29](https://ref.ly/Josh22:29); [AMO 5:22](https://ref.ly/Amos5:22), [AMO 5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:24)

The fellowship offering was shared
----------------------------------

### God received the best

[LEV 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Lev3:3-Lev3:5), [LEV 3:9–3:11](https://ref.ly/Lev3:9-Lev3:11), [LEV 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Lev3:14-Lev3:16)

### The priests received their share as food

[LEV 7:29–7:34](https://ref.ly/Lev7:29-Lev7:34)

### The people ate the rest

[LEV 10:14](https://ref.ly/Lev10:14)

Fellowship offerings affirmed the covenant relationship
-------------------------------------------------------

The Sinai covenant with Israel: [EXO 20:24](https://ref.ly/Exod20:24), [EXO 24:4–24:6](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4-Exod24:6); ; [JOS 8:31–8:32](https://ref.ly/Josh8:31-Josh8:32); [1SA 10:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:8), [1SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:15); ; [1KI 9:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:25)

The celebration of fellowship offerings
---------------------------------------

### At seasonal festivals

[LEV 23:19](https://ref.ly/Lev23:19) *the Feast of Weeks*; ; [NUM 29:39](https://ref.ly/Num29:39) *the Feast of Tabernacles*

### On other religious and national occasions

At the completion of the time of separation of a Nazirite: [NUM 6:14](https://ref.ly/Num6:14), [NUM 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/Num6:17-Num6:18); [NUM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Num10:10) *at New Moons and other festivals*; [NUM 7:17](https://ref.ly/Num7:17), [NUM 7:23](https://ref.ly/Num7:23), [NUM 7:29](https://ref.ly/Num7:29); [NUM 15:8](https://ref.ly/Num15:8); ; [DEU 27:7](https://ref.ly/Deut27:7); [2SA 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:17-2Sam6:18) *on bringing the ark to Jerusalem*; ; [2SA 24:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:25) *on locating the site for the temple*; [1KI 8:63–8:64](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:63-1Kgs8:64) parallel [2CH 7:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:7) *at the dedication of the temple*; [2CH 29:35](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:35), [2CH 30:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:22), [2CH 31:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:2), [2CH 33:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:16); [PRO 7:14](https://ref.ly/Prov7:14); [EZK 45:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:15), [EZK 45:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:17), [EZK 46:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:2), [EZK 46:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:12)

Fellowship offerings in idolatrous worship
------------------------------------------

[EXO 32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:6)

## 7366: freewill offering (id: 7366)

An offering that in some ways resembled the thank\-offering or peace offering. It was completely voluntary, prompted by an occasion of celebration or spiritual significance. The animal offered was eaten at a festive banquet.

Instructions for presenting a freewill offering
-----------------------------------------------

### The animal to be offered

[LEV 22:17–22:22](https://ref.ly/Lev22:17-Lev22:22), [LEV 22:23](https://ref.ly/Lev22:23) *Some slightly deformed animals were perhaps acceptable because the freewill offering was voluntary.*

### Eating the offering

[LEV 7:16](https://ref.ly/Lev7:16)

Freewill offerings were associated with joy and celebration
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 54:6–54:7](https://ref.ly/Ps54:6-Ps54:7)** *See also* [DEU 16:9–16:12](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:12) *during the Feast of Weeks*

Occasions for making freewill offerings
---------------------------------------

### In maintaining public worship and at times of religious renewal

**[EXO 35:20–35:29](https://ref.ly/Exod35:20-Exod35:29)** *Building the temple.* *See also* [2CH 31:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:14) *Hezekiah's reformation*[EZR 1:4–1:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:4-Ezra1:6), [EZR 2:68](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:68), [EZR 7:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:16), [EZR 8:28](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:28); [NEH 7:70–7:72](https://ref.ly/Neh7:70-Neh7:72)[EZK 46:11–46:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:11-Ezek46:12) *Ezekiel's visionary ideal temple*

### As an additonal offering at certain festivals

[NUM 29:39](https://ref.ly/Num29:39); [DEU 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Deut12:5-Deut12:6), [DEU 12:17–12:18](https://ref.ly/Deut12:17-Deut12:18)

Prophetic criticism of bragging over freewill offerings
-------------------------------------------------------

**[AMO 4:5](https://ref.ly/Amos4:5)** *A sarcastic attack on the offering of freewill gifts as a way to gain prestige.*

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 22:17-22; Leviticus 22:23; Leviticus 7:16; Psalms 54:6-7; Deuteronomy 16:9-12; Exodus 35:20-29; 2 Chronicles 31:14; Ezra 1:4-6; Ezra 2:68; Ezra 7:16; Ezra 8:28; Nehemiah 7:70-72; Ezekiel 46:11-12; Numbers 29:39; Deuteronomy 12:5-6; Deuteronomy 12:17-18; Amos 4:5
* **Associated Articles:** 4605: animals, religious role of (ID: `4605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7364: fellowship offering (ID: `7364@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7476: thank-offering (ID: `7476@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8488: tithing (ID: `8488@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8676: thanksgiving (ID: `8676@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7368: grain offering (id: 7368)

An offering that was usually offered as an accompaniment to animal sacrifices, but in special circumstances was presented alone. It could be cooked in various ways or presented uncooked. The offering could be made for a variety of purposes, depending on the sacrifice it accompanied.

The Hebrew word for `grain offering'' can also mean simply`gift''
-----------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [GEN 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4), [GEN 32:13](https://ref.ly/Gen32:13); [1SA 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:17); [1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21); [MRK 7:11](https://ref.ly/Mark7:11)

The ingredients of the grain offering
-------------------------------------

### Flour, oil and incense

**[LEV 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:1-Lev2:2)**

### Salt

*See also* [LEV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lev2:13) *Salt, which prevented corruption, was offered with all the offerings.*

### No yeast or honey

[LEV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lev2:11)

### Roasted ears of corn

[LEV 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Lev2:14-Lev2:16)

The preparation of the grain offering
-------------------------------------

[LEV 2:4](https://ref.ly/Lev2:4) *oven\-baked like bread*; ; [LEV 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Lev2:5-Lev2:6) *on a griddle or in a pan*

The presentation of the grain offering
--------------------------------------

### By fire

**[LEV 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:2)** *See also* [LEV 2:3](https://ref.ly/Lev2:3) *It might be eaten, but only by the priests and in the sanctuary.*

### Accompanying vow fulfilment or freewill offerings

[NUM 15:1–15:10](https://ref.ly/Num15:1-Num15:10)

### With an animal sacrifice

**[NUM 15:6](https://ref.ly/Num15:6)** *See also* [NUM 15:8–15:9](https://ref.ly/Num15:8-Num15:9) *the larger the animal, the larger the grain offering*

### With the two daily offerings

[EXO 29:38–29:41](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:41)

### Circumstances under which the grain offering was presented alone

[LEV 6:19–6:23](https://ref.ly/Lev6:19-Lev6:23) *when a priest was consecrated*; [NUM 5:15](https://ref.ly/Num5:15), [NUM 5:25](https://ref.ly/Num5:25)

The character of the grain offering
-----------------------------------

### It is most holy

**[LEV 6:17](https://ref.ly/Lev6:17)**

### It is an aroma pleasing to the LORD

*See also* [LEV 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:2), [LEV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Lev2:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 4:4; Genesis 32:13; 1 Samuel 2:17; 1 Kings 4:21; Mark 7:11; Leviticus 2:1-2; Leviticus 2:13; Leviticus 2:11; Leviticus 2:14-16; Leviticus 2:4; Leviticus 2:5-6; Leviticus 2:2; Leviticus 2:3; Numbers 15:1-10; Numbers 15:6; Numbers 15:8-9; Exodus 29:38-41; Leviticus 6:19-23; Numbers 5:15; Numbers 5:25; Leviticus 6:17; Leviticus 2:9
* **Associated Articles:** 4826: fire (ID: `4826@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7766: priests (ID: `7766@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7370: guilt offering (id: 7370)

An offering that resembled the sin offering, but was offered especially in cases where restitution could be made for an unintentional sin.

Circumstances for making the guilt offering
-------------------------------------------

### Unintentional sin against God

**[LEV 5:17](https://ref.ly/Lev5:17)** *See also* [LEV 5:15](https://ref.ly/Lev5:15)

### Sin against a neighbour where restitution can be made

[LEV 5:16](https://ref.ly/Lev5:16) *Where restitution is possible it is a requirement*; ; [LEV 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:5) *A surcharge of twenty per cent was to be added*; ; [NUM 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/Num5:5-Num5:8) *Restitution must be made to God if the wronged person is not accessible.*

The nature of the guilt offering
--------------------------------

### A ram without defect

**[LEV 5:18](https://ref.ly/Lev5:18)**

### Regulations governing the offering

*See also* [LEV 7:1–7:6](https://ref.ly/Lev7:1-Lev7:6); [NUM 18:9](https://ref.ly/Num18:9)

Other situations requiring a guilt offering
-------------------------------------------

[LEV 14:12–14:14](https://ref.ly/Lev14:12-Lev14:14) *cleansing from infectious diseases*; ; [LEV 19:20–19:22](https://ref.ly/Lev19:20-Lev19:22) *wronging a man by intercourse with a slave girl promised to him*; ; [NUM 6:12](https://ref.ly/Num6:12) *cleansing a Nazirite after contact with a dead body*; ; [EZR 10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:19) *for returning exiles who had intermarried*

Guilt offerings in Ezekiel's vision of the temple
-------------------------------------------------

[EZK 40:38–40:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:38-Ezek40:39), [EZK 42:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek42:13), [EZK 44:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:29), [EZK 46:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:20)

The death of the \`\`suffering servant'' is interpreted as a guilt offering
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 53:10](https://ref.ly/Isa53:10)**

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 5:17; Leviticus 5:15; Leviticus 5:16; Leviticus 6:1-5; Numbers 5:5-8; Leviticus 5:18; Leviticus 7:1-6; Numbers 18:9; Leviticus 14:12-14; Leviticus 19:20-22; Numbers 6:12; Ezra 10:19; Ezekiel 40:38-39; Ezekiel 42:13; Ezekiel 44:29; Ezekiel 46:20; Isaiah 53:10
* **Associated Articles:** 2327: Jesus Christ, as servant (ID: `2327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5492: restitution (ID: `5492@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6027: sin, God's remedy for (ID: `6027@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6175: guilt, removal of (ID: `6175@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6682: mediation (ID: `6682@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7160: servants of the Lord (ID: `7160@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7444: sin offering (ID: `7444@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7372: hands, laying on of (id: 7372)

An action of God or human beings, often understood to convey healing, blessing or the gift of the Holy Spirit. It also indicates the conferring of authority upon individuals for the purpose of ministry.

Laying on of hands and the communication of gifts
-------------------------------------------------

### Laying on of hands communicates God's blessing

**[MRK 10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:16)** parallel [MAT 19:15](https://ref.ly/Matt19:15) *See also* [GEN 48:14–48:20](https://ref.ly/Gen48:14-Gen48:20)

### Laying on of hands conveys God's healing

**[LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40)** *See also* [MAT 8:3](https://ref.ly/Matt8:3) parallels [MRK 1:41](https://ref.ly/Mark1:41), [LUK 5:13](https://ref.ly/Luke5:13) *the man with leprosy*; [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18) parallel [MRK 5:23](https://ref.ly/Mark5:23) *Jairus' daughter*; [MAT 9:29–9:30](https://ref.ly/Matt9:29-Matt9:30); [MRK 6:5](https://ref.ly/Mark6:5), [MRK 7:32–7:35](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32-Mark7:35), [MRK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Mark16:18); [LUK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Luke13:13) *the crippled woman*; [ACT 28:8](https://ref.ly/Acts28:8); [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)

### Laying on of hands conveys the gift of the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 8:17](https://ref.ly/Acts8:17)** *See also* [DEU 34:9](https://ref.ly/Deut34:9); [ACT 8:19](https://ref.ly/Acts8:19), [ACT 9:17](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17), [ACT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Acts19:6)

### Laying on of hands conveys reassurance

**[REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17)** *See also* [MAT 17:7](https://ref.ly/Matt17:7)

Laying on of hands in commissioning for service
-----------------------------------------------

**[NUM 27:18–27:23](https://ref.ly/Num27:18-Num27:23)** *See also* [NUM 8:10](https://ref.ly/Num8:10); [DEU 34:9](https://ref.ly/Deut34:9); [ACT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:6), [ACT 13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:3); [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14), [1TI 5:22](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:22); [2TI 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:6)

-----------------------------------------------

**[LEV 1:4](https://ref.ly/Lev1:4)** *See also* [EXO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Exod29:15), [EXO 29:19](https://ref.ly/Exod29:19); [LEV 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1-Lev3:2), [LEV 8:18](https://ref.ly/Lev8:18), [LEV 8:22](https://ref.ly/Lev8:22)

Laying on of hands without reference to God
-------------------------------------------

### Laying hands on people with intent to harm

[GEN 37:27](https://ref.ly/Gen37:27); ; [JOS 2:19](https://ref.ly/Josh2:19); ; [1SA 23:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:7), [1SA 26:9–26:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:9-1Sam26:11), [1SA 26:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:23); ; [ISA 11:14](https://ref.ly/Isa11:14); ; [LUK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12)

### Laying on of hands in punishment

[LEV 24:14](https://ref.ly/Lev24:14); ; [NEH 13:21](https://ref.ly/Neh13:21)

-----------------------------------------------

### God's hand brings favour

**[NEH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Neh2:8)** *See also* [EZR 7:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:6), [EZR 7:28](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:28), [EZR 8:18](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:18); [NEH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Neh2:18)

### God's hand brings protection

**[EZR 8:31](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:31)** *See also* [EZR 7:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:9)

### God's hand brings prophetic revelation

**[EZK 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:3)** *See also* [EZK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:14), [EZK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:22), [EZK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:1), [EZK 33:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:22), [EZK 37:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:1), [EZK 40:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:1)

### God's hand brings judgment

**[EXO 7:4](https://ref.ly/Exod7:4)** *See also* [1SA 5:6–5:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:6-1Sam5:12); [JER 15:6](https://ref.ly/Jer15:6); [EZK 39:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:21); [ACT 13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:11)

## 7374: high places (id: 7374)

Natural heights were sometimes the sites of shrines devoted to the LORD. However, the term usually refers to the places where shrines were set up to foreign gods, especially shrines where the worship of the LORD was in rivalry with Jerusalem.

High places referring to mountainous areas
------------------------------------------

**[HAB 3:19](https://ref.ly/Hab3:19)** *See also* [DEU 32:13](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13); [PSA 18:33](https://ref.ly/Ps18:33) parallel [2SA 22:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:34)

High places express divine lordship
-----------------------------------

**[AMO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Amos4:13)** *See also* [MIC 1:3](https://ref.ly/Mic1:3)

High places referring to shrines to the LORD
--------------------------------------------

### High places were the focus of worship before the building of Solomon's temple

**[1KI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:2-1Kgs3:3)**

### High places associated with Samuel

**[1SA 9:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:12)** *See also* [1SA 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:13-1Sam9:14), [1SA 9:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:19)

### Associated with other prophets

**[1SA 10:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:5)**

High places referring to foreign shrines
----------------------------------------

### In Canaan before the conquest

**[DEU 33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:29)**

### Among Israel's neighbours

**[ISA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2)** *See also* [ISA 16:12](https://ref.ly/Isa16:12)

Israelite use of high places condemned
--------------------------------------

### The building of shrines on high places

**[1KI 12:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:31)**

### High places condemned by God

**[LEV 26:30](https://ref.ly/Lev26:30)** *See also* [NUM 33:52](https://ref.ly/Num33:52); [DEU 12:2](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2)

### High places condemned by the prophets

**[EZK 20:28–20:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:28-Ezek20:29)** *See also* [JER 17:3](https://ref.ly/Jer17:3)

High places associated with foreign gods
----------------------------------------

[JER 32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:35) *Baal*; ; [1KI 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7) *Chemosh and Molech*

Worship at the high places
--------------------------

### High places regarded as unsatisfactory places to worship the LORD

**[2CH 32:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:12)** *See also* [2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4) *The presence of Asherah poles is evidence of a consort for the LORD, typical of Baal worship, but alien to the Biblical concept of God*; [2CH 31:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:1)

### Characteristics of worship at the high places

[2KI 17:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:11) *burning incense*; ; [2KI 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:20) *the presence of priests*; ; [2CH 28:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:25) *sacrifices*; ; [EZK 43:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:7) *a site for the tombs of kings*

Removal of the high places
--------------------------

[LEV 26:30](https://ref.ly/Lev26:30); ; [NUM 33:52](https://ref.ly/Num33:52); ; [2CH 14:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:3), [2CH 17:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:6)

### Commended for the zeal shown

**[2KI 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:5)** *See also* [2KI 23:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8), [2KI 23:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:15)

### Used as a test

[1KI 15:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:14), [1KI 22:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:43); ; [2KI 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:2-2Kgs12:3), [2KI 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:3-2Kgs14:4), [2KI 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:3-2Kgs15:4), [2KI 15:34–15:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:34-2Kgs15:35); ; [2CH 20:32–20:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:32-2Chr20:33)

## 7376: high priest (id: 7376)

One of the most important people in Israel, responsible for the spiritual welfare of the nation and, in particular, for making atonement for the sins of the people. The NT recognises Jesus Christ as bringing this role to fulfilment, making the perfect sacrifice for the sins of his people, as both high priest and the sacrificial victim.

* **Associated Articles:** 7377: high priest, in OT (ID: `7377@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7378: high priest, in NT (ID: `7378@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7379: high priest, Jesus Christ as (ID: `7379@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7377: high priest, in OT (id: 7377)

The OT emphasises the spiritual importance of the high priest, especially as the one who makes atonement for the sins of the nation. It also sets out his duties and responsibilities.

The role of the high priest
---------------------------

### Making atonement on the Day of Atonement

**[LEV 16:3](https://ref.ly/Lev16:3)** *See also* [LEV 16:1–16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:1-Lev16:34)

### Teaching

**[DEU 33:10](https://ref.ly/Deut33:10)** *See also* [EZR 7:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:12); [NEH 8:2](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2)

### Providing oracles

**[LEV 8:8](https://ref.ly/Lev8:8)** *See also* [NUM 27:21](https://ref.ly/Num27:21); [DEU 33:8](https://ref.ly/Deut33:8)

### Interceding

**[EZR 9:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:5)**

### Administering the sanctuary

**[NUM 3:38](https://ref.ly/Num3:38)**

### Anointing kings

**[2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)**

### Supervising censuses

**[NUM 26:1–26:2](https://ref.ly/Num26:1-Num26:2)**

### Encouraging soldiers

**[DEU 20:2](https://ref.ly/Deut20:2)**

Titles used to refer to the high priest
---------------------------------------

### The priest

**[EXO 31:10](https://ref.ly/Exod31:10)**

### The anointed priest

**[LEV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:3)** *See also* [EXO 30:30](https://ref.ly/Exod30:30); [LEV 4:5](https://ref.ly/Lev4:5), [LEV 4:16](https://ref.ly/Lev4:16), [LEV 16:32](https://ref.ly/Lev16:32)

### The high priest

**[LEV 21:10](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10)** *See also* [HAG 1:12–1:14](https://ref.ly/Hag1:12-Hag1:14), [HAG 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Hag2:2-Hag2:4); [ZEC 3:1–3:8](https://ref.ly/Zech3:1-Zech3:8), [ZEC 6:11](https://ref.ly/Zech6:11)

Melchizedek, the king\-priest
-----------------------------

[GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18); ; [PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4) *David's son, as king\-priest, is appointed to the order of Melchizedek, a priesthood superior to that of Aaron*; ; [HEB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Heb7:11)

High priesthood in the law
--------------------------

### Only certain people might be high priests

**[NUM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Num3:10)**

### Consecration of a high priest

**[LEV 8:30](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30)**

### Dignity of the high priesthood

**[DEU 17:12](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12)** *See also* [LEV 21:10](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10)

### Dress of the high priest

**[EXO 28:4](https://ref.ly/Exod28:4)** *See also* [LEV 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:7-Lev8:9), [LEV 16:4](https://ref.ly/Lev16:4)

Other high priests mentioned in the OT
--------------------------------------

[1SA 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:9) *Eli*; ; [1SA 21:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:2) *Ahimelech*; ; [1CH 24:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:3) *Zadok*; ; [2KI 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10) *Uriah*; ; [2KI 22:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:4) *Hilkiah*; ; [2KI 25:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:18) *Seraiah*; ; [NEH 3:1](https://ref.ly/Neh3:1) *Eliashib*

## 7378: high priest, in NT (id: 7378)

Under the Roman administration the high priest remained the senior Jewish leader, but his power was limited by Roman supervision.

High priests of the NT
----------------------

[LUK 3:2](https://ref.ly/Luke3:2); ; [ACT 23:2](https://ref.ly/Acts23:2) *Annas was high priest from A.D. 6 until deposed by the Roman official, Gratus, in A.D. 15\. Though replaced by his son Eleazar and, in A.D. 18, by Caiaphas, his son\-in\-law, Annas retained his authority in Jewish eyes. Strictly, there could only be one high priest at a time. Ananias, the high priest of [Ac 23:2](https://ref.ly/Acts23:2), was not related to the family of Annas.*

Grandeur of the high priest
---------------------------

[JHN 18:10](https://ref.ly/John18:10), [JHN 18:15](https://ref.ly/John18:15) *The courtyard was within a palace*; ; [ACT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Acts6:12), [ACT 7:1](https://ref.ly/Acts7:1) *The high priest was president of the Sanhedrin.*

The high priest's connections with Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------

### Prophesying Jesus Christ's death

**[JHN 11:49–11:50](https://ref.ly/John11:49-John11:50)** *Words spoken by Caiaphas in a political sense were seen to be prophetic, pointing forward to the sacrificing role the high priest would play in the death of Jesus Christ.* *See also* [JHN 11:51–11:52](https://ref.ly/John11:51-John11:52)

### Presiding at Jesus Christ's trial

**[JHN 18:19–18:24](https://ref.ly/John18:19-John18:24)** *See also* [MAT 26:57–26:59](https://ref.ly/Matt26:57-Matt26:59) parallel [MRK 14:53–14:55](https://ref.ly/Mark14:53-Mark14:55); [MAT 26:62–26:65](https://ref.ly/Matt26:62-Matt26:65) parallel [MRK 14:61–14:64](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61-Mark14:64); [LUK 22:54](https://ref.ly/Luke22:54); [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/John18:28) *Caiaphas, the necessary go\-between, brought Jesus Christ before Pilate.*

The high priest's encounters with Peter and John
------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Acts4:7)** *The teaching of Jesus Christ's resurrection was particularly abhorrent to the Sadducees, the priestly party to which Annas and Caiaphas belonged.* *See also* [ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17), [ACT 5:21](https://ref.ly/Acts5:21), [ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29)

The high priest's encounter with Stephen
----------------------------------------

**[ACT 7:1](https://ref.ly/Acts7:1)** *Stephen was thought to have threatened the temple, the focus of high priestly power.* *See also* [ACT 7:57–7:59](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57-Acts7:59)

Encounters of the high priests with Paul
----------------------------------------

### Commissioning Paul to persecute Christians

**[ACT 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Acts9:1-Acts9:2)**

### Ordering that Paul be struck

**[ACT 23:3](https://ref.ly/Acts23:3)** *See also* [JHN 18:21–18:23](https://ref.ly/John18:21-John18:23)

### Taking charges against Paul to the Roman governor

**[ACT 24:1](https://ref.ly/Acts24:1)** *See also* [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/John18:28)

--------------------------------

### Abiathar

**[MRK 2:26](https://ref.ly/Mark2:26)**

### Melchizedek

**[HEB 5:6](https://ref.ly/Heb5:6)**

---------------------------

## 7382: house of God (id: 7382)

The place where God was seen to dwell with his people, initially the tabernacle and subsequently the temple at Jerusalem.

Early references to the house of God
------------------------------------

**[GEN 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Gen28:16-Gen28:17)** *“house of God” was first used by Jacob to commemorate the place where he met with God*; **[GEN 28:19](https://ref.ly/Gen28:19)** *“Bethel” means “house of God”.* *See also* [GEN 28:22](https://ref.ly/Gen28:22)

The tabernacle as the house of God
----------------------------------

### The tabernacle was referred to as such before its construction

**[EXO 23:19](https://ref.ly/Exod23:19)** *See also* [EXO 34:26](https://ref.ly/Exod34:26)

### In the wilderness God said he would dwell with his people in the tabernacle

**[EXO 25:8–25:9](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8-Exod25:9)**

### God's presence came and dwelt in the tabernacle when it was completed

**[EXO 40:33–40:34](https://ref.ly/Exod40:33-Exod40:34)** *See also* [EXO 40:35–40:38](https://ref.ly/Exod40:35-Exod40:38)

The temple as the permanent house of God
----------------------------------------

### David's desire to build the LORD a permanent house

**[2SA 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:1-2Sam7:2)**

### This was not the LORD's will for David

**[2SA 7:5–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:5-2Sam7:7), [2SA 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:12-2Sam7:13)** *See also* [ACT 7:45–7:47](https://ref.ly/Acts7:45-Acts7:47)

### Solomon builds the temple in Jersualem

[1KI 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:4-1Kgs5:5), [1KI 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:1-1Kgs6:2)

### God's presence dwelt there but was conditional upon the faithfulness of the people

**[1KI 8:10–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:10-1Kgs8:13)** parallel [2CH 5:11–6:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:11-2Chr6:2) *See also* [1KI 9:6–9:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:6-1Kgs9:9)

Zerubbabel's temple referred to as the house of God
---------------------------------------------------

**[EZR 1:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:5)** [EZR 2:68](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:68), [EZR 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8-Ezra3:9), [EZR 3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11), [EZR 4:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:24), [EZR 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:2), [EZR 5:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:13), [EZR 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:16-Ezra5:17); [EZR 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:1), [EZR 10:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:6), [EZR 10:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:9); [NEH 6:10](https://ref.ly/Neh6:10), [NEH 8:16](https://ref.ly/Neh8:16), [NEH 10:32](https://ref.ly/Neh10:32), [NEH 10:34–10:38](https://ref.ly/Neh10:34-Neh10:38), [NEH 11:11](https://ref.ly/Neh11:11), [NEH 11:22](https://ref.ly/Neh11:22), [NEH 12:40](https://ref.ly/Neh12:40); [NEH 13:9](https://ref.ly/Neh13:9), [NEH 13:11](https://ref.ly/Neh13:11), [NEH 13:14](https://ref.ly/Neh13:14)

Other names given to the house of God
-------------------------------------

### Sanctuary

[EXO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8)

### House of prayer

[MAT 21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:13) parallels [MRK 11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:17), [LUK 19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:46); ; [ISA 56:7](https://ref.ly/Isa56:7)

### God's dwelling\-place

[ACT 7:45–7:47](https://ref.ly/Acts7:45-Acts7:47)

The need for due reverence in worship
-------------------------------------

[ECC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1)

The house of God under the new covenant
---------------------------------------

### In the hearts of God's people

**[1CO 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:16)**; **[GAL 4:6](https://ref.ly/Gal4:6)** *See also* [ACT 7:48](https://ref.ly/Acts7:48), [ACT 17:24](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24); [1CO 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19); [2CO 1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:22), [2CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:16); [EPH 3:17](https://ref.ly/Eph3:17)

### In the church, the body of Christ

**[HEB 3:6](https://ref.ly/Heb3:6)** *See also* [EPH 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19-Eph2:22); [1PE 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:4-1Pet2:5)

## 7384: household gods (id: 7384)

Known in the Hebrew as “teraphim”, miniature idols small enough for their owners to keep in their homes or to carry with them when travelling.

Examples of the use of household gods
-------------------------------------

**[GEN 31:19](https://ref.ly/Gen31:19)** *See also* [GEN 31:34](https://ref.ly/Gen31:34); [JDG 17:1–17:6](https://ref.ly/Judg17:1-Judg17:6), [JDG 18:18](https://ref.ly/Judg18:18), [JDG 18:30–18:31](https://ref.ly/Judg18:30-Judg18:31); [1SA 19:13–19:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:13-1Sam19:16); [EZK 21:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:21) *Three methods of divination are described here, shaking the arrows (belomancy), consulting the household gods (necromancy) and the examination of the liver of a sacrificed victim (hepatoscopy).*

The use of household gods was contrary to God's law
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 7:25–7:26](https://ref.ly/Deut7:25-Deut7:26)** *See also* [EXO 20:4–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:4-Exod20:6) parallel [DEU 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/Deut5:8-Deut5:10)

The removal and condemnation of household gods
----------------------------------------------

**[2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24)** *See also* [GEN 35:2–35:4](https://ref.ly/Gen35:2-Gen35:4); [ISA 44:9–44:11](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9-Isa44:11); [EZK 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:5-Ezek18:6); [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 31:19; Genesis 31:34; Judges 17:1-6; Judges 18:18; Judges 18:30-31; 1 Samuel 19:13-16; Ezekiel 21:21; Deuteronomy 7:25-26; Exodus 20:4-6; Deuteronomy 5:8-10; 2 Kings 23:24; Genesis 35:2-4; Isaiah 44:9-11; Ezekiel 18:5-6; Zechariah 10:2
* **Associated Articles:** 1080: God, living and self-sustaining (ID: `1080@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4155: divination (ID: `4155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4363: silver (ID: `4363@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5138: bowing (ID: `5138@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5212: arts and crafts (ID: `5212@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6243: adultery, spiritual (ID: `6243@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7324: calf worship (ID: `7324@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8807: profanity (ID: `8807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8840: unfaithfulness, to God (ID: `8840@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7386: incense (id: 7386)

An expensive resin from southern Arabia and the fragrant aroma it emits when offered in worship ritual. It is also called frankincense.

Regulations governing the offering of incense
---------------------------------------------

### The priest was to keep incense burning on the altar daily

**[2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11)** *See also* [EXO 30:7–30:9](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7-Exod30:9); [NUM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Num4:16); [DEU 33:10](https://ref.ly/Deut33:10); [1SA 2:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:28); [1CH 6:49](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:49), [1CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:29); [LUK 1:9](https://ref.ly/Luke1:9)

### Only the priests were to burn incense

Korah, Dathan and Abiram: [NUM 16:16–16:18](https://ref.ly/Num16:16-Num16:18), [NUM 16:35](https://ref.ly/Num16:35), [NUM 16:40](https://ref.ly/Num16:40), [NUM 16:46–16:47](https://ref.ly/Num16:46-Num16:47); [2CH 26:16–26:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16-2Chr26:19) *Uzziah's pride*

### Failure to burn incense is disobedience

[2CH 29:7–29:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:7-2Chr29:8)

### The blending of the incense

**[EXO 30:34–30:38](https://ref.ly/Exod30:34-Exod30:38)** *See also* [EXO 25:6](https://ref.ly/Exod25:6) parallel [EXO 35:8](https://ref.ly/Exod35:8); [EXO 35:28](https://ref.ly/Exod35:28)

Occasions for offering incense
------------------------------

At the setting up of the tabernacle: [EXO 39:38](https://ref.ly/Exod39:38), [EXO 40:5](https://ref.ly/Exod40:5), [EXO 40:26–40:27](https://ref.ly/Exod40:26-Exod40:27); [LEV 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:1-Lev2:2), [LEV 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Lev2:15-Lev2:16), [LEV 6:15](https://ref.ly/Lev6:15); [LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11) *not to accompany a sin offering*; ; [LEV 16:12](https://ref.ly/Lev16:12) *on the Day of Atonement*; ; [LEV 24:7](https://ref.ly/Lev24:7) *to accompany bread and oil*; ; [NUM 7:86](https://ref.ly/Num7:86) *As part of the offerings at the dedication of the tabernacle, each leader presented a golden incense dish.*

The altar of incense
--------------------

**[EXO 30:1](https://ref.ly/Exod30:1)** *See also* [EXO 30:27](https://ref.ly/Exod30:27), [EXO 31:8](https://ref.ly/Exod31:8), [EXO 31:11](https://ref.ly/Exod31:11), [EXO 35:15](https://ref.ly/Exod35:15), [EXO 37:25–37:29](https://ref.ly/Exod37:25-Exod37:29); [1CH 28:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:18)

Burning incense to foreign gods
-------------------------------

### Incense offered to other gods will not bring answers to prayer

[JER 11:12](https://ref.ly/Jer11:12), [JER 44:23](https://ref.ly/Jer44:23)

### Israel disobeyed the LORD's commands

**[JER 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Jer7:9-Jer7:10)** *See also* [JER 18:15](https://ref.ly/Jer18:15), [JER 19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:13), [JER 32:29](https://ref.ly/Jer32:29), [JER 44:5](https://ref.ly/Jer44:5); [EZK 16:18–16:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:18-Ezek16:19), [EZK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:28); [HOS 11:2](https://ref.ly/Hos11:2)

### The practice continued under successive kings

In the early days of Solomon's reign: [1KI 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:3), [1KI 11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:8); [1KI 22:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:43) *Jehoshaphat*; ; [2KI 12:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:3) *Joash*; ; [2KI 14:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:4) *Amaziah*; ; [2KI 15:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:4) *Azariah*; ; [2KI 15:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:35) *Jotham*; ; [2KI 16:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:4) parallel [2CH 28:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:4) *Ahaz*

### It provoked God's anger

**[JER 44:3](https://ref.ly/Jer44:3)** *See also* [2KI 22:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:17); [ISA 65:3](https://ref.ly/Isa65:3); [JER 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jer1:16), [JER 11:17](https://ref.ly/Jer11:17), [JER 44:5](https://ref.ly/Jer44:5), [JER 44:8](https://ref.ly/Jer44:8), [JER 44:21](https://ref.ly/Jer44:21), [JER 48:35](https://ref.ly/Jer48:35); [HOS 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:13)

The destruction of pagan incense altars
---------------------------------------

### As part of God's punishment for disobedience

[LEV 26:30](https://ref.ly/Lev26:30); ; [EZK 6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:4), [EZK 6:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:6), [EZK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:13)

### Their destruction in religious reformations

[2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4) *under Hezekiah*; [2KI 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:5), [2KI 23:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8); ; [2CH 34:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:4), [2CH 34:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:7); [NEH 13:5](https://ref.ly/Neh13:5), [NEH 13:9](https://ref.ly/Neh13:9)

### Their destruction brought God's blessing and deliverance

[ISA 27:9](https://ref.ly/Isa27:9)

The significance of incense
---------------------------

### A right heart attitude is more important to God

[ISA 66:3](https://ref.ly/Isa66:3); ; [JER 6:20](https://ref.ly/Jer6:20)

### It both accompanies and symbolises prayer

[PSA 141:2](https://ref.ly/Ps141:2); ; [REV 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8), [REV 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Rev8:3-Rev8:4)

### It symbolises worship

[2CH 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:4); ; [DAN 2:46](https://ref.ly/Dan2:46); ; [MAL 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mal1:11); ; [MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11); ; [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4)

### It brings pleasure

[PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9); ; [SNG 3:6](https://ref.ly/Song3:6), [SNG 4:6](https://ref.ly/Song4:6); ; [EZK 20:41](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:41)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 13:11; Exodus 30:7-9; Numbers 4:16; Deuteronomy 33:10; 1 Samuel 2:28; 1 Chronicles 6:49; 1 Chronicles 9:29; Luke 1:9; Numbers 16:16-18; Numbers 16:35; Numbers 16:40; Numbers 16:46-47; 2 Chronicles 26:16-19; 2 Chronicles 29:7-8; Exodus 30:34-38; Exodus 25:6; Exodus 35:8; Exodus 35:28; Exodus 39:38; Exodus 40:5; Exodus 40:26-27; Leviticus 2:1-2; Leviticus 2:15-16; Leviticus 6:15; Leviticus 5:11; Leviticus 16:12; Leviticus 24:7; Numbers 7:86; Exodus 30:1; Exodus 30:27; Exodus 31:8; Exodus 31:11; Exodus 35:15; Exodus 37:25-29; 1 Chronicles 28:18; Jeremiah 11:12; Jeremiah 44:23; Jeremiah 7:9-10; Jeremiah 18:15; Jeremiah 19:13; Jeremiah 32:29; Jeremiah 44:5; Ezekiel 16:18-19; Ezekiel 20:28; Hosea 11:2; 1 Kings 3:3; 1 Kings 11:8; 1 Kings 22:43; 2 Kings 12:3; 2 Kings 14:4; 2 Kings 15:4; 2 Kings 15:35; 2 Kings 16:4; 2 Chronicles 28:4; Jeremiah 44:3; 2 Kings 22:17; Isaiah 65:3; Jeremiah 1:16; Jeremiah 11:17; Jeremiah 44:8; Jeremiah 44:21; Jeremiah 48:35; Hosea 2:13; Leviticus 26:30; Ezekiel 6:4; Ezekiel 6:6; Ezekiel 6:13; 2 Kings 18:4; 2 Kings 23:5; 2 Kings 23:8; 2 Chronicles 34:4; 2 Chronicles 34:7; Nehemiah 13:5; Nehemiah 13:9; Isaiah 27:9; Isaiah 66:3; Jeremiah 6:20; Psalms 141:2; Revelation 5:8; Revelation 8:3-4; 2 Chronicles 2:4; Daniel 2:46; Malachi 1:11; Matthew 2:11; Hebrews 9:4; Proverbs 27:9; Song of Songs 3:6; Song of Songs 4:6; Ezekiel 20:41

## 7388: kinsman-redeemer (id: 7388)

The relative who restores or preserves the full community rights of disadvantaged family members. The concept arises from God's covenant relationship with Israel and points to the redemption of humanity in Jesus Christ.

Covenant rules for the kinsman\-redeemer
----------------------------------------

### The kinsman\-redeemer's obligation to redeem the land

[LEV 25:25–25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:25-Lev25:28); ; [JER 32:6–32:9](https://ref.ly/Jer32:6-Jer32:9)

### The kinsman\-redeemer's obligation to redeem the enslaved

[LEV 25:47–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47-Lev25:55)

### The kinsman\-redeemer's obligation to provide an heir

[GEN 38:8–38:10](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8-Gen38:10); ; [DEU 25:5–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:10); ; [MAT 22:23–22:28](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23-Matt22:28) parallels [MRK 12:18–12:23](https://ref.ly/Mark12:18-Mark12:23), [LUK 20:27–20:33](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27-Luke20:33)

### The kinsman\-redeemer's obligation to avenge death

**[NUM 35:16–35:21](https://ref.ly/Num35:16-Num35:21)**

### The kinsman\-redeemer's obligation to be a trustee

**[NUM 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/Num5:5-Num5:8)**

The kinsman\-redeemer in the book of Ruth
-----------------------------------------

**[RUT 2:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:20)** *See also* [RUT 3:1–4:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:1-Ruth4:17)

The LORD as redeemer
--------------------

**[EXO 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6-Exod6:7)** *See also* [2SA 7:22–7:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:22-2Sam7:24); [ISA 43:1–43:7](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1-Isa43:7), [ISA 54:5–54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:5-Isa54:8); [JER 50:33–50:34](https://ref.ly/Jer50:33-Jer50:34)

The kinsman\-redeemer reflects God's concern for the poor and oppressed
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10-Prov23:11)** *See also* [PSA 68:5–68:6](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5-Ps68:6), [PSA 72:2–72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:2-Ps72:4)

God's provision of Jesus Christ as kinsman\-redeemer
----------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:7)** *See also* [GAL 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13-Gal3:14); [HEB 2:11–2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:11-Heb2:18)

## 7390: Levites (id: 7390)

Assistants to the Aaronic priests, they exercised an auxiliary ministry, caring for the tabernacle. After the ark was brought to Jerusalem by David their role was re\-ordered and extended. They were reconsecrated during the reforms of Josiah and Hezekiah and recommissioned by Ezra and Nehemiah when the Israelites returned from captivity.

The Levites and the care of the tabernacle
------------------------------------------

### The Levites are to transport, erect and guard the tabernacle

**[NUM 1:48–1:53](https://ref.ly/Num1:48-Num1:53)** *See also* [NUM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Num2:17); [DEU 10:8](https://ref.ly/Deut10:8)

### The Gershonite branch of the Levites are to care for the coverings and curtains of the tabernacle

**[NUM 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/Num3:25-Num3:26)** *See also* [NUM 4:24–4:27](https://ref.ly/Num4:24-Num4:27)

### The Kohathite branch of the Levites are to care for the furnishings of the sanctuary

**[NUM 3:31](https://ref.ly/Num3:31)** *See also* [NUM 4:4–4:20](https://ref.ly/Num4:4-Num4:20)

### The Merarite branch of the Levites are to care for the frames of the tabernacle

**[NUM 3:36–3:37](https://ref.ly/Num3:36-Num3:37)** *See also* [NUM 4:29–4:33](https://ref.ly/Num4:29-Num4:33)

The Levites are to have an auxiliary ministry
---------------------------------------------

**[NUM 3:5–3:10](https://ref.ly/Num3:5-Num3:10)** *See also* [NUM 8:19](https://ref.ly/Num8:19), [NUM 18:1–18:7](https://ref.ly/Num18:1-Num18:7)

The Levites are set apart for their ministry
--------------------------------------------

**[NUM 8:20–8:21](https://ref.ly/Num8:20-Num8:21)** *See also* [NUM 8:5–8:15](https://ref.ly/Num8:5-Num8:15)

The Levites' period of service was between the ages of 25 and 50
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 8:23–8:26](https://ref.ly/Num8:23-Num8:26)** *See also* [NUM 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Num4:1-Num4:3) *Some suggest that the reason for the discrepancy in ages between Numbers chapters 4 and 8 is that the Levites had to serve an apprenticeship for the first five years.*

Occasions after the settlement when the Levites transport the ark
-----------------------------------------------------------------

[1SA 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:15); ; [2SA 15:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:24); ; [1KI 8:3–8:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:3-1Kgs8:5) parallel [2CH 5:4–5:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:4-2Chr5:6); ; [1CH 15:1–15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:1-1Chr15:16)

David re\-orders and extends the role of the Levites
----------------------------------------------------

**[1CH 23:2–23:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:2-1Chr23:6)** *See also* [1CH 6:31–6:49](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:31-1Chr6:49), [1CH 16:4–16:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:4-1Chr16:6), [1CH 23:24–23:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:24-1Chr23:32), [1CH 25:1–26:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1-1Chr26:32)
