## 7392: lots, casting of (id: 7392)

A means of determining the will of God, prior to the giving of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost. The casting of lots was also used by pagans for the same purpose. Such use reflects the belief that nothing occurred by chance.

Casting lots to determine the will of God
-----------------------------------------

**[PRO 16:33](https://ref.ly/Prov16:33)**

### In the ministry of the high priest

**[EXO 28:30](https://ref.ly/Exod28:30)** *The “Urim and Thummim” were sacred lots maintained for the purpose of determining God's will.* *See also* [LEV 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:7-Lev8:9), [LEV 16:6–16:10](https://ref.ly/Lev16:6-Lev16:10); [NUM 27:21](https://ref.ly/Num27:21); [DEU 33:8](https://ref.ly/Deut33:8); [1SA 28:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:6); [EZR 2:63](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:63) parallel [NEH 7:65](https://ref.ly/Neh7:65)

### To apportion land

**[NUM 33:54](https://ref.ly/Num33:54)** *See also* [NUM 26:54–26:56](https://ref.ly/Num26:54-Num26:56); [JOS 14:2](https://ref.ly/Josh14:2), [JOS 18:10](https://ref.ly/Josh18:10)

### To select individuals

**[1SA 14:41–14:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:41-1Sam14:42)** *See also* [JOS 7:14–7:18](https://ref.ly/Josh7:14-Josh7:18); [JDG 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/Judg20:9-Judg20:10); [1SA 10:20–10:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:20-1Sam10:21); [JON 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:7)**[ACT 1:15–1:26](https://ref.ly/Acts1:15-Acts1:26)**

### To assign priestly duties

**[1CH 24:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:5)** *See also* [1CH 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:12-1Chr26:13); [NEH 10:34](https://ref.ly/Neh10:34); [LUK 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Luke1:8-Luke1:9)

### To settle disputes

**[PRO 18:18](https://ref.ly/Prov18:18)**

Casting lots as a means of divination
-------------------------------------

**[EZK 21:21–21:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:21-Ezek21:22)** *See also* [EST 3:7](https://ref.ly/Esth3:7), [EST 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/Esth9:24-Esth9:27) *The word “purim” is the plural of “pur” and means “lots”.*

Casting lots as a means of distributing plunder
-----------------------------------------------

## 7394: memorial (id: 7394)

An object, institution or custom established or founded as a reminder of an important person or event held to be worth remembering in the life of the community.

---------------------

**[JOS 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Josh4:4-Josh4:7)** *See also* [EXO 28:9–28:12](https://ref.ly/Exod28:9-Exod28:12) parallel [EXO 39:6–39:7](https://ref.ly/Exod39:6-Exod39:7); [EXO 28:29](https://ref.ly/Exod28:29)

**[LEV 24:7](https://ref.ly/Lev24:7)** *See also* [ISA 66:3](https://ref.ly/Isa66:3)

### A crown

**[ZEC 6:9–6:15](https://ref.ly/Zech6:9-Zech6:15)**

-----------------------

## 7396: Most Holy Place (id: 7396)

The inner sanctuary of the tabernacle set up at Sinai and of Solomon's temple, where the ark of the covenant was kept. It is a symbol of the unapproachable presence of God.

Terms used to describe the Most Holy Place
------------------------------------------

[EXO 26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:33) *“the Most Holy Place” (or “Holy of Holies”), in contrast to the “Holy Place” where the altar stood. This superlative suggests increasing closeness to the presence of God. The distinction is not always observed, however. In Leviticus chapter 16 “Holy Place” is used regularly (translated in NIV as “Most Holy Place” in verses 2, 16, 17, 20, 27, and as “sanctuary area” in verse 3, though it is the inner sanctuary that is referred to, as the phrase “behind the curtain” (verse 2\) shows)*; ; [1KI 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:16) *the “inner sanctuary” in contrast to the outer*; ; [HEB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Heb9:7) *literally “the second (tabernacle)” in contrast to the first or outer*

The structure and design of the Most Holy Place
-----------------------------------------------

### In the wilderness tabernacle

The only details concern the curtains forming the walls of the two sanctuaries, and in particular the dividing curtain which separates the Most Holy Place from the outer sanctuary: [EXO 26:31–26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:31-Exod26:33), [EXO 36:35–36:36](https://ref.ly/Exod36:35-Exod36:36), [EXO 40:21](https://ref.ly/Exod40:21)

### In Solomon's temple

[1KI 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:16), [1KI 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:19-1Kgs6:20) parallel [2CH 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:8); ; [1KI 6:23–6:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:23-1Kgs6:28) parallel [2CH 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:10); ; [1KI 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:31-1Kgs6:32); ; [2CH 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:14)

### In the temple in Ezekiel's vision

[EZK 41:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:4), [EZK 41:15–41:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:15-Ezek41:21), [EZK 41:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:23), [EZK 45:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:3) *In Ezekiel's vision a portion of the land was sacred to the LORD and called the Most Holy Place.*

The significance and function of the Most Holy Place
----------------------------------------------------

### To house the ark of the covenant

**[EXO 26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:33)** *See also* [EXO 40:3](https://ref.ly/Exod40:3); [1KI 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:19), [1KI 8:6–8:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:6-1Kgs8:9) parallel [2CH 5:7–5:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:7-2Chr5:9)

### To house the incense altar (in Solomon's temple)

**[1KI 6:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:22)**; **[HEB 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:3-Heb9:4)** *[Ex 30:6](https://ref.ly/Exod30:6) and [40:5](https://ref.ly/Exod40:5) suggest that the incense altar was housed in front of the curtain, i.e., in the Holy Place, the outer sanctuary.*

### To be a symbol of the presence of God

**[PSA 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ps28:2)** *See also* [EXO 26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:33) *The curtain veiling the Most Holy Place represents the unapproachable presence of the Holy God*; [LEV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2)

The Most Holy Place on the Day of Atonement
-------------------------------------------

**[HEB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Heb9:7)** *See also* [LEV 16:16–16:17](https://ref.ly/Lev16:16-Lev16:17)

The NT understanding of the Most Holy Place as the place of God's heavenly presence
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ enters the Most Holy Place as Mediator

**[HEB 9:8](https://ref.ly/Heb9:8)** *See also* [HEB 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19-Heb6:20), [HEB 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Heb9:11-Heb9:12), [HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Heb9:24)

### Entry to the Most Holy Place is open to all believers

**[HEB 10:19–10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:22)** *See also* [MAT 27:51](https://ref.ly/Matt27:51) parallel [LUK 23:45](https://ref.ly/Luke23:45) *By Jesus Christ's death the dividing curtain has been torn in two.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 26:33; 1 Kings 6:16; Hebrews 9:7; Exodus 26:31-33; Exodus 36:35-36; Exodus 40:21; 1 Kings 6:19-20; 2 Chronicles 3:8; 1 Kings 6:23-28; 2 Chronicles 3:10; 1 Kings 6:31-32; 2 Chronicles 3:14; Ezekiel 41:4; Ezekiel 41:15-21; Ezekiel 41:23; Ezekiel 45:3; Exodus 40:3; 1 Kings 6:19; 1 Kings 8:6-9; 2 Chronicles 5:7-9; 1 Kings 6:22; Hebrews 9:3-4; Exodus 30:6; Exodus 40:5; Psalms 28:2; Leviticus 16:2; Leviticus 16:16-17; Hebrews 9:8; Hebrews 6:19-20; Hebrews 9:11-12; Hebrews 9:24; Hebrews 10:19-22; Matthew 27:51; Luke 23:45

## 7398: New Moon festival (id: 7398)

The festival which marked the consecration to God of each new month in the year.

New Moon festivals were required under the Mosaic law
-----------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 28:11](https://ref.ly/Num28:11)**

New Moon festivals were times of celebration
--------------------------------------------

### They were marked by blowing of trumpets

**[NUM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Num10:10)** *See also* [PSA 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3)

### They were celebrated with offerings

[NUM 28:11–28:15](https://ref.ly/Num28:11-Num28:15); ; [1CH 23:30–23:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:30-1Chr23:31); ; [2CH 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:4), [2CH 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:12-2Chr8:13), [2CH 31:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:3) *reformed temple worship under Hezekiah*; ; [NEH 10:32–10:33](https://ref.ly/Neh10:32-Neh10:33) *New Moon festivals were restored on the return from exile*; ; [EZK 45:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:17), [EZK 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:1), [EZK 46:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:3), [EZK 46:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:6)

### They were times when normal work ceased

David was to dine with Saul: [1SA 20:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:5), [1SA 20:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:18), [1SA 20:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:24); [2KI 4:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:23) *the Shunammite woman and her husband*; ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5) *Some saw them as a hindrance to business.*

A wrong inner attitude at New Moon celebrations incurs God's anger
------------------------------------------------------------------

[ISA 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Isa1:13-Isa1:14); ; [HOS 2:11](https://ref.ly/Hos2:11), [HOS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Hos5:7)

NT reference to the New Moon festival
-------------------------------------

**[COL 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:16-Col2:17)**

## 7400: New Year, the (id: 7400)

With the exception of one reference ([Ex 12:2](https://ref.ly/Exod12:2)) the New Year is never specifically dated. The religious calendar apparently began in the spring and the agricultural calendar in the autumn. Later Judaism began its calendar in the autumn.

Evidence that the autumn was regarded as the New Year
-----------------------------------------------------

### The dedication of Solomon's temple

**[1KI 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:2)** *Solomon waited 11 months after the completion of the temple (see [1Ki 6:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:38)), before dedicating it, most likely because the great Feast of Tabernacles marked the beginning of the year.*

### Instructions for celebrating the Year of Jubilee

**[LEV 25:8–25:9](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:9)** *The Year of Jubilee was to be proclaimed on the tenth day of the seventh month, which was also the Day of Atonement.* *See also* [LEV 16:29](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29)

### Ezekiel's vision of the new temple

**[EZK 40:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:1)** *This vision of the new temple takes place at the beginning of the year. The “tenth of the month” refers presumably to the seventh month.*

### Jeroboam's institution of an autumnal festival

**[1KI 12:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:32)** *This autumn festival was clearly intended to be equivalent not only to the feast in Judah, but also to other pagan New Year festivals, in spite of the eighth month being distinctive.* *See also* [1KI 12:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:33)

Evidence that the spring was regarded as the New Year
-----------------------------------------------------

### The institution of the Passover

**[EXO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Exod12:2)** *This clearly links the spring Feast of Passover to the New Year.* *See also* [LEV 23:5](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5); [NUM 9:2](https://ref.ly/Num9:2)

### Ezekiel's vision of the worship of the new temple

**[EZK 45:18–45:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:18-Ezek45:19)** *This purification of Ezekiel's (ideal) temple resembles the Day of Atonement but takes place in the spring.* *See also* [EZK 45:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:21)

Occasions associating the New Year with the autumn
--------------------------------------------------

### The sounding of trumpets

**[NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1)** *See also* [LEV 23:24](https://ref.ly/Lev23:24)

### The Day of Atonement

**[LEV 23:27](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27)**

### The Feast of Tabernacles

**[DEU 16:13](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13)** *See also* [LEV 23:34](https://ref.ly/Lev23:34)

Occasions associating the New Year with the spring
--------------------------------------------------

### Passover

**[LEV 23:5](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5)** *See also* [EXO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:11)

### The Feast of Unleavened bread

**[LEV 23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:6)** *See also* [EXO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17)

### The Feast of Firstfruits

**[LEV 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Lev23:10-Lev23:11)** *This is also known as the Feast of Weeks ([Ex 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16); [Lev 23:9–21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:9-Lev23:21)).*

## 7402: offerings (id: 7402)

Anything offered up to God by human beings, including sacrificial offerings. A distinction can be made between offerings which involve the taking of life and other gifts made to God.

Sacrificial offerings as a sign of gratitude or repentance
----------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4)**; **[PSA 107:22](https://ref.ly/Ps107:22)**

Tithes were a regular form of offering
--------------------------------------

**[GEN 28:20–28:22](https://ref.ly/Gen28:20-Gen28:22)**; **[LEV 27:30](https://ref.ly/Lev27:30)** *See also* [GEN 14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:20)

Part of every harvest was given as a freewill offering to God
-------------------------------------------------------------

As firstfruits: [EXO 22:29](https://ref.ly/Exod22:29); ; [NUM 18:12](https://ref.ly/Num18:12); ; [DEU 18:4](https://ref.ly/Deut18:4); ; [NEH 10:35](https://ref.ly/Neh10:35); [LEV 19:23–19:25](https://ref.ly/Lev19:23-Lev19:25) *Fruit from a tree could not be eaten until the fifth year*; ; [LEV 23:38](https://ref.ly/Lev23:38) *Extra gifts were offered on the Sabbath.*

Offerings and the tabernacle
----------------------------

### For its construction

**[EXO 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Exod25:1-Exod25:2)** parallel [EXO 35:5](https://ref.ly/Exod35:5)

### At its dedication

*See also* [NUM 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Num7:2-Num7:3)

Offerings and Solomon's temple
------------------------------

### David's personal gift

[1CH 29:2–29:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:2-1Chr29:5)

### Offerings for upkeep and restoration

In the reign of Joash: [2KI 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:4-2Kgs12:5); ; [2CH 24:8–24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:8-2Chr24:12); [2KI 22:4–22:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:4-2Kgs22:6) parallel [2CH 34:9–34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:9-2Chr34:11)

Offerings and the second temple
-------------------------------

[EZR 3:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:5), [EZR 7:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:16), [EZR 8:24–8:30](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:24-Ezra8:30)

Offerings and Herod's temple
----------------------------

[MRK 12:41–12:43](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:43) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:3) *The poor widow was participating in a regular practice*; ; [LUK 21:5](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5)

Some offerings are not acceptable
---------------------------------

**[MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24)** *Offerings are unacceptable unless the worshipper is reconciled to God.* *See also* [GEN 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gen4:3-Gen4:5) *Cain's offering was not accepted on account of his incorrect attitude.*[AMO 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Amos4:4-Amos4:5); [LUK 18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:12)[MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23) parallel [LUK 11:42](https://ref.ly/Luke11:42) *Meticulous tithing, without mercy or justice, is unacceptable.*

Offerings in the future kingdom
-------------------------------

### From the Gentile nations

[PSA 76:11](https://ref.ly/Ps76:11); ; [ISA 18:7](https://ref.ly/Isa18:7); ; [MAL 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mal1:11)

### From God's people

[EZK 20:40](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:40), [EZK 44:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:30)

Gifts and offerings in the NT
-----------------------------

### To help the needy

[ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35), [ACT 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29-Acts11:30); ; [1CO 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:1-1Cor16:4) *In several of his letters Paul mentions the freewill offering he was organising for poor Christians at Jerusalem*; ; [HEB 13:16](https://ref.ly/Heb13:16)

### To support God's servants

[PHP 4:10–4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:10-Phil4:18)

Offerings of praise
-------------------

[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)

God's people are themselves an offering to God
----------------------------------------------

[JER 2:3](https://ref.ly/Jer2:3); ; [JAS 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jas1:18); ; [REV 14:4](https://ref.ly/Rev14:4)

Offerings as \`\`Corban''
-------------------------

**[MRK 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9-Mark7:13)** *“Corban” is a technical Jewish religious term, meaning “something dedicated, especially on oath”. What Jesus Christ says implies that once the children had donated the amount they would have spent on their ageing parents for religious purposes, they were no longer responsible for their upkeep.*

## 7404: ordinances (id: 7404)

Specific acts of worship commanded by God, sometimes including the sacraments.

The ordinance of Passover and Unleavened Bread
----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:14](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14)** *See also* [EXO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17), [EXO 12:24](https://ref.ly/Exod12:24), [EXO 12:43](https://ref.ly/Exod12:43), [EXO 13:10](https://ref.ly/Exod13:10); [NUM 9:12](https://ref.ly/Num9:12)

The ordinance of the Day of Atonement
-------------------------------------

**[LEV 16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:34)** *See also* [LEV 16:29](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29), [LEV 16:31](https://ref.ly/Lev16:31), [LEV 23:31](https://ref.ly/Lev23:31)

The ordinance of the red\-heifer ritual
---------------------------------------

Other occasions of worship as ordinances
----------------------------------------

-----------------------------------------

### The Aaronic priests

**[EXO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Exod29:9)** *See also* [EXO 28:43](https://ref.ly/Exod28:43), [EXO 30:21](https://ref.ly/Exod30:21); [LEV 7:36](https://ref.ly/Lev7:36), [LEV 10:9](https://ref.ly/Lev10:9); [NUM 10:8](https://ref.ly/Num10:8)

### The Levites

Ordinances in Christian worship
-------------------------------

**[1CO 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24-1Cor11:25)** parallels [MAT 26:26–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26-Matt26:28), [MRK 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:17–22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:17-Luke22:20) *See also* [MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20) *Jesus Christ's commands to observe baptism and the Lord's Supper makes them ordinances of Christian worship*; [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:15), [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8), [ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:16)

Ordinances were to be permanent
-------------------------------

-------------------------------------

**[PSA 19:9](https://ref.ly/Ps19:9)**

## 7406: Passover (id: 7406)

One of the major OT feasts. It specifically commemorates the exodus from Egypt as an act of God's deliverance. In Israel's calendar of feasts it was always celebrated with the Feast of Unleavened Bread.

The institution of the Passover
-------------------------------

**[EXO 12:25–12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:25-Exod12:27)** *The Hebrew term “Passover” derives from a verb meaning “to pass over” with the sense of “to spare”.*

Celebrating the Passover
------------------------

### Instructions concerning its observance

*See also* [EXO 12:2–12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:2-Exod12:11), [EXO 12:46–12:47](https://ref.ly/Exod12:46-Exod12:47); [NUM 9:1–9:5](https://ref.ly/Num9:1-Num9:5); [DEU 16:1–16:8](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:8)

### Combined with the Feast of Unleavened Bread

[EXO 34:25](https://ref.ly/Exod34:25)

### The need for ritual cleansing

[JHN 11:55](https://ref.ly/John11:55)

### Provision for ritual uncleanness

[NUM 9:6–9:13](https://ref.ly/Num9:6-Num9:13)

### Provision for non\-Jews

[EXO 12:43–12:45](https://ref.ly/Exod12:43-Exod12:45), [EXO 12:48–12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48-Exod12:49); ; [NUM 9:14](https://ref.ly/Num9:14)

### Its observance in the OT

These occasions are times of spiritual renewal, when the nation remembered how the LORD had saved them: [JOS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Josh5:10); ; [2KI 23:21–23:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:21-2Kgs23:23); ; [2CH 30:1–30:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:1-2Chr30:5), [2CH 30:13–30:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:13-2Chr30:20); ; [EZR 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:19-Ezra6:21)

### Ezekiel's vision of the future observance of the Passover

[EZK 45:21–45:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:21-Ezek45:24)

The Passover in the NT
----------------------

### Its observance

[LUK 2:41–2:42](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41-Luke2:42); ; [JHN 6:4](https://ref.ly/John6:4); ; [ACT 12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4)

### It was celebrated by Jesus Christ

**[LUK 22:15](https://ref.ly/Luke22:15)** *At Passover time Jesus Christ himself was sacrificed and he inaugurated the Lord's Supper.* *See also* [MAT 26:17–26:19](https://ref.ly/Matt26:17-Matt26:19) parallels [MRK 14:12–14:16](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12-Mark14:16), [LUK 22:7–22:13](https://ref.ly/Luke22:7-Luke22:13); [JHN 2:23](https://ref.ly/John2:23) *Jesus Christ observed the Passover like other Jews.*

### Jesus Christ identified with the Passover lamb

**[1CO 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7)** *See also* [JHN 1:29](https://ref.ly/John1:29), [JHN 1:36](https://ref.ly/John1:36), [JHN 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36) *This may refer to the Passover victim*; [EXO 12:46](https://ref.ly/Exod12:46); [REV 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5-Rev5:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 12:25-27; Exodus 12:2-11; Exodus 12:46-47; Numbers 9:1-5; Deuteronomy 16:1-8; Exodus 34:25; John 11:55; Numbers 9:6-13; Exodus 12:43-45; Exodus 12:48-49; Numbers 9:14; Joshua 5:10; 2 Kings 23:21-23; 2 Chronicles 30:1-5; 2 Chronicles 30:13-20; Ezra 6:19-21; Ezekiel 45:21-24; Luke 2:41-42; John 6:4; Acts 12:4; Luke 22:15; Matthew 26:17-19; Mark 14:12-16; Luke 22:7-13; John 2:23; 1 Corinthians 5:7; John 1:29; John 1:36; John 19:36; Exodus 12:46; Revelation 5:5-6

## 7408: Pentecost (id: 7408)

One of Israel's three major agricultural festivals, the second great feast of the Jewish year. It is a harvest festival, also known as the Feast of Weeks. The original Jewish significance of this festival has been overshadowed by the Christian celebration of the coming of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost.

Pentecost was one of Israel's three major agricultural festivals
----------------------------------------------------------------

### It was also known as the Feast of Weeks

**[DEU 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:10)** *See also* [EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22); [DEU 16:16–16:17](https://ref.ly/Deut16:16-Deut16:17); [2CH 8:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:13)

### It is also referred to as the Feast of Harvest

[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)

Instructions for the celebration of Pentecost
---------------------------------------------

### Its timing and sacrifices

[LEV 23:15–23:21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:21) *The “fifty days” (verse 16\) gives rise to the term “Pentecost” which is derived from the Greek word for “fiftieth”*; ; [NUM 28:26–28:31](https://ref.ly/Num28:26-Num28:31); ; [JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24)

### Its link with the deliverance from Egypt

**[DEU 16:12](https://ref.ly/Deut16:12)** *Pentecost later became associated with covenant renewal and the giving of the Law.*

Pentecost in the NT
-------------------

### It was observed by Paul

*See also* [ACT 20:16](https://ref.ly/Acts20:16); [1CO 16:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:8)

### Its association with the coming of the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:4), [ACT 2:16–2:21](https://ref.ly/Acts2:16-Acts2:21), [ACT 11:15](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15)** *See also* [JOL 2:28–2:32](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28-Joel2:32)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 16:9-10; Exodus 34:22; Deuteronomy 16:16-17; 2 Chronicles 8:13; Exodus 23:16; Leviticus 23:15-21; Numbers 28:26-31; Jeremiah 5:24; Deuteronomy 16:12; Acts 20:16; 1 Corinthians 16:8; Acts 2:1-4; Acts 2:16-21; Acts 11:15; Joel 2:28-32
* **Associated Articles:** 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3040: Holy Spirit, promise of (ID: `3040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3242: Holy Spirit, baptism with (ID: `3242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3257: Holy Spirit, gift of (ID: `3257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church (ID: `3275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4018: life, spiritual (ID: `4018@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4951: month (ID: `4951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7361: Feast of Weeks (ID: `7361@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7410: phylactery (id: 7410)

A leather box containing four sections of the law ([Ex 11:13–21](https://ref.ly/Exod11:13-Exod11:21)) written on parchment, and which Jewish men tied to the forehead and left arm (close to the heart) before morning prayers. The practice of wearing phylacteries arose, probably after the exile, from a literal interpretation by pious Jews of passages such as [Ex 13:9](https://ref.ly/Exod13:9).

The significance of wearing phylacteries
----------------------------------------

### A reminder of God's deliverance

**[EXO 13:11–13:16](https://ref.ly/Exod13:11-Exod13:16)** *See also* [EXO 13:1–13:10](https://ref.ly/Exod13:1-Exod13:10)

### A reminder of God's requirements

**[DEU 11:13–11:21](https://ref.ly/Deut11:13-Deut11:21)** *Phylacteries, when worn, were to be an outward sign of the importance of taking God's word into the heart*; **[PSA 119:11](https://ref.ly/Ps119:11)**; **[PRO 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Prov3:1-Prov3:3)** *See also* [DEU 6:4–6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:4-Deut6:9), [DEU 30:14](https://ref.ly/Deut30:14); [ROM 10:8](https://ref.ly/Rom10:8); [DEU 32:46](https://ref.ly/Deut32:46); [PSA 37:31](https://ref.ly/Ps37:31), [PSA 40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:8); [PRO 6:21](https://ref.ly/Prov6:21), [PRO 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Prov7:2-Prov7:3); [JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33)

### A sign of allegiance and belonging

**[ISA 44:5](https://ref.ly/Isa44:5)**; **[REV 7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:3)** *See also* [SNG 8:6](https://ref.ly/Song8:6); [REV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Rev9:4), [REV 14:1](https://ref.ly/Rev14:1)

The practice of wearing phylacteries
------------------------------------

**[MAT 23:5](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5)** *Jesus Christ condemns the Pharisees for wearing phylacteries for outward show.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 11:13-21; Exodus 13:9; Exodus 13:11-16; Exodus 13:1-10; Deuteronomy 11:13-21; Psalms 119:11; Proverbs 3:1-3; Deuteronomy 6:4-9; Deuteronomy 30:14; Romans 10:8; Deuteronomy 32:46; Psalms 37:31; Psalms 40:8; Proverbs 6:21; Proverbs 7:2-3; Jeremiah 31:33; Isaiah 44:5; Revelation 7:3; Song of Songs 8:6; Revelation 9:4; Revelation 14:1; Matthew 23:5
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5012: heart (ID: `5012@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5035: mind (ID: `5035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7394: memorial (ID: `7394@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7412: priesthood (id: 7412)

The body of people responsible for priestly duties, especially in relation to worship and sacrifice. The OT identifies Aaron and his heirs as having special priestly duties. In the NT, all believers are understood to share in this priesthood. Jesus Christ fulfils the OT concept of priesthood by being both the high priest and sacrifice, through which forgiveness of sins is completely achieved.

* **Associated Articles:** 7413: priesthood, in OT (ID: `7413@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7414: priesthood, in NT (ID: `7414@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7413: priesthood, in OT (id: 7413)

The institution of the priesthood or the particular body of people responsible for priestly duties.

The priesthood as an institution
--------------------------------

### It was a perpetual institution

**[EXO 40:15](https://ref.ly/Exod40:15)** *See also* [NUM 18:1](https://ref.ly/Num18:1), [NUM 25:13](https://ref.ly/Num25:13)

### It was distinct from the laity

**[NUM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Num3:10)**

### It was subject to the king's authority

**[1KI 2:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:27)**

### It was a privilege

**[NUM 18:7](https://ref.ly/Num18:7)** *See also* [EXO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Exod29:9); [NUM 16:10](https://ref.ly/Num16:10); [JOS 18:7](https://ref.ly/Josh18:7)

The priesthood as the body of people responsible for priestly duties
--------------------------------------------------------------------

### The Aaronic priesthood

**[EXO 28:41](https://ref.ly/Exod28:41)** *See also* [1CH 6:49](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:49); [NEH 10:38](https://ref.ly/Neh10:38)

### The Shiloh priesthood

**[JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1)** *See also* [1SA 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:14); [1KI 2:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:27); [PSA 78:60](https://ref.ly/Ps78:60) *The Shiloh priesthood ended when the Philistines took Shiloh (see [1Sa 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:17))*; [JER 7:12](https://ref.ly/Jer7:12)

### The Melchizedek priesthood

**[PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4)** *See also* [GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18); [HEB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Heb5:10), [HEB 7:11–7:17](https://ref.ly/Heb7:11-Heb7:17)

### The Anathoth priesthood

**[JER 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jer1:1)** *See also* [JOS 21:18](https://ref.ly/Josh21:18); [1KI 2:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:27)

### The Zadokite priesthood

**[EZK 40:46](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:46)** *The priestly line of Zadok lasted until 170 B.C.*

### The Levitical priesthood

**[DEU 17:9](https://ref.ly/Deut17:9)** *The term “the priests, who are Levites” appears only in Deuteronomy. Some think it reflects a situation in which all Levites were priests. Not all passages support this view, however, and the phrase may simply distinguish the ministry of the Levitical priests from the priesthood of the king.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 40:15; Numbers 18:1; Numbers 25:13; Numbers 3:10; 1 Kings 2:27; Numbers 18:7; Exodus 29:9; Numbers 16:10; Joshua 18:7; Exodus 28:41; 1 Chronicles 6:49; Nehemiah 10:38; Joshua 18:1; 1 Samuel 3:14; Psalms 78:60; 1 Samuel 4:17; Jeremiah 7:12; Psalms 110:4; Genesis 14:18; Hebrews 5:10; Hebrews 7:11-17; Jeremiah 1:1; Joshua 21:18; Ezekiel 40:46; Deuteronomy 17:9

## 7414: priesthood, in NT (id: 7414)

Jesus Christ recognised the function of the OT priesthood, but the gospel belief in Christ as high priest and the priesthood of all believers superseded the earlier concept.

Jesus Christ acknowledged the role of the Jerusalem priesthood
--------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [MAT 8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:4) parallels [MRK 1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:44), [LUK 5:14](https://ref.ly/Luke5:14)

Characteristics of the Levitical priesthood
-------------------------------------------

### It was equivalent to the order of Aaron

**[HEB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Heb7:11)**

### It was incompatible with membership of the tribe of Judah

**[HEB 7:14](https://ref.ly/Heb7:14)**

### It was not established by divine oath

**[HEB 7:20](https://ref.ly/Heb7:20)**

Characteristics of the Melchizedek priesthood
---------------------------------------------

**[HEB 7:15–7:16](https://ref.ly/Heb7:15-Heb7:16)**

How Jesus Christ's priesthood resembled the OT priesthood
---------------------------------------------------------

### He fulfilled the requirement of humanity

**[HEB 2:17](https://ref.ly/Heb2:17)** *See also* [HEB 5:1](https://ref.ly/Heb5:1)

### He was not self\-appointed

**[HEB 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Heb5:4-Heb5:5)**

### He resembled Melchizedek

**[HEB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Heb5:10)**

How Jesus Christ's priesthood contrasts with the Levitical priesthood
---------------------------------------------------------------------

### It involves a change in the applicability of the law

**[HEB 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Heb7:12-Heb7:13)**

### It lasts for ever

**[HEB 7:23–7:24](https://ref.ly/Heb7:23-Heb7:24)** *See also* [HEB 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Heb10:11-Heb10:12)

### It was based on a once\-for\-all sacrifice, not of animals but of himself

**[HEB 9:26](https://ref.ly/Heb9:26)** *See also* [HEB 9:12](https://ref.ly/Heb9:12)

### It is effective, not simply illustrative

**[HEB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:14)** *See also* [HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Heb8:5), [HEB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1), [HEB 10:4](https://ref.ly/Heb10:4)

### It is not that of a mere man, but of the Son of God

**[HEB 7:28](https://ref.ly/Heb7:28)** *See also* [HEB 4:14](https://ref.ly/Heb4:14), [HEB 7:26](https://ref.ly/Heb7:26)

Characteristics of the priesthood of all believers
--------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)**

### They are to reflect the holiness of their great high priest

**[1PE 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:15)** *See also* [HEB 10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:10)

### They are to offer spiritual sacrifices

**[1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5)** *See also* [ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1); [PHP 4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:18); [HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)

### They are to intercede for others before God

**[1TI 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1)** *See also* [REV 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8), [REV 5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:10)

### They are to represent God before other human beings

**[2CO 5:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:20)** *See also* [EPH 3:7–3:11](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7-Eph3:11)

## 7416: purification (id: 7416)

Cleansing from sin or uncleanness, by means of ritual washing under the old covenant, but through the blood of Jesus Christ under the new covenant.

Purification from uncleanness
-----------------------------

### After contact with something unclean

**[LEV 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Lev11:39-Lev11:40)** *See also* [LEV 15:1–15:12](https://ref.ly/Lev15:1-Lev15:12), [LEV 15:19–15:23](https://ref.ly/Lev15:19-Lev15:23), [LEV 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15-Lev17:16); [NUM 19:10–19:20](https://ref.ly/Num19:10-Num19:20)

### After disease

**[LEV 15:13–15:15](https://ref.ly/Lev15:13-Lev15:15)** *See also* [LEV 14:1–14:32](https://ref.ly/Lev14:1-Lev14:32)

### After childbirth and menstruation

**[LUK 2:22](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22)** *See also* [LEV 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:8); [2SA 11:2–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:4)

Purification from sin
---------------------

**[1JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/1John1:7)** *See also* [NUM 19:1–19:9](https://ref.ly/Num19:1-Num19:9); [JOB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Job1:5); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3), [HEB 9:16–9:24](https://ref.ly/Heb9:16-Heb9:24); [1JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:9)

----------------------

### Priests and Levites

**[EZR 6:20](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:20)** *See also* [EXO 30:18–30:21](https://ref.ly/Exod30:18-Exod30:21); [LEV 16:24–16:28](https://ref.ly/Lev16:24-Lev16:28); [NUM 8:5–8:22](https://ref.ly/Num8:5-Num8:22), [NUM 19:7](https://ref.ly/Num19:7); [2CH 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:6); [NEH 12:30](https://ref.ly/Neh12:30), [NEH 13:22](https://ref.ly/Neh13:22), [NEH 13:30](https://ref.ly/Neh13:30)

### Lay people

**[ACT 21:24–21:26](https://ref.ly/Acts21:24-Acts21:26)** *See also* [GEN 35:1–35:2](https://ref.ly/Gen35:1-Gen35:2); [NUM 19:20](https://ref.ly/Num19:20), [NUM 31:19](https://ref.ly/Num31:19); [2CH 30:13–30:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:13-2Chr30:20); [ISA 66:17](https://ref.ly/Isa66:17)

Purification of inanimate objects
---------------------------------

### The temple

**[2CH 34:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:8)** *See also* [2CH 29:15–29:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:15-2Chr29:19); [NEH 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/Neh13:6-Neh13:9); [EZK 45:18–45:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:18-Ezek45:20)

### Sacred objects

**[EXO 29:36](https://ref.ly/Exod29:36)** *See also* [LEV 8:15](https://ref.ly/Lev8:15); [1CH 23:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:28)

### Other items

**[LEV 14:48–14:53](https://ref.ly/Lev14:48-Lev14:53)** *See also* [NUM 31:19–31:24](https://ref.ly/Num31:19-Num31:24)

-------------------------------

**[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)** *See also* [MAL 3:3](https://ref.ly/Mal3:3)

Purification of the inner person
--------------------------------

**[2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1)** *See also* [MRK 7:2–7:8](https://ref.ly/Mark7:2-Mark7:8); [ACT 15:5–15:11](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5-Acts15:11); [TIT 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:14); [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8); [1PE 1:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:22); [1JN 3:3](https://ref.ly/1John3:3)

## 7418: Purim (id: 7418)

A Jewish festival of rejoicing and feasting celebrated in the month of Adar (February\-March) commemorating the deliverance of the Jews recorded in the book of Esther.

The origins of Purim
--------------------

### Esther's position as queen

[EST 4:14](https://ref.ly/Esth4:14)

### Haman's death

[EST 7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:10)

### The right of the Jews to avenge themselves on their enemies

[EST 8:11](https://ref.ly/Esth8:11)

### Explanation of the name

**[EST 9:26](https://ref.ly/Esth9:26)** *The word “pur”, meaning “lots”, relates to the lots cast to determine the time set for the massacre.*

The date for celebrating Purim
------------------------------

*See also* [EST 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/Esth9:17-Esth9:19) *those living in Susa celebrated on 15th Adar, those in rural towns on 14th*

The establishment of Purim
--------------------------

[EST 9:27](https://ref.ly/Esth9:27), [EST 3:7](https://ref.ly/Esth3:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Esther 4:14; Esther 7:10; Esther 8:11; Esther 9:26; Esther 9:17-19; Esther 9:27; Esther 3:7
* **Associated Articles:** 1355: providence (ID: `1355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4951: month (ID: `4951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6634: deliverance (ID: `6634@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7354: feasts and festivals (ID: `7354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7505: Jews, the (ID: `7505@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7420: Rabbi (id: 7420)

An honorific title, best translated as “master” or “teacher”, and used to refer to distinguished teachers of the Jewish law. Jesus Christ is called Rabbi in the Gospels, indicating that he was regarded as a respected teacher by his audiences.

John the Baptist's addressed as Rabbi by his disciples
------------------------------------------------------

[JHN 3:26](https://ref.ly/John3:26)

Jesus Christ addressed as Rabbi
-------------------------------

### By two of John the Baptist's disciples

**[JHN 1:38](https://ref.ly/John1:38)**

### By his own disciples

**[JHN 4:31](https://ref.ly/John4:31)** *See also* [JHN 6:25](https://ref.ly/John6:25), [JHN 9:2](https://ref.ly/John9:2), [JHN 11:8](https://ref.ly/John11:8)

### By Peter

**[MRK 9:5](https://ref.ly/Mark9:5)** *See also* [MRK 11:21](https://ref.ly/Mark11:21)

### By Nathanael and Nicodemus

[JHN 1:49](https://ref.ly/John1:49), [JHN 3:2](https://ref.ly/John3:2)

### By Judas Iscariot

**[MAT 26:25](https://ref.ly/Matt26:25)** *At the Last Supper*; **[MAT 26:49](https://ref.ly/Matt26:49)** parallel [MRK 14:45](https://ref.ly/Mark14:45) *In the Garden of Gethsemane.*

Jesus Christ addressed as Rabboni, the Aramaic form of Rabbi
------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [MRK 10:51](https://ref.ly/Mark10:51); [JHN 20:16](https://ref.ly/John20:16)

Jesus Christ taught that his disciples were not to be called Rabbi
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 23:7–23:8](https://ref.ly/Matt23:7-Matt23:8)**

* **Associated Passages:** John 3:26; John 1:38; John 4:31; John 6:25; John 9:2; John 11:8; Mark 9:5; Mark 11:21; John 1:49; John 3:2; Matthew 26:25; Matthew 26:49; Mark 14:45; Mark 10:51; John 20:16; Matthew 23:7-8
* **Associated Articles:** 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of (ID: `2203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7464: teachers of the law (ID: `7464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7793: teachers (ID: `7793@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7422: ritual (id: 7422)

The regulations governing Jewish religious life and worship, especially sacrifices, ritual cleanliness and food laws. By the sacrificial death of Jesus Christ these rituals have been fulfilled and play no part in the new covenant.

The purpose of ritual sacrifices
--------------------------------

### To atone for sin

**[LEV 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/Lev5:12-Lev5:13)** *See also* [EXO 29:38–29:43](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:43); [LEV 1:3–1:13](https://ref.ly/Lev1:3-Lev1:13), [LEV 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:1-Lev4:3), [LEV 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13-Lev4:14), [LEV 4:20–4:23](https://ref.ly/Lev4:20-Lev4:23), [LEV 4:26–4:28](https://ref.ly/Lev4:26-Lev4:28), [LEV 4:31–4:35](https://ref.ly/Lev4:31-Lev4:35), [LEV 5:13](https://ref.ly/Lev5:13), [LEV 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:7), [LEV 7:1–7:7](https://ref.ly/Lev7:1-Lev7:7), [LEV 16:1–16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:1-Lev16:34)

### To maintain fellowship between God and his people

[LEV 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1-Lev3:5), [LEV 7:11–7:15](https://ref.ly/Lev7:11-Lev7:15) *The offering was part burnt and part eaten in a fellowship meal and expressed covenant relationship with God.*

### To express worship and gratitude to God

The grain offering was the only bloodless offering but it accompanied the other offerings and was burnt whole to express gratitude to God for the harvest and for particular blessings: [LEV 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Lev2:1-Lev2:3), [LEV 2:8–2:16](https://ref.ly/Lev2:8-Lev2:16), [LEV 6:14–6:23](https://ref.ly/Lev6:14-Lev6:23), [LEV 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Lev7:9-Lev7:10), [LEV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Lev9:4); ; [NUM 6:14–6:17](https://ref.ly/Num6:14-Num6:17), [NUM 28:3–28:13](https://ref.ly/Num28:3-Num28:13)

Rituals for purification from ritual uncleanness
------------------------------------------------

### Childbirth

**[LUK 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22-Luke2:24)** *See also* [LEV 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:8)

### Unclean diseases

**[MRK 1:40–1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40-Mark1:44)** parallels [MAT 8:2–8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:4), [LUK 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12-Luke5:14) *See also* [LEV 14:2–14:7](https://ref.ly/Lev14:2-Lev14:7)

### Unclean discharges

[LEV 15:32–15:33](https://ref.ly/Lev15:32-Lev15:33)

### Touching dead bodies

[NUM 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/Num19:11-Num19:13)

Rituals relating to food
------------------------

### Clean and unclean foods

**[LEV 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:2)** *See also* [LEV 11:44–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:44-Lev11:47)

### The eating of blood is forbidden

[GEN 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Gen9:4-Gen9:5); ; [LEV 17:11–17:12](https://ref.ly/Lev17:11-Lev17:12); ; [ACT 15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:20)

Rituals relating to the priesthood
----------------------------------

**[LEV 8:30](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30)** *See also* [EXO 29:1–29:9](https://ref.ly/Exod29:1-Exod29:9)

Rituals relating to the place of worship
----------------------------------------

**[2CH 29:4–29:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:4-2Chr29:5)** *See also* [LEV 16:15–16:20](https://ref.ly/Lev16:15-Lev16:20); [1KI 8:62–8:63](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:62-1Kgs8:63) parallel [2CH 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:4-2Chr7:5); [EZR 6:16–6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:16-Ezra6:17)

Rituals relating to special days
--------------------------------

### Festivals

[LEV 23:37](https://ref.ly/Lev23:37)

### The Sabbath

[EXO 31:12–31:17](https://ref.ly/Exod31:12-Exod31:17); ; [MAT 12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:2) parallels [MRK 2:24](https://ref.ly/Mark2:24), [LUK 6:2](https://ref.ly/Luke6:2)

The initiation ritual of circumcision
-------------------------------------

[GEN 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10-Gen17:14)

Ritual only has meaning when accompanied by obedience
-----------------------------------------------------

Rituals of the old covenant are fulfilled by Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17)** *See also* [MRK 2:27–2:28](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27-Mark2:28); [COL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Col2:11); [HEB 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1-Heb10:3), [HEB 10:8–10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:8-Heb10:10)

Old covenant rituals are abolished under the new covenant
---------------------------------------------------------

## 7424: ritual law (id: 7424)

The rules and regulations regarding the conduct of the priests and people of Israel in approaching God, especially in worship. They were to be holy as God is holy. The perfect sacrifice of Jesus Christ on the cross is seen by NT writers as fulfilling this demand for holiness and obedience.

The ritual law and the priesthood
---------------------------------

### The priests were to be careful to obey God's commandments

**[LEV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Lev22:9)** *See also* [LEV 10:10](https://ref.ly/Lev10:10), [LEV 22:3](https://ref.ly/Lev22:3)

### The priests had to avoid ceremonial uncleanness

Contact with the dead caused uncleanness and was forbidden to the high priest: [LEV 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:3), [LEV 21:11](https://ref.ly/Lev21:11); ; [NUM 19:11](https://ref.ly/Num19:11), [NUM 19:14](https://ref.ly/Num19:14); [LEV 21:16–21:23](https://ref.ly/Lev21:16-Lev21:23) *No\-one with a physical defect could serve as a priest*; ; [LEV 22:20–22:25](https://ref.ly/Lev22:20-Lev22:25) *The sacrifices offered by the priests also had to be without defect.*

The ritual law and the people of Israel
---------------------------------------

### The people were called to holiness

**[LEV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Lev19:2), [LEV 20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:26)** *See also* [LEV 11:44–11:45](https://ref.ly/Lev11:44-Lev11:45), [LEV 20:7](https://ref.ly/Lev20:7), [LEV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Lev21:8), [LEV 22:32](https://ref.ly/Lev22:32)

### The ritual law stipulated what they could eat

**[LEV 11:46–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:46-Lev11:47)** *See also* [GEN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Gen7:2), [GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20); [LEV 11:1–11:23](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:23) parallel [DEU 14:3–14:20](https://ref.ly/Deut14:3-Deut14:20); [DEU 14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:21)

### The ritual law regarding childbirth

[LEV 12:1–12:5](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:5); ; [LUK 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22-Luke2:24)

Jesus Christ's obedience and perfect sacrifice on the cross fulfilled the demands of the ritual law
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 10:8–10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:8-Heb10:10)** *See also* [EPH 2:15](https://ref.ly/Eph2:15); [COL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Col2:14); [HEB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Heb7:11), [HEB 7:18](https://ref.ly/Heb7:18), [HEB 7:27](https://ref.ly/Heb7:27), [HEB 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1-Heb10:3), [HEB 10:11–10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:11-Heb10:14)

## 7426: ritual washing (id: 7426)

The act of washing for consecration or for purification from uncleanness. It involved washing all or part of the body or one's clothing. Jesus Christ's attitude to the Pharisees reflected, not a disavowal of ritual washing, but disapproval of their emphasis on the outward, rather than inward, forms of religion.

Ritual washing for consecration
-------------------------------

**[EXO 19:10–19:11](https://ref.ly/Exod19:10-Exod19:11)** *See also* [EXO 19:14](https://ref.ly/Exod19:14), [EXO 40:12–40:15](https://ref.ly/Exod40:12-Exod40:15); [NUM 8:5–8:7](https://ref.ly/Num8:5-Num8:7), [NUM 8:21–8:22](https://ref.ly/Num8:21-Num8:22)

Ritual washing for cleansing
----------------------------

**[LEV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15)** *See also* [LEV 13:6](https://ref.ly/Lev13:6), [LEV 13:34](https://ref.ly/Lev13:34), [LEV 15:16–15:18](https://ref.ly/Lev15:16-Lev15:18), [LEV 15:21–15:23](https://ref.ly/Lev15:21-Lev15:23); [NUM 19:10](https://ref.ly/Num19:10), [NUM 19:19](https://ref.ly/Num19:19)

Methods of ritual washing
-------------------------

### Washing clothes

**[NUM 31:24](https://ref.ly/Num31:24)** *See also* [LEV 6:27](https://ref.ly/Lev6:27), [LEV 11:24–11:28](https://ref.ly/Lev11:24-Lev11:28), [LEV 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Lev11:39-Lev11:40), [LEV 13:58](https://ref.ly/Lev13:58), [LEV 14:43–14:47](https://ref.ly/Lev14:43-Lev14:47); [NUM 19:21](https://ref.ly/Num19:21); [REV 7:14](https://ref.ly/Rev7:14), [REV 22:14](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14)

### Bathing

**[LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9)** *See also* [LEV 15:4–15:13](https://ref.ly/Lev15:4-Lev15:13), [LEV 15:27](https://ref.ly/Lev15:27), [LEV 16:26–16:28](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26-Lev16:28), [LEV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15); [NUM 19:7–19:8](https://ref.ly/Num19:7-Num19:8); [DEU 23:9–23:11](https://ref.ly/Deut23:9-Deut23:11); [2KI 5:9–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:9-2Kgs5:14); [PSA 51:7](https://ref.ly/Ps51:7); [HEB 10:19–10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:22)

### Sprinkling with the water of purification

**[NUM 19:17–19:19](https://ref.ly/Num19:17-Num19:19)** *See also* [NUM 8:5–8:7](https://ref.ly/Num8:5-Num8:7), [NUM 19:13](https://ref.ly/Num19:13); [EZK 36:24–36:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:24-Ezek36:25)

### Washing hands and feet

**[EXO 30:17–30:21](https://ref.ly/Exod30:17-Exod30:21)** *See also* [EXO 40:30–40:32](https://ref.ly/Exod40:30-Exod40:32); [DEU 21:6–21:9](https://ref.ly/Deut21:6-Deut21:9); [JHN 13:5–13:10](https://ref.ly/John13:5-John13:10); [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8)

Consequences of the failure to perform ritual washing
-----------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 19:20](https://ref.ly/Num19:20)** *See also* [LEV 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15-Lev17:16); [NUM 19:13](https://ref.ly/Num19:13)

The NT perception of ritual washing
-----------------------------------

### It was practised in NT times

**[JHN 2:6](https://ref.ly/John2:6)** *See also* [JHN 3:25](https://ref.ly/John3:25)

### The Pharisees over\-emphasised its importance

**[MAT 15:1–15:11](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:11)** parallel [MRK 7:1–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:23) *See also* [LUK 11:37–11:41](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37-Luke11:41)

### Jesus Christ's atoning work rendered it unnecessary

**[HEB 9:6–9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:6-Heb9:14)**

## 7428: Sabbath (id: 7428)

The day of rest laid down for the people of God. The OT treated the seventh day of the week (Saturday) as the Sabbath, a custom continued in modern Judaism. The Christian church, in recognition of the importance of the resurrection of Jesus Christ, observed a day of rest on the first day of the week (Sunday).

* **Associated Articles:** 7429: Sabbath, in OT (ID: `7429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7430: Sabbath, in NT (ID: `7430@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7431: Sabbath, sabbatical year (ID: `7431@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7429: Sabbath, in OT (id: 7429)

The Sabbath of rest is grounded in God's work of creation. Observance of a Sabbath day is distinctive of the people of God.

The Sabbath grounded in creation itself
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:3](https://ref.ly/Gen2:3)** *See also* [PSA 118:24](https://ref.ly/Ps118:24)

The purpose of the Sabbath
--------------------------

### To remember God's work in creation

**[EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11)** *See also* [GEN 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gen2:2); [EXO 35:2](https://ref.ly/Exod35:2)

### To remember the exodus

**[DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15)** *The idea of rest relates the concept of the Sabbath to the ultimate conquest of Canaan under David and to the return from exile.* *See also* [GEN 8:4](https://ref.ly/Gen8:4); [2SA 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:1), [2SA 7:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:11); [PSA 95:10–95:11](https://ref.ly/Ps95:10-Ps95:11); [HEB 4:9](https://ref.ly/Heb4:9); [REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13)

### To be a sign of the relationship between Israel and God and to give refreshment

**[EXO 31:17](https://ref.ly/Exod31:17)** *See also* [DEU 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:14)

The Law required the Sabbath to be a holy day free from work
------------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 23:3](https://ref.ly/Lev23:3)** *See also* [EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21), [EXO 35:3](https://ref.ly/Exod35:3); [LEV 23:38](https://ref.ly/Lev23:38); [ISA 56:2](https://ref.ly/Isa56:2), [ISA 58:13](https://ref.ly/Isa58:13)

The Sabbath was linked with celebration of the New Moon
-------------------------------------------------------

**[2KI 4:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:23)** *See also* [ISA 1:13](https://ref.ly/Isa1:13); [EZK 46:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:3); [HOS 2:11](https://ref.ly/Hos2:11); [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

Abuses of the Sabbath
---------------------

[EXO 16:27–16:28](https://ref.ly/Exod16:27-Exod16:28) *gathering manna*; ; [NUM 15:32](https://ref.ly/Num15:32) *gathering wood*; ; [NEH 13:15–13:18](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15-Neh13:18) *doing ordinary work*; [NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31); ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5); [JER 17:21](https://ref.ly/Jer17:21) *carrying loads*

Punishments for infringing the Sabbath law
------------------------------------------

### The death penalty

[EXO 31:14](https://ref.ly/Exod31:14); ; [NUM 15:35](https://ref.ly/Num15:35)

### Disaster for Jerusalem

[JER 17:27](https://ref.ly/Jer17:27); ; [EZK 20:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:13), [EZK 22:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:8), [EZK 22:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:15)

Sacrifices to be offered on the Sabbath
---------------------------------------

### Bread

[LEV 24:8](https://ref.ly/Lev24:8); ; [1CH 9:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:32)

### Burnt offerings

[NUM 28:9–28:10](https://ref.ly/Num28:9-Num28:10); ; [1CH 23:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:31); ; [2CH 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:4)

### Other offerings

[EZK 46:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:3; Psalms 118:24; Exodus 20:8-11; Genesis 2:2; Exodus 35:2; Deuteronomy 5:12-15; Genesis 8:4; 2 Samuel 7:1; 2 Samuel 7:11; Psalms 95:10-11; Hebrews 4:9; Revelation 14:13; Exodus 31:17; Deuteronomy 5:12-14; Leviticus 23:3; Exodus 34:21; Exodus 35:3; Leviticus 23:38; Isaiah 56:2; Isaiah 58:13; 2 Kings 4:23; Isaiah 1:13; Ezekiel 46:3; Hosea 2:11; Amos 8:5; Exodus 16:27-28; Numbers 15:32; Nehemiah 13:15-18; Nehemiah 10:31; Jeremiah 17:21; Exodus 31:14; Numbers 15:35; Jeremiah 17:27; Ezekiel 20:13; Ezekiel 22:8; Ezekiel 22:15; Leviticus 24:8; 1 Chronicles 9:32; Numbers 28:9-10; 1 Chronicles 23:31; 2 Chronicles 2:4; Ezekiel 46:4
* **Associated Articles:** 4005: creation (ID: `4005@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4975: week (ID: `4975@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5056: rest (ID: `5056@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5378: law, OT (ID: `5378@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7430: Sabbath, in NT (id: 7430)

The NT develops the OT teaching on the Sabbath in three important directions. It declares that the Sabbath should not be observed in a legalistic manner; the Sabbath\-rest is treated as an important symbol of the Christian doctrine of salvation; and finally, the NT itself indicates how Sunday, rather than Saturday, came to be seen as the Christian Sabbath.

Gospel incidents connected with the Sabbath
-------------------------------------------

### Exorcism

[MRK 1:21–1:25](https://ref.ly/Mark1:21-Mark1:25) parallel [LUK 4:31–4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:31-Luke4:35)

### Healing

[MAT 12:9–12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:9-Matt12:14) parallels [MRK 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:1-Mark3:6), [LUK 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:6-Luke6:11); ; [MRK 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Mark1:30-Mark1:31) parallel [LUK 4:38–4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:38-Luke4:40); ; [LUK 13:10–13:17](https://ref.ly/Luke13:10-Luke13:17), [LUK 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1-Luke14:6); ; [JHN 5:5–5:18](https://ref.ly/John5:5-John5:18), [JHN 9:1–9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:16)

### Teaching

[MRK 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2) parallel [MAT 13:54](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54); ; [LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16)

### Other references

[MAT 28:1](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1) parallel [MRK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Mark16:1); ; [LUK 23:55–23:56](https://ref.ly/Luke23:55-Luke23:56); ; [JHN 12:2](https://ref.ly/John12:2)

Jesus Christ's teaching regarding the Sabbath
---------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ observes the Sabbath regulation

**[LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16)** *See also* [MAT 24:20](https://ref.ly/Matt24:20); [ACT 1:12](https://ref.ly/Acts1:12)

### Human well\-being is more important than rigid observance of the Law

**[MRK 2:27–2:28](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27-Mark2:28)** *See also* [MAT 12:3](https://ref.ly/Matt12:3)

### Ceremonial observance must give way before any higher, or more spiritual, motive

**[MAT 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Matt12:5-Matt12:6)** *See also* [LUK 6:5](https://ref.ly/Luke6:5)

Sabbath reading of Scripture provided an opportunity for reaching the Jews
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2)** *See also* [ACT 13:14](https://ref.ly/Acts13:14), [ACT 13:27](https://ref.ly/Acts13:27), [ACT 13:42](https://ref.ly/Acts13:42), [ACT 13:44](https://ref.ly/Acts13:44), [ACT 15:21](https://ref.ly/Acts15:21), [ACT 16:13](https://ref.ly/Acts16:13), [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4)

Sabbath observance was optional for Gentile Christians
------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16)**

The Lord's Day
--------------

**[REV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10)** *At an early stage the Sabbath was replaced by Sunday (the first day of the week) as the day for rest and worship.* *See also* [JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/John20:19), [JHN 20:26](https://ref.ly/John20:26); [ACT 20:7](https://ref.ly/Acts20:7); [1CO 16:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:2)

The Sabbath\-rest is seen as a symbol of the salvation of the people of God
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 4:1](https://ref.ly/Heb4:1)** *The OT promise of rest was unfulfilled, on account of Israel's disobedience*; **[HEB 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Heb3:18-Heb3:19), [HEB 4:9](https://ref.ly/Heb4:9)** *The OT promise of rest remains open.*

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 1:21-25; Luke 4:31-35; Matthew 12:9-14; Mark 3:1-6; Luke 6:6-11; Mark 1:30-31; Luke 4:38-40; Luke 13:10-17; Luke 14:1-6; John 5:5-18; John 9:1-16; Mark 6:2; Matthew 13:54; Luke 4:16; Matthew 28:1; Mark 16:1; Luke 23:55-56; John 12:2; Matthew 24:20; Acts 1:12; Mark 2:27-28; Matthew 12:3; Matthew 12:5-6; Luke 6:5; Acts 17:2; Acts 13:14; Acts 13:27; Acts 13:42; Acts 13:44; Acts 15:21; Acts 16:13; Acts 18:4; Colossians 2:16; Revelation 1:10; John 20:19; John 20:26; Acts 20:7; 1 Corinthians 16:2; Hebrews 4:1; Hebrews 3:18-19; Hebrews 4:9

## 7431: Sabbath, sabbatical year (id: 7431)

Just as the seventh day was observed as a day of rest, so the OT regarded every seventh year as a sabbath year, to be set aside for rest.

The sabbath year
----------------

### As a time for leaving fields fallow

**[EXO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:11)** *See also* [LEV 25:4](https://ref.ly/Lev25:4), [LEV 26:34](https://ref.ly/Lev26:34); [NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31) *An important feature was the opportunity for the poor to benefit from unharvested fields.*

### As a time for cancelling debts

**[DEU 15:1](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1)** *See also* [DEU 15:9](https://ref.ly/Deut15:9), [DEU 31:10](https://ref.ly/Deut31:10); [NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31)

### As a time for freeing slaves

**[DEU 15:12](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12)** *See also* [JER 34:14](https://ref.ly/Jer34:14)

The Year of Jubilee as seven Sabbaths
-------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:8–25:12](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:12)**

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 23:10-11; Leviticus 25:4; Leviticus 26:34; Nehemiah 10:31; Deuteronomy 15:1; Deuteronomy 15:9; Deuteronomy 31:10; Deuteronomy 15:12; Jeremiah 34:14; Leviticus 25:8-12
* **Associated Articles:** 1653: numbers, 6-10 (ID: `1653@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4206: land (ID: `4206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6658: freedom (ID: `6658@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7447: slavery, in OT (ID: `7447@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7482: Year of Jubilee (ID: `7482@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7434: sacrifice (id: 7434)

An important aspect of the relationship between God and humanity but whereas the OT describes many sacrifices, the NT announces the fulfilment of sacrifice in Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7436: sacrifice, NT fulfilment of (ID: `7436@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7435: sacrifice, in OT (id: 7435)

An act that involved offering to God the life of an animal. It expressed gratitude for God's goodness or acknowledgment of sin. It was also associated with establishing a covenant.

Sacrifice was an integral part of worship
-----------------------------------------

*See also* [GEN 46:1](https://ref.ly/Gen46:1); [EXO 10:24–10:26](https://ref.ly/Exod10:24-Exod10:26); [JDG 13:19](https://ref.ly/Judg13:19); [1SA 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:3); [1KI 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:4)

Sacrifices were a means of offering thanks to God
-------------------------------------------------

[GEN 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4) *Abel*; ; [GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20) *Noah delivered from the flood*; ; [EXO 18:12](https://ref.ly/Exod18:12) *Jethro after God had delivered the Israelites*; ; [JDG 11:31](https://ref.ly/Judg11:31) *Jephthah*; ; [1SA 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:15) *after the safe return of the ark*; [EZR 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:3), [EZR 8:35](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:35); [PSA 27:6](https://ref.ly/Ps27:6), [PSA 54:6](https://ref.ly/Ps54:6), [PSA 107:17–107:22](https://ref.ly/Ps107:17-Ps107:22)

Sacrifices were offered at regular religious festivals
------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 23:5–23:8](https://ref.ly/Lev23:5-Lev23:8) *Passover*; ; [LEV 23:18–23:20](https://ref.ly/Lev23:18-Lev23:20) *the Feast of Weeks*; ; [LEV 23:23–23:25](https://ref.ly/Lev23:23-Lev23:25) *the Feast of Trumpets*; [LEV 16:6–16:10](https://ref.ly/Lev16:6-Lev16:10), [LEV 23:26–23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:26-Lev23:32); [LEV 23:33–23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:33-Lev23:36) *the Feast of Tabernacles*

Special occasions were marked by sacrifices
-------------------------------------------

[1KI 8:63](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:63) *the dedication of Solomon's temple*; ; [2CH 29:31–29:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:31-2Chr29:33) *after Hezekiah had purified the temple*; ; [EZR 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:2-Ezra3:3) *the Jews who returned from the Babylonian exile*; ; [EZR 6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:17) *at the completion of the second temple*

Sacrifices as signs of individual and national penitence
--------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:1-Lev4:3), [LEV 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13-Lev4:14); ; [JDG 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/Judg2:1-Judg2:5), [JDG 20:26](https://ref.ly/Judg20:26); ; [1SA 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:8-1Sam7:9); ; [2SA 24:10–24:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:10-2Sam24:25)

The place of sacrifice
----------------------

### It was divinely chosen

**[DEU 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Deut12:13-Deut12:14)** *See also* [LEV 17:3–17:5](https://ref.ly/Lev17:3-Lev17:5); [DEU 12:2–12:6](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2-Deut12:6)

### Rival places of sacrifice caused the Israelites to sin

[1KI 12:28–12:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28-1Kgs12:29), [1KI 12:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:32); ; [2CH 15:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:17), [2CH 31:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:1) *Under Hezekiah the high places were destroyed.*

God condemned certain sacrifices
--------------------------------

### Sacrifices to other gods

**[1KI 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7-1Kgs11:8)** *See also* [NUM 25:1–25:3](https://ref.ly/Num25:1-Num25:3); [2KI 16:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:4), [2KI 16:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:15); [PSA 106:28](https://ref.ly/Ps106:28); [ISA 57:7](https://ref.ly/Isa57:7), [ISA 65:3](https://ref.ly/Isa65:3), [ISA 65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:7); [JER 19:4](https://ref.ly/Jer19:4)

### Human sacrifices

[LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21); ; [DEU 12:31](https://ref.ly/Deut12:31); ; [1KI 16:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:34); ; [2KI 3:26–3:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:26-2Kgs3:27), [2KI 16:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:3), [2KI 17:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:31), [2KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:6); ; [EZK 20:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:31)

Sacrifice to the LORD may be rejected
-------------------------------------

### If it is a substitute for obedience

[1SA 15:20–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:20-1Sam15:22); ; [JER 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Jer7:21-Jer7:22); ; [HOS 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Hos8:11-Hos8:13); ; [AMO 4:4](https://ref.ly/Amos4:4); ; [MRK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Mark12:33)

### If it is a substitute for justice and mercy

[ISA 66:2–66:3](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2-Isa66:3); ; [MIC 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:7-Mic6:8); ; [MAT 9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:13)

### If it is imperfect

[MAL 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:13-Mal1:14)

Blessing is promised when right sacrifices are offered
------------------------------------------------------

[GEN 22:15–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:15-Gen22:18); ; [PSA 4:5](https://ref.ly/Ps4:5), [PSA 50:14–50:15](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14-Ps50:15), [PSA 50:23](https://ref.ly/Ps50:23), [PSA 51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:17)

## 7436: sacrifice, NT fulfilment of (id: 7436)

For the people of the new covenant, sacrifice is fulfilled in Jesus Christ. Christians should have nothing to do with other sacrifices but are to bring their own “spiritual” offerings.

The OT points ahead to the fulfilment of sacrifice in Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------------------------

### A new sacrificial system

**[ISA 56:6–56:7](https://ref.ly/Isa56:6-Isa56:7)** *See also* [ISA 19:21](https://ref.ly/Isa19:21)

### The new people of God

[JER 33:18](https://ref.ly/Jer33:18); ; [EZK 20:40](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:40)

### The vision of the new temple

[EZK 40:46](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:46)

The perfect sacrifice of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ perceived his death as a sacrifice

**[MAT 26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:28)** parallels [MRK 14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20), [1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25) *See also* [HEB 5:1](https://ref.ly/Heb5:1)

### The contrast with the OT sacrificial system

**[HEB 7:27](https://ref.ly/Heb7:27)** *See also* [HEB 9:23–9:26](https://ref.ly/Heb9:23-Heb9:26), [HEB 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1-Heb10:3), [HEB 10:12](https://ref.ly/Heb10:12)

### Jesus Christ's sacrifice of a life dedicated to God makes believers holy

[TIT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:14); ; [HEB 10:8–10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:8-Heb10:10)

The sacrifices of Christians
----------------------------

### Christians offer themselves as living sacrifices

**[ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1)** [PHP 2:17](https://ref.ly/Phil2:17); ; [2TI 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:6)

### The sacrifice of praise and good works

**[HEB 13:15–13:16](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15-Heb13:16)**

Christian attitudes to pagan sacrifice
--------------------------------------

*See also* [1CO 10:18–10:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:18-1Cor10:22), [1CO 10:25–10:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:25-1Cor10:26), [1CO 10:27–10:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:27-1Cor10:29) *Christians must not appear to compromise with idolatrous worship.*

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 56:6-7; Isaiah 19:21; Jeremiah 33:18; Ezekiel 20:40; Ezekiel 40:46; Matthew 26:28; Mark 14:24; Luke 22:20; 1 Corinthians 11:25; Hebrews 5:1; Hebrews 7:27; Hebrews 9:23-26; Hebrews 10:1-3; Hebrews 10:12; Titus 2:14; Hebrews 10:8-10; Romans 12:1; Philippians 2:17; 2 Timothy 4:6; Hebrews 13:15-16; 1 Corinthians 10:18-22; 1 Corinthians 10:25-26; 1 Corinthians 10:27-29

## 7438: sanctuary (id: 7438)

A place or structure set aside for the worship of God. In Scripture the word generally refers to the tabernacle or the Jerusalem temple. By extension the word signifies a location or spiritual state providing refuge from hostile forces, human, spiritual or natural.

The tabernacle
--------------

**[EXO 25:8–25:9](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8-Exod25:9)** *See also* [EXO 36:1](https://ref.ly/Exod36:1); [HEB 8:2](https://ref.ly/Heb8:2), [HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Heb8:5)

### Worship in the tabernacle was divinely ordained in great detail

[EXO 30:7–30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7-Exod30:10); ; [LEV 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/Lev7:37-Lev7:38), [LEV 23:44](https://ref.ly/Lev23:44); ; [EXO 39:1](https://ref.ly/Exod39:1); ; [NUM 3:30–3:31](https://ref.ly/Num3:30-Num3:31); ; [HEB 9:1](https://ref.ly/Heb9:1)

### The people gave generously for the tabernacle

**[EXO 36:4–36:5](https://ref.ly/Exod36:4-Exod36:5)**

### Approach to God's sanctuary was strictly regulated

**[LEV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Lev12:4)** *See also* [LEV 21:21–21:23](https://ref.ly/Lev21:21-Lev21:23); [NUM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Num3:10), [NUM 3:38](https://ref.ly/Num3:38), [NUM 8:19](https://ref.ly/Num8:19), [NUM 18:5](https://ref.ly/Num18:5), [NUM 19:20](https://ref.ly/Num19:20); [2CH 26:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:18)

### A reverent attitude was commanded

**[LEV 19:30](https://ref.ly/Lev19:30)** parallel [LEV 26:2](https://ref.ly/Lev26:2)

The Jerusalem temple
--------------------

### David made extensive preparations for Solomon to build the temple

**[1CH 22:17–22:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:17-1Chr22:19)** *See also* [1CH 28:2–28:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:2-1Chr28:3), [1CH 28:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:6)

### The inner sanctuary of the temple corresponded to the Most Holy Place in the tabernacle

**[1KI 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:19)** *See also* [1KI 8:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:6) parallel [2CH 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:7); [1CH 6:49](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:49); [HEB 9:1–9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:1-Heb9:4)

Some places were considered sacred because of God's presence and activity
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 15:17](https://ref.ly/Exod15:17)** *See also* [GEN 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Gen28:16-Gen28:17); [EXO 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Exod3:1-Exod3:5); [PSA 15:1](https://ref.ly/Ps15:1), [PSA 63:2](https://ref.ly/Ps63:2), [PSA 73:16–73:17](https://ref.ly/Ps73:16-Ps73:17); [JER 17:12](https://ref.ly/Jer17:12)

Conduct that displeases God will lead to a nation's sanctuaries being destroyed
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Disobedience to God

**[LEV 26:31](https://ref.ly/Lev26:31)** *See also* [2CH 30:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:8); [EZK 7:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:24); [LAM 2:7](https://ref.ly/Lam2:7); [AMO 7:9](https://ref.ly/Amos7:9); [MAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Mal2:11)

### Dishonesty in dealings

**[EZK 28:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:18)**

### Idolatry

*See also* [LEV 20:3](https://ref.ly/Lev20:3); [EZK 8:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:6), [EZK 23:38–23:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:38-Ezek23:39)

### Profaning the sanctuary

[EZK 44:7–44:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:7-Ezek44:8); ; [ZEP 3:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:4)

Sanctuary from vengeance: the cities of refuge
----------------------------------------------

**[JOS 20:2–20:3](https://ref.ly/Josh20:2-Josh20:3)** *See also* [NUM 35:6](https://ref.ly/Num35:6), [NUM 35:9–35:12](https://ref.ly/Num35:9-Num35:12)

God himself is his people's sanctuary
-------------------------------------

**[PSA 31:20](https://ref.ly/Ps31:20)** *See also* [DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27); [PSA 17:7–17:9](https://ref.ly/Ps17:7-Ps17:9), [PSA 27:1](https://ref.ly/Ps27:1), [PSA 32:7](https://ref.ly/Ps32:7), [PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1), [PSA 64:1–64:2](https://ref.ly/Ps64:1-Ps64:2), [PSA 91:4](https://ref.ly/Ps91:4); [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4); [EZK 11:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:16); [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39); [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7); [HEB 6:19](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19)

Non\-material \`\`sanctuaries'' of God
--------------------------------------

### Heaven, where God is perfectly worshipped

**[PSA 102:19](https://ref.ly/Ps102:19)** *See also* [HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Heb9:24)

### God's worshipping people, in whom God dwells

**[PSA 114:2](https://ref.ly/Ps114:2)** *See also* [1CO 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19); [EPH 2:21](https://ref.ly/Eph2:21); [1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5)

### Jesus Christ himself as the \`\`place'' where God dwelt on earth

[JHN 2:19–2:21](https://ref.ly/John2:19-John2:21)

## 7440: scapegoat (id: 7440)

A goat which was sent into the wilderness by the high priest, as a symbol of the removal of the sins of the people.

The scapegoat as part of an OT ritual
-------------------------------------

**[LEV 16:7–16:10](https://ref.ly/Lev16:7-Lev16:10), [LEV 16:20–16:22](https://ref.ly/Lev16:20-Lev16:22)** *See also* [LEV 16:26](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26); [ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4)

The scapegoat anticipates Jesus Christ's atonement
--------------------------------------------------

[JHN 1:29](https://ref.ly/John1:29); ; [2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21); ; [HEB 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:11-Heb9:14) *Jesus Christ's death is here contrasted with the OT sacrificial system.*

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 16:7-10; Leviticus 16:20-22; Leviticus 16:26; Isaiah 53:4; John 1:29; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Hebrews 9:11-14
* **Associated Articles:** 4651: goat (ID: `4651@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6614: atonement (ID: `6614@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6750: sin-bearer (ID: `6750@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7442: shrine (id: 7442)

A local sanctuary, often dedicated to pagan worship. Before the building of the temple the Israelites worshipped at several divinely sanctioned shrines; afterwards, worship at a central sanctuary was required. True worship of God required that pagan shrines be destroyed. It was Israel's failure to abandon these religious sites that led to syncretism and apostasy.

Common features of pagan shrines
--------------------------------

### A location on high ground under trees

**[EZK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:13)** *Mountains were considered the dwelling\- places of the gods, whilst trees were associated with fertility.* *See also* [2KI 17:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:10); [ISA 1:29](https://ref.ly/Isa1:29) *sacred trees*; [JER 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jer2:20); [EZK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:11) *mountain shrines*; [EZK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:28)

### Idols and sacred symbols

**[ISA 44:13](https://ref.ly/Isa44:13)** *See also* [2KI 10:26–10:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:26-2Kgs10:27), [2KI 17:29–17:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:29-2Kgs17:31); [2CH 34:3–34:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:3-2Chr34:4); [ACT 19:24](https://ref.ly/Acts19:24)

### A serving priesthood

[2KI 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:5), [2KI 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:20)

### Sacrifice to the deity

**[JER 48:35](https://ref.ly/Jer48:35)** *See also* [1KI 22:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:43); [2KI 12:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:3), [2KI 16:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:4) parallel [2CH 28:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:4); [2CH 28:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:25)

### Shrine\-prostitution

[GEN 38:21–38:22](https://ref.ly/Gen38:21-Gen38:22); ; [DEU 23:17](https://ref.ly/Deut23:17); ; [1KI 14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:24), [1KI 15:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:12); ; [JOB 36:14](https://ref.ly/Job36:14)

Pagan shrines were to be destroyed
----------------------------------

### On Israel's entry into Canaan

**[DEU 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2-Deut12:3)** *See also* [EXO 34:13–34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:13-Exod34:14); [NUM 33:52](https://ref.ly/Num33:52); [DEU 7:5](https://ref.ly/Deut7:5)

### As a sign of religious reformation

**[JDG 6:25](https://ref.ly/Judg6:25)**; **[2KI 23:8–23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8-2Kgs23:20)** *See also* [2KI 10:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:25); [2CH 14:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:3), [2CH 17:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:6); [2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4)

Shrines in Israel for the worship of God
----------------------------------------

### Worship at the high places

**[1KI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:2-1Kgs3:3)** *Following Canaanite custom, Israel set up shrines on high ground possibly, against God's instruction, at old Baal sites. Worship at these non\-legitimate sites was condemned.* *See also* [2CH 33:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:17)

### Worship at sites sanctioned by God

**[EXO 20:24](https://ref.ly/Exod20:24)** *See also* [JDG 6:22–6:24](https://ref.ly/Judg6:22-Judg6:24); [1SA 9:12–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:12-1Sam9:14), [1SA 10:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:5)

### Examples of shrines dedicated to God

Bethel: [GEN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:8); ; [JDG 21:2](https://ref.ly/Judg21:2); [JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1); ; [JDG 21:19](https://ref.ly/Judg21:19); [JDG 20:1](https://ref.ly/Judg20:1); ; [1SA 7:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:6); [1KI 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:4-1Kgs3:5); ; [1CH 16:39–16:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:39-1Chr16:40); ; [2CH 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:13)

### A central place of worship was later required

**[2KI 18:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:22)** parallel [ISA 36:7](https://ref.ly/Isa36:7) *Following the building of the temple, worship in various scattered shrines was condemned, probably because of associations with pagan worship.* *See also* [DEU 12:4–12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:4-Deut12:7) *The central sanctuary was, initially, the place where the tabernacle was located. Eventually this was superseded by the temple*; [2SA 7:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:13) parallel [1CH 17:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:12)

Israel's failure to abandon worship at the shrines led to apostasy
------------------------------------------------------------------

### Improper worship at shrines dedicated to God

**[JDG 17:5](https://ref.ly/Judg17:5)** *See also* [JDG 18:30–18:31](https://ref.ly/Judg18:30-Judg18:31); [1KI 12:28–12:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28-1Kgs12:31), [1KI 13:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:32); [2KI 17:32–17:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:32-2Kgs17:33); [2CH 11:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:15); [AMO 4:4](https://ref.ly/Amos4:4)

### The corruption of temple worship

[2KI 23:4–23:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:4-2Kgs23:7); ; [EZK 8:14–8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:14-Ezek8:16)

### Shrines dedicated to false gods

**[1KI 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:23-1Kgs14:24)**; **[EZK 8:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:12)** *See also* [1KI 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7-1Kgs11:8); [2KI 21:2–21:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:2-2Kgs21:3)JER [7:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:31), [JER 19:5](https://ref.ly/Jer19:5), [JER 32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:35)[AMO 5:26](https://ref.ly/Amos5:26); [ACT 7:43](https://ref.ly/Acts7:43)

### Israel's spiritual adultery

**[EZK 16:24–16:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:24-Ezek16:25)** *See also* [JER 3:6](https://ref.ly/Jer3:6); [EZK 16:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:39), [EZK 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:6); [HOS 4:12–4:13](https://ref.ly/Hos4:12-Hos4:13)

### God's judgment on false places of worship

**[EZK 6:3–6:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:3-Ezek6:6)** *See also* [LEV 26:30–26:31](https://ref.ly/Lev26:30-Lev26:31); [HOS 10:8](https://ref.ly/Hos10:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 6:13; 2 Kings 17:10; Isaiah 1:29; Jeremiah 2:20; Ezekiel 18:11; Ezekiel 20:28; Isaiah 44:13; 2 Kings 10:26-27; 2 Kings 17:29-31; 2 Chronicles 34:3-4; Acts 19:24; 2 Kings 23:5; 2 Kings 23:20; Jeremiah 48:35; 1 Kings 22:43; 2 Kings 12:3; 2 Kings 16:4; 2 Chronicles 28:4; 2 Chronicles 28:25; Genesis 38:21-22; Deuteronomy 23:17; 1 Kings 14:24; 1 Kings 15:12; Job 36:14; Deuteronomy 12:2-3; Exodus 34:13-14; Numbers 33:52; Deuteronomy 7:5; Judges 6:25; 2 Kings 23:8-20; 2 Kings 10:25; 2 Chronicles 14:3; 2 Chronicles 17:6; 2 Kings 18:4; 1 Kings 3:2-3; 2 Chronicles 33:17; Exodus 20:24; Judges 6:22-24; 1 Samuel 9:12-14; 1 Samuel 10:5; Genesis 12:8; Judges 21:2; Joshua 18:1; Judges 21:19; Judges 20:1; 1 Samuel 7:6; 1 Kings 3:4-5; 1 Chronicles 16:39-40; 2 Chronicles 1:13; 2 Kings 18:22; Isaiah 36:7; Deuteronomy 12:4-7; 2 Samuel 7:13; 1 Chronicles 17:12; Judges 17:5; Judges 18:30-31; 1 Kings 12:28-31; 1 Kings 13:32; 2 Kings 17:32-33; 2 Chronicles 11:15; Amos 4:4; 2 Kings 23:4-7; Ezekiel 8:14-16; 1 Kings 14:23-24; Ezekiel 8:12; 1 Kings 11:7-8; 2 Kings 21:2-3; 2 Kings 7:31; Jeremiah 19:5; Jeremiah 32:35; Amos 5:26; Acts 7:43; Ezekiel 16:24-25; Jeremiah 3:6; Ezekiel 16:39; Ezekiel 18:6; Hosea 4:12-13; Ezekiel 6:3-6; Leviticus 26:30-31; Hosea 10:8
