## 7444: sin offering (id: 7444)

Offerings to make atonement for both moral and ritual offences. The NT sees them as foreshadowing the death of Jesus Christ as an offering for human sin.

Occasions when a sin offering was required
------------------------------------------

Unintentional sins against God's laws: [LEV 4:2](https://ref.ly/Lev4:2); ; [HEB 9:7](https://ref.ly/Heb9:7); [LEV 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Lev5:1-Lev5:4), [LEV 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lev4:20) *The consequence of the offering is atonement and forgiveness.*; [LEV 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:6-Lev12:8); ; [LUK 2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:24); [LEV 14:13–14:31](https://ref.ly/Lev14:13-Lev14:31); ; [MRK 1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:44); [LEV 15:15](https://ref.ly/Lev15:15), [LEV 15:30](https://ref.ly/Lev15:30)

The form the sin offering took depended on who committed the sin
----------------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:3) *a priest who sins*; ; [LEV 4:14](https://ref.ly/Lev4:14) *the sin of the community as a whole*; ; [LEV 4:22–4:23](https://ref.ly/Lev4:22-Lev4:23) *a leader who sins unintentionally*; [LEV 4:27–4:35](https://ref.ly/Lev4:27-Lev4:35), [LEV 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Lev5:5-Lev5:6)

The ceremony when presenting an animal as a sin offering
--------------------------------------------------------

### The guilty identify themselves with the offering before killing it

[LEV 4:4](https://ref.ly/Lev4:4), [LEV 4:15](https://ref.ly/Lev4:15), [LEV 4:24](https://ref.ly/Lev4:24)

### Confession

[LEV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Lev5:5)

### Blood, representing the victim's lifeblood, is put on the altar

[LEV 4:25](https://ref.ly/Lev4:25), [LEV 4:30](https://ref.ly/Lev4:30), [LEV 4:34](https://ref.ly/Lev4:34)

### The best parts, being burned on the altar, are offered to God

[LEV 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Lev4:8-Lev4:10), [LEV 4:26](https://ref.ly/Lev4:26), [LEV 4:35](https://ref.ly/Lev4:35)

### Male members of priestly families may eat what remains

[LEV 6:26](https://ref.ly/Lev6:26), [LEV 6:29](https://ref.ly/Lev6:29), [LEV 10:16–10:20](https://ref.ly/Lev10:16-Lev10:20)

### Special procedures for the offerings of the poor

[LEV 5:7–5:13](https://ref.ly/Lev5:7-Lev5:13)

The special ceremony for priests and the community presenting a sin offering
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Blood is taken into the sanctuary and placed on the altar

[LEV 4:5–4:7](https://ref.ly/Lev4:5-Lev4:7), [LEV 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/Lev4:16-Lev4:18)

### The rest of the animal is incinerated, not eaten

[LEV 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Lev4:11-Lev4:12), [LEV 4:21](https://ref.ly/Lev4:21) *emphasising the identity between the victim and the sin of the community*

Significant occasions when sin offerings were made
--------------------------------------------------

[LEV 8:14–8:17](https://ref.ly/Lev8:14-Lev8:17) *at the ordination of Aaron and his sons*; ; [LEV 16:1–16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:1-Lev16:34) *on the Day of Atonement*; ; [2KI 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:16) *during Josiah's reformation*; ; [2CH 29:21–29:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:21-2Chr29:24) *at the beginning of Hezekiah's reign*; ; [EZR 6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:17) *at the dedication of the second temple*; ; [EZR 8:35](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:35) *when the exiles returned*; [EZK 40:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:39), [EZK 42:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek42:13), [EZK 43:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:19), [EZK 43:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:21)

The NT perspective on the sin offering
--------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ was the supreme sin offering

**[ROM 8:3](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3)**; **[2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21)** *NIV footnote.*

### Animal sacrifices had limited value

*See also* [HEB 9:7–9:10](https://ref.ly/Heb9:7-Heb9:10)

### The death of Jesus Christ brings full cleansing and forgiveness

**[HEB 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:11-Heb9:14)** *See also* [1PE 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18-1Pet1:19)

### Unlike earthly priests, Jesus Christ needed no sin offering

**[HEB 7:27](https://ref.ly/Heb7:27)** *See also* [HEB 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Heb5:1-Heb5:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 4:2; Hebrews 9:7; Leviticus 5:1-4; Leviticus 4:20; Leviticus 12:6-8; Luke 2:24; Leviticus 14:13-31; Mark 1:44; Leviticus 15:15; Leviticus 15:30; Leviticus 4:3; Leviticus 4:14; Leviticus 4:22-23; Leviticus 4:27-35; Leviticus 5:5-6; Leviticus 4:4; Leviticus 4:15; Leviticus 4:24; Leviticus 5:5; Leviticus 4:25; Leviticus 4:30; Leviticus 4:34; Leviticus 4:8-10; Leviticus 4:26; Leviticus 4:35; Leviticus 6:26; Leviticus 6:29; Leviticus 10:16-20; Leviticus 5:7-13; Leviticus 4:5-7; Leviticus 4:16-18; Leviticus 4:11-12; Leviticus 4:21; Leviticus 8:14-17; Leviticus 16:1-34; 2 Kings 12:16; 2 Chronicles 29:21-24; Ezra 6:17; Ezra 8:35; Ezekiel 40:39; Ezekiel 42:13; Ezekiel 43:19; Ezekiel 43:21; Romans 8:3; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Hebrews 9:7-10; Hebrews 9:11-14; 1 Peter 1:18-19; Hebrews 7:27; Hebrews 5:1-3
* **Associated Articles:** 2306: Jesus Christ, as high priest (ID: `2306@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2530: Jesus Christ, death of (ID: `2530@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6614: atonement (ID: `6614@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7314: blood (ID: `7314@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7768: priests, function in OT times (ID: `7768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7446: slavery (id: 7446)

The state of being an unpaid servant, whether through forcible subjection or voluntary submission. Slavery was common in biblical times, and Scripture lays down strict guidelines as to how slaves are to be treated by their masters, stressing the new status that slaves have as a result of being believers in Jesus Christ. The image of slavery is also used to describe the state of sinful human beings.

* **Associated Articles:** 7447: slavery, in OT (ID: `7447@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7448: slavery, in NT (ID: `7448@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7449: slavery, spiritual (ID: `7449@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7447: slavery, in OT (id: 7447)

Slavery in the OT occurs when someone either voluntarily or forcibly becomes completely subject to another person's control.

Reasons for slavery
-------------------

### War

**[DEU 20:10–20:11](https://ref.ly/Deut20:10-Deut20:11)** *See also* [GEN 14:21](https://ref.ly/Gen14:21); [NUM 31:9](https://ref.ly/Num31:9); [2KI 5:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:2); [2CH 28:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:8)

### Purchase

**[LEV 25:44–25:45](https://ref.ly/Lev25:44-Lev25:45)** *See also* [GEN 37:23–37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:23-Gen37:28)

### Debt

**[2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1)** *See also* [EXO 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Exod22:2-Exod22:3); [LEV 25:39](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39), [LEV 25:47–25:48](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47-Lev25:48); [DEU 15:12](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12); [NEH 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:5)

### Birth

**[EXO 21:4](https://ref.ly/Exod21:4)** *See also* [GEN 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Gen17:12-Gen17:13); [ECC 2:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:7); [JER 2:14](https://ref.ly/Jer2:14)

### Conscription of foreigners as forced labour

**[2SA 12:29–12:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:29-2Sam12:31)** *See also* [EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14); [JOS 9:22–9:23](https://ref.ly/Josh9:22-Josh9:23), [JOS 16:10](https://ref.ly/Josh16:10); [JDG 1:28](https://ref.ly/Judg1:28); [1KI 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:20-1Kgs9:21)

Rights of slaves
----------------

### Rights of protection against physical and sexual abuse

**[LEV 25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:43)** *See also* [EXO 21:7–21:11](https://ref.ly/Exod21:7-Exod21:11), [EXO 21:20–21:21](https://ref.ly/Exod21:20-Exod21:21), [EXO 21:26–21:27](https://ref.ly/Exod21:26-Exod21:27)

### Rights to participate in religious feasts and festivals

**[EXO 23:12](https://ref.ly/Exod23:12)** *See also* [EXO 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/Exod20:9-Exod20:10); [DEU 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11-Deut12:12)

### Rights to inherit property

**[GEN 15:3](https://ref.ly/Gen15:3)**

### Rights of asylum

**[DEU 23:15](https://ref.ly/Deut23:15)**

The release of slaves
---------------------

### Release in the Year of Jubilee

**[LEV 25:54–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:54-Lev25:55)** *See also* [LEV 25:39–25:41](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:41)

### Release after six years of service

**[EXO 21:2](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2)** *See also* [EXO 21:3–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:3-Exod21:6); [DEU 15:12–15:18](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:18); [JER 34:8–34:20](https://ref.ly/Jer34:8-Jer34:20) *The six\-year principle was being abused in the time of Jeremiah.*

### Release through redemption by a near relative

[LEV 25:47–25:53](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47-Lev25:53)

The status of slaves
--------------------

### Slaves as property

**[LEV 25:46](https://ref.ly/Lev25:46)** *See also* [EXO 21:32](https://ref.ly/Exod21:32); [LEV 25:39–25:42](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:42) *Fellow Israelites were not to be regarded as property.*

### Slaves as trusted advisors

[1SA 9:5–9:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:5-1Sam9:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 20:10-11; Genesis 14:21; Numbers 31:9; 2 Kings 5:2; 2 Chronicles 28:8; Leviticus 25:44-45; Genesis 37:23-28; 2 Kings 4:1; Exodus 22:2-3; Leviticus 25:39; Leviticus 25:47-48; Deuteronomy 15:12; Nehemiah 5:1-5; Exodus 21:4; Genesis 17:12-13; Ecclesiastes 2:7; Jeremiah 2:14; 2 Samuel 12:29-31; Exodus 1:11-14; Joshua 9:22-23; Joshua 16:10; Judges 1:28; 1 Kings 9:20-21; Leviticus 25:43; Exodus 21:7-11; Exodus 21:20-21; Exodus 21:26-27; Exodus 23:12; Exodus 20:9-10; Deuteronomy 12:11-12; Genesis 15:3; Deuteronomy 23:15; Leviticus 25:54-55; Leviticus 25:39-41; Exodus 21:2; Exodus 21:3-6; Deuteronomy 15:12-18; Jeremiah 34:8-20; Leviticus 25:47-53; Leviticus 25:46; Exodus 21:32; Leviticus 25:39-42; 1 Samuel 9:5-10

## 7448: slavery, in NT (id: 7448)

The NT does not condone slavery but recognises that slavery exists, and that many slaves have become believers. The status of slaves in the church is transformed by the work of Jesus Christ. Slaves were accorded greater respect because they were seen to be spiritually equal with others.

Examples of slavery in the NT
-----------------------------

[ACT 16:16–16:21](https://ref.ly/Acts16:16-Acts16:21) *Paul and Silas deliver a demon\-possessed slave girl*; ; [PHM 1:8–1:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:8-Phlm1:21) *Paul returns the runaway slave Onesimus to his master.*

The gospel transforms the status of slaves
------------------------------------------

**[1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13)**; **[GAL 3:26–3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26-Gal3:28)**

Instructions concerning slavery
-------------------------------

### Instructions to Christian slaves

**[EPH 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5-Eph6:8)** *See also* [1CO 7:20–7:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:20-1Cor7:24); [COL 3:22–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:22-Col3:24); [1TI 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:1-1Tim6:2); [TIT 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:9-Titus2:10); [1PE 2:18–2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18-1Pet2:21)

### Instructions to Christian masters

**[EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)** *See also* [COL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Col4:1); [PHM 1:8–1:17](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:8-Phlm1:17)

### The immorality of trading in slaves

**[1TI 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:10)**

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 16:16-21; Philemon 1:8-21; 1 Corinthians 12:13; Galatians 3:26-28; Ephesians 6:5-8; 1 Corinthians 7:20-24; Colossians 3:22-24; 1 Timothy 6:1-2; Titus 2:9-10; 1 Peter 2:18-21; Ephesians 6:9; Colossians 4:1; Philemon 1:8-17; 1 Timothy 1:9-10
* **Associated Articles:** 2327: Jesus Christ, as servant (ID: `2327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5308: equality (ID: `5308@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5404: masters (ID: `5404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5520: servants (ID: `5520@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5520: servants (ID: `5520@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6754: union with Christ (ID: `6754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7449: slavery, spiritual (id: 7449)

Because of sin humanity is in a state of slavery. On account of the atoning work of Jesus Christ, believers can break free from slavery to sin and enter into the glorious liberty of the children of God.

Humanity is enslaved
--------------------

### To sin

**[JHN 8:33–8:36](https://ref.ly/John8:33-John8:36)** *See also* [ROM 6:12–6:18](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12-Rom6:18), [ROM 7:7–7:25](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7-Rom7:25); [TIT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3); [2PE 2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:19)

### To the law

**[GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1)** *See also* [GAL 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:1-Gal4:7)

### To fear

**[ROM 8:15](https://ref.ly/Rom8:15)** *See also* [HEB 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14-Heb2:15)

Jesus Christ as the example of the true slave
---------------------------------------------

**[JHN 13:1–13:17](https://ref.ly/John13:1-John13:17)** *Washing feet was the responsibility of the slaves of the household.* *See also* [MAT 20:25–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25-Matt20:28) parallel [MRK 10:42–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:45); [PHP 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:11)

Believers as slaves of God
--------------------------

### They are God's servants

**[ROM 6:22](https://ref.ly/Rom6:22)** *See also* [MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24) parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13); [ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); [COL 3:23–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:23-Col3:24); [JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1); [1PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:16); [2PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:1)

### They are sons and heirs

**[GAL 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:1-Gal4:7)** *See also* [ROM 8:12–8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:12-Rom8:17)

### They have friendship with Jesus Christ

**[JHN 15:15](https://ref.ly/John15:15)** *See also* [JAS 2:21–2:23](https://ref.ly/Jas2:21-Jas2:23)

### They have true freedom

**[JHN 8:31–8:36](https://ref.ly/John8:31-John8:36)** *See also* [GAL 4:21–4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:21-Gal4:31)

Believers as slaves of righteousness
------------------------------------

**[ROM 6:15–6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:15-Rom6:23)**

Believers as slaves of others
-----------------------------

**[MAT 20:25–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25-Matt20:28)** parallel [MRK 10:42–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:45) *See also* [1CO 9:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:19); [2CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:5); [GAL 5:13](https://ref.ly/Gal5:13)

The image of slavery in the teaching of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------------------

[MAT 24:45–24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:45-Matt24:51) parallel [LUK 12:42–12:46](https://ref.ly/Luke12:42-Luke12:46) *Slavery involves readiness for the Master's return*; ; [MAT 25:14–25:20](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:20) parallel [LUK 19:12–19:17](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:17) *Slavery involves faithfulness to the Master's purpose*; ; [LUK 17:7–17:10](https://ref.ly/Luke17:7-Luke17:10) *Slavery involves diligence for the Master's good.*

Creation is itself in slavery until Jesus Christ returns
--------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19-Rom8:22)**

* **Associated Passages:** John 8:33-36; Romans 6:12-18; Romans 7:7-25; Titus 3:3; 2 Peter 2:19; Galatians 5:1; Galatians 4:1-7; Romans 8:15; Hebrews 2:14-15; John 13:1-17; Matthew 20:25-28; Mark 10:42-45; Philippians 2:5-11; Romans 6:22; Matthew 6:24; Luke 16:13; Romans 1:1; Colossians 3:23-24; James 1:1; 1 Peter 2:16; 2 Peter 1:1; Romans 8:12-17; John 15:15; James 2:21-23; John 8:31-36; Galatians 4:21-31; Romans 6:15-23; 1 Corinthians 9:19; 2 Corinthians 4:5; Galatians 5:13; Matthew 24:45-51; Luke 12:42-46; Matthew 25:14-20; Luke 19:12-17; Luke 17:7-10; Romans 8:19-22

## 7452: snake, bronze (id: 7452)

The means of salvation for Israel in the wilderness, a parallel to be found in Jesus Christ.

Healing and salvation resulted from looking at the bronze snake
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 21:8–21:9](https://ref.ly/Num21:8-Num21:9)** *See also* [NUM 21:4–21:7](https://ref.ly/Num21:4-Num21:7)

The bronze snake became an object of idolatrous worship
-------------------------------------------------------

**[2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4)**

The bronze snake parallels Jesus Christ's crucifixion
-----------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/John3:14-John3:15)**

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 21:8-9; Numbers 21:4-7; 2 Kings 18:4; John 3:14-15

## 7454: sprinkling (id: 7454)

In the OT, the blood of a sacrificial animal was sprinkled against the altar as a sign of atonement or consecration to the LORD. The NT uses the same image to refer to the gaining of forgiveness through the sacrificial death of Jesus Christ.

Sprinkling of an animal's blood as a sign of atonement
------------------------------------------------------

### On the altar

**[LEV 7:2](https://ref.ly/Lev7:2)** *See also* [EXO 24:6](https://ref.ly/Exod24:6); [LEV 1:5](https://ref.ly/Lev1:5), [LEV 1:11](https://ref.ly/Lev1:11), [LEV 3:2](https://ref.ly/Lev3:2), [LEV 3:8](https://ref.ly/Lev3:8), [LEV 3:13](https://ref.ly/Lev3:13), [LEV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Lev5:9), [LEV 9:12](https://ref.ly/Lev9:12), [LEV 9:18](https://ref.ly/Lev9:18), [LEV 17:6](https://ref.ly/Lev17:6); [NUM 18:17](https://ref.ly/Num18:17); [2KI 16:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:13), [2KI 16:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:15); [2CH 29:22–29:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:22-2Chr29:24), [2CH 30:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:16), [2CH 35:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:11); [EZK 43:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:18), [EZK 43:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:20); [HEB 9:21](https://ref.ly/Heb9:21)

**[LEV 4:6](https://ref.ly/Lev4:6)** *See also* [LEV 4:17](https://ref.ly/Lev4:17)

## 7456: synagogue (id: 7456)

A word meaning literally “a gathering together”. In the NT it sometimes refers to a group of Jews meeting for worship but most often to the building in which they met.

Synagogue worship may have begun during the exile
-------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:1)** *Unable to sacrifice in the temple, the community met together with spiritual leaders.* *See also* [EZK 20:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:1)

A synagogue might be established anywhere
-----------------------------------------

**[MRK 1:21](https://ref.ly/Mark1:21)** *See also* [ACT 6:9](https://ref.ly/Acts6:9) *in Jerusalem*[ACT 16:12–16:13](https://ref.ly/Acts16:12-Acts16:13), [ACT 16:16](https://ref.ly/Acts16:16)[ACT 17:1](https://ref.ly/Acts17:1), [ACT 17:10](https://ref.ly/Acts17:10), [ACT 18:19](https://ref.ly/Acts18:19)

The role of the synagogue
-------------------------

### It was a focus for prayer and the reading of Scripture

**[ACT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Acts13:15)** *See also* [NEH 8:2–8:8](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2-Neh8:8), [NEH 9:3](https://ref.ly/Neh9:3); [LUK 4:15–4:33](https://ref.ly/Luke4:15-Luke4:33)

### It was a centre for community affairs

[MAT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17); ; [MRK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9); ; [LUK 12:11](https://ref.ly/Luke12:11)

### Exclusion from the synagogue was used as a punishment

[JHN 9:22](https://ref.ly/John9:22), [JHN 12:42](https://ref.ly/John12:42)

The leading of synagogue worship
--------------------------------

### It was the responsibility of the synagogue ruler

[MRK 5:22](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22); ; [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8)

### Qualified laymen were allowed to teach

**[LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16)** *See also* [ACT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Acts13:15)

Attendance at the synagogue
---------------------------

### It was the practice of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 9:35](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35)** *See also* [MRK 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2); [LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16); [JHN 6:59](https://ref.ly/John6:59)

### It was the practice of Paul and other Jewish Christians

**[ACT 9:19–9:20](https://ref.ly/Acts9:19-Acts9:20)** *See also* [ACT 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1-Acts14:2), [ACT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2), [ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8)

### The first Christians continued to worship in the synagogues

[LUK 24:53](https://ref.ly/Luke24:53); ; [ACT 19:8–19:9](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8-Acts19:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 14:1; Ezekiel 20:1; Mark 1:21; Acts 6:9; Acts 16:12-13; Acts 16:16; Acts 17:1; Acts 17:10; Acts 18:19; Acts 13:15; Nehemiah 8:2-8; Nehemiah 9:3; Luke 4:15-33; Matthew 10:17; Mark 13:9; Luke 12:11; John 9:22; John 12:42; Mark 5:22; Acts 18:8; Luke 4:16; Matthew 9:35; Mark 6:2; John 6:59; Acts 9:19-20; Acts 14:1-2; Acts 17:2; Acts 19:8; Luke 24:53; Acts 19:8-9

## 7458: tabernacle, the (id: 7458)

The tabernacle (tent) was a portable and temporary shrine used for worship before the temple was built. It denotes the temporary dwelling of God among his people.

* **Associated Articles:** 7459: tabernacle, in OT (ID: `7459@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7460: tabernacle, in NT (ID: `7460@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7459: tabernacle, in OT (id: 7459)

A prefabricated portable structure that was used for worship from the wilderness period until Solomon built the Jerusalem temple.

The tabernacle was the place of God's presence
----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 33:9–33:10](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9-Exod33:10)** *See also* [EXO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8), [EXO 40:34–40:35](https://ref.ly/Exod40:34-Exod40:35)

The construction of the tabernacle
----------------------------------

### It was built at God's command

**[EXO 25:9](https://ref.ly/Exod25:9)** *See also* [EXO 25:40](https://ref.ly/Exod25:40), [EXO 39:32](https://ref.ly/Exod39:32), [EXO 39:43](https://ref.ly/Exod39:43)

### The materials used in its construction

[EXO 25:3–25:7](https://ref.ly/Exod25:3-Exod25:7) *Many of these materials were available in the desert. Others, such as gold, would have been obtained from the Egyptians ([Ex 12:35–36](https://ref.ly/Exod12:35-Exod12:36))*; ; [EXO 35:21–35:24](https://ref.ly/Exod35:21-Exod35:24)

### The plan for its construction

[EXO 26:1](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1), [EXO 26:7](https://ref.ly/Exod26:7), [EXO 26:14–26:15](https://ref.ly/Exod26:14-Exod26:15), [EXO 26:26](https://ref.ly/Exod26:26)

### The skilled labour employed in its construction

**[EXO 31:1–31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:6)** parallel [EXO 35:30–35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:35) *See also* [EXO 35:10](https://ref.ly/Exod35:10), [EXO 35:26](https://ref.ly/Exod35:26), [EXO 36:1–36:2](https://ref.ly/Exod36:1-Exod36:2)

The tabernacle courtyard and its contents
-----------------------------------------

### Its dimensions and construction

[EXO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Exod27:9), [EXO 27:12](https://ref.ly/Exod27:12), [EXO 27:18](https://ref.ly/Exod27:18)

### The altar of burnt offering

[EXO 27:1](https://ref.ly/Exod27:1)

### The laver

[EXO 30:17–30:18](https://ref.ly/Exod30:17-Exod30:18)

The tabernacle's Holy Place and its contents
--------------------------------------------

### Its construction

[EXO 26:36–26:37](https://ref.ly/Exod26:36-Exod26:37)

### The incense altar

[EXO 30:1](https://ref.ly/Exod30:1), [EXO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Exod30:5)

### The lampstand

[EXO 25:31](https://ref.ly/Exod25:31)

### The golden table for the bread of the Presence

[EXO 25:23](https://ref.ly/Exod25:23), [EXO 25:30](https://ref.ly/Exod25:30), [EXO 26:35](https://ref.ly/Exod26:35)

The tabernacle's Most Holy Place and its contents
-------------------------------------------------

### It was separated from the Holy Place by a curtain

[EXO 26:31–26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:31-Exod26:33) *It was designed as a perfect cube with sides of 10 cubits (4\.5 metres).*

### It contained the ark of the covenant

[EXO 26:33–26:34](https://ref.ly/Exod26:33-Exod26:34)

The Levites were responsible for the tabernacle
-----------------------------------------------

[NUM 1:50–1:51](https://ref.ly/Num1:50-Num1:51)

The non\-religious functions of the tabernacle
----------------------------------------------

### As a reference point for locating the different tribes in camp

**[NUM 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Num2:1-Num2:2)** *Symbolising that God was central to the life of the nation.*

### The movement of the cloud of glory showed when it was time to make or break camp

*See also* [EXO 40:36–40:38](https://ref.ly/Exod40:36-Exod40:38)

Places where the tabernacle was sited
-------------------------------------

The tabernacle was dedicated at Sinai: [EXO 40:1–40:2](https://ref.ly/Exod40:1-Exod40:2), [EXO 40:9–40:11](https://ref.ly/Exod40:9-Exod40:11); [JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1); ; [1SA 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:3); ; [PSA 78:60](https://ref.ly/Ps78:60); [1SA 21:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:1) *Nob*; ; [1CH 16:39](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:39) *Gibeon*; ; [2CH 5:2–5:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:2-2Chr5:6) *Jerusalem*

## 7460: tabernacle, in NT (id: 7460)

The NT sees the tabernacle and its ceremonies as symbolising the saving work of Jesus Christ in his incarnation and death and in the experience of believers.

Jesus Christ as the tabernacle where God dwells
-----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14), [JHN 2:19–2:21](https://ref.ly/John2:19-John2:21)**

The tabernacle showed that God was not limited to one place
-----------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [ACT 7:44–7:47](https://ref.ly/Acts7:44-Acts7:47)

The spiritual significance of the tabernacle
--------------------------------------------

[HEB 9:1–9:5](https://ref.ly/Heb9:1-Heb9:5)

The earthly tabernacle as a copy of the heavenly one
----------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Heb8:5)** *See also* [HEB 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Heb8:1-Heb8:2)

### The altar

[REV 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Rev6:9-Rev6:10), [REV 8:3–8:5](https://ref.ly/Rev8:3-Rev8:5)

### The ark of the covenant

[REV 11:19](https://ref.ly/Rev11:19)

### The atonement cover

[EXO 25:22](https://ref.ly/Exod25:22); ; [HEB 4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:16)

### The curtain preventing access to the holiest place

[MAT 27:51](https://ref.ly/Matt27:51); ; [HEB 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19-Heb6:20), [HEB 9:8](https://ref.ly/Heb9:8), [HEB 10:19–10:20](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:20)

Jesus Christ as high priest
---------------------------

### Cleansing the earthly tabernacle

**[HEB 9:21](https://ref.ly/Heb9:21)** *See also* [HEB 9:6–9:8](https://ref.ly/Heb9:6-Heb9:8) *on the Day of Atonement*; [HEB 9:25](https://ref.ly/Heb9:25)

### Through the blood of the cross, Jesus Christ enters the heavenly sanctuary

**[HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Heb9:24)** *See also* [HEB 9:28](https://ref.ly/Heb9:28)

## 7462: tassel (id: 7462)

A fringe of twisted threads attached to the bottom edge of Israelite men's cloaks. The Pharisees lengthened the tassels on their cloaks to draw attention to their piety.

Tassels attached to outer garments
----------------------------------

### On the corners of garments

**[NUM 15:38](https://ref.ly/Num15:38)** *See also* [DEU 22:12](https://ref.ly/Deut22:12)

### On the cloak of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 9:20](https://ref.ly/Matt9:20)** parallels [MRK 5:27](https://ref.ly/Mark5:27), [LUK 8:44](https://ref.ly/Luke8:44) *The woman probably touched the tassels on Jesus Christ's cloak.* *See also* [MAT 14:36](https://ref.ly/Matt14:36) parallel [MRK 6:56](https://ref.ly/Mark6:56)

Tassels as a reminder of God's law
----------------------------------

**[NUM 15:39–15:41](https://ref.ly/Num15:39-Num15:41)** *The movement of the tassels would draw back wandering eyes to God and his law.*

Ostentatious tassels symbolise outward religious show
-----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 23:5–23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5-Matt23:7)** parallels [MRK 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:39), [LUK 20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:46) *See also* [MAT 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:1-Matt6:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 15:38; Deuteronomy 22:12; Matthew 9:20; Mark 5:27; Luke 8:44; Matthew 14:36; Mark 6:56; Numbers 15:39-41; Matthew 23:5-7; Mark 12:38-39; Luke 20:46; Matthew 6:1-6
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4807: colours (ID: `4807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5144: cloak (ID: `5144@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7394: memorial (ID: `7394@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7410: phylactery (ID: `7410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8467: reminders (ID: `8467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8670: remembering (ID: `8670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8767: hypocrisy (ID: `8767@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7464: teachers of the law (id: 7464)

Those professionally trained to develop, teach and apply the OT law. In the application of the law the oral teaching of these people often assumed greater authority than the written law. Thus, by the time of the NT, the teachers of the law were in conflict with Jesus Christ and the apostles, who taught with authority and condemned the outward religious acts that the teachers had fostered.

Teachers of the law mentioned by name
-------------------------------------

### Ezra

[EZR 7:1–7:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:1-Ezra7:21); ; [NEH 8:1–8:18](https://ref.ly/Neh8:1-Neh8:18) *Teachers of the law arose after the exile from amongst the earlier scribes whose duties were more strictly secretarial.*

### Nicodemus

[JHN 3:10](https://ref.ly/John3:10)

### Gamaliel

[ACT 5:34](https://ref.ly/Acts5:34) *Teachers of the law belonged mainly to the Pharisees but as a body were distinct from them.*

Teachers of the law in conflict with Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------

### Concerning righteousness

**[MAT 5:20](https://ref.ly/Matt5:20)**

### Concerning Jesus Christ's authority

**[MAT 9:2–9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2-Matt9:8)** parallels [MRK 2:5–2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:5-Mark2:12), [LUK 5:20–5:26](https://ref.ly/Luke5:20-Luke5:26) *See also* [MRK 3:22–3:27](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22-Mark3:27), [MRK 11:27–11:33](https://ref.ly/Mark11:27-Mark11:33) parallel [LUK 20:1–20:8](https://ref.ly/Luke20:1-Luke20:8)

### Concerning the seeking of signs

**[MAT 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Matt12:38-Matt12:39)**

### Concerning hypocrisy

**[MAT 23:2–23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:2-Matt23:33)** parallels [MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40), [LUK 13:34–13:35](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34-Luke13:35), [LUK 20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:46) *See also* [MAT 15:1–15:8](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:8) parallel [MRK 7:1–7:8](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:8); [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)

### Concerning the children's praise

**[MAT 21:15–21:16](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15-Matt21:16)** *See also* [PSA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ps8:2)

### Concerning Jesus Christ's mixing with sinners

**[MRK 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16-Mark2:17)** parallel [LUK 5:30–5:32](https://ref.ly/Luke5:30-Luke5:32) *See also* [LUK 15:1–15:7](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:7)

### Concerning Jesus Christ's identity

**[MRK 12:35–12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:35-Mark12:37)** *See also* [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1)

### Concerning the Sabbath

**[LUK 6:7–6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:7-Luke6:11)**

The teachers of the law sought to get rid of Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------------------

### By falsely accusing him

**[LUK 23:10](https://ref.ly/Luke23:10)** *See also* [MAT 26:57–26:60](https://ref.ly/Matt26:57-Matt26:60) parallel [MRK 14:53–14:56](https://ref.ly/Mark14:53-Mark14:56) *The teachers of the law were entrusted with the administration of the law as judges in the Sanhedrin*; [JHN 8:3–8:6](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:6)

### By looking for ways to arrest and kill him

**[MRK 11:18](https://ref.ly/Mark11:18)** parallel [LUK 19:47](https://ref.ly/Luke19:47) *See also* [MRK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1) parallel [LUK 22:2](https://ref.ly/Luke22:2); [MRK 14:43](https://ref.ly/Mark14:43); [LUK 20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:19) parallel [MRK 12:12](https://ref.ly/Mark12:12)

### By sending him to be tried before Pilate

**[MRK 15:1](https://ref.ly/Mark15:1)** *See also* [LUK 22:66–23:1](https://ref.ly/Luke22:66-Luke23:1)

The teachers of the law, with others, mocked Christ crucified
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 27:41–27:42](https://ref.ly/Matt27:41-Matt27:42)** parallel [MRK 15:31–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:31-Mark15:32)

Jesus Christ foretold the opposition of the teachers of the law
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)** parallels [MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31), [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22) *See also* [MAT 20:18–20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:18-Matt20:19) parallel [MRK 10:33–10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:33-Mark10:34)

Teachers of the law in conflict with the apostles
-------------------------------------------------

[ACT 4:5–4:7](https://ref.ly/Acts4:5-Acts4:7), [ACT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Acts6:12)

Teachers of the law in a non\-confrontational setting
-----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Matt2:4), [MAT 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:19-Matt8:20), [MAT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Matt13:52)**; **[LUK 20:39](https://ref.ly/Luke20:39)**; **[1TI 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:7)** *See also* [MAT 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28-Matt7:29), [MAT 17:10](https://ref.ly/Matt17:10) parallel [MRK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Mark9:11); [MRK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:22); [ACT 23:9](https://ref.ly/Acts23:9)

## 7466: temples (id: 7466)

Buildings for the worship of local or national gods. After settling in the promised land, Israel felt the need for a permanent building set apart for worship of the LORD. Three temples were built at different periods in Jerusalem. In the NT the individual believer (or the body of the church) is seen as the true temple or dwelling\-place of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 7467: temple, Solomon's (ID: `7467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7468: temple, rebuilding of (ID: `7468@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7469: temple, Herod's (ID: `7469@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7470: temple, significance of (ID: `7470@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7471: temples, heathen (ID: `7471@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7467: temple, Solomon's (id: 7467)

From the exodus until the reign of Solomon, the tabernacle located in various places served as Israel's “temple” and the ordained place of sacrifice. Solomon built the first temple in Jerusalem.

Centres for worship and sacrifice before the temple was built
-------------------------------------------------------------

[JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1); ; [1SA 7:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:2)

David's preparations for the building and worship in the first temple
---------------------------------------------------------------------

### The plan for its construction

[2SA 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:12-2Sam7:13); ; [1CH 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:1) parallel [2SA 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:1); ; [1CH 28:11–28:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:11-1Chr28:12), [1CH 28:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:19)

### The gifts for its construction

[1CH 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:2-1Chr29:3), [1CH 29:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:6)

### Arrangements for the temple worship

[1CH 23:3–23:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:3-1Chr23:5)

Solomon's construction of the temple
------------------------------------

[1KI 5:1–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:1-1Kgs5:13), [1KI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:7); ; [2CH 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:17-2Chr2:18)

The completion of the temple and its furnishings
------------------------------------------------

**[2CH 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:5)** *Although it followed the basic pattern of the tabernacle, Solomon intended the temple to reflect God's greatness.* *See also* [1KI 6:20–6:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:20-1Kgs6:22) parallel [2CH 3:4–3:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:4-2Chr3:9) *The inner walls were overlaid with gold*; [1KI 6:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:27) parallel [2CH 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:11-2Chr3:12) *The wings of the cherubim reached from wall to wall*; [1KI 6:381](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:381)KI [7:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:23) parallel [2CH 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:2); [1KI 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:26-1Kgs7:27) parallel [2CH 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:5); [1KI 7:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:30)[1KI 7:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:49) *not one golden lampstand but ten*; [2CH 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:8) *not one table for the bread of the Presence but ten*

The dedication of the temple
----------------------------

### At the Feast of Tabernacles

[1KI 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:2) parallel [2CH 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:3)

### The glory of the LORD filled the temple

[1KI 8:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:6) parallel [2CH 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:7); ; [1KI 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:10-1Kgs8:11); ; [2CH 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:11), [2CH 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13-2Chr5:14)

### The offering of sacrifices

[1KI 8:62–8:63](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:62-1Kgs8:63) parallel [2CH 7:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:5)

### Solomon's prayer

**[1KI 8:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:27)** parallel [2CH 6:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:18)

The temple after the division of the kingdom
--------------------------------------------

*See also* [1KI 12:26–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26-1Kgs12:30); [2CH 13:4–13:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:4-2Chr13:12)

Later kings of Judah used the temple treasures for political purposes
---------------------------------------------------------------------

[1KI 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:18-1Kgs15:19) parallel [2CH 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:2-2Chr16:3) *Asa took silver and gold to persuade the king of Aram to be his ally against Israel*; ; [2KI 12:17–12:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:17-2Kgs12:18) *Joash sent money and treasures to prevent the king of Aram attacking Jerusalem*; ; [2KI 16:7–16:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7-2Kgs16:8) parallel [2CH 28:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:21) *Ahaz used gold and silver to gain the support of the king of Assyria against his enemies*; ; [2KI 18:14–18:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:14-2Kgs18:15) *Hezekiah used temple silver to placate the king of Assyria.*

Foreign kings pillaged the temple
---------------------------------

[1KI 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:25-1Kgs14:26) parallel [2CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:9) *Shishak king of Egypt*; ; [2KI 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:13); ; [2CH 36:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:18) *Nebuchadnezzar*

Under certain kings the temple was cleansed and repaired
--------------------------------------------------------

Joash: [2KI 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:4-2Kgs12:5) parallel [2CH 24:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:5); ; [2CH 24:13–24:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:13-2Chr24:14); [2KI 15:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:35) parallel [2CH 27:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:3) *Jotham*; [2CH 29:3–29:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:3-2Chr29:5), [2CH 29:15–29:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:15-2Chr29:16), [2CH 29:18–29:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:18-2Chr29:19), [2CH 29:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:25); [2KI 22:3–22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:3-2Kgs22:7) parallel [2CH 34:8–34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:8-2Chr34:11) *Josiah*

The destruction of the temple by the Babylonians
------------------------------------------------

[2KI 25:13–25:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:13-2Kgs25:15) parallel [JER 52:17–52:19](https://ref.ly/Jer52:17-Jer52:19); ; [2CH 36:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:18)

The temple and the ministry of the prophets
-------------------------------------------

### Isaiah received his call in the temple

[ISA 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1-Isa6:8)

### Ezekiel had a vision of the glory of God departing

[EZK 10:18–10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:18-Ezek10:19)

### Micah's prophecy concerning the temple's fate

[JER 26:18](https://ref.ly/Jer26:18); ; [MIC 3:12](https://ref.ly/Mic3:12)

### Jeremiah's ministry was closely linked to the temple

[JER 7:2](https://ref.ly/Jer7:2), [JER 7:4](https://ref.ly/Jer7:4), [JER 7:14](https://ref.ly/Jer7:14), [JER 19:14](https://ref.ly/Jer19:14)

## 7468: temple, rebuilding of (id: 7468)

Following the exile Solomon's ruined temple was rebuilt and became the focus of the Jewish faith worldwide.

Preparations for rebuilding the temple
--------------------------------------

[EZR 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:1-Ezra1:4) parallel [2CH 36:22–36:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:22-2Chr36:23) *Cyrus gave permission for the exiled Jews to return to Jerusalem to rebuild the temple (538 B.C.)*; ; [EZR 1:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:7) *He sent back the temple treasures.*

Rebuilding the temple under the leadership of Jeshua and Zerubbabel
-------------------------------------------------------------------

[EZR 3:3–3:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:3-Ezra3:6), [EZR 3:8–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8-Ezra3:13), [EZR 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:1-Ezra4:3)

Interruptions to the rebuilding
-------------------------------

### The Jews' enemies persuaded Artaxerxes to order the work to cease

[EZR 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:8), [EZR 4:12–4:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:12-Ezra4:15), [EZR 4:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:17), [EZR 4:20–4:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:20-Ezra4:21), [EZR 4:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:24)

### The prophets Haggai and Zechariah called Zerubbabel and the people to restart work

**[HAG 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hag1:4)** *See also* [EZR 5:1–5:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:1-Ezra5:12); [HAG 1:5–1:7](https://ref.ly/Hag1:5-Hag1:7), [HAG 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Hag1:14-Hag1:15)

### The rebuilding authorised by King Darius

[EZR 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:3), [EZR 5:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:11), [EZR 5:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:13), [EZR 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:3-Ezra6:5), [EZR 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:7-Ezra6:8)

### The dedication and worship of the rebuilt temple

[EZR 6:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:16), [EZR 7:11–7:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:11-Ezra7:17)

The post\-exilic temple and its worship was regarded as the spiritual centre for Jews worldwide
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[PSA 87:1–87:7](https://ref.ly/Ps87:1-Ps87:7), [PSA 135:21](https://ref.ly/Ps135:21)

The temple is the place where God is to be praised
--------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4), [PSA 134:1–134:2](https://ref.ly/Ps134:1-Ps134:2)** *See also* [PSA 135:19–135:20](https://ref.ly/Ps135:19-Ps135:20), [PSA 138:2](https://ref.ly/Ps138:2)

Prophecies of restoration of the temple
---------------------------------------

**[ZEC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Zech1:16)** *See also* [JER 31:6](https://ref.ly/Jer31:6), [JER 31:23](https://ref.ly/Jer31:23), [JER 33:18](https://ref.ly/Jer33:18); [EZK 40:1–43:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:1-Ezek43:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 1:1-4; 2 Chronicles 36:22-23; Ezra 1:7; Ezra 3:3-6; Ezra 3:8-13; Ezra 4:1-3; Ezra 4:8; Ezra 4:12-15; Ezra 4:17; Ezra 4:20-21; Ezra 4:24; Haggai 1:4; Ezra 5:1-12; Haggai 1:5-7; Haggai 1:14-15; Ezra 5:3; Ezra 5:11; Ezra 5:13; Ezra 6:3-5; Ezra 6:7-8; Ezra 6:16; Ezra 7:11-17; Psalms 87:1-7; Psalms 135:21; Psalms 100:4; Psalms 134:1-2; Psalms 135:19-20; Psalms 138:2; Zechariah 1:16; Jeremiah 31:6; Jeremiah 31:23; Jeremiah 33:18; Ezekiel 40:1-43:27

## 7469: temple, Herod's (id: 7469)

Herod's temple, familiar to Jesus Christ and his disciples and frequented by the first Christians, was destroyed by the Romans in A.D. 70\.

The building of Herod the Great's temple
----------------------------------------

[JHN 2:20](https://ref.ly/John2:20)

Jesus Christ visited the temple and taught in its courts
--------------------------------------------------------

### He was taken there as a baby

[LUK 2:22](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22), [LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Luke2:34), [LUK 2:36–2:38](https://ref.ly/Luke2:36-Luke2:38)

### He stayed in the temple courts after his parents had returned home from the Passover Feast

[LUK 2:41–2:43](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41-Luke2:43), [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Luke2:46)

### He cast out of the temple court those who used it for material profit

[MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13) parallels [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46); ; [JHN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:16)

### He taught in the temple

[MRK 12:35](https://ref.ly/Mark12:35), [MRK 12:41–12:43](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:43) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4); ; [JHN 7:14](https://ref.ly/John7:14); [JHN 7:32](https://ref.ly/John7:32), [JHN 7:45–7:46](https://ref.ly/John7:45-John7:46); [JHN 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/John8:2-John8:3), [JHN 10:22–10:24](https://ref.ly/John10:22-John10:24)

### The religious leaders challenged his authority

[MAT 21:23](https://ref.ly/Matt21:23) parallels [MRK 11:27–11:28](https://ref.ly/Mark11:27-Mark11:28), [LUK 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Luke20:1-Luke20:2)

### He foretold the complete destruction of the temple buildings

[MAT 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Matt24:1-Matt24:2) parallels [MRK 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Mark13:1-Mark13:2), [LUK 21:5–21:6](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5-Luke21:6)

The crucifixion and the temple
------------------------------

### One of the charges against Jesus Christ was that he had said he would destroy the temple

**[MAT 26:60–26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:60-Matt26:61)**

### The curtain of the temple was torn in two at Jesus Christ's death

**[MAT 27:51](https://ref.ly/Matt27:51)** *The curtain symbolised the barrier between God and his people caused by their sins.*

Believers in Jerusalem met in the temple courtyards
---------------------------------------------------

*See also* [ACT 2:46](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46), [ACT 5:12](https://ref.ly/Acts5:12) *Solomon's Colonnade was on the east side of the temple courtyard.*

Apostles taught and healed in the temple courtyards
---------------------------------------------------

[ACT 3:1–3:26](https://ref.ly/Acts3:1-Acts3:26), [ACT 5:19–5:26](https://ref.ly/Acts5:19-Acts5:26)

Stephen was stoned for allegedly speaking against the temple
------------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 6:12–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:12-Acts6:14)**[ACT 7:48–7:49](https://ref.ly/Acts7:48-Acts7:49)**

Paul was accused of taking Gentiles into temple courtyards forbidden to them
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [ACT 21:27–21:28](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:28)

## 7470: temple, significance of (id: 7470)

The temple was both idealised and spiritualised by OT and NT writers.

The OT concept of an ideal temple in the future age of salvation
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 2:3](https://ref.ly/Isa2:3)** parallel [MIC 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mic4:2) *See also* [ISA 56:4–56:7](https://ref.ly/Isa56:4-Isa56:7), [ISA 66:20](https://ref.ly/Isa66:20); [JER 33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:11); [EZK 37:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:28), [EZK 40:2–40:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:2-Ezek40:4) *Ezekiel received a vision of a rebuilt temple to which the glory of the LORD had returned. Chapters 40–48 describe this temple and its worship in detail*; [HAG 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Hag2:6-Hag2:8); [ZEC 14:16](https://ref.ly/Zech14:16); [MAL 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Mal3:1-Mal3:3)

The superstitious belief that the presence of the temple in Jerusalem safeguarded the city
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The origin of such a belief

[2KI 19:32–19:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:32-2Kgs19:36); ; [PSA 132:13–132:18](https://ref.ly/Ps132:13-Ps132:18)

### The prophets knew that disobedience to God would be judged in spite of the temple

**[MIC 3:12](https://ref.ly/Mic3:12)** *See also* [JER 7:4](https://ref.ly/Jer7:4), [JER 7:12–7:14](https://ref.ly/Jer7:12-Jer7:14)

The link between the earthly temple and heaven
----------------------------------------------

### Heaven is described as God's temple

**[PSA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Ps11:4)** *See also* [HAB 2:20](https://ref.ly/Hab2:20); [HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Heb8:5), [HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Heb9:24); [REV 11:19](https://ref.ly/Rev11:19), [REV 14:15–14:17](https://ref.ly/Rev14:15-Rev14:17), [REV 15:5–15:8](https://ref.ly/Rev15:5-Rev15:8)

### The ultimate \`\`city of God'' needs no temple, since it is permeated by God's presence

**[REV 21:22](https://ref.ly/Rev21:22)** *See also* [REV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Rev21:3)

The risen Christ as the temple where God's glory is revealed
------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 2:19–2:21](https://ref.ly/John2:19-John2:21)**

The church as the temple where God's presence dwells
----------------------------------------------------

### The individual believer as the temple of the Holy Spirit

**[1CO 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19)**

### The local church as God's temple

*See also* [1CO 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:16-1Cor3:17)

### The whole church as God's temple

**[EPH 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:21-Eph2:22)** *See also* [2CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:16); [1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5) *“house” here alludes to the temple. The church offers not animal sacrifices but “spiritual sacrifices” of praise and service.*

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 2:3; Micah 4:2; Isaiah 56:4-7; Isaiah 66:20; Jeremiah 33:11; Ezekiel 37:28; Ezekiel 40:2-4; Haggai 2:6-8; Zechariah 14:16; Malachi 3:1-3; 2 Kings 19:32-36; Psalms 132:13-18; Micah 3:12; Jeremiah 7:4; Jeremiah 7:12-14; Psalms 11:4; Habakkuk 2:20; Hebrews 8:5; Hebrews 9:24; Revelation 11:19; Revelation 14:15-17; Revelation 15:5-8; Revelation 21:22; Revelation 21:3; John 2:19-21; 1 Corinthians 6:19; 1 Corinthians 3:16-17; Ephesians 2:21-22; 2 Corinthians 6:16; 1 Peter 2:5
* **Associated Articles:** 1680: types (ID: `1680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7110: body of Christ (ID: `7110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7241: Jerusalem, significance of (ID: `7241@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8470: respect, for God's character (ID: `8470@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8626: worship, places of (ID: `8626@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9145: Messianic age (ID: `9145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9410: heaven (ID: `9410@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7471: temples, heathen (id: 7471)

Other nations also built temples to their gods, and apostate kings of Judah and Israel were condemned for erecting heathen temples in their own countries.

Heathen temples
---------------

The temple of Dagon: [JDG 16:28–16:30](https://ref.ly/Judg16:28-Judg16:30); ; [1SA 5:2–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:2-1Sam5:4); [1SA 31:8–31:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:8-1Sam31:10); ; [2KI 5:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:18) *the temple of Rimmon*; ; [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14)

Idolatrous temples in Israel and Judah
--------------------------------------

[1KI 12:26–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26-1Kgs12:30) *Jeroboam's temples at Bethel and Dan*; [1KI 16:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:32); ; [2KI 10:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:21), [2KI 10:23–10:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:23-2Kgs10:27); [2CH 23:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:17) *A temple to Baal in Judah was destroyed in the reign of Joash.*

The command to destroy pagan places of worship in Israel
--------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Deut12:2-Deut12:3)**

Holy things and holy places were used for pagan worship
-------------------------------------------------------

Ahaz: [2KI 16:10–16:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10-2Kgs16:13); ; [2CH 28:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:23); [2KI 21:4–21:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:4-2Kgs21:5); ; [2CH 33:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:5), [2CH 33:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:7); [2CH 24:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:7) *Athaliah's sons used objects from the temple for the worship of the Baals*; ; [EZK 8:12–8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:12-Ezek8:16) *God showed Ezekiel a vision of people worshipping false gods in the temple*; ; [2CH 26:16–26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16-2Chr26:20) *Uzziah incurred judgment by usurping the priestly task of offering incense in the temple.*

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 16:28-30; 1 Samuel 5:2-4; 1 Samuel 31:8-10; 2 Kings 5:18; Nahum 1:14; 1 Kings 12:26-30; 1 Kings 16:32; 2 Kings 10:21; 2 Kings 10:23-27; 2 Chronicles 23:17; Deuteronomy 12:2-3; 2 Kings 16:10-13; 2 Chronicles 28:23; 2 Kings 21:4-5; 2 Chronicles 33:5; 2 Chronicles 33:7; 2 Chronicles 24:7; Ezekiel 8:12-16; 2 Chronicles 26:16-20
* **Associated Articles:** 4812: darkness, and God's judgment (ID: `4812@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5688: firstborn (ID: `5688@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6239: prostitution (ID: `6239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7236: Israel, as united kingdom (ID: `7236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7245: Judah, kingdom of (ID: `7245@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7442: shrine (ID: `7442@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8625: worship, acceptable attitudes in (ID: `8625@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8704: apostasy (ID: `8704@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7474: Tent of Meeting (id: 7474)

A tent pitched by Moses outside the Israelite camp in the wilderness. There Moses met with God and others would enquire of the LORD. God's presence was shown there by a pillar of cloud. It seems to predate the construction and setting up of the tabernacle, after which, the term became synonymous with the tabernacle.

Moses pitched the Tent of Meeting outside the Israelite camp
------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 33:7](https://ref.ly/Exod33:7)** *The tent was outside the camp because of the LORD's estrangement from his people ([Ex 33:3](https://ref.ly/Exod33:3)) following their making the golden calf. It was possibly a temporary structure used until the tabernacle was completed.*

The Tent of Meeting was where the faithful met with God
-------------------------------------------------------

### Moses

*See also* [EXO 33:9](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9), [EXO 33:11](https://ref.ly/Exod33:11)

### Those who wished to enquire of the LORD

**[EXO 33:7](https://ref.ly/Exod33:7)**

### Joshua

**[EXO 33:11](https://ref.ly/Exod33:11)**

The pillar of cloud at the Tent of Meeting
------------------------------------------

### It indicated God's presence

**[EXO 33:9](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9)**

### Those who saw the cloud worshipped the LORD

**[EXO 33:10](https://ref.ly/Exod33:10)**

The Tent of Meeting and the tabernacle
--------------------------------------

### This Tent of Meeting may be distinct from the tabernacle

**[EXO 25:8–25:9](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8-Exod25:9)**

### The tabernacle was constructed (in accordance with detailed instructions) after the Tent of Meeting

*See also* [EXO 35:10–35:11](https://ref.ly/Exod35:10-Exod35:11), [EXO 39:32–39:43](https://ref.ly/Exod39:32-Exod39:43)

### The tabernacle was set up after the Tent of Meeting

[EXO 40:1–40:2](https://ref.ly/Exod40:1-Exod40:2), [EXO 40:33](https://ref.ly/Exod40:33)

The Tent of Meeting as a synonym for the tabernacle
---------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 40:2](https://ref.ly/Exod40:2)** *Afterwards “Tent of Meeting” became a synonym for the tabernacle, especially the tent part of the whole complex.* *See also* [EXO 40:6–40:7](https://ref.ly/Exod40:6-Exod40:7), [EXO 40:22](https://ref.ly/Exod40:22), [EXO 40:24](https://ref.ly/Exod40:24), [EXO 40:29–40:30](https://ref.ly/Exod40:29-Exod40:30), [EXO 40:34–40:35](https://ref.ly/Exod40:34-Exod40:35); [LEV 1:1](https://ref.ly/Lev1:1), [LEV 24:3](https://ref.ly/Lev24:3); [NUM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Num1:1), [NUM 31:54](https://ref.ly/Num31:54); [DEU 31:14–31:15](https://ref.ly/Deut31:14-Deut31:15); [JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1), [JOS 19:51](https://ref.ly/Josh19:51); [1SA 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:22); [1KI 8:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:4) parallel [2CH 5:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:5); [1CH 6:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:32), [1CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:21); [2CH 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 33:7; Exodus 33:3; Exodus 33:9; Exodus 33:11; Exodus 33:10; Exodus 25:8-9; Exodus 35:10-11; Exodus 39:32-43; Exodus 40:1-2; Exodus 40:33; Exodus 40:2; Exodus 40:6-7; Exodus 40:22; Exodus 40:24; Exodus 40:29-30; Exodus 40:34-35; Leviticus 1:1; Leviticus 24:3; Numbers 1:1; Numbers 31:54; Deuteronomy 31:14-15; Joshua 18:1; Joshua 19:51; 1 Samuel 2:22; 1 Kings 8:4; 2 Chronicles 5:5; 1 Chronicles 6:32; 1 Chronicles 9:21; 2 Chronicles 1:3
* **Associated Articles:** 5101: Moses (ID: `5101@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5578: tents (ID: `5578@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7251: pillar of cloud and fire (ID: `7251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7306: ark of the covenant (ID: `7306@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7358: Feast of Tabernacles (ID: `7358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7459: tabernacle, in OT (ID: `7459@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8626: worship, places of (ID: `8626@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8648: enquiring of God (ID: `8648@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7476: thank-offering (id: 7476)

Together with vows and freewill offerings this was one of the three kinds of fellowship offering.

Presenting a thank\-offering
----------------------------

### An animal sacrifice

**[LEV 7:15](https://ref.ly/Lev7:15)** *See also* [LEV 22:29](https://ref.ly/Lev22:29)

### Accompanied by bread, wafers and cakes

[LEV 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Lev7:12-Lev7:13) *Cakes made with yeast can be offered, since these are not to be burned on the altar.*

### The priest shares the meal

[LEV 7:14](https://ref.ly/Lev7:14), [LEV 22:29–22:30](https://ref.ly/Lev22:29-Lev22:30)

The occasions for presenting thank\-offerings
---------------------------------------------

### For deliverance from distress, death or sickness

**[PSA 50:23](https://ref.ly/Ps50:23), [PSA 107:21–107:22](https://ref.ly/Ps107:21-Ps107:22)** *See also* [PSA 56:12–56:13](https://ref.ly/Ps56:12-Ps56:13) *deliverance from death*; [PSA 116:17](https://ref.ly/Ps116:17) *Deliverance from sickness; the earlier verses show the psalmist has been ill.*

### Following a vow

**[PSA 7:17](https://ref.ly/Ps7:17)** *A vow to praise God in anticipation of an answer to prayer.* *See also* [PSA 66:13–66:14](https://ref.ly/Ps66:13-Ps66:14)

### At times of religious renewal

[2CH 29:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:31) *Hezekiah's reformation*; ; [2CH 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:15-2Chr33:16) *Manasseh's repentance*; [JER 17:26](https://ref.ly/Jer17:26), [JER 33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 7:15; Leviticus 22:29; Leviticus 7:12-13; Leviticus 7:14; Leviticus 22:29-30; Psalms 50:23; Psalms 107:21-22; Psalms 56:12-13; Psalms 116:17; Psalms 7:17; Psalms 66:13-14; 2 Chronicles 29:31; 2 Chronicles 33:15-16; Jeremiah 17:26; Jeremiah 33:11
* **Associated Articles:** 4554: yeast (ID: `4554@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5741: vows (ID: `5741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7364: fellowship offering (ID: `7364@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7366: freewill offering (ID: `7366@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8352: thankfulness (ID: `8352@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8676: thanksgiving (ID: `8676@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7478: washing (id: 7478)

The physical or ritual cleansing of part or all of a person's body, a person's clothing or certain vessels. Washing can be symbolic of purification from defilement or cleansing from sin. It can also be part of preparation for a special act of religious service.

Washing as the cleansing of the body, clothing or certain vessels
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 30:17–30:21](https://ref.ly/Exod30:17-Exod30:21)**; **[LEV 13:53–13:54](https://ref.ly/Lev13:53-Lev13:54)** *See also* [EXO 40:30–40:32](https://ref.ly/Exod40:30-Exod40:32); [LEV 6:27](https://ref.ly/Lev6:27), [LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9), [LEV 15:2–15:13](https://ref.ly/Lev15:2-Lev15:13), [LEV 16:24](https://ref.ly/Lev16:24), [LEV 16:26–16:28](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26-Lev16:28); [NUM 31:24](https://ref.ly/Num31:24); [2CH 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:6) parallel [1KI 7:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:38); [JHN 2:6](https://ref.ly/John2:6)

Jesus Christ taught that inner purity was more important than ritual washing
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 15:17–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:17-Matt15:20)** parallel [MRK 7:20–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:20-Mark7:23) *See also* [MRK 7:1–7:9](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:9) parallel [MAT 15:1–15:3](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:3); [MRK 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14-Mark7:15) parallel [MAT 15:10–15:11](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10-Matt15:11); [LUK 11:37–11:41](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37-Luke11:41)

Washing as a symbolic act
-------------------------

### Of purification from defilement

**[LEV 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/Lev11:24-Lev11:25)** *See also* [LEV 11:28](https://ref.ly/Lev11:28), [LEV 11:40](https://ref.ly/Lev11:40), [LEV 13:6](https://ref.ly/Lev13:6), [LEV 13:34](https://ref.ly/Lev13:34), [LEV 15:4–15:8](https://ref.ly/Lev15:4-Lev15:8), [LEV 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15-Lev17:16); [DEU 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Deut23:10-Deut23:11); [2KI 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:10)

### Of cleansing from sin

**[1CO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:11)**; **[EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27)** *See also* [PSA 51:2](https://ref.ly/Ps51:2), [PSA 51:7](https://ref.ly/Ps51:7); [ISA 1:16](https://ref.ly/Isa1:16), [ISA 4:4](https://ref.ly/Isa4:4); [JER 4:14](https://ref.ly/Jer4:14); [ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:16); [TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5); [HEB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:22); [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8); [REV 7:14](https://ref.ly/Rev7:14), [REV 22:14](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14)

### Of personal cleansing before a special act of religious service

**[LEV 16:24](https://ref.ly/Lev16:24)** *See also* [EXO 19:10–19:11](https://ref.ly/Exod19:10-Exod19:11), [EXO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Exod29:4), [EXO 40:12–40:13](https://ref.ly/Exod40:12-Exod40:13), [EXO 40:30–40:32](https://ref.ly/Exod40:30-Exod40:32); [LEV 8:6](https://ref.ly/Lev8:6); [NUM 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/Num8:6-Num8:7), [NUM 8:21](https://ref.ly/Num8:21)

### Washing one's hands was used as a symbolic declaration of innocence

**[DEU 21:6–21:7](https://ref.ly/Deut21:6-Deut21:7)** *See also* [PSA 26:6](https://ref.ly/Ps26:6), [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13); [MAT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:24)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 30:17-21; Leviticus 13:53-54; Exodus 40:30-32; Leviticus 6:27; Leviticus 14:8-9; Leviticus 15:2-13; Leviticus 16:24; Leviticus 16:26-28; Numbers 31:24; 2 Chronicles 4:6; 1 Kings 7:38; John 2:6; Matthew 15:17-20; Mark 7:20-23; Mark 7:1-9; Matthew 15:1-3; Mark 7:14-15; Matthew 15:10-11; Luke 11:37-41; Leviticus 11:24-25; Leviticus 11:28; Leviticus 11:40; Leviticus 13:6; Leviticus 13:34; Leviticus 15:4-8; Leviticus 17:15-16; Deuteronomy 23:10-11; 2 Kings 5:10; 1 Corinthians 6:9-11; Ephesians 5:25-27; Psalms 51:2; Psalms 51:7; Isaiah 1:16; Isaiah 4:4; Jeremiah 4:14; Acts 22:16; Titus 3:5; Hebrews 10:22; James 4:8; Revelation 7:14; Revelation 22:14; Exodus 19:10-11; Exodus 29:4; Exodus 40:12-13; Leviticus 8:6; Numbers 8:6-7; Numbers 8:21; Deuteronomy 21:6-7; Psalms 26:6; Psalms 73:13; Matthew 27:24
* **Associated Articles:** 4293: water (ID: `4293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5129: bathing (ID: `5129@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5153: foot-washing (ID: `5153@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5226: basin (ID: `5226@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7342: cleanliness (ID: `7342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7416: purification (ID: `7416@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7426: ritual washing (ID: `7426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8218: consecration (ID: `8218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8325: purity, nature of (ID: `8325@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7480: wave offering (id: 7480)

A gift specially dedicated to God. The term itself may not be intended to convey a particular action.

The wave offering was part of every fellowship offering
-------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 7:34](https://ref.ly/Lev7:34)** *Although given to the priests, the offering belonged to the LORD.* *See also* [LEV 7:28–7:32](https://ref.ly/Lev7:28-Lev7:32), [LEV 9:21](https://ref.ly/Lev9:21)

The wave offering was eaten by the priests
------------------------------------------

[EXO 29:26–29:28](https://ref.ly/Exod29:26-Exod29:28); ; [LEV 10:15](https://ref.ly/Lev10:15); ; [NUM 18:11](https://ref.ly/Num18:11), [NUM 18:18](https://ref.ly/Num18:18)

Occasions for making a wave offering
------------------------------------

### As a cereal offering when priests were consecrated

[EXO 29:23–29:24](https://ref.ly/Exod29:23-Exod29:24); ; [LEV 8:27–8:29](https://ref.ly/Lev8:27-Lev8:29)

### As a guilt offering sacrificed at a ceremonial cleansing

[LEV 14:12](https://ref.ly/Lev14:12), [LEV 14:21](https://ref.ly/Lev14:21), [LEV 14:24](https://ref.ly/Lev14:24)

### As part of the ritual when a woman was accused of adultery

[NUM 5:25](https://ref.ly/Num5:25)

### When celebrating harvest

[LEV 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Lev23:10-Lev23:11) *the firstfruits of the harvest*; [LEV 23:15–23:17](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:17), [LEV 23:20](https://ref.ly/Lev23:20)

### When presenting a gift to God

Materials given for constructing the tabernacle: [EXO 35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:22), [EXO 38:24](https://ref.ly/Exod38:24), [EXO 38:29](https://ref.ly/Exod38:29)

### When dedicating the Levites to God

**[NUM 8:11](https://ref.ly/Num8:11)** *The dedication to God of the Levites symbolised the consecration of the whole nation.* *See also* [NUM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Num8:13), [NUM 8:15](https://ref.ly/Num8:15), [NUM 8:21](https://ref.ly/Num8:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 7:34; Leviticus 7:28-32; Leviticus 9:21; Exodus 29:26-28; Leviticus 10:15; Numbers 18:11; Numbers 18:18; Exodus 29:23-24; Leviticus 8:27-29; Leviticus 14:12; Leviticus 14:21; Leviticus 14:24; Numbers 5:25; Leviticus 23:10-11; Leviticus 23:15-17; Leviticus 23:20; Exodus 35:22; Exodus 38:24; Exodus 38:29; Numbers 8:11; Numbers 8:13; Numbers 8:15; Numbers 8:21
* **Associated Articles:** 4442: firstfruits (ID: `4442@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7357: Feast of Firstfruits (ID: `7357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7361: Feast of Weeks (ID: `7361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7364: fellowship offering (ID: `7364@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7370: guilt offering (ID: `7370@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7390: Levites (ID: `7390@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7412: priesthood (ID: `7412@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7482: Year of Jubilee (id: 7482)

Celebrated in the fiftieth year as the culmination of seven sabbatical years. During this year property was restored and Hebrew slaves were released. This year of liberation gave opportunity for a new start, and is alluded to as a type of the Messianic age.

The institution of the Year of Jubilee
--------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:8–25:10](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:10)**

Land was left fallow in the Year of Jubilee
-------------------------------------------

*See also* [LEV 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/Lev25:11-Lev25:12), [LEV 25:20–25:22](https://ref.ly/Lev25:20-Lev25:22) *These instructions apply to the sabbatical year. The Year of Jubilee may have been an additional fallow year, or may have coincided with the seventh sabbatical year.*

The restoration of property in the Year of Jubilee
--------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:13](https://ref.ly/Lev25:13)**

### Regulating the value of property

*See also* [LEV 25:15–25:16](https://ref.ly/Lev25:15-Lev25:16), [LEV 27:16–27:19](https://ref.ly/Lev27:16-Lev27:19), [LEV 27:23](https://ref.ly/Lev27:23)

### Underlining the fact that God is the true owner of the land

**[LEV 25:23–25:24](https://ref.ly/Lev25:23-Lev25:24)** *The restoration of property to those to whom God had originally entrusted it is a reminder that the land belongs to him.* *See also* [JOS 21:43](https://ref.ly/Josh21:43); [1CH 29:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:15); [HEB 11:13](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13)

### A means of preserving the inheritance from God

**[LEV 25:25–25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:25-Lev25:28)** *Families are urged to buy back land assigned to them by God. As a last resort, land is restored in the Year of Jubilee.* *See also* [LEV 25:32–25:33](https://ref.ly/Lev25:32-Lev25:33), [LEV 27:24](https://ref.ly/Lev27:24); [NUM 36:4](https://ref.ly/Num36:4), [NUM 36:7–36:9](https://ref.ly/Num36:7-Num36:9); [1KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:3); [EZK 46:16–46:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:16-Ezek46:18)

The release of Hebrew slaves in the Year of Jubilee
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 15:12–15:15](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:15)** *The release of slaves reflects God's redemption and preserves the freedom he has given*; **[LEV 25:54–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:54-Lev25:55)** *The Israelites may not be held permanently as slaves because they belong to God.* *See also* [LEV 25:39–25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:43), [LEV 25:50–25:52](https://ref.ly/Lev25:50-Lev25:52); [JER 34:8–34:9](https://ref.ly/Jer34:8-Jer34:9), [JER 34:13–34:14](https://ref.ly/Jer34:13-Jer34:14)

The Year of Jubilee is the year of the Lord's favour
----------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19)** *Jesus Christ alludes to the Year of Jubilee in proclaiming spiritual release and restoration.* *See also* [ISA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:2)

## 7505: Jews, the (id: 7505)

A post\-exilic designation for descendants of the people from the kingdom of Judah and also for adherents to their religion from other nations. The term is used extensively in the NT where it has a variety of nuances.

Early use of the term \`\`Jews''
--------------------------------

### Pre\-exilic and exilic use

**[JER 40:11–40:12](https://ref.ly/Jer40:11-Jer40:12)** *See also* [JER 32:12](https://ref.ly/Jer32:12), [JER 34:8–34:9](https://ref.ly/Jer34:8-Jer34:9), [JER 38:19](https://ref.ly/Jer38:19), [JER 40:15](https://ref.ly/Jer40:15), [JER 41:3](https://ref.ly/Jer41:3), [JER 43:8–43:9](https://ref.ly/Jer43:8-Jer43:9), [JER 44:1](https://ref.ly/Jer44:1), [JER 44:26–44:27](https://ref.ly/Jer44:26-Jer44:27), [JER 52:28–52:30](https://ref.ly/Jer52:28-Jer52:30); [DAN 3:8–3:12](https://ref.ly/Dan3:8-Dan3:12)

### Used of the citizens of the province of Judah

**[EZR 5:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:1)** *See also* [EZR 4:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:23), [EZR 5:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:5), [EZR 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:7-Ezra6:8), [EZR 6:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:14); [NEH 2:16](https://ref.ly/Neh2:16), [NEH 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Neh4:1-Neh4:2), [NEH 4:12](https://ref.ly/Neh4:12), [NEH 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/Neh5:6-Neh5:8), [NEH 5:17](https://ref.ly/Neh5:17), [NEH 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Neh6:5-Neh6:7); [ZEC 8:23](https://ref.ly/Zech8:23)

### Used of the descendants of the exiles from the southern kingdom

**[EST 2:5](https://ref.ly/Esth2:5)** *See also* [EZR 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:11-Ezra4:12), [EZR 7:12–7:20](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:12-Ezra7:20); [EST 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:1-Esth3:6), [EST 3:13](https://ref.ly/Esth3:13), [EST 4:3](https://ref.ly/Esth4:3), [EST 4:12–4:14](https://ref.ly/Esth4:12-Esth4:14), [EST 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Esth6:12-Esth6:13), [EST 8:5–8:13](https://ref.ly/Esth8:5-Esth8:13), [EST 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1-Esth9:3), [EST 9:30–9:31](https://ref.ly/Esth9:30-Esth9:31), [EST 10:3](https://ref.ly/Esth10:3)

NT use of the term \`\`Jews''
-----------------------------

### Jewish people in general

**[MRK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mark7:3)** *See also* [MAT 28:15](https://ref.ly/Matt28:15); [LUK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Luke7:3); [JHN 2:6](https://ref.ly/John2:6), [JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9), [JHN 4:19–4:22](https://ref.ly/John4:19-John4:22), [JHN 8:31–8:32](https://ref.ly/John8:31-John8:32), [JHN 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/John11:18-John11:19), [JHN 11:31–11:33](https://ref.ly/John11:31-John11:33), [JHN 11:45](https://ref.ly/John11:45), [JHN 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/John12:9-John12:11), [JHN 18:20](https://ref.ly/John18:20)

### Those Jewish leaders who were hostile to Jesus Christ

**[JHN 19:31](https://ref.ly/John19:31)** *See also* [JHN 2:18–2:20](https://ref.ly/John2:18-John2:20), [JHN 5:1–5:18](https://ref.ly/John5:1-John5:18), [JHN 6:35–6:42](https://ref.ly/John6:35-John6:42), [JHN 7:1](https://ref.ly/John7:1), [JHN 7:11–7:15](https://ref.ly/John7:11-John7:15), [JHN 8:48–8:59](https://ref.ly/John8:48-John8:59), [JHN 9:1–9:34](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:34), [JHN 10:22–10:33](https://ref.ly/John10:22-John10:33), [JHN 18:12–18:14](https://ref.ly/John18:12-John18:14), [JHN 18:28–18:31](https://ref.ly/John18:28-John18:31), [JHN 19:38](https://ref.ly/John19:38), [JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/John20:19)

Jesus Christ as \`\`king of the Jews''
--------------------------------------

**[MAT 27:37](https://ref.ly/Matt27:37)** parallels [MRK 15:26](https://ref.ly/Mark15:26), [LUK 23:38](https://ref.ly/Luke23:38), [JHN 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/John19:19-John19:21) *See also* [MAT 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:2), [MAT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11) parallels [MRK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2), [JHN 18:33](https://ref.ly/John18:33); [MAT 27:27–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:31) parallel [MRK 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:16-Mark15:20); [JHN 19:1–19:6](https://ref.ly/John19:1-John19:6); [MRK 15:6–15:14](https://ref.ly/Mark15:6-Mark15:14) parallel [JHN 18:38–18:40](https://ref.ly/John18:38-John18:40)

-------------------------------------

**[ACT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Acts11:19)**; **[ROM 10:16–10:21](https://ref.ly/Rom10:16-Rom10:21)** *See also* [ACT 2:1–2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:41), [ACT 10:23–10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:23-Acts10:48), [ACT 13:14–13:43](https://ref.ly/Acts13:14-Acts13:43); [ROM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rom1:16), [ROM 15:8–15:9](https://ref.ly/Rom15:8-Rom15:9); [GAL 2:7–2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7-Gal2:9)

Opposition of certain Jews to the gospel
----------------------------------------

**[ACT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Acts20:19)** *See also* [ACT 6:8–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8-Acts6:14), [ACT 9:23–9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:23-Acts9:25), [ACT 9:28–9:30](https://ref.ly/Acts9:28-Acts9:30), [ACT 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Acts12:1-Acts12:3), [ACT 13:44–13:45](https://ref.ly/Acts13:44-Acts13:45), [ACT 13:49–13:50](https://ref.ly/Acts13:49-Acts13:50), [ACT 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1-Acts14:6), [ACT 14:19](https://ref.ly/Acts14:19), [ACT 17:4–17:9](https://ref.ly/Acts17:4-Acts17:9), [ACT 17:13](https://ref.ly/Acts17:13), [ACT 18:1–18:6](https://ref.ly/Acts18:1-Acts18:6), [ACT 18:12–18:17](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12-Acts18:17), [ACT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Acts20:3); [2CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:25); [ACT 21:27–21:36](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:36), [ACT 23:12–23:15](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12-Acts23:15), [ACT 24:1–24:9](https://ref.ly/Acts24:1-Acts24:9), [ACT 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts25:7), [ACT 26:19–26:21](https://ref.ly/Acts26:19-Acts26:21)

----------------------------------------

## 7510: Gentiles (id: 7510)

Those who are not physical descendants of Abraham and were therefore regarded as being excluded from the promise made to him and his descendants. The Gentiles are often referred to as “the nations” in OT writings.

* **Associated Articles:** 7511: Gentiles, in OT (ID: `7511@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7512: Gentiles, in NT (ID: `7512@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7511: Gentiles, in OT (id: 7511)

Under the OT, Gentiles were generally excluded from the worship of the synagogue and from all the privileges of being Jewish. However, the OT made provision for non\-Jews who believed in the God of Israel.

Believing foreigners were permitted to participate in worship
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:48–12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48-Exod12:49)** *See also* [NUM 9:14](https://ref.ly/Num9:14), [NUM 15:13–15:16](https://ref.ly/Num15:13-Num15:16); [1KI 8:41–8:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:41-1Kgs8:43); [ISA 56:6–56:8](https://ref.ly/Isa56:6-Isa56:8)

Foreigners living in Israel were to be treated kindly
-----------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21); [LEV 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:9-Lev19:10), [LEV 24:22](https://ref.ly/Lev24:22); [DEU 26:12–26:15](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12-Deut26:15), [DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19)

Divine promises concerning the Gentiles
---------------------------------------

### All people will be blessed through Abraham

**[GEN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:3)** *See also* [GEN 18:18](https://ref.ly/Gen18:18), [GEN 22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:18); [GAL 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Gal3:8-Gal3:9)

### All nations will bow before God

**[PSA 22:27–22:28](https://ref.ly/Ps22:27-Ps22:28)** *See also* [PSA 86:9](https://ref.ly/Ps86:9); [DAN 7:14](https://ref.ly/Dan7:14); [AMO 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11-Amos9:12)

### The Gentiles will be enlightened

**[ISA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:6)** *See also* [ISA 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:1-Isa9:2), [ISA 42:6](https://ref.ly/Isa42:6), [ISA 60:1–60:3](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1-Isa60:3); [LUK 2:30–2:32](https://ref.ly/Luke2:30-Luke2:32)

### The Messiah will bring justice to the Gentiles

**[MAT 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Matt12:18-Matt12:21)** *See also* [ISA 42:1–42:4](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1-Isa42:4)

### Gentiles will become God's people

**[ROM 9:22–9:25](https://ref.ly/Rom9:22-Rom9:25)** *See also* [HOS 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hos2:23); [1PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:10)

### Gentiles will praise God

**[ROM 15:9–15:12](https://ref.ly/Rom15:9-Rom15:12)** *See also* [DEU 32:43](https://ref.ly/Deut32:43); [2SA 22:50](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:50); [PSA 18:49](https://ref.ly/Ps18:49); [ISA 11:10](https://ref.ly/Isa11:10)

## 7512: Gentiles, in NT (id: 7512)

Although Jesus Christ initially preached to Israel, his mission was soon extended to the Gentiles. From the outset, the Christian church accepted Gentiles as full members, refusing to make any distinction between Jew and Gentile. Paul, “the apostle to the Gentiles”, laid particular emphasis on their role in the purposes of God.

Gentiles in Jesus Christ's earthly ministry
-------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's contact with Gentiles

[MAT 8:5–8:13](https://ref.ly/Matt8:5-Matt8:13) parallel [LUK 7:1–7:10](https://ref.ly/Luke7:1-Luke7:10); ; [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30)

### Jesus Christ's teaching concerning Gentiles

**[MAT 21:33–21:43](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:43)** parallels [MRK 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:11), [LUK 20:9–20:16](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:16)**[LUK 21:20–21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20-Luke21:24)**

### Jesus Christ's instructions to his disciples concerning Gentiles

*See also* [MAT 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:6) *Initially the disciples were commanded to preach to Jews, but after his resurrection, Jesus Christ commissioned them to preach the message of the kingdom of God to all nations.***[MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20)**[MRK 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15-Mark16:16); [LUK 24:46–24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46-Luke24:47)

----------------------
