Paul, apostle to the Gentiles
-----------------------------

**[GAL 2:7–2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7-Gal2:9)** *See also* [ACT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Acts9:15), [ACT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Acts22:21), [ACT 26:15–26:23](https://ref.ly/Acts26:15-Acts26:23); [ROM 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:13-Rom1:15), [ROM 11:13](https://ref.ly/Rom11:13), [ROM 15:15–15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:15-Rom15:19); [GAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:2); [EPH 3:1](https://ref.ly/Eph3:1); [1TI 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:7); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

### Rejected by Jews, Paul turns more to the Gentiles

--------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------

### The question of mixing with Gentiles

**[ACT 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/Acts11:2-Acts11:3)** *See also* [ACT 11:15–11:18](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15-Acts11:18); [GAL 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Gal2:12-Gal2:13)

NT teaching on Gentiles
-----------------------

### Gentiles now share the inheritance of God's people Israel

**[EPH 2:13](https://ref.ly/Eph2:13)**; **[REV 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9-Rev5:10)** *See also* [ROM 3:29](https://ref.ly/Rom3:29), [ROM 11:11–11:21](https://ref.ly/Rom11:11-Rom11:21); [GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:29); [REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rev14:6)

### There is no distinction between Jews and Gentiles

**[GAL 3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:28)** *See also* [ROM 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:9-Rom2:11); [EPH 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19-Eph2:20); [COL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Col3:11)

God calls both Jew and Gentile
------------------------------

**[JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:16)**; **[ACT 13:47–13:48](https://ref.ly/Acts13:47-Acts13:48)** *See also* [ROM 9:24–9:26](https://ref.ly/Rom9:24-Rom9:26); [1CO 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:24-1Cor1:25); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

### Both Jews and Gentiles are justified by faith

**[GAL 3:6–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:6-Gal3:14)** *See also* [ROM 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Rom4:9-Rom4:12), [ROM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Rom4:16); [GAL 5:6](https://ref.ly/Gal5:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 8:5-13; Luke 7:1-10; Matthew 15:21-28; Mark 7:24-30; Matthew 21:33-43; Mark 12:1-11; Luke 20:9-16; Luke 21:20-24; Matthew 10:5-6; Matthew 28:19-20; Mark 16:15-16; Luke 24:46-47; Acts 10:28; Acts 10:9-20; Acts 11:5-14; Acts 10:44-48; Acts 11:15; Acts 15:7-8; Galatians 2:7-9; Acts 9:15; Acts 22:21; Acts 26:15-23; Romans 1:13-15; Romans 11:13; Romans 15:15-19; Galatians 2:2; Ephesians 3:1; 1 Timothy 2:7; 2 Timothy 4:17; Acts 18:6; Acts 13:46; Acts 28:28; Acts 13:48; Acts 14:1-2; Acts 15:12; Acts 21:19; 1 Corinthians 1:23-24; Acts 11:2-3; Acts 11:15-18; Galatians 2:12-13; Acts 15:5-31; Galatians 2:1-5; Galatians 5:1-6; Ephesians 2:13; Revelation 5:9-10; Romans 3:29; Romans 11:11-21; Galatians 3:29; Revelation 7:9; Revelation 14:6; Galatians 3:28; Romans 2:9-11; Ephesians 2:19-20; Colossians 3:11; John 10:16; Acts 13:47-48; Romans 9:24-26; 1 Corinthians 1:24-25; Galatians 3:6-14; Romans 4:9-12; Romans 4:16; Galatians 5:6
* **Associated Articles:** 1115: God, purpose of (ID: `1115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3251: Holy Spirit, filling with (ID: `3251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5112: Peter (ID: `5112@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7142: people of God, in NT (ID: `7142@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7560: Samaritans, the (ID: `7560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7610: Council of Jerusalem (ID: `7610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7515: anti-semitism (id: 7515)

A modern term, used to refer to a hatred of Jews or Arabs. Traces of anti\-semitism may be discerned in biblical times.

The development of anti\-semitism
---------------------------------

**[EXO 1:8–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:8-Exod1:14)** *Strictly speaking, this hostility towards the Israelites in Egypt after the death of Joseph was not anti\-semitism as this word is now understood.* *See also* [EXO 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Exod1:15-Exod1:16), [EXO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:22), [EXO 3:9](https://ref.ly/Exod3:9), [EXO 5:6–5:9](https://ref.ly/Exod5:6-Exod5:9)

Anti\-semitism under Persian rule
---------------------------------

**[EST 3:1–3:14](https://ref.ly/Esth3:1-Esth3:14)** *See also* [EZR 4:1–4:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:1-Ezra4:8), [EZR 4:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:24)

Anti\-semitism under Babylonian rule
------------------------------------

**[DAN 3:8–3:23](https://ref.ly/Dan3:8-Dan3:23)** *See also* [DAN 6:3–6:14](https://ref.ly/Dan6:3-Dan6:14)

Anti\-semitism under Roman rule
-------------------------------

**[ACT 18:1–18:2](https://ref.ly/Acts18:1-Acts18:2)**

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 1:8-14; Exodus 1:15-16; Exodus 1:22; Exodus 3:9; Exodus 5:6-9; Esther 3:1-14; Ezra 4:1-8; Ezra 4:24; Daniel 3:8-23; Daniel 6:3-14; Acts 18:1-2
* **Associated Articles:** 5560: suffering (ID: `5560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5875: hatred (ID: `5875@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7505: Jews, the (ID: `7505@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8292: love (ID: `8292@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8800: prejudice (ID: `8800@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7520: dispersion, the (id: 7520)

A technical term used to refer to God's people as they are scattered throughout the world.

The dispersion of the Jews
--------------------------

### Promised and predicted

**[DEU 4:25–4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:25-Deut4:28)**; **[1KI 14:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:15)** *See also* [DEU 28:15–28:68](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:68); [LEV 26:27–26:35](https://ref.ly/Lev26:27-Lev26:35); [NEH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Neh1:8-Neh1:9); [JER 13:24](https://ref.ly/Jer13:24); [EZK 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:8-Ezek6:10), [EZK 20:23–20:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:23-Ezek20:24), [EZK 22:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:15)

### The prediction fulfilled

**[JER 50:17](https://ref.ly/Jer50:17)**; **[DAN 9:7](https://ref.ly/Dan9:7)** *See also* [EST 3:8](https://ref.ly/Esth3:8); [PSA 44:9–44:16](https://ref.ly/Ps44:9-Ps44:16); [LAM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Lam4:16); [EZK 36:17–36:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:17-Ezek36:20); [ZEP 3:10](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:10); [ZEC 1:19](https://ref.ly/Zech1:19); [JHN 7:35](https://ref.ly/John7:35) *In NT times there were more Jews living outside Palestine than within.*

### The promise of return

**[ISA 11:12](https://ref.ly/Isa11:12)** *See also* [DEU 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Deut4:29-Deut4:31); [JER 31:10](https://ref.ly/Jer31:10); [EZK 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:16-Ezek11:17)

The dispersion of Christians
----------------------------

**[ACT 8:1](https://ref.ly/Acts8:1)** *Christians scattered because of persecution*; **[1PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1)** *See also* [ACT 8:4](https://ref.ly/Acts8:4), [ACT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Acts11:19); [JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 4:25-28; 1 Kings 14:15; Deuteronomy 28:15-68; Leviticus 26:27-35; Nehemiah 1:8-9; Jeremiah 13:24; Ezekiel 6:8-10; Ezekiel 20:23-24; Ezekiel 22:15; Jeremiah 50:17; Daniel 9:7; Esther 3:8; Psalms 44:9-16; Lamentations 4:16; Ezekiel 36:17-20; Zephaniah 3:10; Zechariah 1:19; John 7:35; Isaiah 11:12; Deuteronomy 4:29-31; Jeremiah 31:10; Ezekiel 11:16-17; Acts 8:1; 1 Peter 1:1; Acts 8:4; Acts 11:19; James 1:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1025: God, anger of (ID: `1025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7145: remnant (ID: `7145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7215: exile, the (ID: `7215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8160: seeking God (ID: `8160@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8839: unfaithfulness (ID: `8839@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9165: restoration (ID: `9165@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7525: exclusiveness (id: 7525)

The keeping of a distinctive group identity by drawing clear boundary lines, to separate those within the group from outsiders with whom they are unwilling to associate.

The practice of exclusiveness by God's people, grounded in God's claim to exclusive worship
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Exod20:3)** parallel [DEU 5:7](https://ref.ly/Deut5:7) *See also* [EXO 34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:14); [DEU 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:14-Deut6:15); [2KI 17:35–17:39](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:35-2Kgs17:39); [ISA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Isa42:8)

Exclusiveness in Israel, a practical expression of being a people set apart
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### An expression of being set apart for God

**[LEV 20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:26)** *See also* [LEV 15:31](https://ref.ly/Lev15:31), [LEV 20:23–20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:23-Lev20:24); [DEU 7:1–7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:6)

### An expression of being set apart from others

**[NUM 23:9](https://ref.ly/Num23:9)** *See also* [JOS 23:7](https://ref.ly/Josh23:7); [EZR 6:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:21); [NEH 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Neh13:1-Neh13:3); [EZK 44:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:9)

The practice of exclusiveness
-----------------------------

### Expressed in the avoidance of marrying outsiders

**[NEH 10:30](https://ref.ly/Neh10:30)** *See also* [DEU 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Deut7:3-Deut7:4); [EZR 9:1–9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:1-Ezra9:4), [EZR 10:9–10:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:9-Ezra10:17); [NEH 13:23–13:27](https://ref.ly/Neh13:23-Neh13:27)

### Expressed in a refusal to eat with outsiders

**[MAT 9:10–9:11](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10-Matt9:11)** parallels [MRK 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15-Mark2:16), [LUK 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29-Luke5:30) *See also* [LUK 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:2); [GAL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Gal2:12)

The marks of Jewish exclusiveness
---------------------------------

### Circumcision

**[GEN 17:9–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:14)** *See also* [EXO 12:48](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48); [LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3); [ACT 7:8](https://ref.ly/Acts7:8)

### Festival observance

**[EXO 12:14–12:16](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14-Exod12:16)** *See also* [EXO 12:17–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17-Exod12:20); [LEV 23:26–23:29](https://ref.ly/Lev23:26-Lev23:29); [NUM 9:13](https://ref.ly/Num9:13)

### Observance of food laws

**[LEV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Lev3:17)** *See also* [LEV 7:22–7:27](https://ref.ly/Lev7:22-Lev7:27), [LEV 11:1–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:47), [LEV 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Lev17:10-Lev17:14); [ACT 15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:29) *While food restrictions were not a requirement of the gospel, Gentile believers are here asked to respect the scruples of Jewish Christians in those areas that were traditionally important to them.*

### Sexual relations restricted

**[LEV 18:6](https://ref.ly/Lev18:6)** *See also* [LEV 18:7–18:30](https://ref.ly/Lev18:7-Lev18:30), [LEV 20:10–20:21](https://ref.ly/Lev20:10-Lev20:21); [DEU 27:20–27:23](https://ref.ly/Deut27:20-Deut27:23)

Effects of Jewish exclusiveness
-------------------------------

### Division between Jews and Samaritans

**[JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9)** *See also* [EZR 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:1-Ezra4:3); [MAT 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:6) *Jesus Christ was here dealing with his priority for that time*; [LUK 9:51–9:53](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51-Luke9:53)

### Division between Jews and Gentiles

**[ACT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Acts10:28)** *See also* [MAT 10:5](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5), [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30) *Jesus Christ restricted his earthly ministry in general to the people of Israel, but instigated a mission to the Gentiles after his resurrection*; [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/John18:28)

The problem of Jewish exclusiveness among the first Christians
--------------------------------------------------------------

### The negative evaluation of Gentiles

**[ACT 11:1–11:18](https://ref.ly/Acts11:1-Acts11:18)** *See also* [GAL 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:13), [GAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15)

### The requirement that Gentile believers be circumcised

**[ACT 15:1](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1)** *See also* [ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5); [GAL 5:2–5:6](https://ref.ly/Gal5:2-Gal5:6), [GAL 6:12](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12); [TIT 1:10](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10)

Jewish exclusiveness overturned
-------------------------------

### By God giving his Spirit to the uncircumcised Gentiles

**[ACT 15:8](https://ref.ly/Acts15:8)** *See also* [ACT 10:44–11:18](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts11:18); [1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13); [GAL 3:2–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gal3:2-Gal3:5)

### By the argument of justification by faith

**[GAL 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15-Gal2:16)** *See also* [ACT 15:9–15:11](https://ref.ly/Acts15:9-Acts15:11); [ROM 3:28–3:30](https://ref.ly/Rom3:28-Rom3:30), [ROM 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Rom4:9-Rom4:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:3; Deuteronomy 5:7; Exodus 34:14; Deuteronomy 6:14-15; 2 Kings 17:35-39; Isaiah 42:8; Leviticus 20:26; Leviticus 15:31; Leviticus 20:23-24; Deuteronomy 7:1-6; Numbers 23:9; Joshua 23:7; Ezra 6:21; Nehemiah 13:1-3; Ezekiel 44:9; Nehemiah 10:30; Deuteronomy 7:3-4; Ezra 9:1-4; Ezra 10:9-17; Nehemiah 13:23-27; Matthew 9:10-11; Mark 2:15-16; Luke 5:29-30; Luke 15:1-2; Galatians 2:12; Genesis 17:9-14; Exodus 12:48; Leviticus 12:3; Acts 7:8; Exodus 12:14-16; Exodus 12:17-20; Leviticus 23:26-29; Numbers 9:13; Leviticus 3:17; Leviticus 7:22-27; Leviticus 11:1-47; Leviticus 17:10-14; Acts 15:29; Leviticus 18:6; Leviticus 18:7-30; Leviticus 20:10-21; Deuteronomy 27:20-23; John 4:9; Ezra 4:1-3; Matthew 10:5-6; Luke 9:51-53; Acts 10:28; Matthew 10:5; Matthew 15:21-28; Mark 7:24-30; John 18:28; Acts 11:1-18; Galatians 2:11-13; Galatians 2:15; Acts 15:1; Acts 15:5; Galatians 5:2-6; Galatians 6:12; Titus 1:10; Acts 15:8; Acts 10:44-11:18; 1 Corinthians 12:13; Galatians 3:2-5; Galatians 2:15-16; Acts 15:9-11; Romans 3:28-30; Romans 4:9-12

## 7530: foreigners (id: 7530)

Foreigners played a major role in the life of Israel. They surrounded her, and lived within her borders. In order to fulfil her mission as the bearer of God's law, Israel had to distinguish herself from foreign nations and individuals, especially with regard to their religious beliefs. A clear distinction is made between foreign nations and foreign individuals, who are to be allowed to live freely among Israelites within the land.

The origins of foreign nations
------------------------------

**[GEN 9:19](https://ref.ly/Gen9:19)** *See also* [GEN 11:1–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:1-Gen11:9)

### The foreign nations of Ammon and Moab

[GEN 19:30–19:38](https://ref.ly/Gen19:30-Gen19:38)

### The relation of Israel and Edom

[GEN 25:21–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21-Gen25:34)

Israel's relationship with foreign nations
------------------------------------------

### Israel's captivity in Egypt

**[EXO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6)** *See also* [AMO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3), [AMO 1:6](https://ref.ly/Amos1:6), [AMO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Amos1:9), [AMO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Amos1:11), [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13); [ISA 10:24–10:25](https://ref.ly/Isa10:24-Isa10:25) *God's judgment on Assyria*; [ISA 47:6](https://ref.ly/Isa47:6) *the fall of Babylon prophesied*; [EZK 25:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:3), [EZK 25:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:8), [EZK 25:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:12), [EZK 25:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:15), [EZK 26:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:2); [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14) *Nineveh's destruction foretold*

### Foreign nations as a threat to Israel

**[DEU 7:1–7:4](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:4)** *See also* [GEN 9:25](https://ref.ly/Gen9:25), [GEN 15:16](https://ref.ly/Gen15:16) *“Amorites” and “Canaanites” are both terms used to describe generically the races expelled by Israel from the land of promise, a fate brought on themselves by flagrant sin*; [LEV 18:25](https://ref.ly/Lev18:25), [LEV 20:23](https://ref.ly/Lev20:23); [DEU 18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:12)

### The need for Israel to keep separate from foreign nations

**[LEV 20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:26)** *See also* [NUM 23:9](https://ref.ly/Num23:9); [DEU 7:4](https://ref.ly/Deut7:4); [JOS 23:7](https://ref.ly/Josh23:7); [JDG 2:2](https://ref.ly/Judg2:2); [1KI 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:2), [1KI 16:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:31); [EZR 4:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:3), [EZR 6:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:21), [EZR 9:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:12), [EZR 10:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:11); [NEH 10:30](https://ref.ly/Neh10:30), [NEH 13:3](https://ref.ly/Neh13:3) *Foreign religious beliefs and practices were seen as a constant threat to faith in the LORD. The command that Israel keep apart from foreign nations partly reflects a concern that Israel might adopt religious beliefs which compromise her commitment to the LORD.*

### Foreign nations are described as uncircumcised

**[ISA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Isa52:1)** *Circumcision was a sign of being within God's covenant. But some foreigners were also circumcised ([Jer 9:25–26](https://ref.ly/Jer9:25-Jer9:26)).* *See also* [GEN 34:14](https://ref.ly/Gen34:14); [EXO 12:48](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48); [JDG 14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:3), [JDG 15:18](https://ref.ly/Judg15:18); [1SA 14:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:6), [1SA 17:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:26); [2SA 1:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:20); [EZK 28:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:10), [EZK 32:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:26)

Israel's relation with foreigners within her boundaries
-------------------------------------------------------

### Foreigners are to be treated graciously

**[LEV 19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:34)** *See also* [LEV 19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:10), [LEV 23:22](https://ref.ly/Lev23:22); [DEU 10:19](https://ref.ly/Deut10:19), [DEU 14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:29), [DEU 24:19–24:21](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19-Deut24:21), [DEU 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12-Deut26:13)

### Foreigners must not be oppressed

**[LEV 19:33](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21), [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9); [DEU 24:14](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14), [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17), [DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19); [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); [JER 7:6](https://ref.ly/Jer7:6), [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)

### Foreigners have a positive role to play in Israel

**[ISA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Isa14:1)**

The rights of foreigners within Israel
--------------------------------------

### Foreigners are to share in Sabbath\-rest

*See also* [EXO 20:10](https://ref.ly/Exod20:10) parallel [DEU 5:14](https://ref.ly/Deut5:14); [EXO 23:12](https://ref.ly/Exod23:12)

### Foreigners are to receive a fair trial

[DEU 1:16](https://ref.ly/Deut1:16)

### Foreigners are to participate in festivals and ceremonies

[DEU 16:11](https://ref.ly/Deut16:11), [DEU 16:14](https://ref.ly/Deut16:14), [DEU 26:11](https://ref.ly/Deut26:11), [DEU 29:10–29:11](https://ref.ly/Deut29:10-Deut29:11), [DEU 31:12](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12); ; [2CH 30:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:25)

### Ultimately inheritance rights were envisaged for foreigners

[EZK 47:22–47:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:22-Ezek47:23)

### The obligations of foreigners are similar to those of native Israelites

**[NUM 9:14](https://ref.ly/Num9:14)** *See also* [EXO 12:19](https://ref.ly/Exod12:19), [EXO 12:48–12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48-Exod12:49); [LEV 16:29](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29), [LEV 17:8](https://ref.ly/Lev17:8), [LEV 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Lev17:12-Lev17:13), [LEV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15), [LEV 18:26](https://ref.ly/Lev18:26), [LEV 20:2](https://ref.ly/Lev20:2), [LEV 22:18](https://ref.ly/Lev22:18), [LEV 24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:16) *The penalties for blasphemy apply to Israelite and foreigner alike*; [LEV 24:22](https://ref.ly/Lev24:22); [NUM 15:14–15:16](https://ref.ly/Num15:14-Num15:16) *The rules on the presentation of offerings are to be the same for both the Israelites and the foreigner*; [NUM 19:10](https://ref.ly/Num19:10), [NUM 35:15](https://ref.ly/Num35:15); [EZK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:7)

### Occasional distinctions made for foreigners

[LEV 25:47–25:48](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47-Lev25:48); ; [DEU 14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:21), [DEU 28:43](https://ref.ly/Deut28:43)

## 7535: Greeks (id: 7535)

The inhabitants of Greece and her empire. The term is also used to describe non\-Jews, so that “Greek”, in the NT, is virtually synonymous with “Gentile”. While distinctions between Greek and Jew on the one hand, and Greek and barbarian on the other, were important in the world at large, such divisions are to have no place within the church.

The cultural distinctiveness of Greeks in NT times
--------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 7:35](https://ref.ly/John7:35)** *See also* [MRK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Mark7:26); [ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1), [ACT 9:29](https://ref.ly/Acts9:29); [ROM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Rom1:14)

\`\`Greeks'' used to describe non\-Jews
---------------------------------------

**[ACT 21:27–21:28](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:28)** *See also* [ACT 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Acts16:1-Acts16:3), [ACT 20:21](https://ref.ly/Acts20:21); [GAL 2:3](https://ref.ly/Gal2:3)

\`\`Greeks'' used to refer to God\-fearers
------------------------------------------

**[JHN 12:20](https://ref.ly/John12:20)** *See also* [ACT 17:4](https://ref.ly/Acts17:4), [ACT 17:12](https://ref.ly/Acts17:12), [ACT 17:17](https://ref.ly/Acts17:17), [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4)

Greeks may reject the gospel as foolishness
-------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22-1Cor1:23)** *The high value placed by the Greeks upon human wisdom could hinder their response to the gospel.* *See also* [ACT 17:18–17:34](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18-Acts17:34)

In Christ there is no distinction between Greeks and Jews
---------------------------------------------------------

### Greeks and Jews share the same salvation

**[ROM 10:11–10:13](https://ref.ly/Rom10:11-Rom10:13)** *See also* [ROM 3:28–3:30](https://ref.ly/Rom3:28-Rom3:30); [1CO 1:22–1:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22-1Cor1:24)

### Greeks and Jews are united in Christ

**[COL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Col3:11)** *See also* [1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13); [GAL 3:26–3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26-Gal3:29)

* **Associated Passages:** John 7:35; Mark 7:26; Acts 6:1; Acts 9:29; Romans 1:14; Acts 21:27-28; Acts 16:1-3; Acts 20:21; Galatians 2:3; John 12:20; Acts 17:4; Acts 17:12; Acts 17:17; Acts 18:4; 1 Corinthians 1:22-23; Acts 17:18-34; Romans 10:11-13; Romans 3:28-30; 1 Corinthians 1:22-24; Colossians 3:11; 1 Corinthians 12:13; Galatians 3:26-29
* **Associated Articles:** 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5374: languages (ID: `5374@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5441: philosophy (ID: `5441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6209: pagans (ID: `6209@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7025: church, unity and fellowship of (ID: `7025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7032: unity, of God's people (ID: `7032@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7105: believers (ID: `7105@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7336: circumcision, spiritual (ID: `7336@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7505: Jews, the (ID: `7505@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7512: Gentiles, in NT (ID: `7512@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7545: outsiders (ID: `7545@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7540: Judaism (id: 7540)

Derived from “Judah” (the southern kingdom of the divided monarchy) the term refers to the religion and culture of the Jews from the time of the exile (586 B.C.) onwards.

The exile and the beginnings of Judaism
---------------------------------------

### The people of Judah become known as Jews

[EZR 4:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:12); ; [NEH 4:2](https://ref.ly/Neh4:2); ; [EST 3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:6); ; [JER 32:12](https://ref.ly/Jer32:12); ; [MAT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:2); ; [JHN 2:6](https://ref.ly/John2:6)

### Jewish communities were formed in many places

[JER 44:1](https://ref.ly/Jer44:1); ; [EZR 2:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:1) *many stayed in Babylon*; ; [JHN 7:35](https://ref.ly/John7:35); ; [ACT 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:8-Acts2:11)

### Far from the Jerusalem temple, the exiles had difficulty in knowing how to worship

**[PSA 137:4](https://ref.ly/Ps137:4)** *See also* [HOS 3:4](https://ref.ly/Hos3:4) *This prophecy to Israel also expresses the dilemma of the Jerusalem exiles.*

New forms of religion after the exile, probably beginning in Babylon
--------------------------------------------------------------------

### Emphasis on the Scriptures

[NEH 7:73–8:3](https://ref.ly/Neh7:73-Neh8:3)

### Synagogue worship

[LUK 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16-Luke4:17) *Unable to sacrifice in the temple, the exiles would have focused their worship on the Scriptures, a practice which developed into synagogue worship.*

### Carefully observing the law

Ezra went from Babylon to Jerusalem with the express intention of seeing that God's law was observed: [EZR 7:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:11), [EZR 7:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:14), [EZR 7:25](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:25)

The temple and sacrifice
------------------------

### The returning exiles recommence the sacrifices

[EZR 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:1-Ezra3:6)

### The temple rebuilt

[EZR 3:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8), [EZR 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:14-Ezra6:15)

### The temple as a focus for pilgrims

[JHN 12:20](https://ref.ly/John12:20); ; [ACT 2:5](https://ref.ly/Acts2:5) *They had probably come for Passover and were staying until Pentecost*; ; [ACT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Acts8:27)

### The temple regarded with great reverence

[MAT 26:59–26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:61) parallel [MRK 14:57–14:58](https://ref.ly/Mark14:57-Mark14:58); ; [ACT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Acts6:13), [ACT 21:27–21:29](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:29)

Studying and obeying the law
----------------------------

### Studying Scripture

[JHN 5:39](https://ref.ly/John5:39)

### Total obedience essential

[MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23); ; [JAS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Jas2:10)

### Human rules added to God's law

[MAT 23:16–23:18](https://ref.ly/Matt23:16-Matt23:18); ; [MRK 7:1–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:13) parallel [MAT 15:1–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:9)

### High regard for the Sabbath and traditional rules associated with it

[MAT 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:2) parallels [MRK 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark2:24), [LUK 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:2)

Groups within NT Judaism
------------------------

### Pharisees

**[MRK 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:3-Mark7:4)** *The Pharisees stressed the fulfilment of the law by individuals.* *See also* [LUK 11:38](https://ref.ly/Luke11:38), [LUK 18:10–18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:10-Luke18:12); [ACT 26:5](https://ref.ly/Acts26:5)

### Sadducees

**[ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17)** *A powerful minority group, politically astute whilst theologically conservative, they saw temple worship as the chief purpose of the law.* *See also* [MAT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7); [MRK 12:18](https://ref.ly/Mark12:18) parallels [MAT 22:23](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23), [LUK 20:27](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27)

### Teachers of the law (scribes)

They preserved, copied, taught and administered the law: [MAT 17:10](https://ref.ly/Matt17:10); ; [MRK 9:14](https://ref.ly/Mark9:14); ; [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Luke2:46); ; [ACT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Acts4:5)

### Zealots

[LUK 6:15](https://ref.ly/Luke6:15) *They favoured a “holy war” to drive out the Romans.*

### Non\-Jewish converts

[MAT 23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:15); ; [ACT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:11), [ACT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Acts6:5), [ACT 13:43](https://ref.ly/Acts13:43)

Judaism and Christianity
------------------------

### Conflict between Jesus Christ and the Jewish authorities

**[MRK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1)** *See also* [JHN 11:45–11:50](https://ref.ly/John11:45-John11:50)

### Conflict between the first Christians and Judaism

[JHN 16:2](https://ref.ly/John16:2); ; [ACT 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Acts4:1-Acts4:3), [ACT 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17-Acts5:18); ; [GAL 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Gal1:13-Gal1:14)

### Conflict produced by Jewish influence among the first Christians

**[ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5)** *See also* [ACT 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1-Acts15:2); [GAL 2:11–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:16) *Judaism gave birth to Christianity. After an initial uneasy co\-existence a total break finally ensued.*

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 4:12; Nehemiah 4:2; Esther 3:6; Jeremiah 32:12; Matthew 2:2; John 2:6; Jeremiah 44:1; Ezra 2:1; John 7:35; Acts 2:8-11; Psalms 137:4; Hosea 3:4; Nehemiah 7:73-8:3; Luke 4:16-17; Ezra 7:11; Ezra 7:14; Ezra 7:25; Ezra 3:1-6; Ezra 3:8; Ezra 6:14-15; John 12:20; Acts 2:5; Acts 8:27; Matthew 26:59-61; Mark 14:57-58; Acts 6:13; Acts 21:27-29; John 5:39; Matthew 23:23; James 2:10; Matthew 23:16-18; Mark 7:1-13; Matthew 15:1-9; Matthew 12:1-2; Mark 2:23-24; Luke 6:1-2; Mark 7:3-4; Luke 11:38; Luke 18:10-12; Acts 26:5; Acts 5:17; Matthew 3:7; Mark 12:18; Matthew 22:23; Luke 20:27; Matthew 17:10; Mark 9:14; Luke 2:46; Acts 4:5; Luke 6:15; Matthew 23:15; Acts 2:11; Acts 6:5; Acts 13:43; Mark 14:1; John 11:45-50; John 16:2; Acts 4:1-3; Acts 5:17-18; Galatians 1:13-14; Acts 15:5; Acts 15:1-2; Galatians 2:11-16

## 7545: outsiders (id: 7545)

Israel recognised two general categories of outsiders: those who lived outside Israel, usually referred to as “the nations” or “the Gentiles”, and foreigners resident within Israel's boundaries. The latter, usually referred to as “aliens”, were given considerable privileges under the covenant between God and Israel.

Israel's attitude to the Gentiles as outsiders
----------------------------------------------

### The nations as outsiders

[LEV 20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:26); ; [NEH 1:8](https://ref.ly/Neh1:8); ; [PSA 2:1](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1), [PSA 46:10](https://ref.ly/Ps46:10), [PSA 106:35](https://ref.ly/Ps106:35); ; [HOS 7:8](https://ref.ly/Hos7:8); ; [JOL 2:17](https://ref.ly/Joel2:17)

### The conversion of the Gentiles foretold

**[ISA 60:3](https://ref.ly/Isa60:3)** *See also* [GEN 22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:18); [PSA 22:27](https://ref.ly/Ps22:27); [ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2); [HOS 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hos2:23)

### Israel's mission to the Gentiles was fulfilled in Jesus Christ

**[ACT 13:47](https://ref.ly/Acts13:47)** *See also* [ISA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:6); [LUK 2:32](https://ref.ly/Luke2:32); [ACT 28:28](https://ref.ly/Acts28:28); [ROM 11:11](https://ref.ly/Rom11:11); [EPH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:6)

### Jesus Christ calls both Jew and Gentile

**[JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:16)** *See also* [ACT 13:47–13:48](https://ref.ly/Acts13:47-Acts13:48); [ROM 9:24–9:26](https://ref.ly/Rom9:24-Rom9:26); [1CO 1:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:24); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

Israel's attitude to aliens as outsiders
----------------------------------------

### Aliens are to be treated as members of the people of God

**[EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9)** *See also* [DEU 29:9–29:15](https://ref.ly/Deut29:9-Deut29:15), [DEU 31:12](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12); [JOS 8:33–8:35](https://ref.ly/Josh8:33-Josh8:35)

### Aliens are protected by God

**[PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9)**

### Aliens must not be oppressed

**[JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3)** *See also* [JER 7:2–7:7](https://ref.ly/Jer7:2-Jer7:7); [EZK 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29); [ZEC 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:9-Zech7:10)

### Aliens may share in Israel's worship

[EXO 12:17–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17-Exod12:20), [EXO 12:43–12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:43-Exod12:49); ; [LEV 22:17–22:19](https://ref.ly/Lev22:17-Lev22:19); ; [DEU 16:13–16:15](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13-Deut16:15)

The Samaritans as outsiders in Israel in NT times
-------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9)** *By including Samaritans in his ministry, Jesus Christ demonstrated his concern for those who were treated as outsiders by society.* *See also* [LUK 10:33](https://ref.ly/Luke10:33), [LUK 17:16](https://ref.ly/Luke17:16); [JHN 4:7](https://ref.ly/John4:7)

Christians are compared to aliens in the world
----------------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11)**

The witness of believers to outsiders
-------------------------------------

**[COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5)** *See also* [MRK 4:11](https://ref.ly/Mark4:11); [1CO 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:12-1Cor5:13); [1TH 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11-1Thess4:12); [1TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 20:26; Nehemiah 1:8; Psalms 2:1; Psalms 46:10; Psalms 106:35; Hosea 7:8; Joel 2:17; Isaiah 60:3; Genesis 22:18; Psalms 22:27; Isaiah 9:2; Hosea 2:23; Acts 13:47; Isaiah 49:6; Luke 2:32; Acts 28:28; Romans 11:11; Ephesians 3:6; John 10:16; Acts 13:47-48; Romans 9:24-26; 1 Corinthians 1:24; 2 Timothy 4:17; Exodus 23:9; Deuteronomy 29:9-15; Deuteronomy 31:12; Joshua 8:33-35; Psalms 146:9; Jeremiah 22:3; Jeremiah 7:2-7; Ezekiel 22:29; Zechariah 7:9-10; Exodus 12:17-20; Exodus 12:43-49; Leviticus 22:17-19; Deuteronomy 16:13-15; John 4:9; Luke 10:33; Luke 17:16; John 4:7; 1 Peter 2:11; Colossians 4:5; Mark 4:11; 1 Corinthians 5:12-13; 1 Thessalonians 4:11-12; 1 Timothy 3:7

## 7550: Pharisees (id: 7550)

A Jewish religious party whose members required a very strict adherence to the ritual law and to the traditions of their predecessors. They were hostile to the teaching of Jesus Christ, which they regarded as compromising their interpretation of the law.

* **Associated Articles:** 7551: Pharisees, beliefs of (ID: `7551@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7552: Pharisees, attitudes to Jesus Christ (ID: `7552@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7551: Pharisees, beliefs of (id: 7551)

The Pharisees placed considerable emphasis upon the rigorous upholding of the law, particularly the traditional forms in which the law had been established by their predecessors.

Pharisaic interpretation of the law
-----------------------------------

### Pharisees were the recognised custodians of the law

**[MAT 23:1–23:3](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:3)**

### Pharisees emphasised the traditions of the elders

**[MRK 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:3-Mark7:4)** *For the Pharisees the oral law, the traditions of their predecessors, had an authority equal to that of the written law.* *See also* [MAT 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:2) parallel [MRK 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mark7:5); [GAL 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gal1:14)

Pharisaic observance of the law
-------------------------------

### The Sabbath

**[MAT 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:2)** parallels [MRK 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark2:24), [LUK 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:2) *See also* [LUK 13:14](https://ref.ly/Luke13:14)

### Fasting

**[MAT 9:14](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14)** parallels [MRK 2:18](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18), [LUK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33) *See also* [LUK 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:12)

### Tithing

**[MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23)** parallel [LUK 11:42](https://ref.ly/Luke11:42)

Pharisaic doctrine
------------------

**[ACT 23:6–23:9](https://ref.ly/Acts23:6-Acts23:9)**

Pharisaic proselytism
---------------------

**[MAT 23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:15)**

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 23:1-3; Mark 7:3-4; Matthew 15:1-2; Mark 7:5; Galatians 1:14; Matthew 12:1-2; Mark 2:23-24; Luke 6:1-2; Luke 13:14; Matthew 9:14; Mark 2:18; Luke 5:33; Luke 18:9-12; Matthew 23:23; Luke 11:42; Acts 23:6-9; Matthew 23:15
* **Associated Articles:** 2333: Jesus Christ, attitude to OT (ID: `2333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5381: law, letter and spirit of (ID: `5381@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7430: Sabbath, in NT (ID: `7430@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7464: teachers of the law (ID: `7464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7555: Sadducees (ID: `7555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7570: sects (ID: `7570@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8432: fasting, practice of (ID: `8432@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8488: tithing (ID: `8488@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8824: self-righteousness, nature of (ID: `8824@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9314: resurrection, of the dead (ID: `9314@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7552: Pharisees, attitudes to Jesus Christ (id: 7552)

Pharisees opposed Jesus Christ during his ministry and were among those who brought about his crucifixion. Some Pharisees, however, accepted Christ's teaching and believed in him.

Dialogue between Jesus Christ and Pharisees
-------------------------------------------

### They demanded a sign to prove his messiahship

**[MAT 12:38–12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:38-Matt12:42)** parallel [LUK 11:29–11:32](https://ref.ly/Luke11:29-Luke11:32) *See also* [MAT 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Matt16:1-Matt16:4) parallel [MRK 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Mark8:11-Mark8:12)

### They questioned him on matters of the law

**[MAT 19:3–19:9](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3-Matt19:9)** parallel [MRK 10:2–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:2-Mark10:9) *See also* [MAT 22:15–22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:22) parallels [MRK 12:13–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:20–20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:26); [MAT 22:34–22:40](https://ref.ly/Matt22:34-Matt22:40) parallel [MRK 12:28–12:34](https://ref.ly/Mark12:28-Mark12:34); [JHN 8:3–8:11](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:11)

### They called him Teacher

**[MRK 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:14)** parallel [MAT 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:16) *See also* [MAT 12:38](https://ref.ly/Matt12:38), [MAT 22:34–22:36](https://ref.ly/Matt22:34-Matt22:36); [LUK 7:39–7:40](https://ref.ly/Luke7:39-Luke7:40), [LUK 19:39](https://ref.ly/Luke19:39), [LUK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Luke20:21), [LUK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Luke20:28); [JHN 3:2](https://ref.ly/John3:2), [JHN 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:4)

### Jesus Christ questioned them

**[MAT 22:41–22:46](https://ref.ly/Matt22:41-Matt22:46)** parallels [MRK 12:35–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:35-Mark12:40), [LUK 20:41–20:44](https://ref.ly/Luke20:41-Luke20:44) *See also* [MAT 12:9–12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:9-Matt12:14) parallels [MRK 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:1-Mark3:6), [LUK 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:6-Luke6:11); [MAT 21:24–21:27](https://ref.ly/Matt21:24-Matt21:27) parallels [MRK 11:29–11:33](https://ref.ly/Mark11:29-Mark11:33), [LUK 20:3–20:8](https://ref.ly/Luke20:3-Luke20:8); [LUK 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1-Luke14:6)

Social contact between Jesus Christ and Pharisees
-------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 7:36](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36)** *See also* [MRK 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:2); [LUK 11:37](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37), [LUK 13:31](https://ref.ly/Luke13:31), [LUK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1)

Jesus Christ criticised the Pharisees
-------------------------------------

**[LUK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Luke12:1)** *See also* [MAT 5:20](https://ref.ly/Matt5:20), [MAT 16:5–16:12](https://ref.ly/Matt16:5-Matt16:12) parallel [MRK 8:14–8:21](https://ref.ly/Mark8:14-Mark8:21); [MAT 21:33–21:45](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:45) parallels [MRK 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:11), [LUK 20:9–20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:19); [MAT 23:1–23:36](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:36) parallels [MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40), [LUK 20:45–20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:47); [LUK 11:37–11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37-Luke11:52), [LUK 16:1–16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1-Luke16:15), [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)

The Pharisees opposed Jesus Christ
----------------------------------

**[LUK 11:53–11:54](https://ref.ly/Luke11:53-Luke11:54)** [MAT 9:2–9:7](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2-Matt9:7) parallels [MRK 2:3–2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:3-Mark2:12), [LUK 5:17–5:26](https://ref.ly/Luke5:17-Luke5:26); [MAT 9:10–9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10-Matt9:13) parallels [MRK 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15-Mark2:17), [LUK 5:29–5:32](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29-Luke5:32); [MAT 9:32–9:34](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32-Matt9:34), [MAT 12:22–12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22-Matt12:24); ; [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22); [MAT 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:2) parallels [MRK 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark2:24), [LUK 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:2); [MAT 12:9–12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:9-Matt12:14); ; [MRK 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:1-Mark3:6); ; [LUK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Luke6:7), [LUK 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1-Luke14:6); ; [JHN 9:13–9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:13-John9:16); [LUK 7:36–7:39](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36-Luke7:39), [LUK 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:2), [LUK 19:37–19:40](https://ref.ly/Luke19:37-Luke19:40); ; [JHN 8:13](https://ref.ly/John8:13), [JHN 9:39–9:41](https://ref.ly/John9:39-John9:41)

The Pharisees were among those who engineered Jesus Christ's death
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:6)** parallels [MAT 12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14), [LUK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:11) *See also* [MAT 21:45–21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:45-Matt21:46) parallels [MRK 12:12](https://ref.ly/Mark12:12), [LUK 20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:19); [MRK 12:13](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13) parallel [LUK 20:20](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20); [MAT 27:62–27:64](https://ref.ly/Matt27:62-Matt27:64); [JHN 7:32–7:49](https://ref.ly/John7:32-John7:49)

Some Pharisees believed in Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------

**[PHP 3:4–3:11](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:11)** *See also* [JHN 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/John3:1-John3:2), [JHN 12:42](https://ref.ly/John12:42), [JHN 19:38–19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:38-John19:40); [ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5), [ACT 23:6–23:9](https://ref.ly/Acts23:6-Acts23:9), [ACT 26:5](https://ref.ly/Acts26:5)

## 7555: Sadducees (id: 7555)

An influential Jewish party that denied the existence of the spirit world and the possibility of resurrection. Its members are portrayed as opposing Jesus Christ and the first Christians.

Many priests were Sadducees
---------------------------

**[ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17)**

Sadducees rejected the concepts of resurrection and spirit world
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 23:8](https://ref.ly/Acts23:8)**

Sadducees questioned Jesus Christ about the resurrection
--------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 22:23–22:32](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23-Matt22:32)** parallels [MRK 12:18–12:27](https://ref.ly/Mark12:18-Mark12:27), [LUK 20:27–20:38](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27-Luke20:38)

Sadducees denounced by John the Baptist
---------------------------------------

**[MAT 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7-Matt3:10)**

Sadducees denounced by Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 16:6–16:12](https://ref.ly/Matt16:6-Matt16:12)** *See also* [MAT 21:28–21:45](https://ref.ly/Matt21:28-Matt21:45) *The chief priests were probably Sadducees (see [Ac 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17)).*

The Sadducees oppose the first Christians
-----------------------------------------

**[ACT 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Acts4:1-Acts4:3)** *See also* [ACT 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17-Acts5:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 5:17; Acts 23:8; Matthew 22:23-32; Mark 12:18-27; Luke 20:27-38; Matthew 3:7-10; Matthew 16:6-12; Matthew 21:28-45; Acts 4:1-3; Acts 5:17-18
* **Associated Articles:** 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of (ID: `2363@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4110: angels (ID: `4110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4554: yeast (ID: `4554@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5098: John the Baptist (ID: `5098@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7330: chief priests (ID: `7330@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7570: sects (ID: `7570@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8787: opposition, to God's person and work (ID: `8787@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9314: resurrection, of the dead (ID: `9314@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7560: Samaritans, the (id: 7560)

The people of the northern kingdom of Israel, named after its capital city established by Omri. At the time of the NT, Samaritans were despised by Jews, on account of their intermarriage with Gentiles after the fall of the northern kingdom in 721 B.C. However, the NT presents them as generally responding favourably to the gospel.

Samaria as the name of the northern kingdom
-------------------------------------------

**[1KI 21:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1)** *The entire northern kingdom is represented by the name of its royal capital, just as Jerusalem often represents Judah.* *See also* [1KI 18:1–18:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:1-1Kgs18:6); [2KI 17:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:24), [2KI 23:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:19); [JER 31:5](https://ref.ly/Jer31:5)

The fall of Samaria
-------------------

**[2KI 17:3–17:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:3-2Kgs17:5)**

### Samaria's population deported

**[2KI 17:6–17:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:6-2Kgs17:18)** *Sargon II of Assyria completed the siege begun by Shalmaneser V, deporting (according to his own annals) over 27,000 of Samaria's inhabitants.* *See also* [2KI 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:11-2Kgs18:12)

### Samaria resettled by other peoples

**[2KI 17:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:24)** *This resettlement, the first of several, led to intermarriage, and is seen by many as the origin of the Samaritans of NT times.* *See also* [EZR 4:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:2), [EZR 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:9-Ezra4:10)

Religion in Samaria after its fall
----------------------------------

### Syncretism arises in Samaria

**[2KI 17:25–17:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:25-2Kgs17:41)** *Those resettled brought their own gods with them, but also worshipped the LORD as the god of the land. Ultimately these inter\-mixed peoples would abandon their polytheism and would accept the law of Moses.*

### Some in Samaria remain faithful and still make pilgrimage to Jerusalem

**[2CH 30:10–30:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:10-2Chr30:11)** *See also* [2CH 30:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:1), [2CH 34:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:9); [JER 41:4–41:5](https://ref.ly/Jer41:4-Jer41:5)

Samaritans and the restoration of Jerusalem
-------------------------------------------

### Samaritans offer to rebuild the temple with the returning exiles but are rejected

**[EZR 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:1-Ezra4:5)** *The response of the Samaritans to this rejection, based on the Jews' desire to keep the faith pure, reflects the double\-mindedness of their offer.*

### Ongoing opposition from the Samaritans to the work of the returned exiles

*See also* [EZR 4:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:6), [EZR 4:7–4:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:7-Ezra4:23)

Samaria's religious background by the time of the NT
----------------------------------------------------

### Samaria had established its own temple

**[JHN 4:20](https://ref.ly/John4:20)** *The Samaritans built their own temple on Mount Gerizim. It was later destroyed by the Jews, which led to a hardening of attitudes between the two groups.*

### Samaritan Scriptures contained only the Pentateuch

**[JHN 4:22](https://ref.ly/John4:22)** *Jesus Christ highlights the limited revelation of the Samaritans. Since their Scriptures contained only the Pentateuch, they were expecting a Messiah that they could know little about.*

### Samaritan and Jewish attitudes to one another

**[JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9)** *Samaritans were seen as being very lax in their religious observance, and Jews would not therefore share drinking vessels with them. By NT times the gulf between Jew and Samaritan was quite wide and bitter.* *See also* [LUK 9:51–9:56](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51-Luke9:56) *The three\-day journey from Galilee to Jerusalem involved overnight accommodation, which the Samaritans generally refused, causing many Jews to travel on the eastern side of the Jordan.*

Samaritans and the ministry of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------

### Initially Jesus Christ instructs his disciples not to go to the Samaritans

**[MAT 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:6)**

### Jesus Christ is opposed by some Samaritans

*See also* [LUK 9:51–9:56](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51-Luke9:56)

### Jesus Christ ministers to a Samaritan woman and many Samaritans believe in him

[JHN 4:4–4:30](https://ref.ly/John4:4-John4:30), [JHN 4:39–4:42](https://ref.ly/John4:39-John4:42)

### Jesus Christ heals a Samaritan leper

[LUK 17:11–17:19](https://ref.ly/Luke17:11-Luke17:19)

### Jesus Christ tells a parable about a good Samaritan

[LUK 10:30–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30-Luke10:37) *For the Jews, the concept of a “good” Samaritan would have seemed very strange; but Jesus Christ shows that love and faith is not restricted to Israel's boundaries.*

Samaria and the ministry of the early first Christians
------------------------------------------------------

### The risen Christ includes Samaria in the church's mission

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)**

### The church takes the gospel to Samaria

**[ACT 8:1](https://ref.ly/Acts8:1)** *See also* [ACT 8:4–8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:4-Acts8:13), [ACT 8:25](https://ref.ly/Acts8:25)

### The apostles pray for the gift of the Spirit for the Samaritan converts

**[ACT 8:14–8:17](https://ref.ly/Acts8:14-Acts8:17)** *The delay in their reception of the Spirit until the apostles came from Jerusalem may have been God's way of removing the old Jewish\-Samaritan divide in the infant church.*

### The Samaritan church grows

**[ACT 9:31](https://ref.ly/Acts9:31)**

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 21:1; 1 Kings 18:1-6; 2 Kings 17:24; 2 Kings 23:19; Jeremiah 31:5; 2 Kings 17:3-5; 2 Kings 17:6-18; 2 Kings 18:11-12; Ezra 4:2; Ezra 4:9-10; 2 Kings 17:25-41; 2 Chronicles 30:10-11; 2 Chronicles 30:1; 2 Chronicles 34:9; Jeremiah 41:4-5; Ezra 4:1-5; Ezra 4:6; Ezra 4:7-23; John 4:20; John 4:22; John 4:9; Luke 9:51-56; Matthew 10:5-6; John 4:4-30; John 4:39-42; Luke 17:11-19; Luke 10:30-37; Acts 1:8; Acts 8:1; Acts 8:4-13; Acts 8:25; Acts 8:14-17; Acts 9:31

## 7565: Sanhedrin (id: 7565)

The Hebrew form of the Greek word “synedrion”, a council. The high court of the Jews, presided over by the high priest. Its 71 members included chief priests, elders and teachers of the law.

Jesus Christ and the Sanhedrin
------------------------------

### Herod called together the Sanhedrin on hearing of the birth of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Matt2:4)** *This is probably a reference to the entire Sanhedrin.*

### Jesus Christ acknowledged the authority of the Sanhedrin

**[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)**

### Jesus Christ foretold that he would suffer at the hands of the Sanhedrin

**[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)** parallels [MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31), [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22) *See also* [MAT 20:18](https://ref.ly/Matt20:18) parallel [MRK 10:33](https://ref.ly/Mark10:33)

### These prophecies were fulfilled

**[MAT 26:57–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:57-Matt26:68)** parallel [MRK 14:58–14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:58-Mark14:65)

### The Sanhedrin plotted to kill Jesus Christ after he had raised Lazarus

**[JHN 11:47–11:53](https://ref.ly/John11:47-John11:53)**

### The Sanhedrin, powerless to execute anyone, brought Jesus Christ to Pilate

**[JHN 18:28–18:31](https://ref.ly/John18:28-John18:31)**

Nicodemus and Joseph of Arimathea were members of the Sanhedrin
---------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [JHN 3:1](https://ref.ly/John3:1); [MRK 15:43](https://ref.ly/Mark15:43) parallel [LUK 23:50–23:52](https://ref.ly/Luke23:50-Luke23:52)

The term Sanhedrin could also apply to local councils
-----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17)** parallel [MRK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9)

Peter and John were brought before the Sanhedrin
------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 4:5–4:21](https://ref.ly/Acts4:5-Acts4:21)** *See also* [ACT 5:21](https://ref.ly/Acts5:21), [ACT 5:27](https://ref.ly/Acts5:27), [ACT 5:34](https://ref.ly/Acts5:34), [ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:41)

Stephen appeared before the Sanhedrin
-------------------------------------

[ACT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Acts6:12), [ACT 6:15](https://ref.ly/Acts6:15)

### Stephen angered the Sanhedrin by his defence

[ACT 7:54](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54), [ACT 7:57–7:58](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57-Acts7:58)

Paul and the Sanhedrin
----------------------

### Paul was commissioned by the chief priests to persecute the church

**[ACT 9:14](https://ref.ly/Acts9:14)** *[Ac 22:5](https://ref.ly/Acts22:5) makes it clear that the entire Sanhedrin was involved.* *See also* [ACT 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Acts9:1-Acts9:2), [ACT 9:21](https://ref.ly/Acts9:21), [ACT 26:10–26:12](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10-Acts26:12)

### Paul was brought before the Sanhedrin

**[ACT 22:30–23:1](https://ref.ly/Acts22:30-Acts23:1)** *See also* [ACT 23:6](https://ref.ly/Acts23:6), [ACT 23:28](https://ref.ly/Acts23:28), [ACT 24:20](https://ref.ly/Acts24:20)

### Conspirators plotted to bring Paul before the Sanhedrin again, but he escaped

[ACT 23:12–23:15](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12-Acts23:15), [ACT 23:20](https://ref.ly/Acts23:20)

### The Sanhedrin brought Paul to the Roman governor, Festus

[ACT 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts25:2), [ACT 25:15](https://ref.ly/Acts25:15)

## 7570: sects (id: 7570)

The Pharisees described as a sect
---------------------------------

**[ACT 26:5](https://ref.ly/Acts26:5)** *The word “sect” in the NT does not have the same negative connotations as in modern society. The Greek word for “sect” is also translated as “party” in the NIV.* *See also* [LUK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Luke5:30); [ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5)

The Sadducees described as a sect
---------------------------------

**[ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17)**

Christians described as a sect
------------------------------

**[ACT 24:14](https://ref.ly/Acts24:14)** *See also* [ACT 24:5](https://ref.ly/Acts24:5), [ACT 28:21–28:22](https://ref.ly/Acts28:21-Acts28:22)

Sectarianism condemned
----------------------

[1CO 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3-1Cor3:4); ; [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20); ; [GAL 5:20](https://ref.ly/Gal5:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 26:5; Luke 5:30; Acts 15:5; Acts 5:17; Acts 24:14; Acts 24:5; Acts 28:21-22; 1 Corinthians 3:3-4; 2 Corinthians 12:20; Galatians 5:20
* **Associated Articles:** 7120: Christians (ID: `7120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7540: Judaism (ID: `7540@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7555: Sadducees (ID: `7555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8237: doctrine, false (ID: `8237@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8766: heresies (ID: `8766@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7600: History of God's people in NT (id: 7600)

* 7610: Council of Jerusalem
* 7620: disciples
* 7630: Twelve, the
* **Associated Articles:** 7610: Council of Jerusalem (ID: `7610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7620: disciples (ID: `7620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7630: Twelve, the (ID: `7630@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7610: Council of Jerusalem (id: 7610)

The Council of Jerusalem was possibly the first \`\`assembly'' of the Christian church, held c. A.D. 49
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The Council met to consider whether circumcision was necessary for salvation

**[ACT 15:1](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1)** *See also* [ROM 4:9](https://ref.ly/Rom4:9); [GAL 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Gal5:2-Gal5:3)

### Paul and Barnabas came from Antioch to Jerusalem to meet with the apostles and elders

[ACT 15:2–15:12](https://ref.ly/Acts15:2-Acts15:12)

### The Council agreed not to make it difficult for Gentiles who turned to God

**[ACT 15:19–15:21](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:21)** *The Council was concerned to avoid forcing Gentiles to adopt Jewish customs (such as circumcision), and at the same time to require Gentiles to behave considerately towards their “weaker brothers” of Jewish birth, not all of whom could be expected to acquire immediately the emancipated outlook on food\-laws and the like that Peter and Paul had.*

### James showed how the decision had confirmed an earlier prophecy

*See also* [ACT 15:15–15:18](https://ref.ly/Acts15:15-Acts15:18); [AMO 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11-Amos9:12)

### The written judgment was taken to the Gentile believers by Paul, Barnabas, Silas and Judas

[ACT 15:23–15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:23-Acts15:29), [ACT 16:4](https://ref.ly/Acts16:4)

### The Council's judgment proved to be a great encouragement to the Gentile believers

[ACT 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Acts15:30-Acts15:31), [ACT 16:5](https://ref.ly/Acts16:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 15:1; Romans 4:9; Galatians 5:2-3; Acts 15:2-12; Acts 15:19-21; Acts 15:15-18; Amos 9:11-12; Acts 15:23-29; Acts 16:4; Acts 15:30-31; Acts 16:5
* **Associated Articles:** 5108: Paul, life of (ID: `5108@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7510: Gentiles (ID: `7510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7540: Judaism (ID: `7540@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7706: apostles (ID: `7706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7720: elders, in the church (ID: `7720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8414: encouragement (ID: `8414@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7620: disciples (id: 7620)

The group of people called to follow and serve Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 7621: disciples, calling of (ID: `7621@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7622: disciples, characteristics of (ID: `7622@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7621: disciples, calling of (id: 7621)

The term is applied to Christians in general, not to the Twelve only. It emphasises the importance of Jesus Christ's followers learning from Christ as their teacher.

Disciples learning from their master
------------------------------------

**[ISA 8:16](https://ref.ly/Isa8:16)**; **[AMO 7:14](https://ref.ly/Amos7:14)** *See also* [1KI 20:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:35); [NEH 3:8](https://ref.ly/Neh3:8); [ISA 19:11](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11); [MAT 10:24](https://ref.ly/Matt10:24); [MRK 2:18](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18) parallel [LUK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33); [LUK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Luke6:40); [JHN 1:35](https://ref.ly/John1:35), [JHN 9:28](https://ref.ly/John9:28)

Those referred to as disciples in the NT
----------------------------------------

### The disciples as the apostles

**[MAT 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1-Matt10:2)** *The apostles were uniquely appointed as founding pillars of the church, but in some respects they were also representative disciples.*

### The disciples as believers in general

**[MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19)**; **[ACT 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Acts19:1-Acts19:2)** *Christian believers, not fully mature.* *See also* [LUK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Luke6:13), [LUK 6:17](https://ref.ly/Luke6:17); [ACT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2), [ACT 11:26](https://ref.ly/Acts11:26)

### The disciples as an intimate circle of believers

[MAT 18:1](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1); ; [MRK 4:10](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10)

How disciples are called
------------------------

### It sometimes implies physically following Jesus Christ and leaving everything

[MAT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Matt4:19) parallel [MRK 1:16–1:20](https://ref.ly/Mark1:16-Mark1:20); ; [MAT 8:18–8:22](https://ref.ly/Matt8:18-Matt8:22) parallel [LUK 9:57–9:60](https://ref.ly/Luke9:57-Luke9:60); ; [MAT 9:9](https://ref.ly/Matt9:9) parallel [MRK 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mark2:14)

### Calling in a more general spiritual sense

**[JHN 21:19](https://ref.ly/John21:19)** *See also* [LUK 9:23](https://ref.ly/Luke9:23), [LUK 14:25–14:27](https://ref.ly/Luke14:25-Luke14:27); [JHN 8:12](https://ref.ly/John8:12), [JHN 12:26](https://ref.ly/John12:26), [JHN 21:22](https://ref.ly/John21:22)

How disciples learn from Jesus Christ the teacher
-------------------------------------------------

### By repeating the words of Jesus Christ

**[LUK 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:1-Luke11:4)** *The rhythmical form of much of Jesus Christ's teaching suggests he intended his words to be remembered verbatim.* *See also* [MAT 5:3–5:10](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3-Matt5:10)

### By signs

**[JHN 2:11](https://ref.ly/John2:11)** *See also* [MRK 5:37](https://ref.ly/Mark5:37), [MRK 5:40](https://ref.ly/Mark5:40) parallel [LUK 8:51](https://ref.ly/Luke8:51)

### By understanding the OT Scriptures

**[JHN 1:45](https://ref.ly/John1:45)** *See also* [LUK 18:31–18:33](https://ref.ly/Luke18:31-Luke18:33), [LUK 24:27](https://ref.ly/Luke24:27), [LUK 24:32](https://ref.ly/Luke24:32), [LUK 24:44–24:45](https://ref.ly/Luke24:44-Luke24:45)

### By understanding the parables

**[MRK 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:11-Mark4:12)** parallels [MAT 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:10-Matt13:11), [LUK 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:9-Luke8:10) *See also* [MRK 4:34](https://ref.ly/Mark4:34)

### By practical experience of mission

**[LUK 10:23–10:24](https://ref.ly/Luke10:23-Luke10:24)** *These words follow the sending out of the seventy\-two.* *See also* [MRK 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:6-Mark6:11); [LUK 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:2) parallel [MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1)

### In periods of being alone with Jesus Christ

**[MRK 6:31](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31)** *See also* [MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)

### In spite of their faulty understanding

**[LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25)** *See also* [MRK 9:32](https://ref.ly/Mark9:32) parallel [LUK 9:45](https://ref.ly/Luke9:45); [LUK 18:34](https://ref.ly/Luke18:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 8:16; Amos 7:14; 1 Kings 20:35; Nehemiah 3:8; Isaiah 19:11; Matthew 10:24; Mark 2:18; Luke 5:33; Luke 6:40; John 1:35; John 9:28; Matthew 10:1-2; Matthew 28:19; Acts 19:1-2; Luke 6:13; Luke 6:17; Acts 6:2; Acts 11:26; Matthew 18:1; Mark 4:10; Matthew 4:19; Mark 1:16-20; Matthew 8:18-22; Luke 9:57-60; Matthew 9:9; Mark 2:14; John 21:19; Luke 9:23; Luke 14:25-27; John 8:12; John 12:26; John 21:22; Luke 11:1-4; Matthew 5:3-10; John 2:11; Mark 5:37; Mark 5:40; Luke 8:51; John 1:45; Luke 18:31-33; Luke 24:27; Luke 24:32; Luke 24:44-45; Mark 4:11-12; Matthew 13:10-11; Luke 8:9-10; Mark 4:34; Luke 10:23-24; Mark 6:6-11; Luke 9:1-2; Matthew 10:1; Mark 6:31; Matthew 16:21; Luke 24:25; Mark 9:32; Luke 9:45; Luke 18:34
* **Associated Articles:** 1416: miracles, nature of (ID: `1416@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of (ID: `2363@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5113: Peter, the disciple (ID: `5113@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5438: parables (ID: `5438@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7120: Christians (ID: `7120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7630: Twelve, the (ID: `7630@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7706: apostles (ID: `7706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7948: mission (ID: `7948@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8114: discipleship (ID: `8114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8120: following Jesus Christ (ID: `8120@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7622: disciples, characteristics of (id: 7622)

The content of discipleship is most clearly seen in the Christian's relationship to Jesus Christ. But this also affects a person's relationship to God the Father, the cross, the church and the world.

Disciples and their relationship to Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------

### Coming to Christ and following him

**[MAT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Matt4:19)** *See also* [MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28); [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21); [LUK 18:16](https://ref.ly/Luke18:16)

### Receiving him

**[REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20)** *See also* [JHN 1:12](https://ref.ly/John1:12)

### Learning from him

**[MAT 11:29](https://ref.ly/Matt11:29)** *See also* [MAT 24:32](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32) parallel [MRK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28)

### Lovingly obeying him

**[JHN 14:15](https://ref.ly/John14:15)**

### Trusting him

**[JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1)**

### Confessing him

**[MAT 10:38](https://ref.ly/Matt10:38)** parallel [LUK 14:27](https://ref.ly/Luke14:27) *See also* [MRK 8:38](https://ref.ly/Mark8:38) parallel [LUK 9:26](https://ref.ly/Luke9:26)

### Imitating him

[1CO 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:1); ; [1PE 2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21)

### Reflecting him

[MAT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:16)

### Abiding in him

**[JHN 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4)** *See also* [JHN 6:66–6:68](https://ref.ly/John6:66-John6:68)

Disciples and their relationship to God the Father
--------------------------------------------------

### True sons and daughters of God

**[MAT 5:44–5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:44-Matt5:45)**

### Discipleship and glorifying God

*See also* [MAT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:16)

Disciples and their relationship to the cross
---------------------------------------------

### Self\-denial

**[MAT 16:24](https://ref.ly/Matt16:24)** parallels [MRK 8:34](https://ref.ly/Mark8:34), [LUK 9:23](https://ref.ly/Luke9:23)

Disciples and their relationship to the church
----------------------------------------------

### Treating other Christians as family

**[MRK 3:34–3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:34-Mark3:35)** *See also* [MAT 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:17); [JHN 15:12](https://ref.ly/John15:12), [JHN 15:17](https://ref.ly/John15:17)

Disciples and their relationship to the world
---------------------------------------------

### They are to be active in the world but are to be distinct from it

**[MAT 5:13](https://ref.ly/Matt5:13)** *See also* [MAT 5:14–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:14-Matt5:16); [JHN 17:11–17:23](https://ref.ly/John17:11-John17:23)

### They are to make other disciples

**[MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19)** *See also* [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 4:19; Matthew 11:28; Mark 10:21; Luke 18:16; Revelation 3:20; John 1:12; Matthew 11:29; Matthew 24:32; Mark 13:28; John 14:15; John 14:1; Matthew 10:38; Luke 14:27; Mark 8:38; Luke 9:26; 1 Corinthians 11:1; 1 Peter 2:21; Matthew 5:16; John 15:4; John 6:66-68; Matthew 5:44-45; Matthew 16:24; Mark 8:34; Luke 9:23; Mark 3:34-35; Matthew 18:15-17; John 15:12; John 15:17; Matthew 5:13; Matthew 5:14-16; John 17:11-23; Matthew 28:19; Acts 1:8

## 7630: Twelve, the (id: 7630)

The inner circle of the twelve disciples, called by Jesus Christ to follow him and carry on his mission after his death and resurrection.

* **Associated Articles:** 7631: Twelve, calling of (ID: `7631@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7632: Twelve, diverse characters of (ID: `7632@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7631: Twelve, calling of (id: 7631)

Jesus Christ's chosen companions, whom he called and trained to carry on his mission after his death and resurrection.

The calling of the Twelve
-------------------------

### Their names

**[MAT 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1-Matt10:4)** parallel [MRK 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:19) *See also* [LUK 6:12–6:16](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12-Luke6:16); [ACT 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Acts1:12-Acts1:13)

### Their selection was preceded by prayer

[LUK 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12-Luke6:13)

### As Jesus Christ's companions, they were to share in his mission

[MRK 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:15), [MRK 6:7–6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7-Mark6:13) parallels [MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1), [MAT 10:9–10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:9-Matt10:14), [LUK 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:6); ; [LUK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1)

### Jesus Christ promised them authority at the final judgment

[MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28) parallel [LUK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:30)

After Judas' death the Twelve became the Eleven
-----------------------------------------------

[MAT 28:16](https://ref.ly/Matt28:16); ; [MRK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:14); ; [LUK 24:9](https://ref.ly/Luke24:9), [LUK 24:33](https://ref.ly/Luke24:33); ; [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14)

### The Eleven became the Twelve again with the election of Matthias

[ACT 1:26](https://ref.ly/Acts1:26)

The ministry of the Twelve after the resurrection supplemented by the appointment of deacons
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:3)

The symbolic significance of the Twelve
---------------------------------------

### The twelve tribes of Israel

[GEN 49:28](https://ref.ly/Gen49:28); ; [EZK 47:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:13); ; [MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28) parallel [LUK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:30) *Twelve symbolises completeness and is associated with the rule of God.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 10:1-4; Mark 3:14-19; Luke 6:12-16; Acts 1:12-13; Luke 6:12-13; Mark 3:14-15; Mark 6:7-13; Matthew 10:1; Matthew 10:9-14; Luke 9:1-6; Luke 8:1; Matthew 19:28; Luke 22:30; Matthew 28:16; Mark 16:14; Luke 24:9; Luke 24:33; Acts 2:14; Acts 1:26; Acts 6:2-3; Genesis 49:28; Ezekiel 47:13

## 7632: Twelve, diverse characters of (id: 7632)

The Twelve were men of varied background, gifts and character.

The varied backgrounds of the Twelve
------------------------------------

Peter, Andrew, James and John were fishermen: [MAT 4:18](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18), [MAT 4:21](https://ref.ly/Matt4:21); [MAT 9:9](https://ref.ly/Matt9:9) *Matthew was a tax collector*; ; [MAT 10:4](https://ref.ly/Matt10:4) *Simon was a political activist.*

The varied roles of the Twelve
------------------------------

### Some took a prominent role in the group

**[GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9)**

### Some played minor but significant roles

*See also* [JHN 1:40–1:42](https://ref.ly/John1:40-John1:42) *Andrew brought Peter to Jesus Christ*; [JHN 1:45–1:46](https://ref.ly/John1:45-John1:46) *Philip brought Nathanael*; [JHN 12:20–12:22](https://ref.ly/John12:20-John12:22) *Philip and Andrew brought the Greeks to Jesus Christ.*

The varied temperaments of the Twelve
-------------------------------------

[MAT 14:28](https://ref.ly/Matt14:28) *Peter was impetuous*; ; [LUK 9:54](https://ref.ly/Luke9:54) *James and John were fiery*; ; [LUK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Luke22:4) *Judas Iscariot was a traitorous loner*; ; [JHN 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25) *Thomas was cautious.*

The Twelve had a common call to discipleship and witness
--------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22)

The fallibility of the Twelve
-----------------------------

[MRK 8:31–8:33](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31-Mark8:33) *Peter misunderstood*; ; [MRK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:14) *Jesus Christ rebuked the Eleven for not believing reports of his resurrection*; ; [LUK 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/Luke9:12-Luke9:13) *Jesus Christ tested their reaction to human need*; ; [JHN 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:8-John14:9) *Philip asked the way to the Father.*

Jesus Christ gave the Twelve special teaching
---------------------------------------------

[MRK 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:11) *about his parables*; ; [MRK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Mark9:35) *about power and position*; ; [LUK 18:31](https://ref.ly/Luke18:31) *about his destiny*

Jesus Christ spent his last days and hours with the Twelve
----------------------------------------------------------

[MRK 11:11](https://ref.ly/Mark11:11) *after the triumphal entry into Jerusalem*; ; [MRK 14:17](https://ref.ly/Mark14:17) *at the Last Supper*

The Twelve failed Jesus Christ
------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's concern that they would leave him

[JHN 6:67–6:70](https://ref.ly/John6:67-John6:70)

### Judas betrayed him

[MAT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:16) parallels [MRK 14:10](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10), [LUK 22:3–22:4](https://ref.ly/Luke22:3-Luke22:4)

### They failed to support him in his final agony of prayer

[MAT 26:40–26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40-Matt26:41) parallels [MRK 14:37–14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:37-Mark14:38), [LUK 22:45–22:46](https://ref.ly/Luke22:45-Luke22:46)

### Peter disowned Jesus Christ

[MAT 26:74–26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:74-Matt26:75) parallels [MRK 14:71–14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:71-Mark14:72), [LUK 22:60–22:61](https://ref.ly/Luke22:60-Luke22:61)

### All abandoned Jesus Christ

[MAT 26:56](https://ref.ly/Matt26:56) *the exception was “the disciple whom Jesus loved”, present at the crucifixion ([Jn 19:25–27](https://ref.ly/John19:25-John19:27))*
