## 7703: apologetics (id: 7703)

The art of explaining the faith in such a way as to make a reasoned defence against its detractors. Paul's Areopagus sermon is a classic example of biblical apologetics, which can be of value to all who are called upon to defend the faith today.

Paul's Areopagus sermon, a classic example of biblical apologetics
------------------------------------------------------------------

### Paul speaks to his listeners on their own ground, starting on their own terms

**[ACT 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:22-Acts17:23)**

### Paul presents the Christian position

**[ACT 17:24–17:27](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24-Acts17:27)**

### Paul supports his argument in a culturally appropriate way

**[ACT 17:28](https://ref.ly/Acts17:28)** *Paul quotes from two secular writers, first from the Cretan poet Epimenides, and secondly from the Cilician poet Aratus.*

### Paul concludes his argument and calls for a response

**[ACT 17:29–17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:29-Acts17:31)**

Apologetics as a regular feature of Paul's ministry
---------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4)** *See also* [ACT 9:26–9:30](https://ref.ly/Acts9:26-Acts9:30), [ACT 18:19](https://ref.ly/Acts18:19), [ACT 19:8–19:10](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8-Acts19:10), [ACT 28:17–28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:17-Acts28:31)

Apologetics is part of the work of church leaders
-------------------------------------------------

**[TIT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:9)**

All Christians share responsibility for the task of apologetics
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)**

Other examples of Christians engaging in apologetics
----------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 18:28](https://ref.ly/Acts18:28)** *See also* [ACT 2:14–2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14-Acts2:41), [ACT 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8-Acts6:10)

Apologetics alone is an inadequate way of presenting the gospel
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:17–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17-1Cor1:25)** *See also* [1CO 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:1-1Cor2:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:22-23; Acts 17:24-27; Acts 17:28; Acts 17:29-31; Acts 18:4; Acts 9:26-30; Acts 18:19; Acts 19:8-10; Acts 28:17-31; Titus 1:9; 1 Peter 3:15; Acts 18:28; Acts 2:14-41; Acts 6:8-10; 1 Corinthians 1:17-25; 1 Corinthians 2:1-5
* **Associated Articles:** 2420: gospel (ID: `2420@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5038: mind, the human (ID: `5038@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5050: reason (ID: `5050@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5293: defence, human (ID: `5293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5622: witnesses (ID: `5622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7724: evangelists (ID: `7724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7751: persuasion (ID: `7751@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7756: preaching, content of (ID: `7756@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7760: preachers, responsibilities of (ID: `7760@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8424: evangelism (ID: `8424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8495: witnessing (ID: `8495@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7706: apostles (id: 7706)

Appointed representatives, deriving authority and responsibilities from the person or group represented. The word refers especially to Jesus Christ's twelve disciples and also to Paul. Scripture identifies the functions of apostles and their authority, which is derived directly from Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 7707: apostles, designation of (ID: `7707@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7708: apostles, function of (ID: `7708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7709: apostles, authority of (ID: `7709@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7707: apostles, designation of (id: 7707)

An apostle is one who is “sent” by Jesus Christ. The word is used to refer especially to the inner circle of Christ's twelve disciples, but is also used specifically to refer to Paul, who was commissioned as an apostle by the risen Christ. The term is also applied more loosely to certain other leading Christians in the NT period.

Jesus Christ as an apostle
--------------------------

**[HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Heb3:1)** *This is the only place where the word “apostle” is used of Jesus Christ, though many passages refer to him as “sent”*; **[JHN 20:21](https://ref.ly/John20:21)** *Jesus Christ, as God's “sent one”, sets the standard and pattern for all who are likewise “sent” (i.e., for all apostolic ministry).* *See also* [MAT 10:40](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40); [MRK 9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:37); [JHN 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/John5:23-John5:24), [JHN 12:44–12:45](https://ref.ly/John12:44-John12:45)

The twelve apostles called by Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------

### Their appointment

**[MRK 3:13–3:19](https://ref.ly/Mark3:13-Mark3:19)** parallels [MAT 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1-Matt10:4), [LUK 6:12–6:16](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12-Luke6:16)

### Their number

**[JHN 6:70](https://ref.ly/John6:70)** *“the Twelve” is frequently used to designate these specially chosen disciples. The number twelve evokes the twelve tribes of Israel.* *See also* [MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28), [MAT 20:17](https://ref.ly/Matt20:17); [MRK 11:11](https://ref.ly/Mark11:11); [LUK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1); [ACT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2); [1CO 15:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:5); [REV 21:14](https://ref.ly/Rev21:14)

### Their commissioning by the risen Christ

**[MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20)**

### The replacement for Judas

*See also* [ACT 1:15–1:26](https://ref.ly/Acts1:15-Acts1:26) *The symbolic number (twelve) is thus preserved.*

### The qualifications for being an apostle

**[ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22)**

Paul as an apostle
------------------

### His self\-designation

**[GAL 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gal1:1)** *See also* [ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); [1CO 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:1); [1TI 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:7)

### His commissioning by Jesus Christ

[ACT 26:12–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:12-Acts26:18) *Although not one of the Twelve, Paul saw the risen Christ and was directly commissioned by him, sent especially to the Gentiles*; ; [1CO 15:3–15:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:3-1Cor15:10); ; [GAL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Gal2:8)

### Paul's apostleship and the church

**[1CO 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:1-1Cor9:2)** *In Paul's case the sense of title or office and the ministry and function virtually coincide. He is an apostle both because he has seen and been commissioned by the risen Christ and because he functioned as an apostle by founding churches.*

Other apostles
--------------

### Barnabas

Here `apostles'' may refer to their ministry as missionary evangelists or to their role as`sent'' by the church at Antioch: [ACT 14:4](https://ref.ly/Acts14:4), [ACT 14:14](https://ref.ly/Acts14:14)

### James, the Lord's brother

[1CO 15:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:7) *The most natural meaning of the words is that James is an apostle. This interpretation is confirmed by other references to James*; ; [GAL 1:19](https://ref.ly/Gal1:19), [GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9)

### Andronicus and Junias

[ROM 16:7](https://ref.ly/Rom16:7)

### Silas

[1TH 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6)

### \`\`Apostles'' of the churches

[2CO 8:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:23) *“representatives” is the translation here for “apostles”*; ; [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25) *“messenger” is the translation here for “apostle”*

False apostles
--------------

[2CO 11:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:5) *Paul ironically refers to his opponents as “super\-apostles”*; ; [2CO 11:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13), [2CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:11); ; [REV 2:2](https://ref.ly/Rev2:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 3:1; John 20:21; Matthew 10:40; Mark 9:37; John 5:23-24; John 12:44-45; Mark 3:13-19; Matthew 10:1-4; Luke 6:12-16; John 6:70; Matthew 19:28; Matthew 20:17; Mark 11:11; Luke 8:1; Acts 6:2; 1 Corinthians 15:5; Revelation 21:14; Matthew 28:18-20; Acts 1:15-26; Acts 1:21-22; Galatians 1:1; Romans 1:1; 1 Corinthians 1:1; 1 Timothy 2:7; Acts 26:12-18; 1 Corinthians 15:3-10; Galatians 2:8; 1 Corinthians 9:1-2; Acts 14:4; Acts 14:14; 1 Corinthians 15:7; Galatians 1:19; Galatians 2:9; Romans 16:7; 1 Thessalonians 2:6; 2 Corinthians 8:23; Philippians 2:25; 2 Corinthians 11:5; 2 Corinthians 11:13; 2 Corinthians 12:11; Revelation 2:2
* **Associated Articles:** 2057: Jesus Christ, obedience of (ID: `2057@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2327: Jesus Christ, as servant (ID: `2327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2339: Jesus Christ, example of (ID: `2339@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5109: Paul, apostle to Gentiles (ID: `5109@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5114: Peter, the apostle (ID: `5114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7160: servants of the Lord (ID: `7160@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7621: disciples, calling of (ID: `7621@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7631: Twelve, calling of (ID: `7631@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7739: missionaries (ID: `7739@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7708: apostles, function of (id: 7708)

In the NT apostles are servants, first of Jesus Christ and then of the church. Apostolic service embraces a wide range of specific functions.

The functions of apostles during the earthly life and ministry of Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Being with Jesus Christ

**[MRK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14)** *See also* [MAT 17:1](https://ref.ly/Matt17:1); [MRK 6:30–6:32](https://ref.ly/Mark6:30-Mark6:32) parallel [LUK 9:10](https://ref.ly/Luke9:10)

### Learning from Jesus Christ

**[MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31)** *See also* [MAT 18:1–18:4](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1-Matt18:4); [MRK 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:12); [ACT 1:2](https://ref.ly/Acts1:2)

### Being sent out on a mission by Jesus Christ

**[LUK 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:6)** parallels [MAT 10:5–10:10](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:10), [MRK 6:7–6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7-Mark6:13) *As Jesus Christ's representatives their ministry was an extension of his.* *See also* [MRK 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:15)

### Being commissioned to witness to Jesus Christ

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8), [ACT 10:39](https://ref.ly/Acts10:39)** *See also* [MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20); [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15); [LUK 24:46–24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46-Luke24:49); [JHN 17:18](https://ref.ly/John17:18), [JHN 20:21](https://ref.ly/John20:21)

Apostles in the early church
----------------------------

### They were witnesses of the resurrection

**[ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22)** *See also* [ACT 2:32](https://ref.ly/Acts2:32), [ACT 3:15](https://ref.ly/Acts3:15), [ACT 4:33](https://ref.ly/Acts4:33), [ACT 13:31](https://ref.ly/Acts13:31); [1PE 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1)

### They were servants of Jesus Christ

**[2PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:1)** *See also* [ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); [1CO 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:1); [2CO 11:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23); [PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1); [TIT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Titus1:1)

### They were servants of the church

**[2CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:4)** *See also* [ROM 15:25](https://ref.ly/Rom15:25); [2CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:5), [2CO 11:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:8); [1PE 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:3)

### They were the foundations of the church

**[EPH 2:20](https://ref.ly/Eph2:20)** *Probably a reference to the apostolic preaching. The absence of the article before “prophets” suggests that “the apostles and prophets” represents one group of people, not two.* *See also* [1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28); [GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9); [JUD 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jude1:17) *the prophetic ministry of the apostles*

### They preached the gospel

**[ROM 1:1–1:5](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1-Rom1:5)** *See also* [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20); [ROM 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:19-Rom15:20); [1CO 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17); [GAL 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Gal1:15-Gal1:16)

### They were guardians and teachers of the truth

**[ACT 2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:42)** *See also* [JHN 16:13–16:15](https://ref.ly/John16:13-John16:15); [ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42), [ACT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2); [EPH 3:2–3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:2-Eph3:6); [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15); [2TI 1:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:11)

### They founded churches

**[1CO 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:1-1Cor9:2)** *See also* [1CO 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:5-1Cor3:6), [1CO 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:10)

### They were authority figures within the church

[ACT 6:2–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:6) *The apostles took the initiative. Whilst they did not themselves appoint the Seven, they confirmed their appointment.*

### They were called to suffer

**[2TI 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:11-2Tim1:12)** *See also* [ACT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:18); [1CO 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:9); [2CO 11:23–11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:29)

### They were empowered by the Spirit

**[ACT 4:8](https://ref.ly/Acts4:8)** *See also* [JHN 20:22](https://ref.ly/John20:22); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8), [ACT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:4); [EPH 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Eph3:4-Eph3:5)

### They were able to bestow the Spirit

[ACT 8:14–8:18](https://ref.ly/Acts8:14-Acts8:18) *Peter and John officially induct Samaritans as members of the body of Christ*; ; [ACT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Acts19:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 3:14; Matthew 17:1; Mark 6:30-32; Luke 9:10; Mark 8:31; Matthew 18:1-4; Mark 4:10-12; Acts 1:2; Luke 9:1-6; Matthew 10:5-10; Mark 6:7-13; Mark 3:14-15; Acts 1:8; Acts 10:39; Matthew 28:18-20; Mark 16:15; Luke 24:46-49; John 17:18; John 20:21; Acts 1:21-22; Acts 2:32; Acts 3:15; Acts 4:33; Acts 13:31; 1 Peter 5:1; 2 Peter 1:1; Romans 1:1; 1 Corinthians 4:1; 2 Corinthians 11:23; Philippians 1:1; Titus 1:1; 2 Corinthians 13:4; Romans 15:25; 2 Corinthians 4:5; 2 Corinthians 11:8; 1 Peter 5:1-3; Ephesians 2:20; 1 Corinthians 12:28; Galatians 2:9; Jude 1:17; Romans 1:1-5; Mark 16:20; Romans 15:19-20; 1 Corinthians 1:17; Galatians 1:15-16; Acts 2:42; John 16:13-15; Acts 5:42; Acts 6:2; Ephesians 3:2-6; 2 Thessalonians 2:15; 2 Timothy 1:11; 1 Corinthians 9:1-2; 1 Corinthians 3:5-6; 1 Corinthians 3:10; Acts 6:2-6; 2 Timothy 1:11-12; Acts 5:18; 1 Corinthians 4:9; 2 Corinthians 11:23-29; Acts 4:8; John 20:22; Acts 2:4; Ephesians 3:4-5; Acts 8:14-18; Acts 19:6

## 7709: apostles, authority of (id: 7709)

Apostolic authority derives directly from Jesus Christ, who commissions apostles. It is rigorously upheld within the church.

Apostolic authority is based on divine commission
-------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 1:5](https://ref.ly/Rom1:5)** *See also* [ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); [2CO 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:1); [GAL 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gal1:1); [EPH 1:1](https://ref.ly/Eph1:1); [COL 1:1](https://ref.ly/Col1:1); [1TI 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:1); [2TI 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:1)

Apostolic authority derives from Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

**[MAT 10:40](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40)** *See also* [MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20); [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7); [2PE 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:2)

The authority of apostles is related to their ministry role
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28)** *There appears to be a definite order of priority with apostles at the head.* *See also* [EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11)

The exercise of apostolic authority within the church
-----------------------------------------------------

### In building up the body

**[2CO 13:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:10)** *Paul had special authority as founder of this church.* *See also* [2CO 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:8)

### In general oversight

[ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35), [ACT 6:2–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:6), [ACT 15:6](https://ref.ly/Acts15:6), [ACT 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/Acts15:22-Acts15:23), [ACT 16:4](https://ref.ly/Acts16:4)

### In teaching

**[1TH 4:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:2)** *See also* [GAL 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Gal1:11-Gal1:12); [1TH 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:4), [1TH 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:8)

### In appointing leaders

[ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23)

### In church discipline

[ACT 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Acts5:3-Acts5:4), [ACT 5:7–5:9](https://ref.ly/Acts5:7-Acts5:9); ; [1CO 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:3-1Cor5:5)

### The right to be supported

**[1TH 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6)** *Paul did not demand such support (see [1Th 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9)).* *See also* [1CO 9:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:4), [1CO 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:11-1Cor9:14); [2CO 11:7–11:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:7-2Cor11:9)

The marks of a true apostle
---------------------------

### False apostles exist

**[1CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2)** *See also* [2CO 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:4-2Cor11:5), [2CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:11)

### Fruit confirms apostolic ministry

[2CO 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:1-2Cor3:3)

### Signs confirm apostolic authority

**[2CO 12:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:12)** *See also* [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20); [ACT 5:12](https://ref.ly/Acts5:12); [ROM 15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 1:5; Romans 1:1; 2 Corinthians 1:1; Galatians 1:1; Ephesians 1:1; Colossians 1:1; 1 Timothy 1:1; 2 Timothy 1:1; Matthew 10:40; Matthew 28:18-20; Mark 6:7; 2 Peter 3:2; 1 Corinthians 12:28; Ephesians 4:11; 2 Corinthians 13:10; 2 Corinthians 10:8; Acts 4:34-35; Acts 6:2-6; Acts 15:6; Acts 15:22-23; Acts 16:4; 1 Thessalonians 4:2; Galatians 1:11-12; 1 Thessalonians 2:4; 1 Thessalonians 4:8; Acts 14:23; Acts 5:3-4; Acts 5:7-9; 1 Corinthians 5:3-5; 1 Thessalonians 2:6; 1 Thessalonians 2:9; 1 Corinthians 9:4; 1 Corinthians 9:11-14; 2 Corinthians 11:7-9; 1 Corinthians 9:2; 2 Corinthians 11:4-5; 2 Corinthians 12:11; 2 Corinthians 3:1-3; 2 Corinthians 12:12; Mark 16:20; Acts 5:12; Romans 15:19

## 7712: convincing (id: 7712)

Persuading one's hearers of the truth of one's case. Scripture gives examples of people who have been convinced of the truth of God's word and of the gospel.

Examples of prophets convincing their hearers
---------------------------------------------

### Elijah at Mount Carmel

**[1KI 18:20–18:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:20-1Kgs18:39)**

### Jonah at Nineveh

**[JON 3:1–3:9](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1-Jonah3:9)**

Hardness of heart can prevent individuals from being convinced of the truth
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The ministry of the prophets

**[JER 6:10](https://ref.ly/Jer6:10)** *See also* [1KI 22:15–22:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:15-1Kgs22:28) parallel [2CH 18:14–18:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:14-2Chr18:27); [JER 35:15](https://ref.ly/Jer35:15); [EZK 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:4-Ezek2:5); [AMO 7:10–7:13](https://ref.ly/Amos7:10-Amos7:13)

### The ministry of Jesus Christ

**[JHN 12:37–12:40](https://ref.ly/John12:37-John12:40)** *See also* [ISA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1), [ISA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:10); [MAT 13:13–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:13-Matt13:15) parallels [MRK 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:12), [LUK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:10); [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); [JHN 7:25–7:43](https://ref.ly/John7:25-John7:43)

Examples of individuals convinced of the truth of the gospel
------------------------------------------------------------

### The Ethiopian eunuch

[ACT 8:26–8:38](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26-Acts8:38)

### Paul

**[ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39)** *See also* [2CO 5:14–5:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:14-2Cor5:15); [2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:12)

### Paul sought to convince others of the truth of the gospel

**[ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4)** *See also* [ACT 17:2–17:4](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2-Acts17:4), [ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8), [ACT 26:28–26:29](https://ref.ly/Acts26:28-Acts26:29), [ACT 28:23–28:24](https://ref.ly/Acts28:23-Acts28:24); [2CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:11)

## 7715: deacons (id: 7715)

Those who “serve” in the Christian community, assisting the elders or overseers (bishops). The Greek word which the NIV translates four times as “deacon” occurs a further 25 times in the NT, where it is translated as “servant”. It seems that all Christians have a general servant role, whereas some (deacons) are called to a specific office of service within the church.

The Seven: assistants to the apostles
-------------------------------------

### Why they were needed

**[ACT 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:4)** *The verb “wait” means “to serve”.*

### Their qualifications and selection

**[ACT 6:3](https://ref.ly/Acts6:3)**

### Their names and appointment

**[ACT 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:5-Acts6:6)** *Although the Seven are not actually called “deacons”, they were probably the earliest example of Christians carrying out the tasks which came to be linked with deacons.*

Deacons were assistants to overseers in Philippi
------------------------------------------------

**[PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1)** *All Christians are “saints” (that is, set apart for God), but overseers and deacons have specific roles assigned to them.*

Qualifications for men and women deacons
----------------------------------------

**[1TI 3:8–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8-1Tim3:13)** *There is no NT description of the precise role of deacons (although [Ac 6:1–4](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:4) may imply a certain role). These qualifications would be appropriate for people responsible for financial management, administration, and social service, such as that offered to widows*; **[1TI 3:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:11)** *The women may be deacons' wives (NIV text), or deaconesses (NIV footnote).*

Phoebe, a deaconess
-------------------

**[ROM 16:1](https://ref.ly/Rom16:1)** *NIV footnote.*

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 6:1-4; Acts 6:3; Acts 6:5-6; Philippians 1:1; 1 Timothy 3:8-13; 1 Timothy 3:11; Romans 16:1
* **Associated Articles:** 2327: Jesus Christ, as servant (ID: `2327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5520: servants (ID: `5520@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7026: church, leadership of (ID: `7026@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7372: hands, laying on of (ID: `7372@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7706: apostles (ID: `7706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7718: elders (ID: `7718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7734: leaders, spiritual (ID: `7734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7748: overseers (ID: `7748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7784: shepherd (ID: `7784@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7939: ministry (ID: `7939@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8492: watchfulness, of leaders (ID: `8492@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7718: elders (id: 7718)

Senior figures within Israel and the Christian church who are given special responsibilities for their people.

* **Associated Articles:** 7719: elders, as community leaders (ID: `7719@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7720: elders, in the church (ID: `7720@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7719: elders, as community leaders (id: 7719)

Since older men act as responsible leaders in many communities, “elder” is used as the title for a recognised leader.

Elders in early Israel
----------------------

### Elders represented their people

**[EXO 19:7](https://ref.ly/Exod19:7)** *See also* [EXO 3:16](https://ref.ly/Exod3:16), [EXO 4:29](https://ref.ly/Exod4:29), [EXO 12:21–12:28](https://ref.ly/Exod12:21-Exod12:28), [EXO 24:1](https://ref.ly/Exod24:1)

### Elders assisted Moses

**[NUM 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Num11:16-Num11:17)** *See also* [EXO 18:21–18:26](https://ref.ly/Exod18:21-Exod18:26); [NUM 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/Num11:24-Num11:25)

### Elders met in a national assembly

[JOS 23:2](https://ref.ly/Josh23:2), [JOS 24:1](https://ref.ly/Josh24:1); ; [JDG 21:16](https://ref.ly/Judg21:16)

### Elders functioned as judges, sometimes in the town gate

[DEU 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:12); ; [JDG 8:14](https://ref.ly/Judg8:14); ; [RUT 4:1–4:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:12)

Elders during the period of the monarchy
----------------------------------------

### Tribal and town elders

[1SA 30:26–30:31](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:26-1Sam30:31); ; [2KI 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:5), [2KI 23:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:1); ; [JER 19:1](https://ref.ly/Jer19:1)

### Elders were local representatives at national functions

**[1KI 8:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:1)**

### Elders advised the king

**[1KI 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:6-1Kgs12:8)**

### Elders functioned as judges at the town gate

**[PRO 31:23](https://ref.ly/Prov31:23)** *See also* [1KI 21:8–21:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:14)

Elders in Israel during and after the exile
-------------------------------------------

[EZR 10:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:8), [EZR 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:14); ; [EZK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:1), [EZK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:1), [EZK 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:1-Ezek20:3)

---------------------------

### Elders were local town and synagogue leaders

[MRK 5:22](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22); ; [LUK 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Luke7:1-Luke7:5)

### Elders were members of the Sanhedrin

**[MRK 15:1](https://ref.ly/Mark15:1)** *See also* [MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)

### Elders were guardians of religious traditions

**[MAT 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:2)** parallel [MRK 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mark7:5) *See also* [MRK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mark7:3)

## 7720: elders, in the church (id: 7720)

In the NT a group of leaders responsible for managing and teaching a local church. As the name implies, they were normally men of experience and maturity. Although women played a very significant part in early Christian ministry, there is no specific NT evidence for women as elders.

Elders in the Jerusalem church
------------------------------

**[ACT 15:4](https://ref.ly/Acts15:4)** *See also* [ACT 11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:30), [ACT 15:2](https://ref.ly/Acts15:2), [ACT 15:6](https://ref.ly/Acts15:6), [ACT 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/Acts15:22-Acts15:23), [ACT 16:4](https://ref.ly/Acts16:4), [ACT 21:18](https://ref.ly/Acts21:18)

Elders are also called \`\`overseers''
--------------------------------------

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *In [Ac 20:17](https://ref.ly/Acts20:17) they are described as “elders”. Here they are “overseers”.* *See also* [TIT 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6-Titus1:7)

Elders are appointed by apostles or their delegates
---------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23)** *Here, as always in the NT, people are appointed to a joint eldership. There is no NT example of a single elder leading a church.* *See also* [TIT 1:5](https://ref.ly/Titus1:5)

Qualifications for elders
-------------------------

**[TIT 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6-Titus1:9)** *As in [Ac 20:17,28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:17,Acts20:28), “elder” is here synonymous with “overseer”.* *See also* [1TI 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1-1Tim3:7)

The role of elders
------------------

### As leaders

**[1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)** *See also* [1TI 3:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:5)

### As teachers

**[1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)** *See also* [1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2)

### As pastors and guardians against false teaching

**[ACT 20:28–20:31](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28-Acts20:31)** *See also* [TIT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:9); [1PE 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:4)

### In the healing ministry

**[JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)**

The remuneration and correction of elders
-----------------------------------------

*See also* [1TI 5:17–5:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:20)

Elders are to receive respect and submission from church members
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)** *“leaders” is an alternative term for “elders”.* *See also* [1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12); [HEB 13:7](https://ref.ly/Heb13:7)

The twenty\-four elders in heaven
---------------------------------

### The elders are seated on thrones

**[REV 4:4](https://ref.ly/Rev4:4)** *These elders may be angelic beings corresponding to the 24 orders of Levites (1Chronicles chapters 24–25\) or symbolise Israel and the church (compare [Rev 21:12–14](https://ref.ly/Rev21:12-Rev21:14)).* *See also* [REV 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5-Rev5:6), [REV 11:16](https://ref.ly/Rev11:16), [REV 14:3](https://ref.ly/Rev14:3)

### The elders worship God and the Lamb

**[REV 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:10-Rev4:11)** *See also* [REV 5:8–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8-Rev5:14), [REV 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11-Rev7:13), [REV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Rev19:4)

## 7724: evangelists (id: 7724)

Those called by God to announce good news and, especially, to proclaim the good news of Jesus Christ. The work was first committed to the apostles and then to other believers gifted by the Holy Spirit for the task.

* **Associated Articles:** 7725: evangelists, identity of (ID: `7725@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7726: evangelists, ministry of (ID: `7726@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7725: evangelists, identity of (id: 7725)

Some OT figures announced good news, but most evangelists are seen in the NT. The task was first given by Jesus Christ to the apostles and then to other believers. All Christians have a part in evangelism; there is, too, a special role of evangelist within the church, fulfilled by those with a particular gifting from the Holy Spirit.

An evangelist as one who announces good news
--------------------------------------------

### God as an announcer of good news

**[GAL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gal3:8)** *See also* [GEN 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:2-Gen12:3), [GEN 22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:18), [GEN 26:4](https://ref.ly/Gen26:4); [JER 31:3–31:4](https://ref.ly/Jer31:3-Jer31:4); [ACT 10:36](https://ref.ly/Acts10:36)

### OT messengers announce good news

**[PSA 96:2–96:3](https://ref.ly/Ps96:2-Ps96:3)** parallel [1CH 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:23-1Chr16:24)**[ISA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Isa40:9)** *The good news of the end of the Babylonian exile.* *See also* [2KI 7:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:9); [PSA 40:10](https://ref.ly/Ps40:10); [ISA 40:1–40:5](https://ref.ly/Isa40:1-Isa40:5), [ISA 41:27](https://ref.ly/Isa41:27), [ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7); [NAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Nah1:15) *the good news of the deliverance from Assyria*; [MAT 12:41](https://ref.ly/Matt12:41) parallel [LUK 11:32](https://ref.ly/Luke11:32); [ROM 10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:15); [2PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:5)

### Angels announce good news

**[LUK 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Luke2:10-Luke2:11)** *See also* [LUK 1:19](https://ref.ly/Luke1:19); [REV 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rev14:6)

### John the Baptist announces good news

**[LUK 3:18](https://ref.ly/Luke3:18)** *See also* [LUK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Luke3:3), [LUK 16:16](https://ref.ly/Luke16:16)

Jesus Christ as an evangelist
-----------------------------

**[LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19)** *See also* [ISA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:2); [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23), [MAT 9:35](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35); [MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15); [LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43), [LUK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1)

The disciples as evangelists
----------------------------

### Jesus Christ sends out the Twelve

**[MRK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14)** *See also* [MAT 10:7–10:8](https://ref.ly/Matt10:7-Matt10:8); [LUK 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:2), [LUK 9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:6)

### Jesus Christ commissions the apostles

**[MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15)** *See also* [MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20); [LUK 24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:47); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)

### The evangelism of the apostles

**[ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42)** *See also* [ACT 8:25](https://ref.ly/Acts8:25) *Peter and John preach the gospel in Samaria*; [ACT 15:7](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7) *Peter preaches the gospel to Cornelius.*

------------------------------

### Evangelists are gifted by God

**[EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11)**

### Paul as an evangelist

**[ACT 20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:24)** *See also* [ROM 1:9](https://ref.ly/Rom1:9), [ROM 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:19-Rom15:20); [1CO 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17); [2CO 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:12); [GAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7)

### Philip the evangelist

### Other evangelists

## 7726: evangelists, ministry of (id: 7726)

Evangelists proclaim the good news of Jesus Christ, calling those who hear to believe in him. This may be in the context of missionary journeys, often in partnership with others, or within a more settled ministry. Carrying out the task requires integrity and commitment in the face of hardships and danger; it also brings joy at seeing the church grow.

The task of evangelists
-----------------------

### Announcing good news

**[ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7)**; **[LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43)** *See also* [ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42), [ACT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12), [ACT 11:20](https://ref.ly/Acts11:20); [ROM 10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:15)

### Proclaiming the gospel

**[ROM 15:16](https://ref.ly/Rom15:16), [ROM 15:20–15:21](https://ref.ly/Rom15:20-Rom15:21)** *See also* [ACT 8:5](https://ref.ly/Acts8:5), [ACT 15:7](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7); [1CO 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17), [1CO 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:16), [1CO 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:1-1Cor15:2); [2CO 10:15–10:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:15-2Cor10:16)

### Testifying to the truth about Jesus Christ

**[ACT 10:42–10:43](https://ref.ly/Acts10:42-Acts10:43)** *See also* [JHN 1:35–1:36](https://ref.ly/John1:35-John1:36), [JHN 15:26–15:27](https://ref.ly/John15:26-John15:27); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8), [ACT 2:32–2:33](https://ref.ly/Acts2:32-Acts2:33), [ACT 17:2–17:3](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2-Acts17:3); [1JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:3)

### Calling people to believe in Jesus Christ

**[ROM 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/Rom10:13-Rom10:14)** *See also* [MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15); [ACT 13:38–13:39](https://ref.ly/Acts13:38-Acts13:39), [ACT 16:30–16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:30-Acts16:34), [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4), [ACT 26:28](https://ref.ly/Acts26:28); [ROM 1:5](https://ref.ly/Rom1:5); [1CO 15:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:11); [2CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:11)

The context of the evangelist's work
------------------------------------

### Missionary journeys

[ACT 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/Acts13:4-Acts13:5), [ACT 14:24–14:25](https://ref.ly/Acts14:24-Acts14:25), [ACT 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9-Acts16:10)

### In partnership with others

**[LUK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Luke10:1)** *See also* [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7); [ACT 10:23](https://ref.ly/Acts10:23), [ACT 14:1](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1), [ACT 16:6](https://ref.ly/Acts16:6), [ACT 17:14–17:15](https://ref.ly/Acts17:14-Acts17:15); [PHP 1:5](https://ref.ly/Phil1:5)

### Within a settled period of ministry

**[2TI 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:5)** *Timothy as a pastor is also to do the work of an evangelist.* *See also* [ACT 18:9–18:11](https://ref.ly/Acts18:9-Acts18:11) *Paul spends a year and a half in Corinth*; [ACT 28:30–28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:30-Acts28:31) *Paul preaches the gospel whilst under house arrest in Rome*; [1TI 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:13-1Tim4:14); [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)

The evangelist's integrity
--------------------------

**[2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17)** *See also* [2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:12); [1TH 2:3–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:3-1Thess2:5)

The evangelist's commitment
---------------------------

### Working so as not to be a burden

**[1TH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:8-1Thess2:9)** *See also* [ACT 20:33–20:34](https://ref.ly/Acts20:33-Acts20:34); [1CO 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:12), [1CO 9:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:6)

### Facing hardship and danger

**[2CO 11:23–11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:29)** [ACT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:18), [ACT 20:23–20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:23-Acts20:24), [ACT 21:33](https://ref.ly/Acts21:33); ; [EPH 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Eph6:19-Eph6:20); [ACT 5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:40), [ACT 16:22–16:23](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22-Acts16:23); [ACT 7:58](https://ref.ly/Acts7:58), [ACT 14:19](https://ref.ly/Acts14:19); [ACT 9:29](https://ref.ly/Acts9:29), [ACT 21:30–21:31](https://ref.ly/Acts21:30-Acts21:31); [ACT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Acts20:19); ; [1TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2); [ROM 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Rom9:1-Rom9:2), [ROM 10:1](https://ref.ly/Rom10:1)

Financial support for evangelists
---------------------------------

**[1CO 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:11-1Cor9:14)** *See also* [1TI 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:18)

The evangelist's blessing
-------------------------

### Joy at seeing the church grow

**[1TH 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:7-1Thess3:9)** *See also* [ACT 15:3](https://ref.ly/Acts15:3); [1TH 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:19-1Thess2:20)

### The satisfaction of seeing new converts grow in their faith

[1CO 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:4-1Cor1:7); ; [EPH 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Eph1:15-Eph1:16); ; [PHP 1:3–1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:3-Phil1:6); ; [1TH 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:2-1Thess1:3)

## 7730: explanation (id: 7730)

The provision of the understanding of a dream, vision, parable, teaching or other matter, the meaning of which is less than obvious.

Examples of the explanation of dreams
-------------------------------------

### By Joseph

[GEN 40:6–40:19](https://ref.ly/Gen40:6-Gen40:19), [GEN 41:10–41:32](https://ref.ly/Gen41:10-Gen41:32)

### By Daniel

[DAN 2:16](https://ref.ly/Dan2:16), [DAN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Dan2:24), [DAN 2:27–2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:27-Dan2:45), [DAN 5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:12), [DAN 5:17](https://ref.ly/Dan5:17)

### By angels

[DAN 7:15–7:18](https://ref.ly/Dan7:15-Dan7:18), [DAN 7:23–7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:23-Dan7:27)

Examples of the explanation of visions
--------------------------------------

### By Gabriel

**[DAN 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Dan8:15-Dan8:16)**

### By an angel

*See also* [ZEC 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/Zech1:8-Zech1:10), [ZEC 1:18–1:21](https://ref.ly/Zech1:18-Zech1:21); [REV 17:3–17:7](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3-Rev17:7), [REV 17:18](https://ref.ly/Rev17:18)

### By Peter

[ACT 11:4–11:17](https://ref.ly/Acts11:4-Acts11:17)

Jesus Christ gave his disciples explanations
--------------------------------------------

### Of his parables

**[MRK 4:34](https://ref.ly/Mark4:34)** *See also* [MAT 15:15–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:15-Matt15:20) parallel [MRK 7:17–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:17-Mark7:23); [MAT 13:36–13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:36-Matt13:43)

### Of the Scriptures referring to himself and his relationship to his Father

**[LUK 24:27](https://ref.ly/Luke24:27)** *See also* [JHN 16:25](https://ref.ly/John16:25), [JHN 16:29–16:30](https://ref.ly/John16:29-John16:30)

### Of his suffering, death and resurrection

**[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)** parallels [MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31), [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22) *See also* [JHN 4:25–4:26](https://ref.ly/John4:25-John4:26)

The gospel was explained
------------------------

By Peter, at Pentecost: [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14), [ACT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Acts2:16); [ACT 8:30–8:31](https://ref.ly/Acts8:30-Acts8:31), [ACT 8:34–8:35](https://ref.ly/Acts8:34-Acts8:35); [ACT 17:2–17:3](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2-Acts17:3), [ACT 28:23](https://ref.ly/Acts28:23); [ACT 18:24–18:26](https://ref.ly/Acts18:24-Acts18:26) *by Aquila and Priscilla*

Examples of other explanations
------------------------------

[JDG 14:12–14:17](https://ref.ly/Judg14:12-Judg14:17) *The Philistines desperately wanted the answer to Samson's riddle.*; [1SA 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:9), [1SA 10:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:25); [1KI 4:29–4:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:29-1Kgs4:34), [1KI 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1-1Kgs10:3) parallel [2CH 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1-2Chr9:2); ; [NEH 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Neh8:7-Neh8:8)

## 7733: leaders (id: 7733)

God calls individuals to positions of leadership, both spiritual and political. Such leadership is seen as carrying responsibilities towards both God and his people and is to be exercised with humility.

* **Associated Articles:** 7734: leaders, spiritual (ID: `7734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7735: leaders, political (ID: `7735@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7734: leaders, spiritual (id: 7734)

Under the old covenant God called priests and prophets as spiritual leaders. Later the Pharisees gave spiritual leadership. After Jesus Christ had inaugurated the new covenant, authority was given to the apostles, who in turn appointed leaders in the churches they founded.

Priests as spiritual leaders
----------------------------

### They were called by God

**[HEB 5:4](https://ref.ly/Heb5:4)** *See also* [EXO 28:1](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1); [DEU 18:5](https://ref.ly/Deut18:5)

### The high priest represented the people in the presence of God

**[HEB 5:1](https://ref.ly/Heb5:1)** *See also* [EXO 28:29–28:30](https://ref.ly/Exod28:29-Exod28:30); [NUM 27:21](https://ref.ly/Num27:21); [HEB 8:3](https://ref.ly/Heb8:3)

### Priests taught and explained God's law

**[MAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Mal2:7)** *See also* [LEV 10:11](https://ref.ly/Lev10:11); [HAG 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Hag2:11-Hag2:13)

### Examples of priests as spiritual leaders

[1SA 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:18) *The priest Eli was described as leading Israel*; ; [1SA 7:5–7:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:5-1Sam7:9) *Samuel acted as priest on behalf of the people*; ; [2CH 24:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:14) *Jehoiada the priest influenced Joash to serve God.*

Prophets as spiritual leaders
-----------------------------

### They were called by God

**[2PE 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:21)** *See also* [ISA 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8-Isa6:9); [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5)

### Examples of prophets as spiritual leaders

Samuel led the people from idolatry back to the worship of God: [1SA 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:3-1Sam7:4), [1SA 12:20–12:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:20-1Sam12:24); [1KI 18:18–18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:18-1Kgs18:21) *Elijah*; ; [HAG 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:7-Hag1:9) *Haggai*

Spiritual leaders in NT times
-----------------------------

[MAT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Matt2:4) *officially recognised teachers of the law*; ; [MAT 16:1](https://ref.ly/Matt16:1), [MAT 21:23](https://ref.ly/Matt21:23) *At a local level, elders had social and religious authority in local councils. Nationally, they were included in the Sanhedrin*; ; [MAT 23:2](https://ref.ly/Matt23:2), [MAT 26:59](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59)

The failure of spiritual leaders
--------------------------------

### In the OT

[ISA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Isa28:7); ; [JER 23:11](https://ref.ly/Jer23:11), [JER 23:13–23:17](https://ref.ly/Jer23:13-Jer23:17), [JER 23:25–23:32](https://ref.ly/Jer23:25-Jer23:32); ; [MAL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:1-Mal2:2)

### In the NT

[MAT 23:4–23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:4-Matt23:15); ; [MRK 10:42–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:45) *Jesus Christ taught his disciples the qualities required for spiritual leadership.*

Spiritual leadership in the church
----------------------------------

### The apostles delegated authority

[ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35), [ACT 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:3), [ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23); ; [TIT 1:5](https://ref.ly/Titus1:5)

### The qualifications for church leadership

[1TI 3:1–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1-1Tim3:9); ; [TIT 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6-Titus1:9)

### Advice to leaders

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *See also* [ACT 20:31](https://ref.ly/Acts20:31), [ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35); [1TI 5:17–5:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:20); [1PE 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2-1Pet5:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 5:4; Exodus 28:1; Deuteronomy 18:5; Hebrews 5:1; Exodus 28:29-30; Numbers 27:21; Hebrews 8:3; Malachi 2:7; Leviticus 10:11; Haggai 2:11-13; 1 Samuel 4:18; 1 Samuel 7:5-9; 2 Chronicles 24:14; 2 Peter 1:21; Isaiah 6:8-9; Jeremiah 1:5; 1 Samuel 7:3-4; 1 Samuel 12:20-24; 1 Kings 18:18-21; Haggai 1:7-9; Matthew 2:4; Matthew 16:1; Matthew 21:23; Matthew 23:2; Matthew 26:59; Isaiah 28:7; Jeremiah 23:11; Jeremiah 23:13-17; Jeremiah 23:25-32; Malachi 2:1-2; Matthew 23:4-15; Mark 10:42-45; Acts 4:34-35; Acts 6:2-3; Acts 14:23; Titus 1:5; 1 Timothy 3:1-9; Titus 1:6-9; Acts 20:28; Acts 20:31; Acts 20:35; 1 Timothy 5:17-20; 1 Peter 5:2-3

## 7735: leaders, political (id: 7735)

Since God is the ultimate ruler of all things, earthly authority derives from him. Although political leadership was exercised in various ways in Israel, there was always some overlap between political and spiritual leadership.

Israel had a variety of political leaders
-----------------------------------------

### Judges

[JDG 2:16](https://ref.ly/Judg2:16), [JDG 4:4](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4)

### Kings

[1SA 8:1–8:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:1-1Sam8:21), [1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1), [1SA 16:1–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1-1Sam16:13); ; [2SA 7:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:12); ; [1KI 1:28–1:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:28-1Kgs1:40)

### Tribal elders and judges

[EXO 19:7](https://ref.ly/Exod19:7); ; [DEU 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Deut21:1-Deut21:2); ; [RUT 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:2); ; [JOB 29:7–29:12](https://ref.ly/Job29:7-Job29:12)

### Foreign powers imposed rulers on Israel

[LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1)

Examples of those who were both political and spiritual leaders
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 7:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:15)** *Samuel was both prophet and judge.* [EXO 34:1](https://ref.ly/Exod34:1), [EXO 34:27–34:28](https://ref.ly/Exod34:27-Exod34:28); [JDG 6:25–6:27](https://ref.ly/Judg6:25-Judg6:27) *Gideon*; ; [2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4) parallel [2CH 31:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:1) *Hezekiah*; ; [1CH 6:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:31) *David*

Political leaders, like spiritual ones, were called by God
----------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 11:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:31)** *The prophet Ahijah confers authority on the leader of a revolt.* *See also* [EXO 3:10](https://ref.ly/Exod3:10) *Moses*; [JDG 6:14](https://ref.ly/Judg6:14) *Gideon*[1SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1), [1SA 16:6–16:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:6-1Sam16:12)[ISA 45:1–45:2](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1-Isa45:2) *Cyrus was a Persian king, yet he is described as God's “anointed”.*

To reject God's leadership is to reject God
-------------------------------------------

**[1SA 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:6-1Sam8:8)** *See also* [1SA 8:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:5), [1SA 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:19-1Sam8:20) *Israel wanted a human ruler so that they might be like the surrounding nations.*

Responsibilities of leaders
---------------------------

### To protect the weak

[PSA 72:12–72:14](https://ref.ly/Ps72:12-Ps72:14)

### To administer justice

[PSA 72:2](https://ref.ly/Ps72:2); ; [PRO 20:8](https://ref.ly/Prov20:8), [PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4), [PRO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Prov29:14); ; [ISA 9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:7), [ISA 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/Isa11:3-Isa11:4)

### To shepherd the people

[PSA 78:70–78:72](https://ref.ly/Ps78:70-Ps78:72); ; [EZK 34:2–34:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:2-Ezek34:6)

Attributes required in leaders
------------------------------

**[ISA 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1-Isa11:2)** *Being anointed by the Spirit of God.* [DEU 17:18–17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18-Deut17:20); ; [ISA 11:3](https://ref.ly/Isa11:3); [ISA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:5) *trustworthiness*; ; [PRO 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Prov8:15-Prov8:16) *wisdom*

The failure of leaders
----------------------

### Through exploiting their subjects

**[MAT 20:25](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25)** *See also* [DEU 17:16–17:17](https://ref.ly/Deut17:16-Deut17:17); [1SA 8:11–8:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11-1Sam8:17); [1KI 12:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:4); [JER 22:13](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13)

### Through usurping religious authority

[1SA 13:8–13:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:8-1Sam13:13); ; [2CH 26:16–26:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16-2Chr26:18)

### Through apostasy

[1KI 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7-1Kgs11:8); ; [2KI 21:1–21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:1-2Kgs21:6)

### Through trusting in alliances with heathen powers

**[ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1)** *See also* [2KI 20:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:12), [2KI 20:17–20:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:17-2Kgs20:18); [ISA 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:2); [HOS 7:11](https://ref.ly/Hos7:11)

Godless leadership is evil
--------------------------

**[LUK 13:32](https://ref.ly/Luke13:32)** *See also* [DAN 7:1–7:7](https://ref.ly/Dan7:1-Dan7:7); [REV 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Rev13:1-Rev13:3)

People must have a right attitude to political leaders
------------------------------------------------------

### They are to be obeyed

**[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [ROM 13:5](https://ref.ly/Rom13:5); [1PE 2:13–2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:17)

### But obedience to God must come first

**[ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29)** *See also* [DAN 3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:18), [DAN 6:13](https://ref.ly/Dan6:13)

### Prayers are to be made for them

[1TI 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1-1Tim2:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 2:16; Judges 4:4; 1 Samuel 8:1-21; 1 Samuel 10:1; 1 Samuel 16:1-13; 2 Samuel 7:12; 1 Kings 1:28-40; Exodus 19:7; Deuteronomy 21:1-2; Ruth 4:1-2; Job 29:7-12; Luke 3:1; 1 Samuel 7:15; Exodus 34:1; Exodus 34:27-28; Judges 6:25-27; 2 Kings 18:4; 2 Chronicles 31:1; 1 Chronicles 6:31; 1 Kings 11:31; Exodus 3:10; Judges 6:14; 1 Samuel 16:1; 1 Samuel 16:6-12; Isaiah 45:1-2; 1 Samuel 8:6-8; 1 Samuel 8:5; 1 Samuel 8:19-20; Psalms 72:12-14; Psalms 72:2; Proverbs 20:8; Proverbs 29:4; Proverbs 29:14; Isaiah 9:7; Isaiah 11:3-4; Psalms 78:70-72; Ezekiel 34:2-6; Isaiah 11:1-2; Deuteronomy 17:18-20; Isaiah 11:3; Isaiah 11:5; Proverbs 8:15-16; Matthew 20:25; Deuteronomy 17:16-17; 1 Samuel 8:11-17; 1 Kings 12:4; Jeremiah 22:13; 1 Samuel 13:8-13; 2 Chronicles 26:16-18; 1 Kings 11:7-8; 2 Kings 21:1-6; Isaiah 31:1; 2 Kings 20:12; 2 Kings 20:17-18; Isaiah 30:1-2; Hosea 7:11; Luke 13:32; Daniel 7:1-7; Revelation 13:1-3; Romans 13:1; Romans 13:5; 1 Peter 2:13-17; Acts 5:29; Daniel 3:18; Daniel 6:13; 1 Timothy 2:1-2

## 7739: missionaries (id: 7739)

Those called and commissioned by God to fulfil specific tasks, especially that of taking the gospel to those who have not heard it. Missionaries may be sent to other towns, cities or nations, or may be called to fulfil their task closer to home.

* **Associated Articles:** 7740: missionaries, call of (ID: `7740@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7741: missionaries, task of (ID: `7741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7742: missionaries, support for (ID: `7742@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7740: missionaries, call of (id: 7740)

God calls and sends out his people into the world. All believers are called to share the good news of the gospel, though some are specially commissioned by God, confirmed by the church and compelled by the gospel message to missionary service.

The call to all God's people
----------------------------

**[PSA 96:2–96:3](https://ref.ly/Ps96:2-Ps96:3)** parallel [1CH 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:23-1Chr16:24)**[MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19)** *See also* [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15); [JHN 20:21](https://ref.ly/John20:21); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)

Missionaries in the OT
----------------------

### Those sent to their own people

**[ISA 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8-Isa6:10)** *See also* [2PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:5) *Noah*; [JER 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jer2:1-Jer2:4) *Jeremiah's ministry extended further but was primarily to his own people, Judah*; [EZK 2:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:3) *Ezekiel*

### Those sent to other peoples

**[2KI 17:27–17:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:27-2Kgs17:28)** *A priest from Samaria returns to teach the resettled population about the LORD.* [ISA 42:6](https://ref.ly/Isa42:6), [ISA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:6); ; [ACT 13:47](https://ref.ly/Acts13:47); [AMO 7:15](https://ref.ly/Amos7:15) *Amos, from Judah, is sent to the northern kingdom of Israel.*; [JON 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:2), [JON 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1-Jonah3:2)

Missionaries in the NT
----------------------

### John the Baptist

**[JHN 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/John1:6-John1:7)** *See also* [MAL 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Mal4:5-Mal4:6); [MAT 11:10](https://ref.ly/Matt11:10) parallel [LUK 7:27](https://ref.ly/Luke7:27); [MRK 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Mark1:2-Mark1:4); [MAL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Mal3:1); [ISA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3); [JHN 1:33](https://ref.ly/John1:33)

### Jesus Christ's disciples

**[LUK 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:6)** *Healing and deliverance accompanies preaching as part of the disciples' mission.* *See also* [MAT 10:5–10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:16) *During Jesus Christ's earthly ministry the disciples' mission was limited to Israel*; [MRK 6:7–6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7-Mark6:11); [LUK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Luke10:1)

### Other believers

**[ACT 11:19–11:20](https://ref.ly/Acts11:19-Acts11:20)** *See also* [ACT 8:5](https://ref.ly/Acts8:5) *Philip*; [ACT 18:24](https://ref.ly/Acts18:24) *Apollos*; [ACT 26:15–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:15-Acts26:18) *Paul*

Receiving the missionary call
-----------------------------

### The call given by God

**[JER 1:4–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jer1:4-Jer1:10)** *See also* [MAT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Matt4:19) *Peter and Andrew*; [ACT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26) *Philip*; [ACT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Acts22:21) *Paul*

### The call confirmed by the church

**[ACT 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:2-Acts13:3)** *See also* [ACT 14:26–14:27](https://ref.ly/Acts14:26-Acts14:27), [ACT 15:40](https://ref.ly/Acts15:40), [ACT 22:14–22:15](https://ref.ly/Acts22:14-Acts22:15) *Paul's call to be a missionary to the Gentiles is communicated through Ananias*; [GAL 2:7–2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7-Gal2:9) *Paul's mission to the Gentiles is recognised by the other apostles.*

### The call through circumstances

**[ACT 8:4](https://ref.ly/Acts8:4)** *Persecution drove the early church to wider mission.* *See also* [GEN 45:4–45:5](https://ref.ly/Gen45:4-Gen45:5) *Joseph sold into slavery in Egypt*; [DAN 1:3–1:6](https://ref.ly/Dan1:3-Dan1:6) *Daniel among those taken into exile in Babylon*; [ACT 18:1–18:2](https://ref.ly/Acts18:1-Acts18:2) *Priscilla and Aquila left Rome because of persecution.*

### Missionaries compelled by the message

**[ACT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:20)** *See also* [1CO 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:16); [2CO 5:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 96:2-3; 1 Chronicles 16:23-24; Matthew 28:19; Mark 16:15; John 20:21; Acts 1:8; Isaiah 6:8-10; 2 Peter 2:5; Jeremiah 2:1-4; Ezekiel 2:3; 2 Kings 17:27-28; Isaiah 42:6; Isaiah 49:6; Acts 13:47; Amos 7:15; Jonah 1:1-2; Jonah 3:1-2; John 1:6-7; Malachi 4:5-6; Matthew 11:10; Luke 7:27; Mark 1:2-4; Malachi 3:1; Isaiah 40:3; John 1:33; Luke 9:1-6; Matthew 10:5-16; Mark 6:7-11; Luke 10:1; Acts 11:19-20; Acts 8:5; Acts 18:24; Acts 26:15-18; Jeremiah 1:4-10; Matthew 4:19; Acts 8:26; Acts 22:21; Acts 13:2-3; Acts 14:26-27; Acts 15:40; Acts 22:14-15; Galatians 2:7-9; Acts 8:4; Genesis 45:4-5; Daniel 1:3-6; Acts 18:1-2; Acts 4:18-20; 1 Corinthians 9:16; 2 Corinthians 5:14
* **Associated Articles:** 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5112: Peter (ID: `5112@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5624: witnesses, to Jesus Christ (ID: `5624@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7724: evangelists (ID: `7724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7772: prophets (ID: `7772@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8495: witnessing (ID: `8495@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7741: missionaries, task of (id: 7741)

God commits various tasks to his servants. A principal task of those sent by God is to take the good news of the gospel to those who have not heard it, and so to be a means through which others come to faith.

The need for missionaries
-------------------------

**[ROM 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:14-Rom10:15)** *See also* [ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7); [MAT 9:37–9:38](https://ref.ly/Matt9:37-Matt9:38) parallel [LUK 10:2](https://ref.ly/Luke10:2)

The work of missionaries
------------------------

### Proclaiming good news

**[ISA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:2)** *See also* [LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19)**[LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43)** *See also* [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23), [MAT 9:35](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35)**[ACT 11:20](https://ref.ly/Acts11:20)** *See also* [ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42), [ACT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12), [ACT 8:25](https://ref.ly/Acts8:25), [ACT 14:6–14:7](https://ref.ly/Acts14:6-Acts14:7); [ROM 15:16](https://ref.ly/Rom15:16)

### Calling people to turn to God

**[ACT 26:20](https://ref.ly/Acts26:20)** *See also* [2CH 30:6–30:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:6-2Chr30:9); [MRK 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4) parallel [LUK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Luke3:3); [2CO 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:19-2Cor5:20)

### Announcing judgment

**[JON 3:4](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:4)** *See also* [JER 25:30–25:31](https://ref.ly/Jer25:30-Jer25:31), [JER 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/Jer26:12-Jer26:13); [EZK 21:2–21:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:2-Ezek21:3); [ACT 10:42](https://ref.ly/Acts10:42)

### Teaching and baptising new believers

**[MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20)** *See also* [ACT 2:41–2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:41-Acts2:42), [ACT 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12-Acts8:13); [1CO 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:17)

### Taking the message further afield

**[2CO 10:15–10:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:15-2Cor10:16)** *See also* [ROM 15:18–15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:18-Rom15:20), [ROM 15:28](https://ref.ly/Rom15:28); [MAT 24:14](https://ref.ly/Matt24:14); [LUK 24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:47); [ACT 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9-Acts16:10), [ACT 23:11](https://ref.ly/Acts23:11)

### Strengthening newly established churches

**[ACT 18:23](https://ref.ly/Acts18:23)** *See also* [ACT 8:14–8:17](https://ref.ly/Acts8:14-Acts8:17), [ACT 14:21–14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:21-Acts14:23), [ACT 15:36–15:41](https://ref.ly/Acts15:36-Acts15:41), [ACT 16:4–16:5](https://ref.ly/Acts16:4-Acts16:5)

Examples of other specific assignments
--------------------------------------

[EXO 3:10](https://ref.ly/Exod3:10) *Moses is sent by God to Pharoah*; ; [JOS 22:3](https://ref.ly/Josh22:3) *The eastern tribes complete their mission to help the others take possession of Canaan*; ; [JDG 6:14](https://ref.ly/Judg6:14) *Gideon is sent by God against the Midianites*; ; [1SA 15:18–15:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:18-1Sam15:20) *Saul is given the task of destroying the Amalekites*; ; [1SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1) *Samuel is sent by God to anoint David as king*; ; [ISA 48:15](https://ref.ly/Isa48:15) *Cyrus is appointed to bring deliverance from Babylon.*; [ACT 11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:30), [ACT 12:25](https://ref.ly/Acts12:25); [EPH 6:22](https://ref.ly/Eph6:22); ; [COL 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Col4:7-Col4:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 10:14-15; Isaiah 52:7; Matthew 9:37-38; Luke 10:2; Isaiah 61:1-2; Luke 4:18-19; Luke 4:43; Matthew 4:23; Matthew 9:35; Acts 11:20; Acts 5:42; Acts 8:12; Acts 8:25; Acts 14:6-7; Romans 15:16; Acts 26:20; 2 Chronicles 30:6-9; Mark 1:4; Luke 3:3; 2 Corinthians 5:19-20; Jonah 3:4; Jeremiah 25:30-31; Jeremiah 26:12-13; Ezekiel 21:2-3; Acts 10:42; Matthew 28:19-20; Acts 2:41-42; Acts 8:12-13; 1 Corinthians 4:17; 2 Corinthians 10:15-16; Romans 15:18-20; Romans 15:28; Matthew 24:14; Luke 24:47; Acts 16:9-10; Acts 23:11; Acts 18:23; Acts 8:14-17; Acts 14:21-23; Acts 15:36-41; Acts 16:4-5; Exodus 3:10; Joshua 22:3; Judges 6:14; 1 Samuel 15:18-20; 1 Samuel 16:1; Isaiah 48:15; Acts 11:30; Acts 12:25; Ephesians 6:22; Colossians 4:7-8
* **Associated Articles:** 2420: gospel (ID: `2420@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God (ID: `6717@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7027: church, purpose and mission of (ID: `7027@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7754: preaching (ID: `7754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7924: fellowship, in Christian service (ID: `7924@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7948: mission (ID: `7948@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8413: edification (ID: `8413@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8424: evangelism (ID: `8424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7742: missionaries, support for (id: 7742)

Though facing hardships, missionaries receive strength from God to enable them to carry out their task. They are entitled, too, to receive support, encouragement and financial assistance from other believers.

Missionaries face hardships
---------------------------

### Physical and emotional stress

**[MAT 10:17–10:23](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17-Matt10:23)** parallels [MRK 13:11–13:13](https://ref.ly/Mark13:11-Mark13:13), [LUK 21:12–21:19](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12-Luke21:19)**[2CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:5)** *See also* [ROM 8:35–8:36](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:36); [2CO 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:11-2Cor4:13), [2CO 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:3-2Cor6:5), [2CO 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:8-2Cor6:10), [2CO 11:23–11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:29); [PHP 4:12](https://ref.ly/Phil4:12)

### Opposition to the message

**[1CO 16:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:9)** *See also* [ACT 4:17–4:18](https://ref.ly/Acts4:17-Acts4:18), [ACT 6:9](https://ref.ly/Acts6:9), [ACT 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6-Acts13:8); [1TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2)

Missionaries strengthened by God
--------------------------------

### The Holy Spirit gives power to witness

**[JHN 20:21–20:22](https://ref.ly/John20:21-John20:22)** *See also* [LUK 24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:49); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8); [ROM 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:18-Rom15:19); [1CO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4-1Cor2:5)

### Jesus Christ promises his presence

**[MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20)** *See also* [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20); [ACT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:10)

Missionaries supported by the church
------------------------------------

### Missionaries supported by the sending church

**[ACT 14:26](https://ref.ly/Acts14:26)** *See also* [ACT 13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:3), [ACT 15:40](https://ref.ly/Acts15:40)

### Missionaries supported by believers

**[ACT 18:27](https://ref.ly/Acts18:27)** *See also* [ROM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24); [2CO 8:23–8:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:23-2Cor8:24); [TIT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Titus3:13)

### Missionary work supported by church leaders

**[ACT 8:14](https://ref.ly/Acts8:14)** *See also* [ACT 11:22–11:23](https://ref.ly/Acts11:22-Acts11:23), [ACT 15:30–15:32](https://ref.ly/Acts15:30-Acts15:32); [GAL 2:7–2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7-Gal2:9)

### Missionaries given financial support

**[PHP 4:14–4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14-Phil4:18)** *See also* [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25); [1CO 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:11-1Cor9:14), [1CO 16:5–16:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:5-1Cor16:6); [GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6); [1TH 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6-1Thess2:8)

### Missionaries supported in prayer

**[COL 4:3](https://ref.ly/Col4:3)** *See also* [EPH 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Eph6:19-Eph6:20); [PHP 1:19](https://ref.ly/Phil1:19); [1TH 5:25](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:25); [2TH 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:1)

### Missionaries encouraged by visits and news

**[ACT 28:15](https://ref.ly/Acts28:15)** *See also* [PHP 2:19](https://ref.ly/Phil2:19); [2TI 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:9-2Tim4:11); [TIT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Titus3:12); [PHM 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:12-Phlm1:13)
