## 7745: ordination (id: 7745)

The appointment to a public ministry or office, including investing with the authority required for this ministry. The most important biblical model of ordination is provided by the OT priesthood.

Ordination of the OT priesthood
-------------------------------

### The ceremony of ordination

**[EXO 28:41](https://ref.ly/Exod28:41)** *See also* [EXO 29:29–29:46](https://ref.ly/Exod29:29-Exod29:46), [EXO 30:30](https://ref.ly/Exod30:30); [LEV 8:30](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30), [LEV 8:33](https://ref.ly/Lev8:33); [NUM 3:3](https://ref.ly/Num3:3)

### The role of the ordained priest

To properly apply the blood of the ordination offering: [EXO 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/Exod29:20-Exod29:21); ; [LEV 7:37](https://ref.ly/Lev7:37), [LEV 8:18](https://ref.ly/Lev8:18); [LEV 16:32](https://ref.ly/Lev16:32) *to make atonement*; ; [LEV 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:12) *to observe a strict code of behaviour*

Jesus is the Messiah, the anointed one
--------------------------------------

[MAT 16:16](https://ref.ly/Matt16:16) *The Greek word “Christos”, like the Hebrew word “Messiah”, means “anointed (by God)”.*

Ordination in the NT
--------------------

### Jesus Christ appointed disciples without reference to a ceremony of ordination

[MRK 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:15) parallel [LUK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Luke6:13)

### The apostles commissioned people for service by laying on of hands

**[ACT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:6)** *See also* [NUM 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Num8:10-Num8:11), [NUM 27:18–27:23](https://ref.ly/Num27:18-Num27:23); [ACT 13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:3) *Barnabas and Saul were commissioned for missionary work.*

### The appointment of elders

[ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23); ; [1TI 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:18), [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14), [1TI 5:22](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:22); ; [2TI 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:6); ; [TIT 1:5](https://ref.ly/Titus1:5), [TIT 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6-Titus1:9) *Certain qualities are required in an appointed elder or minister.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 28:41; Exodus 29:29-46; Exodus 30:30; Leviticus 8:30; Leviticus 8:33; Numbers 3:3; Exodus 29:20-21; Leviticus 7:37; Leviticus 8:18; Leviticus 16:32; Leviticus 21:10-12; Matthew 16:16; Mark 3:14-15; Luke 6:13; Acts 6:6; Numbers 8:10-11; Numbers 27:18-23; Acts 13:3; Acts 14:23; 1 Timothy 1:18; 1 Timothy 4:14; 1 Timothy 5:22; 2 Timothy 1:6; Titus 1:5; Titus 1:6-9
* **Associated Articles:** 2206: Jesus, the Christ (ID: `2206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7304: anointing (ID: `7304@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7372: hands, laying on of (ID: `7372@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7412: priesthood (ID: `7412@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7706: apostles (ID: `7706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7715: deacons (ID: `7715@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7718: elders (ID: `7718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7734: leaders, spiritual (ID: `7734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7767: priests, institution in OT times (ID: `7767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7943: ministry, in the church (ID: `7943@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7748: overseers (id: 7748)

In the OT a variety of supervisory roles are mentioned. In the NT “overseers” (traditionally translated “bishops”) describes the role of local church leaders.

Overseers in the OT
-------------------

### In secular life

[EXO 5:6](https://ref.ly/Exod5:6); ; [RUT 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:5-Ruth2:6); ; [1KI 5:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:16); ; [2CH 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:2); ; [NEH 3:5](https://ref.ly/Neh3:5); ; [PRO 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:7)

### In religious life

[NUM 8:22](https://ref.ly/Num8:22); ; [1CH 25:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:2); ; [2CH 23:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:18); ; [EZR 3:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:9); ; [NEH 11:11](https://ref.ly/Neh11:11); ; [JER 20:1](https://ref.ly/Jer20:1)

Overseers in the NT
-------------------

### In secular life

[MAT 20:8](https://ref.ly/Matt20:8)

### In the church

**[PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1)** *A title for local church leaders.*

### The role of local church \`\`elders''

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *Here, as elsewhere in the NT, “elders” and “overseers” are virtually synonymous.* *See also* [1PE 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:4)

### Their qualifications

**[1TI 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1-1Tim3:7)** *See also* [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7)

### A title of Jesus Christ

**[1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:25)**

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 5:6; Ruth 2:5-6; 1 Kings 5:16; 2 Chronicles 2:2; Nehemiah 3:5; Proverbs 6:6-7; Numbers 8:22; 1 Chronicles 25:2; 2 Chronicles 23:18; Ezra 3:9; Nehemiah 11:11; Jeremiah 20:1; Matthew 20:8; Philippians 1:1; Acts 20:28; 1 Peter 5:1-4; 1 Timothy 3:1-7; Titus 1:7; 1 Peter 2:25
* **Associated Articles:** 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of (ID: `2203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7026: church, leadership of (ID: `7026@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7715: deacons (ID: `7715@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7720: elders, in the church (ID: `7720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7734: leaders, spiritual (ID: `7734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7789: shepherd, as church leader (ID: `7789@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7939: ministry (ID: `7939@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8422: equipping, spiritual (ID: `8422@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7751: persuasion (id: 7751)

Attempting to win others over to one's own point of view. It can be either positive, as with preaching the gospel, or it can spring from a malign intent to seduce people from the truth.

Means of persuasion
-------------------

### The persuasiveness of argument

**[ACT 16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:15)** *See also* [JDG 19:3](https://ref.ly/Judg19:3), [JDG 19:7](https://ref.ly/Judg19:7); [2SA 3:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:35); [ACT 5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:40), [ACT 19:32–19:41](https://ref.ly/Acts19:32-Acts19:41), [ACT 21:14](https://ref.ly/Acts21:14)

### The persuasiveness of proof

**[ACT 1:3](https://ref.ly/Acts1:3)** *See also* [GEN 45:25–45:28](https://ref.ly/Gen45:25-Gen45:28)

### The persuasiveness of experience

**[1CO 14:24–14:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:24-1Cor14:25)**

The first Christians were persuaded of the truth of their faith
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:12)** *See also* [ROM 4:20–4:21](https://ref.ly/Rom4:20-Rom4:21), [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39); [2TI 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:14)

The apostles tried to persuade others of the truth of the gospel
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4)** *See also* [ACT 17:2–17:4](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2-Acts17:4), [ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8), [ACT 26:26–26:29](https://ref.ly/Acts26:26-Acts26:29), [ACT 28:23–28:24](https://ref.ly/Acts28:23-Acts28:24); [2CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:11)

Scripture advises patience when seeking to persuade those in power
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 25:15](https://ref.ly/Prov25:15)** *See also* [LUK 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/Luke18:1-Luke18:5)

Scripture warns against the persuasiveness of evil
--------------------------------------------------

### The persuasiveness of adulterers

**[PRO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Prov7:21)** *See also* [PRO 5:3](https://ref.ly/Prov5:3), [PRO 7:5](https://ref.ly/Prov7:5)

### The persuasiveness of false prophets

**[JER 28:15](https://ref.ly/Jer28:15)**

Examples of God's servants suffering through the persuasiveness of others
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 27:20](https://ref.ly/Matt27:20)** parallel [MRK 15:11](https://ref.ly/Mark15:11) *See also* [ACT 14:19](https://ref.ly/Acts14:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 16:15; Judges 19:3; Judges 19:7; 2 Samuel 3:35; Acts 5:40; Acts 19:32-41; Acts 21:14; Acts 1:3; Genesis 45:25-28; 1 Corinthians 14:24-25; 2 Timothy 1:12; Romans 4:20-21; Romans 8:38-39; 2 Timothy 3:14; Acts 18:4; Acts 17:2-4; Acts 19:8; Acts 26:26-29; Acts 28:23-24; 2 Corinthians 5:11; Proverbs 25:15; Luke 18:1-5; Proverbs 7:21; Proverbs 5:3; Proverbs 7:5; Jeremiah 28:15; Matthew 27:20; Mark 15:11; Acts 14:19
* **Associated Articles:** 5472: proof, as evidence (ID: `5472@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5842: eloquence (ID: `5842@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6146: deceit, and God's nature (ID: `6146@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6241: seduction (ID: `6241@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7703: apologetics (ID: `7703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7712: convincing (ID: `7712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7774: prophets, false (ID: `7774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8318: patience (ID: `8318@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8459: perseverance (ID: `8459@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8495: witnessing (ID: `8495@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8749: false teachers (ID: `8749@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7754: preaching (id: 7754)

The announcing of the good news of God by his servants through the faithful revelation of God's will, the exposition of God's word and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, the Son of God. Jesus Christ had an important preaching ministry.

* **Associated Articles:** 7755: preaching, importance of (ID: `7755@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7756: preaching, content of (ID: `7756@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7757: preaching, effects of (ID: `7757@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7758: preachers, call of (ID: `7758@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7759: preachers, qualifications for (ID: `7759@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7760: preachers, responsibilities of (ID: `7760@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7761: preaching, and Holy Spirit (ID: `7761@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7762: preaching, of Jesus Christ (ID: `7762@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7755: preaching, importance of (id: 7755)

Preaching has a central place among God's people and is vital to their life and growth. It is authorised by God, empowered by the Holy Spirit and expressed supremely by Jesus Christ.

Preaching has its origin in God
-------------------------------

### It is a divine command

**[MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15)** parallel [MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20) *See also* [JON 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:2); [MAT 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:7) parallels [MRK 6:7–6:12](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7-Mark6:12), [LUK 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:6)

### It is rooted in God's grace

**[EPH 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7-Eph3:9)** *See also* [ISA 6:1–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1-Isa6:10); [ROM 15:15–15:16](https://ref.ly/Rom15:15-Rom15:16)

### It is empowered by God's Spirit

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)** *See also* [ISA 61:1–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:3); [LUK 24:46–24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46-Luke24:49); [ACT 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:11), [ACT 4:8–4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:8-Acts4:12), [ACT 10:44](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44); [1CO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4-1Cor2:5)

The importance of preaching and Jesus Christ
--------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ himself came to preach

**[MRK 1:38](https://ref.ly/Mark1:38)** parallel [LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43) *See also* [EPH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Eph2:17)

### Jesus Christ's own ministry involved much preaching

**[MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23)** *See also* [MAT 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:1-Matt11:5) parallel [LUK 7:18–7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:18-Luke7:22)

### Jesus Christ commissioned his disciples to preach

**[MRK 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:15)** *See also* [MAT 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:7) parallel [LUK 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:2)

The importance of preaching and the church
------------------------------------------

### It is a natural part of the church's life

**[ACT 8:4](https://ref.ly/Acts8:4)** *See also* [ACT 3:11–3:26](https://ref.ly/Acts3:11-Acts3:26), [ACT 15:35](https://ref.ly/Acts15:35)

### It is a trust from God

**[GAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7)** *See also* [1TH 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:4); [1TI 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:11); [TIT 1:3](https://ref.ly/Titus1:3)

### It is an integral aspect of key ministries in the church

**[1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2)** *See also* [EPH 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:12); [2TI 4:2–4:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2-2Tim4:5); [TIT 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7-Titus1:9)

### Its importance to Paul

**[1CO 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17-1Cor1:18)** *See also* [ACT 9:20–9:22](https://ref.ly/Acts9:20-Acts9:22), [ACT 18:5](https://ref.ly/Acts18:5); [ROM 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:14-Rom1:15); [1CO 1:22–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22-1Cor1:25)

### It is an apostolic command

**[1TI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:13)** *See also* [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)

The importance of preaching for salvation
-----------------------------------------

**[ROM 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:14-Rom10:15)** *See also* [ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7); [ROM 10:17](https://ref.ly/Rom10:17); [1CO 1:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:21)

The importance of preaching means preachers and teachers will be judged more strictly
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JAS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1)** *See also* [ROM 2:17–2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:17-Rom2:24)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 16:15; Matthew 28:18-20; Jonah 1:1-2; Matthew 10:5-7; Mark 6:7-12; Luke 9:1-6; Ephesians 3:7-9; Isaiah 6:1-10; Romans 15:15-16; Acts 1:8; Isaiah 61:1-3; Luke 24:46-49; Acts 2:1-11; Acts 4:8-12; Acts 10:44; 1 Corinthians 2:4-5; Mark 1:38; Luke 4:43; Ephesians 2:17; Matthew 4:23; Matthew 11:1-5; Luke 7:18-22; Mark 3:14-15; Luke 9:1-2; Acts 8:4; Acts 3:11-26; Acts 15:35; Galatians 2:7; 1 Thessalonians 2:4; 1 Timothy 1:11; Titus 1:3; 1 Timothy 3:2; Ephesians 4:11-12; 2 Timothy 4:2-5; Titus 1:7-9; 1 Corinthians 1:17-18; Acts 9:20-22; Acts 18:5; Romans 1:14-15; 1 Corinthians 1:22-25; 1 Timothy 4:13; 2 Timothy 4:2; Romans 10:14-15; Isaiah 52:7; Romans 10:17; 1 Corinthians 1:21; James 3:1; Romans 2:17-24
* **Associated Articles:** 1427: prophecy (ID: `1427@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1610: Scripture (ID: `1610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1660: Sermon on the Mount (ID: `1660@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of (ID: `2363@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5622: witnesses (ID: `5622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7703: apologetics (ID: `7703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7772: prophets (ID: `7772@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7793: teachers (ID: `7793@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8495: witnessing (ID: `8495@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7756: preaching, content of (id: 7756)

Preaching is centred on the nature and will of God and his claims on all people. Expressed in prophecy, declaration or teaching, it includes the proclamation of the way of salvation to unbelievers and instruction about the faith to believers.

Preaching and the revelation of God's character, word and will
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 20:26–20:27](https://ref.ly/Acts20:26-Acts20:27)** *See also* [EXO 8:1](https://ref.ly/Exod8:1); [1KI 12:21–12:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:21-1Kgs12:24); [JER 7:1–7:11](https://ref.ly/Jer7:1-Jer7:11); [EZK 2:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:3-Ezek3:4)

Preaching and the declaration of the gospel
-------------------------------------------

### Declaring the kingdom

**[MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15)** parallel [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17) *See also* [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23) parallel [LUK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1); [ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8), [ACT 20:25](https://ref.ly/Acts20:25)

### Declaring the person of Jesus Christ and his life

**[ACT 2:22](https://ref.ly/Acts2:22)** *See also* [ACT 10:36–10:38](https://ref.ly/Acts10:36-Acts10:38), [ACT 28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:31); [2CO 1:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:19)

### Declaring the facts of the cross and the resurrection

**[ACT 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23-Acts2:24)** *See also* [ACT 5:30](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30), [ACT 10:39–10:42](https://ref.ly/Acts10:39-Acts10:42), [ACT 13:28–13:31](https://ref.ly/Acts13:28-Acts13:31); [1CO 1:22–1:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22-1Cor1:24), [1CO 15:12–15:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:12-1Cor15:17)

### Declaring the victory and exaltation of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 2:33–2:35](https://ref.ly/Acts2:33-Acts2:35)** *See also* [ACT 5:31](https://ref.ly/Acts5:31); [1PE 3:18–3:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:18-1Pet3:22)

### Declaring that Jesus is both Messiah (Christ) and Lord

**[ACT 2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:36)** *See also* [ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42), [ACT 8:5](https://ref.ly/Acts8:5), [ACT 9:20–9:22](https://ref.ly/Acts9:20-Acts9:22), [ACT 10:36](https://ref.ly/Acts10:36), [ACT 18:5](https://ref.ly/Acts18:5)

### Declaring the call to repent

**[ACT 17:30](https://ref.ly/Acts17:30)** *See also* [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15) parallel [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17); [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 3:19](https://ref.ly/Acts3:19), [ACT 26:20](https://ref.ly/Acts26:20)

### Declaring the promise of forgiveness

**[ACT 13:38](https://ref.ly/Acts13:38)** *See also* [LUK 24:46–24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46-Luke24:47); [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 5:31](https://ref.ly/Acts5:31), [ACT 10:43](https://ref.ly/Acts10:43)

Preaching finds expression in the teaching of believers
-------------------------------------------------------

### The central place of teaching in the lives of the first Christians

**[ACT 2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:42)** *See also* [ACT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2), [ACT 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/Acts11:25-Acts11:26), [ACT 15:35](https://ref.ly/Acts15:35), [ACT 18:24–18:26](https://ref.ly/Acts18:24-Acts18:26), [ACT 20:20](https://ref.ly/Acts20:20)

### Teaching from the Scriptures

**[ACT 18:11](https://ref.ly/Acts18:11)** *See also* [2CH 17:7–17:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:7-2Chr17:9); [NEH 8:2–8:8](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2-Neh8:8)

### Teaching on how to live

**[MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20)** *See also* [EPH 4:20–4:24](https://ref.ly/Eph4:20-Eph4:24); [1TH 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:1-1Thess4:2); [TIT 2:1–2:15](https://ref.ly/Titus2:1-Titus2:15)

Preaching and the edification of believers
------------------------------------------

**[2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)** *See also* [ACT 13:42–13:43](https://ref.ly/Acts13:42-Acts13:43), [ACT 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Acts14:21-Acts14:22), [ACT 20:2](https://ref.ly/Acts20:2); [1CO 14:26–14:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26-1Cor14:31)

Preaching and the continuation of apostolic doctrine
----------------------------------------------------

### Preaching should be rooted in apostolic doctrine, which is to be faithfully handed on

**[2TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:2)** *See also* [1CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:2); [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15); [2TI 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:13-2Tim1:14)

### Preaching that does not conform to apostolic doctrine is to be rejected

**[1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4)** *See also* [GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9); [1TI 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:7); [TIT 1:9–1:14](https://ref.ly/Titus1:9-Titus1:14)

Preaching and the rejection of merely human wisdom
--------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:1-1Cor2:5)** *See also* [1CO 1:18–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18-1Cor1:25)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 20:26-27; Exodus 8:1; 1 Kings 12:21-24; Jeremiah 7:1-11; Ezekiel 2:3-3:4; Mark 1:14-15; Matthew 4:17; Matthew 4:23; Luke 8:1; Acts 19:8; Acts 20:25; Acts 2:22; Acts 10:36-38; Acts 28:31; 2 Corinthians 1:19; Acts 2:23-24; Acts 5:30; Acts 10:39-42; Acts 13:28-31; 1 Corinthians 1:22-24; 1 Corinthians 15:12-17; Acts 2:33-35; Acts 5:31; 1 Peter 3:18-22; Acts 2:36; Acts 5:42; Acts 8:5; Acts 9:20-22; Acts 10:36; Acts 18:5; Acts 17:30; Mark 1:15; Acts 2:38; Acts 3:19; Acts 26:20; Acts 13:38; Luke 24:46-47; Acts 10:43; Acts 2:42; Acts 6:2; Acts 11:25-26; Acts 15:35; Acts 18:24-26; Acts 20:20; Acts 18:11; 2 Chronicles 17:7-9; Nehemiah 8:2-8; Matthew 28:19-20; Ephesians 4:20-24; 1 Thessalonians 4:1-2; Titus 2:1-15; 2 Timothy 4:2; Acts 13:42-43; Acts 14:21-22; Acts 20:2; 1 Corinthians 14:26-31; 2 Timothy 2:2; 1 Corinthians 11:2; 2 Thessalonians 2:15; 2 Timothy 1:13-14; 1 Timothy 1:3-4; Galatians 1:6-9; 1 Timothy 4:1-7; Titus 1:9-14; 1 Corinthians 2:1-5; 1 Corinthians 1:18-25
* **Associated Articles:** 1610: Scripture (ID: `1610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2357: Jesus Christ, parables of (ID: `2357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2427: gospel, transmission of (ID: `2427@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2560: Jesus Christ, resurrection of (ID: `2560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3224: Holy Spirit, and preaching (ID: `3224@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7730: explanation (ID: `7730@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8234: doctrine (ID: `8234@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8424: evangelism (ID: `8424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8750: false teachings (ID: `8750@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7757: preaching, effects of (id: 7757)

Faithful preaching leads to, through the grace of God, the repentance of sinners, the birth of faith and the nourishment of believers. It can also evoke hostility from unbelievers.

The basis of effective preaching
--------------------------------

### It depends upon the grace of God

**[ACT 4:33](https://ref.ly/Acts4:33)** *See also* [ISA 55:10–55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10-Isa55:11); [ACT 14:26–14:27](https://ref.ly/Acts14:26-Acts14:27); [1CO 15:10–15:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:10-1Cor15:11)

### It depends upon the power of the cross, not human wisdom

**[1CO 1:17–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17-1Cor1:25)** *See also* [1CO 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:1-1Cor2:5)

### It depends upon the power of the Holy Spirit

**[LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19)** *See also* [ISA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:2); [ACT 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:11), [ACT 10:44–10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:48); [1CO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4-1Cor2:5)

### It requires effective, supporting prayer

**[COL 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Col4:3-Col4:4)** *See also* [ACT 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29-Acts4:31), [ACT 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:4), [ACT 13:1–13:5](https://ref.ly/Acts13:1-Acts13:5); [2TH 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:1)

### It needs to be received with faith

**[HEB 4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:2)** *See also* [1TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:13)

Preaching and the repentance of sinners
---------------------------------------

**[MAT 12:41](https://ref.ly/Matt12:41)** parallel [LUK 11:32](https://ref.ly/Luke11:32) *See also* [JON 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1-Jonah3:10); [MAT 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:1-Matt3:6) parallels [MRK 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/Mark1:3-Mark1:5), [LUK 3:3–3:6](https://ref.ly/Luke3:3-Luke3:6)

Preaching and the birth of faith
--------------------------------

**[ACT 4:4](https://ref.ly/Acts4:4)** *See also* [ACT 2:38–2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38-Acts2:41), [ACT 8:9–8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9-Acts8:13), [ACT 17:11–17:12](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11-Acts17:12); [1CO 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:1-1Cor15:2)

Preaching and the nourishment of believers
------------------------------------------

**[ACT 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Acts14:21-Acts14:22)** *See also* [ROM 16:25–16:27](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25-Rom16:27); [EPH 4:11–4:16](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:16); [COL 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:6-Col2:7)

Effective preaching and its authentication by miracles
------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20)** *See also* [MAT 4:23–4:25](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23-Matt4:25); [LUK 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:6) parallels [MAT 10:5–10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:14), [MRK 6:7–6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7-Mark6:11); [ACT 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29-Acts4:30), [ACT 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Acts8:5-Acts8:8), [ACT 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1-Acts14:3)

Inappropriate responses to preaching
------------------------------------

### Amazement

**[MAT 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28-Matt7:29)** *See also* [LUK 4:31–4:32](https://ref.ly/Luke4:31-Luke4:32) parallel [MRK 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:21-Mark1:22); [ACT 2:5–2:12](https://ref.ly/Acts2:5-Acts2:12), [ACT 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13-Acts4:14)

### Offence

**[MRK 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:1-Mark6:6)** parallel [MAT 13:54–13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54-Matt13:58) *See also* [1CO 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22-1Cor1:23)

### Mere academic interest

[ACT 17:16–17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16-Acts17:32) *Paul in Athens and before the Areopagus*; ; [ACT 24:22–24:26](https://ref.ly/Acts24:22-Acts24:26) *Paul before Felix and Drusilla*

### Mockery

**[ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32)** *See also* [2CH 36:15–36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:16); [ACT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Acts2:13)

### Hostility

**[ACT 17:13](https://ref.ly/Acts17:13)** *See also* [ACT 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Acts4:1-Acts4:3), [ACT 5:27–5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:27-Acts5:40), [ACT 7:54–7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54-Acts7:60), [ACT 13:49–13:51](https://ref.ly/Acts13:49-Acts13:51), [ACT 17:5–17:9](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5-Acts17:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 4:33; Isaiah 55:10-11; Acts 14:26-27; 1 Corinthians 15:10-11; 1 Corinthians 1:17-25; 1 Corinthians 2:1-5; Luke 4:18-19; Isaiah 61:1-2; Acts 2:1-11; Acts 10:44-48; 1 Corinthians 2:4-5; Colossians 4:3-4; Acts 4:29-31; Acts 6:2-4; Acts 13:1-5; 2 Thessalonians 3:1; Hebrews 4:2; 1 Thessalonians 2:13; Matthew 12:41; Luke 11:32; Jonah 3:1-10; Matthew 3:1-6; Mark 1:3-5; Luke 3:3-6; Acts 4:4; Acts 2:38-41; Acts 8:9-13; Acts 17:11-12; 1 Corinthians 15:1-2; Acts 14:21-22; Romans 16:25-27; Ephesians 4:11-16; Colossians 2:6-7; Mark 16:20; Matthew 4:23-25; Luke 9:1-6; Matthew 10:5-14; Mark 6:7-11; Acts 4:29-30; Acts 8:5-8; Acts 14:1-3; Matthew 7:28-29; Luke 4:31-32; Mark 1:21-22; Acts 2:5-12; Acts 4:13-14; Mark 6:1-6; Matthew 13:54-58; 1 Corinthians 1:22-23; Acts 17:16-32; Acts 24:22-26; Acts 17:32; 2 Chronicles 36:15-16; Acts 2:13; Acts 17:13; Acts 4:1-3; Acts 5:27-40; Acts 7:54-60; Acts 13:49-51; Acts 17:5-9

## 7758: preachers, call of (id: 7758)

God declares his word through called and anointed preachers. He overcomes their reluctance, helps their weakness, authorises their message and confirms the truth of what they declare.

God's call of preachers
-----------------------

### It is not dependent upon background

**[AMO 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Amos7:14-Amos7:15)** *See also* [JER 1:1–1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:1-Jer1:5); [MAT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18-Matt4:20) parallels [MRK 1:16–1:19](https://ref.ly/Mark1:16-Mark1:19), [LUK 5:1–5:11](https://ref.ly/Luke5:1-Luke5:11); [ACT 9:10–9:16](https://ref.ly/Acts9:10-Acts9:16)

### It is not dependent upon ability

**[EXO 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10-Exod4:12)** *See also* [JER 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6-Jer1:9); [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13)

### It is not dependent upon willingness

**[EXO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Exod4:13)** *See also* [JON 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:3); [ACT 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Acts9:1-Acts9:6)

### It is a matter of God's sovereign choice

**[JER 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:4-Jer1:5)** *See also* [ISA 49:1–49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:1-Isa49:6); [EZK 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:1-Ezek2:5); [AMO 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Amos7:14-Amos7:15); [LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19); [ISA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:2); [GAL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Gal1:15)

God's commissioning of preachers
--------------------------------

### They are commissioned to declare God's word

**[EZK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:1)**; **[ROM 10:13–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:13-Rom10:15)** *See also* [JER 1:9](https://ref.ly/Jer1:9); [JON 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1-Jonah3:2); [MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20)

### They are to declare God's word with authority

**[TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Titus2:15)** *See also* [JER 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jer1:9-Jer1:10); [MRK 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:15); [LUK 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:2)

### They are to declare God's word without fear

**[JER 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Jer1:7-Jer1:8)** *See also* [EZK 2:3–2:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:3-Ezek2:7), [EZK 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:7-Ezek3:9); [ACT 5:27–5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:27-Acts5:29); [2TI 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:6-2Tim1:8)

God's constraint upon preachers
-------------------------------

**[1CO 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:16)** *See also* [JER 20:8–20:9](https://ref.ly/Jer20:8-Jer20:9); [ACT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:20); [ROM 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:14-Rom1:15)

God's confirmation of his call upon preachers
---------------------------------------------

**[ACT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:3)** *See also* [EXO 4:1–4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:1-Exod4:9); [1KI 17:17–17:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:17-1Kgs17:24); [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20); [ACT 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29-Acts4:31); [1CO 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Amos 7:14-15; Jeremiah 1:1-5; Matthew 4:18-20; Mark 1:16-19; Luke 5:1-11; Acts 9:10-16; Exodus 4:10-12; Jeremiah 1:6-9; Acts 4:13; Exodus 4:13; Jonah 1:1-3; Acts 9:1-6; Jeremiah 1:4-5; Isaiah 49:1-6; Ezekiel 2:1-5; Luke 4:18-19; Isaiah 61:1-2; Galatians 1:15; Ezekiel 3:1; Romans 10:13-15; Jeremiah 1:9; Jonah 3:1-2; Matthew 28:18-20; Titus 2:15; Jeremiah 1:9-10; Mark 3:14-15; Luke 9:1-2; Jeremiah 1:7-8; Ezekiel 2:3-7; Ezekiel 3:7-9; Acts 5:27-29; 2 Timothy 1:6-8; 1 Corinthians 9:16; Jeremiah 20:8-9; Acts 4:18-20; Romans 1:14-15; Acts 14:3; Exodus 4:1-9; 1 Kings 17:17-24; Mark 16:20; Acts 4:29-31; 1 Corinthians 2:4
* **Associated Articles:** 1690: word of God (ID: `1690@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5107: Paul (ID: `5107@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5115: Peter, preacher and teacher (ID: `5115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5335: herald (ID: `5335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6666: grace (ID: `6666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7620: disciples (ID: `7620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7724: evangelists (ID: `7724@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7759: preachers, qualifications for (id: 7759)

Those entrusted with the task of preaching must ensure that their lives are in line with their message. They must be of good character and conduct, be consistent in all they teach and do and be accountable to others.

Preachers must be of good character
-----------------------------------

**[1TI 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:12)** *See also* [1TH 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9-1Thess2:10); [1TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:16); [2TI 2:20–2:26](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:20-2Tim2:26); [TIT 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Titus2:7-Titus2:8); [JAS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1) *That the judgment is based on character, and not just on teaching, seems clear from the references to guarding the tongue which follow.*

Preachers must practise what they preach
----------------------------------------

**[MAT 23:2–23:4](https://ref.ly/Matt23:2-Matt23:4)** *See also* [ROM 2:21–2:23](https://ref.ly/Rom2:21-Rom2:23); [GAL 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:14)

Preachers must be accountable to others and to God
--------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 6:30](https://ref.ly/Mark6:30)** parallel [LUK 9:10](https://ref.ly/Luke9:10) *See also* [ACT 14:26–14:27](https://ref.ly/Acts14:26-Acts14:27), [ACT 21:17–21:19](https://ref.ly/Acts21:17-Acts21:19); [GAL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:1-Gal2:2)

Preachers must not look for honour for themselves
-------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40)** parallels [MAT 23:5–23:12](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5-Matt23:12), [LUK 20:45–20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:47) *See also* [ACT 14:11–14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:15); [1CO 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:5-1Cor3:6); [1TH 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:3-1Thess2:6)

Preachers must not look for personal gain
-----------------------------------------

**[2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17)** *See also* [ACT 20:33–20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:33-Acts20:35); [2CO 11:7–11:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:7-2Cor11:9); [1TH 2:6–2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6-1Thess2:9)

Preachers must be people of integrity
-------------------------------------

**[2CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:2)** *See also* [2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:12); [1TH 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:3-1Thess2:6)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Timothy 4:12; 1 Thessalonians 2:9-10; 1 Timothy 4:16; 2 Timothy 2:20-26; Titus 2:7-8; James 3:1; Matthew 23:2-4; Romans 2:21-23; Galatians 2:11-14; Mark 6:30; Luke 9:10; Acts 14:26-27; Acts 21:17-19; Galatians 2:1-2; Mark 12:38-40; Matthew 23:5-12; Luke 20:45-47; Acts 14:11-15; 1 Corinthians 3:5-6; 1 Thessalonians 2:3-6; 2 Corinthians 2:17; Acts 20:33-35; 2 Corinthians 11:7-9; 1 Thessalonians 2:6-9; 2 Corinthians 4:2; 2 Corinthians 1:12
* **Associated Articles:** 3239: Holy Spirit, anointing of (ID: `3239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5714: men (ID: `5714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5769: behaviour (ID: `5769@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5842: eloquence (ID: `5842@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7720: elders, in the church (ID: `7720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7944: ministry, qualifications for (ID: `7944@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8114: discipleship (ID: `8114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8203: character (ID: `8203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8265: godliness (ID: `8265@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8266: holiness (ID: `8266@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8276: humility (ID: `8276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7760: preachers, responsibilities of (id: 7760)

Those entrusted with the responsibility of preaching are to discharge it faithfully, boldly and persistently in the power of the Holy Spirit. The Christian community should honour faithful preachers and challenge those who fail to honour their responsibility.

The responsibilities of preachers
---------------------------------

### They must be diligent in their preaching

**[2TI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15)** *See also* [1TI 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:13-1Tim4:16); [2TI 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:1-2Tim4:5)

### They must be faithful in their preaching

**[ACT 20:20](https://ref.ly/Acts20:20)** *See also* [JER 26:1–26:2](https://ref.ly/Jer26:1-Jer26:2), [JER 42:4](https://ref.ly/Jer42:4); [ACT 20:25–20:27](https://ref.ly/Acts20:25-Acts20:27)

### They must be persistent in their preaching

**[2TI 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:1-2Tim4:5)** *See also* [ACT 18:4–18:6](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4-Acts18:6), [ACT 20:31](https://ref.ly/Acts20:31); [1TH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:1-1Thess2:2)

### They must be bold in their preaching

**[ACT 28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:31)** *See also* [JER 26:7–26:15](https://ref.ly/Jer26:7-Jer26:15); [AMO 7:10–7:17](https://ref.ly/Amos7:10-Amos7:17); [ACT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:20), [ACT 5:27–5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:27-Acts5:29), [ACT 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1-Acts14:3)

### They must be encouraging in their preaching

**[ACT 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Acts14:21-Acts14:22)** *See also* [ACT 15:32](https://ref.ly/Acts15:32); [1CO 14:3–14:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:3-1Cor14:5); [1TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:11-1Thess2:12); [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)

### They must be filled with the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)**; **[1CO 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4)** *See also* [ACT 4:31–4:33](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31-Acts4:33); [1TH 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:5); [1PE 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:12)

### They must be compassionate in their preaching

**[MRK 6:34](https://ref.ly/Mark6:34)** *See also* [MAT 9:35–9:38](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35-Matt9:38)

The responsibilities of the church towards preachers
----------------------------------------------------

### Faithful preachers should be honoured

**[1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)** *See also* [MAT 13:53–13:57](https://ref.ly/Matt13:53-Matt13:57) parallel [MRK 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2-Mark6:4); [LUK 10:3–10:8](https://ref.ly/Luke10:3-Luke10:8); [HEB 13:7](https://ref.ly/Heb13:7)

### Unfaithful preachers are to be exposed and will be judged

**[GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9)** *See also* [JER 14:14–14:16](https://ref.ly/Jer14:14-Jer14:16); [EZK 13:1–13:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:1-Ezek13:23); [1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4), [1TI 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:7); [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11); [2PE 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1-2Pet2:3); [REV 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14-Rev2:16), [REV 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20-Rev2:23)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Timothy 2:15; 1 Timothy 4:13-16; 2 Timothy 4:1-5; Acts 20:20; Jeremiah 26:1-2; Jeremiah 42:4; Acts 20:25-27; Acts 18:4-6; Acts 20:31; 1 Thessalonians 2:1-2; Acts 28:31; Jeremiah 26:7-15; Amos 7:10-17; Acts 4:18-20; Acts 5:27-29; Acts 14:1-3; Acts 14:21-22; Acts 15:32; 1 Corinthians 14:3-5; 1 Thessalonians 2:11-12; 2 Timothy 4:2; Acts 1:8; 1 Corinthians 2:4; Acts 4:31-33; 1 Thessalonians 1:5; 1 Peter 1:12; Mark 6:34; Matthew 9:35-38; 1 Timothy 5:17; Matthew 13:53-57; Mark 6:2-4; Luke 10:3-8; Hebrews 13:7; Galatians 1:6-9; Jeremiah 14:14-16; Ezekiel 13:1-23; 1 Timothy 1:3-4; 1 Timothy 4:1-7; Titus 1:10-11; 2 Peter 2:1-3; Revelation 2:14-16; Revelation 2:20-23

## 7766: priests (id: 7766)

A group of men charged with the responsibility of mediating between God and his people, ensuring proper worship, and maintaining the spiritual health of the people of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 7767: priests, institution in OT times (ID: `7767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7768: priests, function in OT times (ID: `7768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7769: priests, types of in NT times (ID: `7769@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7770: priests, tasks in NT times (ID: `7770@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7767: priests, institution in OT times (id: 7767)

Priests played an important role in the OT and were regarded as having a special status and place in the life of Israel.

Pagan priests in the OT
-----------------------

[1SA 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:1-1Sam6:2); ; [2KI 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:5); ; [HOS 10:5](https://ref.ly/Hos10:5); ; [ZEP 1:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:4)

The special status of priests
-----------------------------

[EZR 1:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:5); ; [ISA 24:2](https://ref.ly/Isa24:2); ; [HOS 4:9](https://ref.ly/Hos4:9); ; [MIC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Mic3:11)

Associated with the descendants of Levi through Aaron
-----------------------------------------------------

### The exclusive nature of the priesthood

**[1CH 6:49](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:49)** *Only God has the right to nominate those who mediate between him and humanity.* *See also* [EXO 6:16–6:20](https://ref.ly/Exod6:16-Exod6:20) *This genealogy shows Moses and Aaron were both descended from Levi*; [EXO 28:1](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1), [EXO 29:44](https://ref.ly/Exod29:44), [EXO 30:30](https://ref.ly/Exod30:30); [NUM 16:40](https://ref.ly/Num16:40)DEU [18:1](https://ref.ly/Num18:1); [JER 33:21](https://ref.ly/Jer33:21)

### Micah's priest

**[JDG 17:13](https://ref.ly/Judg17:13)**

### The Shiloh priesthood

**[1CH 24:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:3)** *See also* [1CH 6:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:27)

### The priests in Dan

**[JDG 18:30](https://ref.ly/Judg18:30)**

### Descendants of Zadok

**[EZK 44:15–44:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:15-Ezek44:16)** *See also* [2SA 8:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:17); [1CH 6:50–6:53](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:50-1Chr6:53), [1CH 24:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:3)

Criticism of priestly appointments that were not Levitical
----------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 12:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:31)** *See also* [1KI 13:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:33); [2CH 11:13–11:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:13-2Chr11:14), [2CH 13:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:9)

The appointment of priests
--------------------------

### Their preliminary purification

**[EXO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Exod29:4)** *See also* [EXO 30:19](https://ref.ly/Exod30:19), [EXO 40:12](https://ref.ly/Exod40:12)

### Their anointing

**[EXO 30:30–30:32](https://ref.ly/Exod30:30-Exod30:32)** *See also* [LEV 8:30](https://ref.ly/Lev8:30); [NUM 3:3](https://ref.ly/Num3:3) *Kings and priests also were anointed.*

### Their consecration

**[EXO 29:1](https://ref.ly/Exod29:1)** *See also* [EXO 29:12–29:14](https://ref.ly/Exod29:12-Exod29:14), [EXO 29:18](https://ref.ly/Exod29:18), [EXO 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/Exod29:20-Exod29:21); [LEV 8:12](https://ref.ly/Lev8:12)

### Their ordination

**[EXO 29:35](https://ref.ly/Exod29:35)** *See also* [EXO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Exod29:9), [EXO 29:29](https://ref.ly/Exod29:29), [EXO 29:33](https://ref.ly/Exod29:33); [LEV 8:22](https://ref.ly/Lev8:22), [LEV 8:33](https://ref.ly/Lev8:33); [JDG 17:5](https://ref.ly/Judg17:5), [JDG 17:12](https://ref.ly/Judg17:12)

The dress of the priests
------------------------

**[EXO 28:40](https://ref.ly/Exod28:40)** *See also* [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42), [EXO 39:27–39:29](https://ref.ly/Exod39:27-Exod39:29); [LEV 8:13](https://ref.ly/Lev8:13)

## 7768: priests, function in OT times (id: 7768)

Priests had a unique role: administering the sanctuary, offering sacrifice and leading and teaching the people. Accordingly they were set apart by a special code of conduct.

The priests were set apart
--------------------------

### They were consecrated to the LORD

**[LEV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Lev22:9)** *See also* [EXO 28:1](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1), [EXO 28:4](https://ref.ly/Exod28:4), [EXO 28:41](https://ref.ly/Exod28:41), [EXO 29:1](https://ref.ly/Exod29:1), [EXO 29:44](https://ref.ly/Exod29:44), [EXO 30:34](https://ref.ly/Exod30:34), [EXO 31:10](https://ref.ly/Exod31:10), [EXO 35:19](https://ref.ly/Exod35:19), [EXO 39:41](https://ref.ly/Exod39:41)

### They were not to incur uncleanness

**[LEV 21:1](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1)** *Some exceptions to uncleanness by touching a corpse are listed in verses 2–3\.* *See also* [EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25)

### They were not to shave or mutilate themselves

**[LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5)** *See also* [EZK 44:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:20)

### They were not to marry any but a virtuous Israelite

**[LEV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Lev21:7)** *See also* [EZK 44:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:22)

### They were not to drink wine before entering the sanctuary

**[LEV 10:9](https://ref.ly/Lev10:9)** *See also* [EZK 44:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:21)

### They were disqualified by physical defects

**[LEV 21:17](https://ref.ly/Lev21:17)** *See also* [LEV 21:18–21:22](https://ref.ly/Lev21:18-Lev21:22)

### They were not to allow outsiders to eat the sacred offerings

**[LEV 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/Lev22:15-Lev22:16)** *See also* [LEV 24:9](https://ref.ly/Lev24:9); [1SA 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:4); [MAT 12:4](https://ref.ly/Matt12:4)

### They were not to own land in Israel

**[NUM 18:20](https://ref.ly/Num18:20)** *See also* [EZK 44:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:28)

### They were not to offer unacceptable sacrifices

**[LEV 22:20](https://ref.ly/Lev22:20)** *See also* [MAL 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Mal1:7-Mal1:9)

### They were to obey God's law meticulously

**[LEV 22:31](https://ref.ly/Lev22:31)** *See also* [EZK 44:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:24)

The priests supervised the sanctuary
------------------------------------

### They only had access to holy things

**[NUM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Num3:10)** *See also* [NUM 4:5](https://ref.ly/Num4:5); [EZK 40:45–40:46](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:45-Ezek40:46), [EZK 44:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:16); [JOL 2:17](https://ref.ly/Joel2:17)

### They tended the sanctuary lamps

**[EXO 27:21](https://ref.ly/Exod27:21)**

### They carried the ark

**[DEU 31:9](https://ref.ly/Deut31:9)** *See also* [JOS 3:8](https://ref.ly/Josh3:8), [JOS 3:15](https://ref.ly/Josh3:15), [JOS 3:17](https://ref.ly/Josh3:17), [JOS 4:9](https://ref.ly/Josh4:9), [JOS 4:16](https://ref.ly/Josh4:16), [JOS 6:12](https://ref.ly/Josh6:12)

The priests supervised the sacrifices
-------------------------------------

**[LEV 1:5](https://ref.ly/Lev1:5)** *See also* [LEV 1:8](https://ref.ly/Lev1:8), [LEV 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:2); [1CH 9:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:30)

The priests led the nation
--------------------------

### As role models

**[MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6)**

### As teachers

**[EZK 44:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:23)** *See also* [LEV 10:10](https://ref.ly/Lev10:10); [DEU 31:10–31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:10-Deut31:13); [EZR 7:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:6); [MAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Mal2:7)

### As judges

**[DEU 17:9](https://ref.ly/Deut17:9)** *See also* [DEU 19:17](https://ref.ly/Deut19:17), [DEU 21:5](https://ref.ly/Deut21:5); [2CH 19:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:8); [EZK 44:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:24)

### As encouragers in battle

**[NUM 10:8](https://ref.ly/Num10:8)** *See also* [JOS 6:4](https://ref.ly/Josh6:4); [2CH 13:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:12)

Prophetic criticism of the priests
----------------------------------

**[LAM 4:13](https://ref.ly/Lam4:13)** *See also* [JER 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jer2:8), [JER 6:13](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13); [EZK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:26), [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26); [HOS 4:6–4:9](https://ref.ly/Hos4:6-Hos4:9), [HOS 6:9](https://ref.ly/Hos6:9); [ZEP 3:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 22:9; Exodus 28:1; Exodus 28:4; Exodus 28:41; Exodus 29:1; Exodus 29:44; Exodus 30:34; Exodus 31:10; Exodus 35:19; Exodus 39:41; Leviticus 21:1; Ezekiel 44:25; Leviticus 21:5; Ezekiel 44:20; Leviticus 21:7; Ezekiel 44:22; Leviticus 10:9; Ezekiel 44:21; Leviticus 21:17; Leviticus 21:18-22; Leviticus 22:15-16; Leviticus 24:9; 1 Samuel 21:4; Matthew 12:4; Numbers 18:20; Ezekiel 44:28; Leviticus 22:20; Malachi 1:7-9; Leviticus 22:31; Ezekiel 44:24; Numbers 3:10; Numbers 4:5; Ezekiel 40:45-46; Ezekiel 44:16; Joel 2:17; Exodus 27:21; Deuteronomy 31:9; Joshua 3:8; Joshua 3:15; Joshua 3:17; Joshua 4:9; Joshua 4:16; Joshua 6:12; Leviticus 1:5; Leviticus 1:8; Leviticus 2:2; 1 Chronicles 9:30; Malachi 2:6; Ezekiel 44:23; Leviticus 10:10; Deuteronomy 31:10-13; Ezra 7:6; Malachi 2:7; Deuteronomy 17:9; Deuteronomy 19:17; Deuteronomy 21:5; 2 Chronicles 19:8; Numbers 10:8; Joshua 6:4; 2 Chronicles 13:12; Lamentations 4:13; Jeremiah 2:8; Jeremiah 6:13; Ezekiel 7:26; Ezekiel 22:26; Hosea 4:6-9; Hosea 6:9; Zephaniah 3:4
* **Associated Articles:** 5358: judges (ID: `5358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6682: mediation (ID: `6682@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7404: ordinances (ID: `7404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7424: ritual law (ID: `7424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7464: teachers of the law (ID: `7464@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7769: priests, types of in NT times (id: 7769)

The NT points to the fulfilment of the OT priesthood in Jesus Christ, and develops the idea that all Christian believers can be seen as priests. It also records the continuing role of priests in the life of Israel and pagan nations at this time.

Pagan priests
-------------

**[ACT 14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:13)**

Jewish priests
--------------

### The chief priests

**[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)**

### The Sadducees

**[ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17)** *The Sadducees were a conservative party composed entirely of priests who were influential in the Sanhedrin.*

### Zechariah

**[LUK 1:5](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5)** *See also* [1CH 24:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr24:10)

### Emissaries to John the Baptist

**[JHN 1:19](https://ref.ly/John1:19)** *Levites were closely associated with priests as temple personnel, perhaps mentioned here because of their teaching function.* *See also* [2CH 35:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:3); [NEH 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:7-Neh8:9); [LUK 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Luke10:31-Luke10:32)

Jesus Christ as high priest
---------------------------

**[HEB 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Heb8:1-Heb8:2)** *See also* [HEB 4:14–4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:14-Heb4:16)

Christians as priests
---------------------

**[REV 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Rev1:5-Rev1:6)** *See also* [1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5), [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9); [REV 5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:10), [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 14:13; Matthew 16:21; Acts 5:17; Luke 1:5; 1 Chronicles 24:10; John 1:19; 2 Chronicles 35:3; Nehemiah 8:7-9; Luke 10:31-32; Hebrews 8:1-2; Hebrews 4:14-16; Revelation 1:5-6; 1 Peter 2:5; 1 Peter 2:9; Revelation 5:10; Revelation 20:6
* **Associated Articles:** 2306: Jesus Christ, as high priest (ID: `2306@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6684: mediator (ID: `6684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7330: chief priests (ID: `7330@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7378: high priest, in NT (ID: `7378@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7414: priesthood, in NT (ID: `7414@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7555: Sadducees (ID: `7555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7565: Sanhedrin (ID: `7565@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8266: holiness (ID: `8266@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7770: priests, tasks in NT times (id: 7770)

The priests mentioned in the NT performed various functions, all of which are fulfilled in Jesus Christ and the church, which is his body.

Priests offered sacrifices
--------------------------

### Sacrifices made by pagan priests

**[ACT 14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:13)**

### Jewish sacrifice

**[HEB 10:11](https://ref.ly/Heb10:11)**

### The sacrifice of Jesus Christ

**[HEB 9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:14)** *Jesus Christ is both priest and victim: his own body takes the place occupied by sacrifices in the OT ritual.* *See also* [HEB 10:10](https://ref.ly/Heb10:10)

### The Christian sacrifice of a surrendered life

**[ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1)** *See also* [HEB 13:16](https://ref.ly/Heb13:16)

### The Christian sacrifice of praise

**[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)**

Priests made intercession
-------------------------

### The burning of incense

**[LUK 1:9](https://ref.ly/Luke1:9)** *See also* [EXO 30:7–30:8](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7-Exod30:8)

### Christian intercession

**[REV 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8)** *See also* [1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5); [REV 1:6](https://ref.ly/Rev1:6), [REV 5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:10), [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6)

Priests were guardians of tradition
-----------------------------------

### They were trained in the teaching and application of OT law

[MAT 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/Matt2:3-Matt2:6); ; [JHN 1:19](https://ref.ly/John1:19)

### The Christian \`\`tradition''

[1CO 15:1–15:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:1-1Cor15:5); ; [2TI 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:13-2Tim1:14), [2TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:2); ; [JUD 1:3](https://ref.ly/Jude1:3)

Priests declared people clean or unclean
----------------------------------------

### They supplied ritual certification of cleansing

**[MAT 8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:4)** parallels [MRK 1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:44), [LUK 5:14](https://ref.ly/Luke5:14) *See also* [LEV 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Lev13:2-Lev13:3), [LEV 14:2](https://ref.ly/Lev14:2); [LUK 17:14](https://ref.ly/Luke17:14)

### Christians pronounce forgiveness

[JHN 20:21–20:23](https://ref.ly/John20:21-John20:23); ; [JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 14:13; Hebrews 10:11; Hebrews 9:14; Hebrews 10:10; Romans 12:1; Hebrews 13:16; Hebrews 13:15; Luke 1:9; Exodus 30:7-8; Revelation 5:8; 1 Peter 2:5; Revelation 1:6; Revelation 5:10; Revelation 20:6; Matthew 2:3-6; John 1:19; 1 Corinthians 15:1-5; 2 Timothy 1:13-14; 2 Timothy 2:2; Jude 1:3; Matthew 8:4; Mark 1:44; Luke 5:14; Leviticus 13:2-3; Leviticus 14:2; Luke 17:14; John 20:21-23; James 5:16
* **Associated Articles:** 5588: traditions (ID: `5588@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7719: elders, as community leaders (ID: `7719@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7939: ministry (ID: `7939@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8611: prayer, for others (ID: `8611@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7772: prophets (id: 7772)

Individuals called and empowered by God to declare his will to his people, including the disclosure of his future intentions to save and judge his people. The term refers particularly to the great OT prophets, such as Isaiah and Jeremiah, who were raised up to guide the people of God at critical moments in their history and whose ministries and teaching remain of continuing importance today. At several points in his ministry, Jesus Christ was acclaimed as a prophet.

* **Associated Articles:** 7773: prophets, role of (ID: `7773@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7774: prophets, false (ID: `7774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7775: prophets, lives of (ID: `7775@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7776: prophet, Jesus Christ as (ID: `7776@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7773: prophets, role of (id: 7773)

As agents of God, prophets acted in various capacities, as was required of them, in order to carry out his will and fulfil his purpose.

Prophets as God's spokesmen
---------------------------

### They heralded impending judgment

**[JON 3:4](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:4)** *See also* [1KI 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:7); [2KI 22:15–22:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:15-2Kgs22:17) parallel [2CH 34:23–34:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:23-2Chr34:25); [JER 1:14–1:16](https://ref.ly/Jer1:14-Jer1:16), [JER 5:14–5:17](https://ref.ly/Jer5:14-Jer5:17); [EZK 5:8–5:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:8-Ezek5:12), [EZK 38:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:17)

### They advocated repentance

**[2KI 17:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:13)** *See also* [2CH 24:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:19); [JER 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Jer3:12-Jer3:13), [JER 35:15](https://ref.ly/Jer35:15), [JER 44:4](https://ref.ly/Jer44:4); [ZEC 1:3–1:6](https://ref.ly/Zech1:3-Zech1:6)

### They conveyed messages from God to the nations

**[EZK 25:8–25:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:8-Ezek25:11)** *See also* [JER 46:13](https://ref.ly/Jer46:13), [JER 47:1](https://ref.ly/Jer47:1), [JER 49:34](https://ref.ly/Jer49:34), [JER 50:1](https://ref.ly/Jer50:1); [EZK 25:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:2), [EZK 25:12–25:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:12-Ezek25:14), [EZK 26:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:3); [AMO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Amos1:9); [EZK 29:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:2); [AMO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3), [AMO 1:6](https://ref.ly/Amos1:6), [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13), [AMO 2:1](https://ref.ly/Amos2:1)

The supernatural activities of prophets
---------------------------------------

### They revealed future events

**[EZK 17:16–17:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:16-Ezek17:21)** *See also* [1KI 11:29–11:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:29-1Kgs11:31), [1KI 20:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:13); [ISA 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:13-Isa7:14) *the birth of Immanuel*; [JER 29:10](https://ref.ly/Jer29:10); [DAN 2:38–2:44](https://ref.ly/Dan2:38-Dan2:44), [DAN 9:2](https://ref.ly/Dan9:2); [MAT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Matt2:15); [HOS 11:1](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1)MAT [2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Hos2:17-Hos2:18); [JER 31:15](https://ref.ly/Jer31:15)[MAT 8:17](https://ref.ly/Matt8:17); [ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4); [ACT 3:18](https://ref.ly/Acts3:18), [ACT 11:27–11:29](https://ref.ly/Acts11:27-Acts11:29), [ACT 21:10–21:11](https://ref.ly/Acts21:10-Acts21:11)

### They received dreams and visions

**[NUM 12:6](https://ref.ly/Num12:6)** *See also* [EZK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:26); [DAN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Dan7:1), [DAN 8:1](https://ref.ly/Dan8:1); [HOS 12:10](https://ref.ly/Hos12:10)

### They worked miracles

**[2KI 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:3)** *See also* [1KI 17:13–17:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:13-1Kgs17:24); [2KI 4:3–4:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:3-2Kgs4:6), [2KI 4:15–4:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:15-2Kgs4:17), [2KI 4:32–4:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:32-2Kgs4:35), [2KI 4:40–4:44](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:40-2Kgs4:44), [2KI 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:5-2Kgs6:6)

Prophets as intermediaries
--------------------------

### Between God and the people

**[DEU 18:16–18:18](https://ref.ly/Deut18:16-Deut18:18)** *See also* [EXO 6:30–7:1](https://ref.ly/Exod6:30-Exod7:1); [2SA 7:4–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:4-2Sam7:7), [2SA 12:7–12:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:7-2Sam12:14), [2SA 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24-2Sam12:25), [2SA 24:11–24:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:11-2Sam24:12); [1KI 20:42](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:42); [2KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:1) parallel [ISA 38:1](https://ref.ly/Isa38:1); [2CH 12:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:5), [2CH 28:9–28:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:9-2Chr28:11)

### Between the people and God

**[1SA 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:9)** *See also* [GEN 20:7](https://ref.ly/Gen20:7); [2KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:11); [2CH 32:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:20); [JER 11:14](https://ref.ly/Jer11:14), [JER 27:18](https://ref.ly/Jer27:18); [EZK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:7)

### Their responsibility as watchmen

**[EZK 3:17–3:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:17-Ezek3:21)** *The intermediary role of the prophet is highlighted by the image of a watchman who receives messages from God and conveys them to the people.* *See also* [EZK 33:1–33:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:1-Ezek33:7); [HOS 9:8](https://ref.ly/Hos9:8)

Prophets as leaders in the community
------------------------------------

### They gave advice

**[ZEC 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Zech7:2-Zech7:3)** *See also* [1SA 9:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:6); [1KI 22:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:6); [2KI 3:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:11), [2KI 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:8-2Kgs6:10), [2KI 22:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:14) parallel [2CH 34:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:22); [2CH 29:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:25); [ISA 37:2](https://ref.ly/Isa37:2)

### They gave encouragement

**[ACT 15:32](https://ref.ly/Acts15:32)** *See also* [EZR 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:1-Ezra5:2); [JER 29:4–29:7](https://ref.ly/Jer29:4-Jer29:7); [EZK 11:17–11:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:17-Ezek11:21); [HAG 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hag1:13), [HAG 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hag2:4)

### They led the people

**[HOS 12:13](https://ref.ly/Hos12:13)** *See also* [DEU 18:15](https://ref.ly/Deut18:15); [1SA 7:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:6); [1KI 18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:40)

### They appointed kings and leaders

**[1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1)** *See also* [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13); [1KI 1:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:34), [1KI 1:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:38), [1KI 19:15–19:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:15-1Kgs19:16); [2KI 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:6)

Prophets as writers
-------------------

**[1CH 29:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:29)** *See also* [2CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:29), [2CH 13:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:22), [2CH 26:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:22), [2CH 32:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:32); [ISA 30:8](https://ref.ly/Isa30:8); [JER 30:2](https://ref.ly/Jer30:2), [JER 36:4](https://ref.ly/Jer36:4), [JER 51:60](https://ref.ly/Jer51:60); [EZK 43:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:11); [HAB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hab2:2)

## 7774: prophets, false (id: 7774)

Men or women who falsely claim to speak on God's behalf. They are denounced in the OT and NT for leading people astray. Scripture provides criteria by which true prophets may be recognised and distinguished from false prophets, who often speak from base motives.

Criteria for recognising true prophets
--------------------------------------

### A true prophet's word will be fulfilled

**[DEU 18:21–18:22](https://ref.ly/Deut18:21-Deut18:22)** *See also* [JER 28:8–28:9](https://ref.ly/Jer28:8-Jer28:9)

### A true prophet's teaching commends righteous behaviour

**[DEU 13:1–13:4](https://ref.ly/Deut13:1-Deut13:4)** *See also* [EZK 13:17–13:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:17-Ezek13:23), [EZK 14:4–14:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:4-Ezek14:8); [LAM 2:14](https://ref.ly/Lam2:14)

### A true prophet's godly life will reflect his calling

**[MAT 7:15–7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15-Matt7:20)** *See also* [ISA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Isa28:7); [JER 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Jer23:10-Jer23:11), [JER 23:14](https://ref.ly/Jer23:14); [ZEP 3:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:4)

### A true prophet will acknowledge Jesus Christ as divine

**[1JN 4:1–4:6](https://ref.ly/1John4:1-1John4:6)**

The motivations of false prophets
---------------------------------

### Their messages originate with themselves

**[JER 23:16](https://ref.ly/Jer23:16)** *See also* [JER 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/Jer14:13-Jer14:14), [JER 23:25–23:32](https://ref.ly/Jer23:25-Jer23:32), [JER 23:36](https://ref.ly/Jer23:36); [EZK 13:1–13:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:1-Ezek13:7)

### Their messages may be occasioned by popular demand

**[JER 29:8–29:9](https://ref.ly/Jer29:8-Jer29:9)** [NUM 22:35](https://ref.ly/Num22:35), [NUM 22:38](https://ref.ly/Num22:38), [NUM 23:12](https://ref.ly/Num23:12), [NUM 23:26](https://ref.ly/Num23:26), [NUM 24:12–24:13](https://ref.ly/Num24:12-Num24:13); ; [1KI 22:13–22:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:13-1Kgs22:14); [ISA 30:10–30:11](https://ref.ly/Isa30:10-Isa30:11); ; [JER 5:30–5:31](https://ref.ly/Jer5:30-Jer5:31); ; [MIC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Mic2:11)

### Their messages may be given for financial reward

**[NEH 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Neh6:12-Neh6:13)** *See also* [JER 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13-Jer6:14); [EZK 13:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:19); [MIC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Mic3:11); [2PE 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1-2Pet2:3)

### Their messages may be the result of deception

**[1KI 22:19–22:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:19-1Kgs22:23)** parallel [2CH 18:18–18:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:18-2Chr18:22) *See also* [EZK 14:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:9)

### Their messages may be inspired by divination

**[JER 14:14](https://ref.ly/Jer14:14)** *See also* [EZK 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:6-Ezek13:7), [EZK 22:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:28); [ACT 13:6](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6)

Examples of false prophets
--------------------------

### In the OT

**[NEH 6:10–6:13](https://ref.ly/Neh6:10-Neh6:13)** *See also* [1KI 22:1–22:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:1-1Kgs22:28) parallel [2CH 18:1–18:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:1-2Chr18:27); [JER 27:9–27:10](https://ref.ly/Jer27:9-Jer27:10), [JER 27:14–27:18](https://ref.ly/Jer27:14-Jer27:18)

### False prophetesses

[NEH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Neh6:14); ; [EZK 13:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:17)

### Prophets of other gods

**[JER 23:13](https://ref.ly/Jer23:13)** *See also* [1KI 18:19–18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:19-1Kgs18:40); [2KI 10:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:19); [JER 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jer2:8)

### In the NT

**[MAT 24:24](https://ref.ly/Matt24:24)** parallel [MRK 13:22](https://ref.ly/Mark13:22) *See also* [MAT 24:10–24:11](https://ref.ly/Matt24:10-Matt24:11); [ACT 13:6–13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6-Acts13:11)REV [2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:20), [REV 16:13](https://ref.ly/Rev16:13), [REV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Rev19:20), [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10)

The punishment of false prophets
--------------------------------

**[DEU 18:20](https://ref.ly/Deut18:20)** *See also* [DEU 13:5](https://ref.ly/Deut13:5); [ZEC 13:3](https://ref.ly/Zech13:3); [JER 14:15](https://ref.ly/Jer14:15)JER [28:15–28:17](https://ref.ly/Jer28:15-Jer28:17), [JER 29:21–29:23](https://ref.ly/Jer29:21-Jer29:23), [JER 29:30–29:32](https://ref.ly/Jer29:30-Jer29:32)[JER 23:34–23:40](https://ref.ly/Jer23:34-Jer23:40); [EZK 13:8–13:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:8-Ezek13:16); [MIC 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Mic3:5-Mic3:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 18:21-22; Jeremiah 28:8-9; Deuteronomy 13:1-4; Ezekiel 13:17-23; Ezekiel 14:4-8; Lamentations 2:14; Matthew 7:15-20; Isaiah 28:7; Jeremiah 23:10-11; Jeremiah 23:14; Zephaniah 3:4; 1 John 4:1-6; Jeremiah 23:16; Jeremiah 14:13-14; Jeremiah 23:25-32; Jeremiah 23:36; Ezekiel 13:1-7; Jeremiah 29:8-9; Numbers 22:35; Numbers 22:38; Numbers 23:12; Numbers 23:26; Numbers 24:12-13; 1 Kings 22:13-14; Isaiah 30:10-11; Jeremiah 5:30-31; Micah 2:11; Nehemiah 6:12-13; Jeremiah 6:13-14; Ezekiel 13:19; Micah 3:11; 2 Peter 2:1-3; 1 Kings 22:19-23; 2 Chronicles 18:18-22; Ezekiel 14:9; Jeremiah 14:14; Ezekiel 13:6-7; Ezekiel 22:28; Acts 13:6; Nehemiah 6:10-13; 1 Kings 22:1-28; 2 Chronicles 18:1-27; Jeremiah 27:9-10; Jeremiah 27:14-18; Nehemiah 6:14; Ezekiel 13:17; Jeremiah 23:13; 1 Kings 18:19-40; 2 Kings 10:19; Jeremiah 2:8; Matthew 24:24; Mark 13:22; Matthew 24:10-11; Acts 13:6-11; Acts 2:20; Revelation 16:13; Revelation 19:20; Revelation 20:10; Deuteronomy 18:20; Deuteronomy 13:5; Zechariah 13:3; Jeremiah 14:15; Jeremiah 28:15-17; Jeremiah 29:21-23; Jeremiah 29:30-32; Jeremiah 23:34-40; Ezekiel 13:8-16; Micah 3:5-6

## 7775: prophets, lives of (id: 7775)

God called individuals from differing backgrounds to speak as prophets on his behalf, sometimes in difficult circumstances.

The backgrounds of the prophets
-------------------------------

[EXO 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Exod2:1-Exod2:10) *Moses was brought up in the Egyptian royal court*; ; [1SA 1:21–1:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:21-1Sam1:28) *Samuel was a temple servant at Shiloh*; ; [1KI 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19-1Kgs19:21) *Elisha was a farmer*; ; [EZK 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:3) *Ezekiel was a priest living in exile in Babylonia.*; [AMO 1:1](https://ref.ly/Amos1:1), [AMO 7:14](https://ref.ly/Amos7:14)

The calling of the prophets
---------------------------

### Their initial encounter with God

**[ISA 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1-Isa6:4)** *See also* [EXO 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:1-Exod3:6); [EZK 1:4–1:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:4-Ezek1:28)

### God communicating his call

**[JER 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:4-Jer1:5)** *See also* [ISA 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8-Isa6:10); [EZK 2:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:3-Ezek3:5); [JON 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1), [JON 3:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1)

### Their response to God's call

**[JER 1:6–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6-Jer1:10)** *See also* [EXO 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Exod3:11-Exod3:12)

The family life of the prophets
-------------------------------

**[JER 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Jer16:1-Jer16:4)** *See also* [ISA 7:3](https://ref.ly/Isa7:3), [ISA 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/Isa8:1-Isa8:4); [EZK 24:15–24:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:15-Ezek24:24); [HOS 1:2–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2-Hos1:11), [HOS 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1-Hos3:5)

The hardships faced by prophets
-------------------------------

### Persecution

**[HEB 11:32–11:38](https://ref.ly/Heb11:32-Heb11:38)** *See also* [ACT 7:52](https://ref.ly/Acts7:52)

### Reviling and beating

**[LUK 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Luke6:22-Luke6:23)** parallel [MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12) *See also* [JER 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Jer20:1-Jer20:2); [JAS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Jas5:10)

### Imprisonment

**[JER 37:11–37:16](https://ref.ly/Jer37:11-Jer37:16)** *See also* [1KI 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:26-1Kgs22:27) parallel [2CH 18:25–18:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:25-2Chr18:26); [2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10); [JER 37:21](https://ref.ly/Jer37:21), [JER 38:6–38:13](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6-Jer38:13)

### Killing

**[1KI 19:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:14)** *See also* [1KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:4), [1KI 18:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:13), [1KI 19:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:2); [JER 26:8–26:11](https://ref.ly/Jer26:8-Jer26:11), [JER 26:20–26:23](https://ref.ly/Jer26:20-Jer26:23); [MAT 23:29–23:31](https://ref.ly/Matt23:29-Matt23:31), [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37) parallel [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34); [LUK 11:47–11:49](https://ref.ly/Luke11:47-Luke11:49); [1TH 2:4–2:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:4-1Thess2:15)

The death of prophets
---------------------

[DEU 34:1–34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:1-Deut34:8) *Moses*; ; [1SA 28:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:3) *Samuel*; ; [2KI 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:11-2Kgs2:12) *Elijah did not die but was taken up in a chariot of fire.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 2:1-10; 1 Samuel 1:21-28; 1 Kings 19:19-21; Ezekiel 1:3; Amos 1:1; Amos 7:14; Isaiah 6:1-4; Exodus 3:1-6; Ezekiel 1:4-28; Jeremiah 1:4-5; Isaiah 6:8-10; Ezekiel 2:3-3:5; Jonah 1:1; Jonah 3:1; Jeremiah 1:6-10; Exodus 3:11-12; Jeremiah 16:1-4; Isaiah 7:3; Isaiah 8:1-4; Ezekiel 24:15-24; Hosea 1:2-11; Hosea 3:1-5; Hebrews 11:32-38; Acts 7:52; Luke 6:22-23; Matthew 5:11-12; Jeremiah 20:1-2; James 5:10; Jeremiah 37:11-16; 1 Kings 22:26-27; 2 Chronicles 18:25-26; 2 Chronicles 16:10; Jeremiah 37:21; Jeremiah 38:6-13; 1 Kings 19:14; 1 Kings 18:4; 1 Kings 18:13; 1 Kings 19:2; Jeremiah 26:8-11; Jeremiah 26:20-23; Matthew 23:29-31; Matthew 23:37; Luke 13:34; Luke 11:47-49; 1 Thessalonians 2:4-15; Deuteronomy 34:1-8; 1 Samuel 28:3; 2 Kings 2:11-12

## 7778: school of prophets (id: 7778)

A company of prophets working and prophesying together under the instruction and leadership of a more experienced prophet. There were also similar companies of false prophets.

A school of prophets used by God to influence others
----------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 19:19–19:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:19-1Sam19:24)** *See also* [1SA 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:5-1Sam10:6), [1SA 10:10–10:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:10-1Sam10:13); [2KI 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1-2Kgs9:3); [EZR 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:2)

Schools of prophets associated with particular places
-----------------------------------------------------

### Bethel

[2KI 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:3)

### Jericho

[2KI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:15)

Leaders of schools of prophets
------------------------------

### Samuel and a school of prophets

[1SA 19:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:20)

### Elisha and a school of prophets

**[2KI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:15)** *See also* [2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1), [2KI 4:38](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:38), [2KI 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:1-2Kgs6:4)

A school of prophets in danger
------------------------------

**[1KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:4)** *See also* [1KI 18:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:13)

Schools of false prophets
-------------------------

[1KI 18:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:19), [1KI 18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:40), [1KI 22:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:6) parallel [2CH 18:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:5); ; [1KI 22:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:10) parallel [2CH 18:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:9); ; [1KI 22:12–22:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:12-1Kgs22:14) parallel [2CH 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:11-2Chr18:13); ; [1KI 22:19–22:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:19-1Kgs22:23) parallel [2CH 18:18–18:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:18-2Chr18:22); ; [NEH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Neh6:14)

## 7781: seer (id: 7781)

Seer as an alternative title for a prophet
------------------------------------------

**[1SA 9:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:9)** *Literally, a “seer” is “one who sees”, especially one who claims to see into the future or to have insight into the will of God.* *See also* [2SA 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:11) parallel [1CH 21:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:9)

Seers used by people to enquire of God
--------------------------------------

**[1SA 9:8–9:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:8-1Sam9:9)**

### David had seers to enquire of God for him

**[1CH 25:1–25:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1-1Chr25:5)** *See also* [2SA 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:11) parallel [1CH 21:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:9); [2SA 15:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:27); [2CH 29:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:25), [2CH 35:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:15)

Seers were used by God to bring his word to the people
------------------------------------------------------

[2KI 17:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:13)

Seers were used by God to bring his word to the king
----------------------------------------------------

[2CH 16:7–16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:7-2Chr16:10), [2CH 33:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:18)

The words of a seer were sometimes unwelcome
--------------------------------------------

[2CH 16:7–16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:7-2Chr16:10); ; [ISA 30:10](https://ref.ly/Isa30:10); ; [AMO 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Amos7:12-Amos7:13)

Samuel was called a seer
------------------------

[1SA 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:11-1Sam9:14), [1SA 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:18-1Sam9:19); ; [1CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:22), [1CH 26:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:28), [1CH 29:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:29)

Asaph, the psalmist, called a seer
----------------------------------

[2CH 29:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:30)

Seers wrote Israel's history
----------------------------

**[2CH 33:18–33:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:18-2Chr33:19)** *See also* [1CH 29:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:29); [2CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:29), [2CH 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:15)

God judges Israel by blinding the seers
---------------------------------------

[ISA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10); ; [MIC 3:7](https://ref.ly/Mic3:7)

## 7784: shepherd (id: 7784)

The work of a shepherd was important and responsible in the rural world of ancient Palestine. Since it involved leading, protecting and feeding a flock, it is seen as a metaphor for the task of leadership. Scripture declares that God is the Shepherd of his people.

* **Associated Articles:** 7785: shepherd, as occupation (ID: `7785@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7786: shepherd, as king and leader (ID: `7786@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7787: shepherd, God the (ID: `7787@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7788: shepherd, Jesus Christ as (ID: `7788@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7789: shepherd, as church leader (ID: `7789@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7785: shepherd, as occupation (id: 7785)

The occupation of a shepherd was both humble and honourable. In the marginal hill country of Palestine it was particularly demanding.

A shepherd as a keeper of flocks of animals
-------------------------------------------

[GEN 46:32](https://ref.ly/Gen46:32) *The Hebrew word refers to a keeper of goats and cattle as well as sheep.*

The tasks of a shepherd
-----------------------

### Tending, feeding and watering

**[PSA 23:2](https://ref.ly/Ps23:2)** *See also* [GEN 29:3](https://ref.ly/Gen29:3), [GEN 26:19–26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:19-Gen26:22); [EXO 22:5](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5); [LUK 13:15](https://ref.ly/Luke13:15); [JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17)

## 7786: shepherd, as king and leader (id: 7786)

Shepherd was a standard term for a leader or king throughout the ancient world. In this sense it often appears in the Bible.

---------------------------------

**[ISA 44:28](https://ref.ly/Isa44:28)** *The prophet describes this non\-Jewish king as God's shepherd*; **[JER 6:3](https://ref.ly/Jer6:3)** *See also* [2SA 7:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:7); [PSA 77:20](https://ref.ly/Ps77:20); [ISA 31:4](https://ref.ly/Isa31:4); [JER 12:10](https://ref.ly/Jer12:10), [JER 49:19](https://ref.ly/Jer49:19), [JER 50:44](https://ref.ly/Jer50:44)

## 7793: teachers (id: 7793)

Those who have responsibility for instructing others, especially in relation to matters of faith and life: in the home, parents; in Israel, priests and teachers of the law and in the NT church, apostles, prophets, pastors and teachers. At a less formal level Christians should seek to teach one another. God is the supreme teacher of his people.

God is the supreme teacher
--------------------------

**[HEB 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Heb8:10-Heb8:11)** *See also* [JER 31:33–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33-Jer31:34); [DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3); [PSA 32:8](https://ref.ly/Ps32:8); [JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26); [1TH 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:9); [TIT 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:12)

## 7796: teaching (id: 7796)

The act of instructing someone in matters of faith and morals, especially in the home or church. Teaching was a major aspect of the ministry of Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7798: teaching, of Jesus Christ (ID: `7798@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7797: teaching (id: 7797)

The apostles' teaching formed the basis of the instruction given to the first Christians. Parts of the NT draw a distinction between preaching and teaching, seeing the former as a means of converting individuals, and the latter as a means of instructing them after conversion.

The importance of teaching
--------------------------

**[ECC 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:11-Eccl12:12)** *“goads” prod the sluggish to action, while “embedded nails” furnish a kind of mental anchorage. “Shepherd” is almost certainly a reference to God himself.* *See also* [PRO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8-Prov1:9), [PRO 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Prov3:1-Prov3:2), [PRO 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1-Prov4:4), [PRO 6:20–6:23](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:23); [MAT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:19); [1CO 14:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:6); [2TI 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2-2Tim4:3)

-------------------------

[PRO 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:6), [PRO 1:20–1:28](https://ref.ly/Prov1:20-Prov1:28)

### Through the law

[DEU 6:6–6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:9), [DEU 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18-Deut11:19), [DEU 27:1–27:26](https://ref.ly/Deut27:1-Deut27:26); ; [PSA 78:5–78:8](https://ref.ly/Ps78:5-Ps78:8)

### Through mutual edification

**[COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)** *See also* [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14); [1TH 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:11); [HEB 5:12](https://ref.ly/Heb5:12)

### Through the instruction of different groups within the church

[EPH 5:22–6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph6:9); ; [COL 3:18–4:1](https://ref.ly/Col3:18-Col4:1); ; [TIT 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:1-Titus2:10); ; [1PE 2:18–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18-1Pet3:7), [1PE 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:5)

The distinction between teaching and preaching
----------------------------------------------

**[ACT 15:35](https://ref.ly/Acts15:35)** *See also* [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23), [MAT 9:35](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35), [MAT 11:1](https://ref.ly/Matt11:1); [LUK 20:1](https://ref.ly/Luke20:1); [ACT 4:2](https://ref.ly/Acts4:2), [ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42), [ACT 28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:31)

Examples of major themes taught in the NT church
------------------------------------------------

### Righteousness from God through faith in Jesus Christ

**[ROM 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/Rom3:21-Rom3:22)** *See also* [GAL 2:20–2:21](https://ref.ly/Gal2:20-Gal2:21)

### Freedom in Christ from the demands of the law

**[GAL 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1-Gal5:3)**

### The humility of Jesus Christ

**[PHP 2:5–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:8)** *See also* [HEB 13:12–13:13](https://ref.ly/Heb13:12-Heb13:13)

### The supremacy of Jesus Christ

**[COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18)**

### The superiority of Jesus Christ

**[HEB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Heb3:3)** *Hebrews also shows Jesus Christ to be superior to the angels, the high priest and the sacrifices.*

### Godly behaviour

**[EPH 4:22–4:24](https://ref.ly/Eph4:22-Eph4:24)** *See also* [ROM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1-Rom12:2); [1TI 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:1-1Tim6:2)

## 7903: baptism (id: 7903)

A washing with water, which symbolises the cleansing of believers from the stain and dirt of sin through the grace of God. Jesus Christ submitted to baptism as an example to believers. Through the work of the Holy Spirit, baptism is linked with union with the risen Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 7904: baptism, of Jesus Christ (ID: `7904@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7905: baptism, with Holy Spirit (ID: `7905@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7906: baptism, in the Gospels (ID: `7906@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7907: baptism, practice of (ID: `7907@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7908: baptism, significance of (ID: `7908@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7904: baptism, of Jesus Christ (id: 7904)

See [2510: Jesus Christ, baptism of](2510.md)



## 7905: baptism, with Holy Spirit (id: 7905)
