## 7906: baptism, in the Gospels (id: 7906)

In the Gospels baptising is based on the symbolic practice in the OT and Judaism of cleansing with water.

Washing was the means of achieving ritual purity
------------------------------------------------

[EXO 30:19–30:20](https://ref.ly/Exod30:19-Exod30:20); ; [MRK 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:3-Mark7:4) *The Greek word here translated “wash” is “baptizo”, from which the English “baptise” comes.*

Sprinkling with water symbolises spiritual cleansing
----------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 36:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:25)** *See also* [ZEC 13:1](https://ref.ly/Zech13:1); [HEB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:22)

Being baptised by John was a confession that cleansing was needed
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 3:11](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11)** *See also* [MAT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:6); [MRK 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4-Mark1:5) parallel [LUK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Luke3:3); [ACT 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/Acts19:1-Acts19:4)

John promised that the Messiah would cleanse with the baptism of the Holy Spirit
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 3:11](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11)** parallels [MRK 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mark1:8), [LUK 3:16](https://ref.ly/Luke3:16) *See also* [ISA 44:3](https://ref.ly/Isa44:3); [EZK 36:25–36:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:25-Ezek36:26)

Baptism involves both water and the Holy Spirit
-----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 3:5](https://ref.ly/John3:5)**

The disciples of Jesus Christ baptised like John's
--------------------------------------------------

*See also* [JHN 3:22–3:23](https://ref.ly/John3:22-John3:23), [JHN 4:1](https://ref.ly/John4:1)

Jesus Christ was baptised by John
---------------------------------

[MAT 3:13–3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:13-Matt3:17) parallels [MRK 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/Mark1:9-Mark1:11), [LUK 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/Luke3:21-Luke3:22), [JHN 1:32–1:33](https://ref.ly/John1:32-John1:33)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 30:19-20; Mark 7:3-4; Ezekiel 36:25; Zechariah 13:1; Hebrews 10:22; Matthew 3:11; Matthew 3:6; Mark 1:4-5; Luke 3:3; Acts 19:1-4; Mark 1:8; Luke 3:16; Isaiah 44:3; Ezekiel 36:25-26; John 3:5; John 3:22-23; John 4:1; Matthew 3:13-17; Mark 1:9-11; Luke 3:21-22; John 1:32-33
* **Associated Articles:** 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2510: Jesus Christ, baptism of (ID: `2510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3242: Holy Spirit, baptism with (ID: `3242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5098: John the Baptist (ID: `5098@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7416: purification (ID: `7416@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8324: purity (ID: `8324@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7907: baptism, practice of (id: 7907)

Baptism is associated with repenting of sin, believing the gospel message and becoming a member of Christ's body.

Baptism is ordained by Jesus Christ himself
-------------------------------------------

**[MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19)** *See also* [ACT 9:17–9:18](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17-Acts9:18), [ACT 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14-Acts16:15)

Baptism is linked with repentance
---------------------------------

**[ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38)** *See also* [HEB 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Heb6:1-Heb6:2) *The “baptisms” referred to here may imply teaching about Jewish rituals or the baptism offered by John the Baptist.*

Baptism follows the decision to believe
---------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:41), [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8)** *See also* [ACT 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12-Acts8:13), [ACT 16:31–16:33](https://ref.ly/Acts16:31-Acts16:33)

Baptism in the name of God or Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------

### Baptism in or into the name of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 19:5](https://ref.ly/Acts19:5)** *See also* [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 8:16](https://ref.ly/Acts8:16), [ACT 10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:48)

### Baptism in the name of the Trinity

[MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20)

Baptism was by immersion
------------------------

**[ROM 6:4](https://ref.ly/Rom6:4)** *The word “buried” implies immersion.* *See also* [ACT 8:38](https://ref.ly/Acts8:38)

Baptism is linked with the gift of the Holy Spirit
--------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mark1:8)**; **[ACT 1:5](https://ref.ly/Acts1:5)** *See also* [ACT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:4), [ACT 11:16](https://ref.ly/Acts11:16)**[1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13)** *All the Christians at Corinth had been spiritually baptised into Christ and thus united with him.*

Manifestations of the Holy Spirit may follow or precede water baptism
---------------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [ACT 8:12–8:17](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12-Acts8:17), [ACT 9:17–9:18](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17-Acts9:18), [ACT 10:44–10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:48)

The person who baptises is of little importance
-----------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:14)** *What matters is not who baptises but the Saviour in whose name it is done.*

Baptism \`\`for the dead''
--------------------------

**[1CO 15:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:29)** *It is not certain if there was a ceremony of baptism “for the dead” in the NT church, nor whether, if it existed, Paul approved of it.*

Passages which may imply infant baptism
---------------------------------------

The \`\`households'' (not merely individuals) may well have included children: [ACT 16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:15), [ACT 16:33](https://ref.ly/Acts16:33), [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8); ; [1CO 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:16); [MRK 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:16); ; [1CO 7:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:14); ; [COL 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:11-Col2:12) *If baptism is here equated with circumcision, this suggests baptism is the sign of the new covenant.*

Passages apparently negating infant baptism
-------------------------------------------

[ACT 2:38–2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38-Acts2:41) *infants cannot repent and believe*; ; [GAL 3:7](https://ref.ly/Gal3:7) *Under the new covenant it is faith in Jesus Christ, not an outward sign, that makes a person a child of God.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 28:19; Acts 9:17-18; Acts 16:14-15; Acts 2:38; Hebrews 6:1-2; Acts 2:41; Acts 18:8; Acts 8:12-13; Acts 16:31-33; Acts 19:5; Acts 8:16; Acts 10:48; Matthew 28:18-20; Romans 6:4; Acts 8:38; Mark 1:8; Acts 1:5; Acts 2:4; Acts 11:16; 1 Corinthians 12:13; Acts 8:12-17; Acts 10:44-48; 1 Corinthians 1:14; 1 Corinthians 15:29; Acts 16:15; Acts 16:33; 1 Corinthians 1:16; Mark 10:13-16; 1 Corinthians 7:14; Colossians 2:11-12; Acts 2:38-41; Galatians 3:7
* **Associated Articles:** 3257: Holy Spirit, gift of (ID: `3257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4293: water (ID: `4293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7105: believers (ID: `7105@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7957: sacraments (ID: `7957@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7908: baptism, significance of (id: 7908)

The NT uses a variety of images to explain the meaning of baptism, such as dying and rising with Christ, sharing in his death and being cleansed from sin.

Baptism is a symbol of the death of Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 12:50](https://ref.ly/Luke12:50)** *Jesus Christ described his death as a baptism or a flood overwhelming him.* *See also* [PSA 42:7](https://ref.ly/Ps42:7), [PSA 69:1–69:2](https://ref.ly/Ps69:1-Ps69:2), [PSA 88:7](https://ref.ly/Ps88:7); [MRK 10:38–10:39](https://ref.ly/Mark10:38-Mark10:39)

Baptism is a symbol of the burial of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Rom6:3-Rom6:4)** *Immersion in water symbolises how the old sinful life is buried with Christ.* [ROM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Rom8:13); ; [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)

Baptism is a symbol of being saved from the flood
-------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:21)** *The flood/baptism symbolises both judgment (the death of sinners and the death of Jesus Christ) and salvation (those in the ark and those in Christ passing safely through judgment).* *See also* [GEN 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Gen7:6-Gen7:7)

Baptism is the gospel equivalent of circumcision
------------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:11-Col2:12)** *The “circumcision in Christ” may refer either to his death on the cross or to baptism.* *See also* [DEU 10:16](https://ref.ly/Deut10:16)

Baptism recalls the exodus
--------------------------

**[1CO 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:1-1Cor10:2)** *In and through the exodus experience Israel was united with and obedient to Moses, as Jesus Christ's people are to him.* *See also* [EXO 14:19–14:24](https://ref.ly/Exod14:19-Exod14:24)

Baptism symbolises washing from sin
-----------------------------------

**[ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:16)** *Baptism is an outward sign of cleansing from sin and from a defiled life and conscience.* *See also* [1CO 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:11); [TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5); [1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:21)

Baptism is a symbol of putting on Christ
----------------------------------------

**[GAL 3:27](https://ref.ly/Gal3:27)** *Baptism is seen as putting on Christ, as one might put on a coat or garment, and thus be “clothed with Christ”.*

Baptism as a symbol of unity
----------------------------

**[EPH 4:5](https://ref.ly/Eph4:5)** *See also* [1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 12:50; Psalms 42:7; Psalms 69:1-2; Psalms 88:7; Mark 10:38-39; Romans 6:3-4; Romans 8:13; Colossians 3:5; 1 Peter 3:21; Genesis 7:6-7; Colossians 2:11-12; Deuteronomy 10:16; 1 Corinthians 10:1-2; Exodus 14:19-24; Acts 22:16; 1 Corinthians 6:11; Titus 3:5; Galatians 3:27; Ephesians 4:5; 1 Corinthians 12:13
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2410: cross, the (ID: `2410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2530: Jesus Christ, death of (ID: `2530@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6029: sin, accepting forgiveness of (ID: `6029@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6728: regeneration (ID: `6728@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6754: union with Christ (ID: `6754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7221: exodus, the (ID: `7221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7336: circumcision, spiritual (ID: `7336@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7478: washing (ID: `7478@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7912: collections (id: 7912)

The financial burden of God's work has always been shared amongst God's people. In the OT, this was mainly by compulsory tithes; in the NT by voluntary contributions.

The collection of compulsory tithes and other religious taxes
-------------------------------------------------------------

### Tithes

**[LEV 27:30](https://ref.ly/Lev27:30)** *See also* [NUM 18:21](https://ref.ly/Num18:21)

### Redemption money

[EXO 30:12](https://ref.ly/Exod30:12); ; [NUM 3:46–3:47](https://ref.ly/Num3:46-Num3:47)

### Temple taxes

[2CH 24:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:5); ; [MAT 17:24](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24)

The collection of voluntary contributions
-----------------------------------------

### For the tabernacle

**[EXO 36:3](https://ref.ly/Exod36:3)** *See also* [NUM 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Num7:2-Num7:3), [NUM 7:84–7:88](https://ref.ly/Num7:84-Num7:88)

### For the temple

[1CH 29:1–29:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1-1Chr29:9)

### For the rebuilding of the temple

[EZR 1:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:4)

### For the teaching ministry of the church

**[GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6)** *See also* [LUK 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1-Luke8:3); [PHP 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Phil4:15-Phil4:16); [1TI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:18)

### For the relief of the poor

**[ROM 15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:26)**; **[1CO 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:1)** *See also* [ACT 2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:45), [ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35), [ACT 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29-Acts11:30)

The aim of collections
----------------------

### To share burdens equally

[2CO 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:13-2Cor8:15)

### To supply people's needs

[2CO 9:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:12)

### To arouse thanksgiving

[2CO 9:12–9:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:12-2Cor9:15)

The right attitude to collections
---------------------------------

### Generosity

[EXO 36:4–36:6](https://ref.ly/Exod36:4-Exod36:6); ; [2CO 8:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:11), [2CO 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6)

### Cheerfulness

[2CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:7)

### Discipline

[1CO 16:2–16:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:2-1Cor16:4)

### Humility

[MAT 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2-Matt6:4)

### A willing spirit

[2CO 8:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:12)

### A sacrificial spirit

[MRK 12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:44) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4); ; [2CO 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:1-2Cor8:4)

The faithful administration of collections
------------------------------------------

[2KI 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:7); ; [ACT 11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:30); ; [2CO 8:19–8:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:19-2Cor8:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 27:30; Numbers 18:21; Exodus 30:12; Numbers 3:46-47; 2 Chronicles 24:5; Matthew 17:24; Exodus 36:3; Numbers 7:2-3; Numbers 7:84-88; 1 Chronicles 29:1-9; Ezra 1:4; Galatians 6:6; Luke 8:1-3; Philippians 4:15-16; 1 Timothy 5:17-18; Romans 15:26; 1 Corinthians 16:1; Acts 2:45; Acts 4:34-35; Acts 11:29-30; 2 Corinthians 8:13-15; 2 Corinthians 9:12; 2 Corinthians 9:12-15; Exodus 36:4-6; 2 Corinthians 8:11; 2 Corinthians 9:6; 2 Corinthians 9:7; 1 Corinthians 16:2-4; Matthew 6:2-4; 2 Corinthians 8:12; Mark 12:41-44; Luke 21:1-4; 2 Corinthians 8:1-4; 2 Kings 22:7; Acts 11:30; 2 Corinthians 8:19-21
* **Associated Articles:** 5449: poverty, remedies for (ID: `5449@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5576: tax collectors (ID: `5576@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5577: taxation (ID: `5577@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8260: generosity (ID: `8260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8434: giving (ID: `8434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8488: tithing (ID: `8488@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7915: confirmation (id: 7915)

The act of publicly affirming something or someone, usually with a view to declaring trustworthiness. God confirmed his covenants, oaths and promises to emphasise his intention to fulfil them and he confirms the gospel to demonstrate its truthfulness.

Confirmation of covenants
-------------------------

**[DEU 4:31](https://ref.ly/Deut4:31)** *See also* [GEN 17:1–17:22](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1-Gen17:22); [DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18); [2KI 23:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:3); [1CH 16:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:17); [PSA 105:10](https://ref.ly/Ps105:10); [DAN 9:26–9:27](https://ref.ly/Dan9:26-Dan9:27)

Confirmation of oaths
---------------------

**[PSA 119:106](https://ref.ly/Ps119:106)** *See also* [GEN 26:1–26:5](https://ref.ly/Gen26:1-Gen26:5)

Confirmation of promises
------------------------

**[ROM 15:8](https://ref.ly/Rom15:8)** *See also* [DEU 29:12–29:13](https://ref.ly/Deut29:12-Deut29:13); [2CH 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:9); [HEB 6:17](https://ref.ly/Heb6:17)

Confirmation of the gospel
--------------------------

**[MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20)** *See also* [ACT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:3); [1CO 1:4–1:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:4-1Cor1:6); [PHP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7); [HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3)

Confirmation of wisdom
----------------------

**[JOB 28:25–28:28](https://ref.ly/Job28:25-Job28:28)**

Confirmation of vows
--------------------

*See also* [NUM 30:1–30:16](https://ref.ly/Num30:1-Num30:16) *A vow made by a woman could be either confirmed or nullified by her father or husband.*

Confirmation of a witness's testimony
-------------------------------------

**[DEU 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15)** *Evidence accepted in court required confirmation by more than one witness.* *See also* [1KI 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:11-1Kgs1:14); [MRK 14:56–14:59](https://ref.ly/Mark14:56-Mark14:59) *The proper judicial procedure was not followed at the trial of Jesus Christ*; [ROM 9:1–9:4](https://ref.ly/Rom9:1-Rom9:4) *Paul here invokes the Holy Spirit as a witness*; [HEB 6:16](https://ref.ly/Heb6:16)

Confirmation of letters
-----------------------

**[ACT 15:27](https://ref.ly/Acts15:27)** *This constituted verification of the authenticity of a letter which could easily have been forged or tampered with.* *See also* [EST 9:29–9:32](https://ref.ly/Esth9:29-Esth9:32)

Confirmation of office or position
----------------------------------

**[1SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:15)**

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 4:31; Genesis 17:1-22; Deuteronomy 8:18; 2 Kings 23:3; 1 Chronicles 16:17; Psalms 105:10; Daniel 9:26-27; Psalms 119:106; Genesis 26:1-5; Romans 15:8; Deuteronomy 29:12-13; 2 Chronicles 1:9; Hebrews 6:17; Mark 16:20; Acts 14:3; 1 Corinthians 1:4-6; Philippians 1:7; Hebrews 2:3; Job 28:25-28; Numbers 30:1-16; Deuteronomy 19:15; 1 Kings 1:11-14; Mark 14:56-59; Romans 9:1-4; Hebrews 6:16; Acts 15:27; Esther 9:29-32; 1 Samuel 11:15
* **Associated Articles:** 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1448: signs (ID: `1448@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2422: gospel, confirmation of (ID: `2422@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5391: letters (ID: `5391@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5429: oaths (ID: `5429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5466: promises (ID: `5466@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5471: proof (ID: `5471@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5623: witnesses, legal (ID: `5623@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5741: vows (ID: `5741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8634: amen (ID: `8634@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8751: false witness (ID: `8751@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7918: excommunication (id: 7918)

The last but reversible step in church discipline, resulting in exclusion from the fellowship of believers. The grounds that Scripture suggests for excommunication include public sin and immorality.

Jesus Christ authorises the church to excommunicate a sinning brother
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:18)** *The binding of sinners is thought to refer to their exclusion from God's people, and loosing, to their restoration after repentance (compare [Mt 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19)).*

Paul exhorts the Corinthian church to excommunicate an immoral brother
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:6)** *By “hand this man over to Satan” (see also [1Ti 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:20)) Paul means to expel him, by putting him out into the devil's territory, in the hope that being officially ostracised, he might repent and forsake his wicked way. Such discipline was necessary not only for the reclaiming of the sinner but also for the safety and purity of the church (verse 6\).* *See also* [1CO 5:11–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:11-1Cor5:13)

Excommunicated offenders who repent are to be lovingly restored to fellowship
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[2CO 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:5-2Cor2:11) *This passage probably refers to the offender mentioned in 1Corinthians chapter 5\.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 18:15-18; Matthew 16:19; 1 Corinthians 5:1-6; 1 Timothy 1:20; 1 Corinthians 5:11-13; 2 Corinthians 2:5-11
* **Associated Articles:** 6040: sinners (ID: `6040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6112: banishment (ID: `6112@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8230: discipline (ID: `8230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8341: separation (ID: `8341@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7921: fellowship (id: 7921)

Association based upon the sharing of something in common. Believers have fellowship with one another on the basis of their common fellowship with God, their participation in the blessings of the gospel and their common task of mission. True fellowship is demonstrated in concern for, and practical commitment to, one another.

* **Associated Articles:** 7922: fellowship, with God (ID: `7922@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7923: fellowship, in the gospel (ID: `7923@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7924: fellowship, in Christian service (ID: `7924@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7925: fellowship, among believers (ID: `7925@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7922: fellowship, with God (id: 7922)

The relationship with God, disrupted by sin yet established through Jesus Christ, which provides the only proper basis for true human fellowship. God's desire for fellowship with humanity is made known through his calling of a people to be his own and to reflect his holiness and love.

God's fellowship with his people is shown by his presence
---------------------------------------------------------

### God's presence with Israel

**[LEV 26:12](https://ref.ly/Lev26:12)** *See also* [EXO 33:14](https://ref.ly/Exod33:14); [ISA 63:9](https://ref.ly/Isa63:9); [HAG 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hag1:13)

### God's presence in the tabernacle

**[EXO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8)** *See also* [EXO 29:45–29:46](https://ref.ly/Exod29:45-Exod29:46), [EXO 40:34–40:36](https://ref.ly/Exod40:34-Exod40:36); [LEV 26:11](https://ref.ly/Lev26:11); [DEU 12:11](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11)

### God's presence in the temple

**[1KI 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:12-1Kgs6:13)** *See also* [1KI 8:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:29) parallel [2CH 6:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:20); [2CH 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:1-2Chr7:2); [ISA 6:1](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1)

### God's presence in the new Jerusalem

**[ZEC 2:10–2:13](https://ref.ly/Zech2:10-Zech2:13)** *See also* [EZK 37:26–37:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:26-Ezek37:28), [EZK 43:4–43:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:4-Ezek43:7), [EZK 48:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek48:35); [REV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Rev21:3)

The church's fellowship with God
--------------------------------

### Fellowship with the Father, Son and Holy Spirit

**[JHN 14:23](https://ref.ly/John14:23)** *See also* [JHN 14:7](https://ref.ly/John14:7), [JHN 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:16-John14:17) *fellowship with the Spirit*

### Fellowship is made possible through Jesus Christ

**[EPH 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/Eph2:18-Eph2:19)** *See also* [ROM 5:10](https://ref.ly/Rom5:10); [2CO 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:18-2Cor5:19); [COL 1:20–1:22](https://ref.ly/Col1:20-Col1:22); [HEB 10:19–10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:22)

### Fellowship with Jesus Christ

**[1CO 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:9)** *See also* [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20) *Jesus Christ's presence with his disciples*; [JHN 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:4-John15:5) *abiding in Christ*; [ROM 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Rom6:4-Rom6:5) *united with Christ in his death and resurrection*; [1CO 10:15–10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:15-1Cor10:16) *communion with Christ at the Lord's Supper*; [PHP 3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10) *sharing in Christ's sufferings*

### Fellowship with God is inseparable from fellowship with one another

**[1JN 1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:3)** *See also* [MAT 18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:20); [MRK 9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:37); [JHN 17:21](https://ref.ly/John17:21); [2CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:11)

The demands of fellowship with God
----------------------------------

### Holiness

**[LEV 20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:26)**; **[2CO 6:14–6:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14-2Cor6:18)** *See also* [EXO 34:12–34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:12-Exod34:14); [EZR 6:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:21); [1CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:11); [EPH 5:8–5:11](https://ref.ly/Eph5:8-Eph5:11); [JAS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jas4:4)

### Obedience to God's will

**[1JN 3:24](https://ref.ly/1John3:24)** *See also* [ISA 57:15](https://ref.ly/Isa57:15); [MAT 12:49–12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:49-Matt12:50) parallels [MRK 3:34–3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:34-Mark3:35), [LUK 8:21](https://ref.ly/Luke8:21); [JHN 14:21](https://ref.ly/John14:21)

Sin separates people from fellowship with God
---------------------------------------------

**[ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2)**; **[1JN 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/1John1:5-1John1:6)** *See also* [GEN 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8); [EZK 39:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:23)

Examples of fellowship with God
-------------------------------

[GEN 5:22](https://ref.ly/Gen5:22) *Enoch*; ; [GEN 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gen6:9) *Noah*; ; [2CH 20:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:7) *Abraham*; [EXO 33:11](https://ref.ly/Exod33:11); ; [NUM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Num12:3); [JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9) *Joshua*; ; [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6) *Levi*

## 7923: fellowship, in the gospel (id: 7923)

A mutual participation in the blessings of God's grace. Believers are united with one another on the basis of their common reception of the benefits of salvation.

Fellowship and the community of God's people
--------------------------------------------

### God calls out a community of people, for fellowship with himself

**[DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6)** *See also* [EXO 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5-Exod19:6); [1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5), [1PE 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9-1Pet2:11)

### God will bless a people united in fellowship

[MAT 18:19–18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:19-Matt18:20); ; [2CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14); ; [JER 31:23–31:25](https://ref.ly/Jer31:23-Jer31:25); ; [1CO 11:29–11:34](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:29-1Cor11:34) *Wrong relationships between God's people hinder the flow of his blessing.*

### God will restore fellowship with his scattered people

**[MIC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mic2:12)** *See also* [ISA 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/Isa11:12-Isa11:13); [JER 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jer3:18), [JER 31:1](https://ref.ly/Jer31:1), [JER 50:4–50:5](https://ref.ly/Jer50:4-Jer50:5)

Sharing in God's grace
----------------------

### Fellowship in a common blessing

**[PSA 106:4–106:5](https://ref.ly/Ps106:4-Ps106:5)** *See also* [NUM 10:32](https://ref.ly/Num10:32); [JOS 22:19](https://ref.ly/Josh22:19)

### Fellowship in a common salvation

**[JUD 1:3](https://ref.ly/Jude1:3)** *See also* [1CO 9:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:23) *sharing in the blessings of the gospel*; [PHP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7) *sharing in God's grace*; [TIT 1:4](https://ref.ly/Titus1:4) *sharing a common faith*; [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Heb3:1) *sharing a common calling*; [1PE 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1) *sharing a common hope*; [2PE 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:4) *sharing the divine nature*; [1JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/1John1:7) *sharing in sanctification*

### Fellowship in a common inheritance

**[COL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Col1:12)** *See also* [ROM 8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:17); [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### Fellowship in God's family

**[HEB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Heb2:11)** *See also* [MAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Mal2:10); [MAT 6:9](https://ref.ly/Matt6:9) parallel [LUK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Luke11:2) *the family prayer in which believers together address God as Father*; [MAT 12:49–12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:49-Matt12:50) parallels [MRK 3:34–3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:34-Mark3:35), [LUK 8:21](https://ref.ly/Luke8:21); [MAT 23:8–23:9](https://ref.ly/Matt23:8-Matt23:9)

### Fellowship between Jew and Gentile

**[EPH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:6)** *See also* [ROM 11:17](https://ref.ly/Rom11:17), [ROM 15:27](https://ref.ly/Rom15:27); [EPH 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Eph2:16-Eph2:18)

Fellowship in holding a common truth
------------------------------------

**[2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15)** *See also* [PSA 119:63](https://ref.ly/Ps119:63); [1CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:2), [1CO 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:2-1Cor15:3); [2TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:2), [2TI 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:14); [TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Titus2:15)

Fellowship in union with Christ
-------------------------------

**[1CO 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16-1Cor10:17)**; **[EPH 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19-Eph2:22)** *See also* [ROM 12:5](https://ref.ly/Rom12:5); [1CO 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:12), [1CO 12:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27); [EPH 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Eph4:4-Eph4:5); [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15); [1PE 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:4-1Pet2:5)

Fellowship through the Holy Spirit
----------------------------------

**[1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13)** *See also* [EZK 36:27–36:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:27-Ezek36:28); [2CO 13:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:14); [EPH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:3); [PHP 2:1](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1)

## 7924: fellowship, in Christian service (id: 7924)

Partnership in a common enterprise. God's people are called to work together especially in the task of mission, to recognise one another's gifts and to give support to one another's ministries.

Fellowship in mission
---------------------

### Partnership in preaching the gospel

**[GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9)** *See also* [MRK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Mark10:7); [LUK 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Luke10:1-Luke10:2); [PHP 1:5](https://ref.ly/Phil1:5)

### Supporting the work of others

**[ACT 14:26](https://ref.ly/Acts14:26)**; **[PHP 4:14–4:16](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14-Phil4:16)** *The Philippians share in Paul's work through their giving.* *See also* [ACT 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:2-Acts13:3), [ACT 15:40](https://ref.ly/Acts15:40); [2CO 11:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:9); [3JN 1:5–1:8](https://ref.ly/3John1:5-3John1:8)

### Standing together in adversity

**[HEB 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32-Heb10:34)** *See also* [2CO 1:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:7); [PHP 1:27–1:30](https://ref.ly/Phil1:27-Phil1:30), [PHP 4:14](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14); [HEB 11:25](https://ref.ly/Heb11:25)

### Fellowship between Paul and his co\-workers

**[PHP 4:3](https://ref.ly/Phil4:3)** [ACT 11:26–11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:26-Acts11:30), [ACT 13:42–13:50](https://ref.ly/Acts13:42-Acts13:50), [ACT 14:1–14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1-Acts14:23), [ACT 15:22–15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:22-Acts15:29); [ROM 16:3](https://ref.ly/Rom16:3) *Priscilla and Aquila*; ; [ROM 16:9](https://ref.ly/Rom16:9) *Urbanus*; ; [ROM 16:21](https://ref.ly/Rom16:21) *Timothy*; ; [2CO 8:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:23) *Titus*; ; [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25) *Epaphroditus*; ; [PHM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:1) *Philemon*; ; [PHM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:24) *Mark, Aristarchus, Demas and Luke*

In fellowship different gifts are combined for effective service
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:12)** *See also* [1CO 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:4-1Cor12:6)

### Spiritual gifts are given to all to share

**[1CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:7)** *See also* [ROM 12:4–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4-Rom12:8); [1CO 12:14–12:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:14-1Cor12:20); [1PE 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10)

### Recognising one another's gifts

**[1CO 12:21–12:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:21-1Cor12:26)**

### Accepting one another's ministries

**[GAL 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7-Gal2:8)** *See also* [1CO 12:27–12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27-1Cor12:31), [1CO 16:15–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15-1Cor16:18); [2PE 3:15–3:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:15-2Pet3:16)

### Examples of sharing in different roles

**[NEH 4:16–4:22](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16-Neh4:22)**; **[1CO 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:5-1Cor3:8)** *See also* [EXO 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Exod4:15-Exod4:16), [EXO 17:10–17:13](https://ref.ly/Exod17:10-Exod17:13); [1CO 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:8-1Cor12:11)

Examples of working together in fellowship
------------------------------------------

**[ECC 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:9-Eccl4:12)** *See also* [DEU 3:18–3:20](https://ref.ly/Deut3:18-Deut3:20) *the Reubenites and the Gadites join with the rest of Israel to conquer Canaan*; [JDG 20:11](https://ref.ly/Judg20:11) *all Israel unites against Gibeah*; [EZR 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8-Ezra3:10) *Those returning from exile work together to rebuild the temple*; [NEH 4:6](https://ref.ly/Neh4:6) *The Israelites work together to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem*; [LUK 5:7–5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:7-Luke5:10) *Peter and Andrew, James and John are business partners.*

## 7925: fellowship, among believers (id: 7925)

The fellowship that believers share as a result of their common union with God through Jesus Christ is expressed in life together. It is evident in worship together, in a love for one another which reflects God's own love and in a practical commitment to one another which is demonstrated in concern for the weak and readiness to share with the poor and needy.

Sharing in the fellowship of God's love
---------------------------------------

**[1JN 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/1John4:10-1John4:12)** *See also* [JHN 13:34](https://ref.ly/John13:34), [JHN 15:12](https://ref.ly/John15:12); [EPH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Eph5:1-Eph5:2); [1JN 3:10](https://ref.ly/1John3:10)

Sharing in the fellowship of a common devotional life
-----------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:42)**

### Worshipping together

**[PSA 55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:14)** *See also* [PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4); [1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26); [EPH 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19); [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)

### Praying together

**[ACT 1:14](https://ref.ly/Acts1:14)** *See also* [ACT 4:24](https://ref.ly/Acts4:24), [ACT 12:12](https://ref.ly/Acts12:12); [JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16)

### Breaking bread together

[1CO 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16-1Cor10:17); ; [2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13); ; [JUD 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12) *Love feasts accompanied the Lord's Supper though these were open to abuse.*

True fellowship means sharing with those in need
------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 13:16](https://ref.ly/Heb13:16)** *See also* [ACT 20:34–20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:34-Acts20:35); [EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28)

### Showing hospitality

**[ROM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Rom12:13)** *See also* [ISA 58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:7); [HEB 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Heb13:1-Heb13:2); [1PE 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:9); [3JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/3John1:8)

### Sharing money and possessions

**[DEU 15:10–15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:10-Deut15:11)**; **[ACT 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44-Acts2:45)** *See also* [DEU 10:18–10:19](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18-Deut10:19) *God's people are to reflect his concern for the needy in society*; [MAT 25:35–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:35-Matt25:36); [LUK 3:11](https://ref.ly/Luke3:11); [ACT 4:32–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32-Acts4:35); [2CO 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:13-2Cor8:15); [1TI 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17-1Tim6:18); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27), [JAS 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Jas2:15-Jas2:16)

### Examples of sharing with the needy

[JOB 31:16–31:20](https://ref.ly/Job31:16-Job31:20) *Job's compassion for the needy*; ; [ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1) *the daily distribution to widows*; ; [ACT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36) *Tabitha's concern for the poor*; [ACT 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29-Acts11:30); ; [ROM 15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:26); ; [2CO 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:3-2Cor8:4)

Strengthening one another in fellowship together
------------------------------------------------

### Bearing with the weak

**[GAL 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1-Gal6:2)** *See also* [ISA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Isa42:3); [ROM 14:1](https://ref.ly/Rom14:1), [ROM 15:1](https://ref.ly/Rom15:1); [1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)

### Strengthening the weak

**[ISA 35:3–35:4](https://ref.ly/Isa35:3-Isa35:4)** *See also* [JOB 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Job4:3-Job4:4)

### Encouraging one another

**[HEB 10:24–10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:24-Heb10:25)** *See also* [1SA 23:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:16); [ROM 1:12](https://ref.ly/Rom1:12); [1TH 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:11); [HEB 13:3](https://ref.ly/Heb13:3)

### Putting the needs of others first

**[ROM 15:2](https://ref.ly/Rom15:2)** *See also* [1CO 10:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:24), [1CO 10:32–10:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:32-1Cor10:33)

True fellowship means living in harmony
---------------------------------------

**[1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)** *See also* [ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16); [EPH 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:2-Eph4:3); [PHP 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1-Phil2:4); [COL 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Col3:12-Col3:14)

### Showing equal concern for all

[ACT 10:34](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34); ; [1CO 12:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:25); ; [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4)

### Examples of fellowship

[NUM 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Num10:31-Num10:32) *Moses and Hobab*; ; [1SA 18:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:3) *David and Jonathan*; ; [2KI 10:15–10:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:15-2Kgs10:16) *Jehu and Jehonadab*

### Failure to exhibit true fellowship

[1SA 30:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:22) *Troublemakers in David's army are unwilling to share the spoils*; ; [1CO 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:11-1Cor1:12) *factions within the church at Corinth*; ; [1CO 11:17–11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:17-1Cor11:22) *Selfishness at love feasts humiliates the poor.*

* **Associated Passages:** 1 John 4:10-12; John 13:34; John 15:12; Ephesians 5:1-2; 1 John 3:10; Acts 2:42; Psalms 55:14; Psalms 42:4; 1 Corinthians 14:26; Ephesians 5:19; Colossians 3:16; Acts 1:14; Acts 4:24; Acts 12:12; James 5:16; 1 Corinthians 10:16-17; 2 Peter 2:13; Jude 1:12; Hebrews 13:16; Acts 20:34-35; Ephesians 4:28; Romans 12:13; Isaiah 58:7; Hebrews 13:1-2; 1 Peter 4:9; 3 John 1:8; Deuteronomy 15:10-11; Acts 2:44-45; Deuteronomy 10:18-19; Matthew 25:35-36; Luke 3:11; Acts 4:32-35; 2 Corinthians 8:13-15; 1 Timothy 6:17-18; James 1:27; James 2:15-16; Job 31:16-20; Acts 6:1; Acts 9:36; Acts 11:29-30; Romans 15:26; 2 Corinthians 8:3-4; Galatians 6:1-2; Isaiah 42:3; Romans 14:1; Romans 15:1; 1 Thessalonians 5:14; Isaiah 35:3-4; Job 4:3-4; Hebrews 10:24-25; 1 Samuel 23:16; Romans 1:12; 1 Thessalonians 5:11; Hebrews 13:3; Romans 15:2; 1 Corinthians 10:24; 1 Corinthians 10:32-33; 1 Peter 3:8; Romans 12:16; Ephesians 4:2-3; Philippians 2:1-4; Colossians 3:12-14; Acts 10:34; 1 Corinthians 12:25; James 2:1-4; Numbers 10:31-32; 1 Samuel 18:3; 2 Kings 10:15-16; 1 Samuel 30:22; 1 Corinthians 1:11-12; 1 Corinthians 11:17-22

## 7927: hymn (id: 7927)

Poetry sung to the praise of God and for the mutual encouragement of believers. Hymns and songs are not clearly distinguished in Scripture, but the NT contains examples of possible early Christian hymns.

Hymns are sung to the praise of God's name
------------------------------------------

**[PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3)**; **[ROM 15:8–15:11](https://ref.ly/Rom15:8-Rom15:11)** *Because of Jesus Christ the Gentiles are now called upon to join with God's people in praising God.* *See also* [DEU 32:43](https://ref.ly/Deut32:43); [2SA 22:50](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:50); [PSA 18:49](https://ref.ly/Ps18:49), [PSA 117:1](https://ref.ly/Ps117:1); [HEB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Heb2:12)

Christians are to sing hymns together for mutual encouragement
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26)** *See also* [EPH 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19); [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16) *“Psalms” refers to the OT psalms (see [Lk 20:42](https://ref.ly/Luke20:42); [24:44](https://ref.ly/Luke24:44); [Ac 1:20](https://ref.ly/Acts1:20); [13:33](https://ref.ly/Acts13:33)) some of which may have been set to music by Christians. “Psalm” could also describe a song newly composed for Christian worship (see [1Co 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26) where “hymn” translates Greek “psalmos”).*

Examples of singing hymns
-------------------------

### By Jesus Christ and his disciples

[MAT 26:30](https://ref.ly/Matt26:30) parallel [MRK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Mark14:26)

### By Paul and Silas in prison

**[ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25)**

Examples of hymns
-----------------

### The Benedictus: Zechariah's song of thanksgiving at the birth of his son, John the Baptist

*See also* [LUK 1:68–1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:68-Luke1:79)

### The Magnificat: Mary's song of praise as the future mother of the Lord

[LUK 1:46–1:55](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:55)

### The Nunc Dimittis: Simeon's response to seeing the child Jesus

[LUK 2:29–2:32](https://ref.ly/Luke2:29-Luke2:32)

### Other examples of possible early Christian hymns

[ROM 11:33–11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:33-Rom11:36); ; [EPH 5:14](https://ref.ly/Eph5:14); ; [PHP 2:6–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:11); ; [COL 1:15–1:20](https://ref.ly/Col1:15-Col1:20); ; [1TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 40:3; Romans 15:8-11; Deuteronomy 32:43; 2 Samuel 22:50; Psalms 18:49; Psalms 117:1; Hebrews 2:12; 1 Corinthians 14:26; Ephesians 5:19; Colossians 3:16; Luke 20:42; Luke 24:44; Acts 1:20; Acts 13:33; Matthew 26:30; Mark 14:26; Acts 16:25; Luke 1:68-79; Luke 1:46-55; Luke 2:29-32; Romans 11:33-36; Ephesians 5:14; Philippians 2:6-11; Colossians 1:15-20; 1 Timothy 3:16

## 7930: Lord's Day, the (id: 7930)

As well as keeping the Sabbath, the first Christians assembled together on the first day of the week to commemorate Jesus Christ's resurrection through the Lord's Supper. The Lord's Day quickly became the focal point of the Christian week, eventually assuming the characteristics of the Jewish Sabbath, namely worship and rest.

The disciples continued to observe the Sabbath
----------------------------------------------

**[LUK 23:56](https://ref.ly/Luke23:56)** *See also* [ACT 13:14](https://ref.ly/Acts13:14), [ACT 13:42](https://ref.ly/Acts13:42), [ACT 16:13](https://ref.ly/Acts16:13), [ACT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2), [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4)

The Lord's Day commemorated Jesus Christ's resurrection
-------------------------------------------------------

### The resurrection took place on the first day of the week

**[MRK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Mark16:9)** *See also* [MAT 28:1–28:7](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1-Matt28:7) parallels [MRK 16:1–16:7](https://ref.ly/Mark16:1-Mark16:7), [LUK 24:1–24:6](https://ref.ly/Luke24:1-Luke24:6), [JHN 20:1](https://ref.ly/John20:1)

### The disciples assembled together on the first day of the week

**[ACT 20:7](https://ref.ly/Acts20:7)** *See also* [JHN 20:19–20:20](https://ref.ly/John20:19-John20:20), [JHN 20:24–20:26](https://ref.ly/John20:24-John20:26)

The Lord's Day took over the role of the Sabbath
------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 16:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:2)** *See also* [REV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 23:56; Acts 13:14; Acts 13:42; Acts 16:13; Acts 17:2; Acts 18:4; Mark 16:9; Matthew 28:1-7; Mark 16:1-7; Luke 24:1-6; John 20:1; Acts 20:7; John 20:19-20; John 20:24-26; 1 Corinthians 16:2; Revelation 1:10
* **Associated Articles:** 2560: Jesus Christ, resurrection of (ID: `2560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7428: Sabbath (ID: `7428@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7933: Lord's Supper (ID: `7933@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7933: Lord's Supper (id: 7933)

The commemoration and remembrance of Jesus Christ's last supper, and all the benefits that result to believers. Other terms have been used subsequently by Christians, including “Communion” and “Eucharist”.

Terms for the Lord's Supper in the NT
-------------------------------------

[ACT 2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:42) *“breaking of bread” could mean both eating together and also celebrating the Lord's Supper*; ; [1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16) *The Greek word “koinonia”, here translated “participation”, is traditionally translated “communion”: hence the term “Holy Communion”*; ; [1CO 11:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20) *the Lord's Supper*; ; [1CO 11:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24) *Underlying “had given thanks” is the Greek “eucharisteo”, from which comes the term “Eucharist”.*

Jesus Christ's institution of the Lord's Supper
-----------------------------------------------

**[1CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:25)** parallels [MAT 26:26–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26-Matt26:28), [MRK 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:17–22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:17-Luke22:20)

Celebrating the Lord's Supper in the NT
---------------------------------------

### As part of an ordinary meal

**[1CO 11:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:21)** *The church was not necessarily wrong to include the sacrament in an ordinary meal; this should have been conducted in a charitable way.*

### On the Lord's day

**[ACT 20:7](https://ref.ly/Acts20:7)** *See also* [JHN 20:26](https://ref.ly/John20:26)

### The fourfold formula for breaking bread: taking, giving thanks, breaking, giving

**[MAT 26:26](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26)** parallels [MRK 14:22](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22), [LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19) *See also* [LUK 24:30](https://ref.ly/Luke24:30); [JHN 6:11](https://ref.ly/John6:11); [1CO 11:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24)

### The sharing of the cup

**[1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25)** parallels [MAT 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:27-Matt26:28), [MRK 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:23-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20)

Themes connected with the Lord's Supper
---------------------------------------

### The Passover

**[1CO 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7-1Cor5:8)** *See also* [JHN 11:50](https://ref.ly/John11:50) *Caiaphas' words may have an unintended Passover significance*; [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/John13:1), [JHN 19:14](https://ref.ly/John19:14), [JHN 19:33](https://ref.ly/John19:33), [JHN 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36); [EXO 12:46](https://ref.ly/Exod12:46); [NUM 9:12](https://ref.ly/Num9:12)

### The new covenant

**[1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25)** parallels [MAT 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:27-Matt26:28), [MRK 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:23-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20) *The reference to the (new) covenant implies a personal relationship with God and sins forgiven.*

### Remembrance

**[1CO 11:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24)** parallel [LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19)

### Thanksgiving, fellowship and unity

**[1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16)** *See also* [MAT 26:26–26:27](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26-Matt26:27) parallels [MRK 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22-Mark14:23), [LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19); [1CO 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:21)

### The Lord's return

**[1CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:26)** *See also* [MAT 26:29](https://ref.ly/Matt26:29) parallels [MRK 14:25](https://ref.ly/Mark14:25), [LUK 22:16](https://ref.ly/Luke22:16); [1CO 16:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:22); [REV 22:20](https://ref.ly/Rev22:20)

### Separation from sin

**[1CO 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:21)** *Paul is referring to feasts in heathen temples.* *See also* [1CO 11:27–11:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:27-1Cor11:32)

### A foretaste of heaven

**[MAT 26:29](https://ref.ly/Matt26:29)** parallel [MRK 14:25](https://ref.ly/Mark14:25)

## 7936: love feast (id: 7936)

The first Christians generally celebrated the Lord's Supper with the accompaniment of a meal, called the “agape” or “love feast”.

OT antecedents of the love feast
--------------------------------

### The Passover meal

*See also* [EXO 12:3–12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:3-Exod12:11), [EXO 12:15–12:16](https://ref.ly/Exod12:15-Exod12:16); [MAT 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Matt26:17-Matt26:18) parallels [MRK 14:12–14:15](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12-Mark14:15), [LUK 22:7–22:12](https://ref.ly/Luke22:7-Luke22:12); [1CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:25)

### Other festive occasions

[DEU 12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:7); ; [NEH 8:10](https://ref.ly/Neh8:10)

The NT practice of the love feast
---------------------------------

### It was linked with the Lord's Supper

**[ACT 2:46](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46)** *See also* [ACT 20:7](https://ref.ly/Acts20:7); [1CO 11:17–11:34](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:17-1Cor11:34)

### The name \`\`love feasts''

**[JUD 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12)** *See also* [2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13); [JHN 13:2](https://ref.ly/John13:2), [JHN 13:34](https://ref.ly/John13:34) *It was at the Last Supper that Jesus Christ gave his new commandment to his disciples to love one another as he had loved them.*

### The charitable distribution of food

[ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1)

Abuses of the love feast
------------------------

### Selfishness, indulgence and ostentation

**[1CO 11:20–11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:22)** *See also* [1CO 11:33–11:34](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:33-1Cor11:34)

### Licentiousness

**[2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13)**

### Ungodly participants, especially false teachers

**[JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4), [JUD 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12)** *See also* [2PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1), [2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13)

The heavenly love feast
-----------------------

### Foretold by Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:29](https://ref.ly/Matt26:29)** parallel [MRK 14:25](https://ref.ly/Mark14:25)

### The marriage supper of the Lamb

**[REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9)** *See also* [ISA 25:6](https://ref.ly/Isa25:6); [MAT 22:2–22:14](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2-Matt22:14) *The parable of the marriage supper indicates the prerequisites for attendance at the supper.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 12:3-11; Exodus 12:15-16; Matthew 26:17-18; Mark 14:12-15; Luke 22:7-12; 1 Corinthians 11:23-25; Deuteronomy 12:7; Nehemiah 8:10; Acts 2:46; Acts 20:7; 1 Corinthians 11:17-34; Jude 1:12; 2 Peter 2:13; John 13:2; John 13:34; Acts 6:1; 1 Corinthians 11:20-22; 1 Corinthians 11:33-34; Jude 1:4; 2 Peter 2:1; Matthew 26:29; Mark 14:25; Revelation 19:9; Isaiah 25:6; Matthew 22:2-14
* **Associated Articles:** 4435: drinking (ID: `4435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4438: eating (ID: `4438@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5385: leisure (ID: `5385@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5946: sensitivity (ID: `5946@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7354: feasts and festivals (ID: `7354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7406: Passover (ID: `7406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7921: fellowship (ID: `7921@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7933: Lord's Supper (ID: `7933@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8821: self-indulgence (ID: `8821@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7939: ministry (id: 7939)

Service of a general, as well as a religious, nature. In its broadest sense ministry refers to service rendered to God or to people. Its more restricted sense refers to the official service of individuals, specially set aside by the church.

* **Associated Articles:** 7940: ministry, of Jesus Christ (ID: `7940@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7941: ministry, of Holy Spirit (ID: `7941@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7942: ministry, nature of (ID: `7942@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7943: ministry, in the church (ID: `7943@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7944: ministry, qualifications for (ID: `7944@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7940: ministry, of Jesus Christ (id: 7940)

See [2300: Jesus Christ, ministry and work of](2300.md)



## 7941: ministry, of Holy Spirit (id: 7941)

See [3200: Holy Spirit, ministry and work of](3200.md)



## 7942: ministry, nature of (id: 7942)

All creatures owe their Creator a ministry of service to him and to humanity. The example and teaching of Jesus Christ illustrate this.

All human beings owe a ministry of service to God
-------------------------------------------------

### The debt owed by the creature to the Creator

**[ROM 1:25](https://ref.ly/Rom1:25)**

### The service of the nations and their rulers

**[PSA 148:7–148:11](https://ref.ly/Ps148:7-Ps148:11)** *See also* [JER 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jer3:17); [JOL 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Joel3:1-Joel3:2); [REV 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:4)

### Examples of the service of rulers

[EZR 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:1-Ezra1:2) *Cyrus of Persia*; [JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9), [JER 27:6](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6), [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10); [ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4), [ROM 13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6); ; [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)

The ministry of the people of God
---------------------------------

### In the OT

**[EXO 19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:6)** *See also* [ISA 61:6](https://ref.ly/Isa61:6)

### In the NT

**[1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5), [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)** *See also* [REV 1:6](https://ref.ly/Rev1:6), [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6) *resurrected martyrs*

The ministry of priests, kings and prophets in the OT
-----------------------------------------------------

### The priests

Aaron: [EXO 28:35](https://ref.ly/Exod28:35); ; [NUM 18:2](https://ref.ly/Num18:2); [DEU 17:12](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12); [1CH 15:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:2); ; [JER 33:22](https://ref.ly/Jer33:22); [EZR 2:63](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:63); ; [HEB 9:6](https://ref.ly/Heb9:6), [HEB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Heb13:10)

### The rulers

David: [2SA 3:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:18); ; [PSA 78:70](https://ref.ly/Ps78:70); ; [LUK 1:69](https://ref.ly/Luke1:69); ; [ACT 4:25](https://ref.ly/Acts4:25); [HAG 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hag2:23) *Zerubbabel, governor of Judah*

### The prophets

Moses: [NUM 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Num12:7-Num12:8); ; [JOS 12:6](https://ref.ly/Josh12:6); ; [2CO 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:7); ; [HEB 3:5](https://ref.ly/Heb3:5); [1SA 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:18) *the young Samuel*; [2KI 9:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:36), [2KI 10:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:10); [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1); ; [ISA 20:3](https://ref.ly/Isa20:3) *Isaiah*; ; [ISA 42:1–42:4](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1-Isa42:4); ; [JER 7:25](https://ref.ly/Jer7:25), [JER 25:4](https://ref.ly/Jer25:4); ; [1PE 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:12); ; [REV 10:7](https://ref.ly/Rev10:7), [REV 11:18](https://ref.ly/Rev11:18)

The ministry of those specially set aside by the church
-------------------------------------------------------

[1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28); ; [1TI 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:10) *Deacons*; ; [TIT 1:5](https://ref.ly/Titus1:5) *Elders*; ; [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2) *Overseers*; ; [EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11)

Jesus Christ fulfils the OT understanding of ministry
-----------------------------------------------------

### His ministry as servant

**[MAT 12:18](https://ref.ly/Matt12:18)** *See also* [ISA 42:1](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1); [MAT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:28) parallel [MRK 10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:45); [ACT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Acts3:13), [ACT 3:26](https://ref.ly/Acts3:26), [ACT 4:27](https://ref.ly/Acts4:27), [ACT 4:30](https://ref.ly/Acts4:30)

### His ministry as shepherd, teacher and prophet

**[JHN 10:11](https://ref.ly/John10:11), [JHN 13:13](https://ref.ly/John13:13)**; **[ACT 3:22](https://ref.ly/Acts3:22)**

### His ministry as priest

**[HEB 6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:20)** *See also* [HEB 8:6](https://ref.ly/Heb8:6)

### His ministry as king

**[JHN 18:37](https://ref.ly/John18:37)**

NT ministry follows the pattern of Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------

### In servanthood

**[MAT 20:26–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:26-Matt20:28)** parallels [MRK 10:43–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:43-Mark10:45), [LUK 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Luke22:26-Luke22:27) *See also* [MAT 23:11](https://ref.ly/Matt23:11) parallel [MRK 10:43](https://ref.ly/Mark10:43)

### In teaching

**[MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20)**

### In shepherding

**[JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17)**

### In priesthood by self\-sacrifice and intercession in prayer

**[PHP 2:17](https://ref.ly/Phil2:17)**; **[1TI 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1)** *See also* [1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5)

### Jesus Christ's servants can expect the same response that he received

**[JHN 15:20](https://ref.ly/John15:20)**

The ministry of angels
----------------------

Angels are both ministers to God's people and fellow\-servants with them: [HEB 1:7](https://ref.ly/Heb1:7), [HEB 1:14](https://ref.ly/Heb1:14); ; [REV 19:10](https://ref.ly/Rev19:10), [REV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Rev22:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 1:25; Psalms 148:7-11; Jeremiah 3:17; Joel 3:1-2; Revelation 15:4; Ezra 1:1-2; Jeremiah 25:9; Jeremiah 27:6; Jeremiah 43:10; Romans 13:4; Romans 13:6; 1 Peter 2:13-14; Exodus 19:6; Isaiah 61:6; 1 Peter 2:5; 1 Peter 2:9; Revelation 1:6; Revelation 20:6; Exodus 28:35; Numbers 18:2; Deuteronomy 17:12; 1 Chronicles 15:2; Jeremiah 33:22; Ezra 2:63; Hebrews 9:6; Hebrews 13:10; 2 Samuel 3:18; Psalms 78:70; Luke 1:69; Acts 4:25; Haggai 2:23; Numbers 12:7-8; Joshua 12:6; 2 Corinthians 3:7; Hebrews 3:5; 1 Samuel 2:18; 2 Kings 9:36; 2 Kings 10:10; 1 Chronicles 25:1; Isaiah 20:3; Isaiah 42:1-4; Jeremiah 7:25; Jeremiah 25:4; 1 Peter 1:12; Revelation 10:7; Revelation 11:18; 1 Corinthians 12:28; 1 Timothy 3:10; Titus 1:5; 1 Peter 5:2; Ephesians 4:11; Matthew 12:18; Isaiah 42:1; Matthew 20:28; Mark 10:45; Acts 3:13; Acts 3:26; Acts 4:27; Acts 4:30; John 10:11; John 13:13; Acts 3:22; Hebrews 6:20; Hebrews 8:6; John 18:37; Matthew 20:26-28; Mark 10:43-45; Luke 22:26-27; Matthew 23:11; Mark 10:43; Matthew 28:20; John 21:15-17; Philippians 2:17; 1 Timothy 2:1; John 15:20; Hebrews 1:7; Hebrews 1:14; Revelation 19:10; Revelation 22:9
