## 7943: ministry, in the church (id: 7943)

The regular ministry of officially appointed or recognised ministers is a particular instance of the duty and call of all God's people.

The responsibilities of those in recognised ministry
----------------------------------------------------

### As shepherds

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *See also* [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

### As watchmen

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)** *See also* [ROM 16:17](https://ref.ly/Rom16:17); [PHP 3:2](https://ref.ly/Phil3:2)

### As teachers

**[1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2)** *See also* [2TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:2), [2TI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24)

### As examples

**[1TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:16)** *See also* [ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28); [1PE 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:3)

What believers owe to those with recognised ministries
------------------------------------------------------

### Respect

**[1TH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12-1Thess5:13)** *See also* [PHP 2:29](https://ref.ly/Phil2:29); [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)

### Obedience

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)** *See also* [1CO 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15-1Cor16:16)

### Support

**[1CO 9:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:14)** *Despite Jesus Christ's command, Paul did not claim his rights in this matter, but supported himself as a tentmaker.* *See also* [LUK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7); [GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6); [1TI 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:18); [DEU 25:4](https://ref.ly/Deut25:4)

### Prayer

**[ROM 15:30](https://ref.ly/Rom15:30)** *See also* [EPH 6:19](https://ref.ly/Eph6:19); [PHP 1:19](https://ref.ly/Phil1:19); [1TH 5:25](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:25); [HEB 13:18](https://ref.ly/Heb13:18)

### Hospitality

**[PHP 2:29–2:30](https://ref.ly/Phil2:29-Phil2:30)** *See also* [GAL 4:14](https://ref.ly/Gal4:14)

### Imitation

**[HEB 13:7](https://ref.ly/Heb13:7)**

Recognised ministers should encourage the ministries of other believers
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 4:17](https://ref.ly/Col4:17)** *See also* [ROM 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6-Rom12:8); [1CO 12:4–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:4-1Cor12:11) *The Holy Spirit allocates gifts and enables ministries. No individual has all the gifts; ministry is shared and mutual*; [EPH 4:7](https://ref.ly/Eph4:7), [EPH 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:13)

The motivation of ministry is mutual love
-----------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:13](https://ref.ly/Gal5:13)** *See also* [ROM 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rom12:10), [ROM 13:8](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8); [COL 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Col3:12-Col3:14); [1TH 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:12); [HEB 13:1](https://ref.ly/Heb13:1); [1PE 1:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:22); [1JN 3:23](https://ref.ly/1John3:23)

How love is expressed in mutual ministry
----------------------------------------

### In humility

**[EPH 4:2](https://ref.ly/Eph4:2)** *See also* [ROM 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rom12:10), [ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16); [GAL 5:26](https://ref.ly/Gal5:26); [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)

### In patience

**[1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)** *See also* [JAS 5:9](https://ref.ly/Jas5:9)

### In acceptance and forbearance

**[ROM 15:7](https://ref.ly/Rom15:7)** *See also* [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:13)

### In kindness, compassion and forgiveness

**[EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32)** *See also* [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:13); [1TH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:15)

### In burden\-bearing

**[GAL 6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:2)**

### In encouragement

**[1TH 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:11)** *See also* [HEB 3:13](https://ref.ly/Heb3:13), [HEB 10:24–10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:24-Heb10:25)

### In teaching and admonition

**[COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)** *See also* [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14); [EPH 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19); [1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)

### In prayer and confession of sin

**[JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16)**

### In hospitality

**[1PE 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:9)**

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 20:28; 1 Peter 5:2; Hebrews 13:17; Romans 16:17; Philippians 3:2; 1 Timothy 3:2; 2 Timothy 2:2; 2 Timothy 2:24; 1 Timothy 4:16; 1 Peter 5:3; 1 Thessalonians 5:12-13; Philippians 2:29; 1 Timothy 5:17; 1 Corinthians 16:15-16; 1 Corinthians 9:14; Luke 10:7; Galatians 6:6; 1 Timothy 5:18; Deuteronomy 25:4; Romans 15:30; Ephesians 6:19; Philippians 1:19; 1 Thessalonians 5:25; Hebrews 13:18; Philippians 2:29-30; Galatians 4:14; Hebrews 13:7; Colossians 4:17; Romans 12:6-8; 1 Corinthians 12:4-11; Ephesians 4:7; Ephesians 4:11-13; Galatians 5:13; Romans 12:10; Romans 13:8; Colossians 3:12-14; 1 Thessalonians 3:12; Hebrews 13:1; 1 Peter 1:22; 1 John 3:23; Ephesians 4:2; Romans 12:16; Galatians 5:26; 1 Peter 5:5; 1 Thessalonians 5:14; James 5:9; Romans 15:7; Colossians 3:13; Ephesians 4:32; 1 Thessalonians 5:15; Galatians 6:2; 1 Thessalonians 5:11; Hebrews 3:13; Hebrews 10:24-25; Colossians 3:16; Romans 15:14; Ephesians 5:19; James 5:16; 1 Peter 4:9
* **Associated Articles:** 3254: Holy Spirit, fruit of (ID: `3254@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church (ID: `3275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6652: forgiveness (ID: `6652@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7622: disciples, characteristics of (ID: `7622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7745: ordination (ID: `7745@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7924: fellowship, in Christian service (ID: `7924@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7966: spiritual gifts (ID: `7966@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8292: love (ID: `8292@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8414: encouragement (ID: `8414@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8445: hospitality (ID: `8445@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7944: ministry, qualifications for (id: 7944)

God, who calls his people to minister, also equips his people. The chief qualifications are a response to God's call, faithfulness, godliness and Christlikeness.

God calls people to minister
----------------------------

### Qualification is by call, not gifting or achievement

**[DEU 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Deut7:7-Deut7:8)** *See also* [DEU 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Deut9:4-Deut9:5)

### God calls those who the world regards as weak or foolish

[1CO 1:27–1:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:27-1Cor1:29)

### Feelings of inadequacy to God's call are common

[EXO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Exod3:11) *Moses*; ; [JDG 6:15](https://ref.ly/Judg6:15) *Gideon*; ; [1SA 9:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:21) *Saul*; ; [1SA 18:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:18) *David*; ; [1KI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:7) *Solomon*; ; [ISA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:5) *Isaiah*; ; [JER 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6) *Jeremiah*

Responding to God's call to minister
------------------------------------

### Readiness and availability

[1SA 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:10); ; [ISA 6:8](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8)

### Faith, rather than natural talent or moral perfection, is required

**[HEB 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Heb11:1-Heb11:2)** *See also* [GEN 27:19–27:24](https://ref.ly/Gen27:19-Gen27:24) *Jacob was a deceiver*; [NUM 27:12–27:14](https://ref.ly/Num27:12-Num27:14) *Moses and Aaron disobeyed God.*[2SA 11:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:4), [2SA 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:14-2Sam11:15)[1KI 11:9–11:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:9-1Kgs11:13) *Solomon disobeyed God's command.*

NT ministers are recognised by call rather than their achievement
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### The Twelve

[MAT 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1-Matt10:4) parallels [MRK 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14-Mark3:19), [LUK 6:12–6:16](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12-Luke6:16) *The Twelve, including Peter and Judas, failed Jesus Christ at critical times.*

### Paul

[ACT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Acts9:15), [ACT 26:6](https://ref.ly/Acts26:6); ; [2CO 4:7–4:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7-2Cor4:12), [2CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7) *Though greatly gifted, Paul was kept humble by his sense of unworthiness, difficulties and disappointments and his “thorn in the flesh”*; ; [1TI 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:16)

Ministry in the NT is described as service
------------------------------------------

### Serving God

[ROM 1:9](https://ref.ly/Rom1:9); ; [JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1)

### Serving Jesus Christ

[ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); ; [JUD 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jude1:1); ; [REV 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rev1:1)

### Serving the gospel

[EPH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7); ; [COL 1:23](https://ref.ly/Col1:23)

### Serving the church

[ROM 15:31](https://ref.ly/Rom15:31), [ROM 16:1](https://ref.ly/Rom16:1); ; [1CO 16:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15); ; [2CO 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:1); ; [EPH 6:21](https://ref.ly/Eph6:21); ; [COL 1:7](https://ref.ly/Col1:7), [COL 1:25](https://ref.ly/Col1:25)

Ministry is described in terms of its source, content or nature
---------------------------------------------------------------

### Its source

It is of the Spirit: [2CO 3:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:6), [2CO 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:8); [2CO 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:1) *It is from God.*

### Its content

[ACT 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:4) *the word of God*; ; [2CO 5:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:18) *reconciliation*

### Its nature

Apostolic: [ACT 1:25](https://ref.ly/Acts1:25); ; [GAL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Gal2:8); [ROM 15:16](https://ref.ly/Rom15:16) *priestly*

Various ministries are equally linked by qualifications of character
--------------------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21) *the replacement for Judas*; ; [ACT 6:3](https://ref.ly/Acts6:3) *the Seven*; [1TI 3:2–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:7); ; [TIT 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7-Titus1:9); [1TI 3:8–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8-1Tim3:13) *deacons*; ; [1TI 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11) *Timothy*

The personal qualifications for ministry
----------------------------------------

### Faithfulness

[1TI 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11-1Tim6:14) *Timothy*; ; [2TI 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:7) *Paul's claim for his own ministry*; [COL 1:7](https://ref.ly/Col1:7), [COL 4:7](https://ref.ly/Col4:7)

### Godliness

[ACT 8:21](https://ref.ly/Acts8:21) *Simon was not right with God.*; [1TI 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11), [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21)

### Christlikeness

[ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22) *The replacement apostle for Judas had to have been with Jesus Christ from the beginning.*

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 7:7-8; Deuteronomy 9:4-5; 1 Corinthians 1:27-29; Exodus 3:11; Judges 6:15; 1 Samuel 9:21; 1 Samuel 18:18; 1 Kings 3:7; Isaiah 6:5; Jeremiah 1:6; 1 Samuel 3:10; Isaiah 6:8; Hebrews 11:1-2; Genesis 27:19-24; Numbers 27:12-14; 2 Samuel 11:4; 2 Samuel 11:14-15; 1 Kings 11:9-13; Matthew 10:1-4; Mark 3:14-19; Luke 6:12-16; Acts 9:15; Acts 26:6; 2 Corinthians 4:7-12; 2 Corinthians 12:7; 1 Timothy 1:16; Romans 1:9; James 1:1; Romans 1:1; Jude 1:1; Revelation 1:1; Ephesians 3:7; Colossians 1:23; Romans 15:31; Romans 16:1; 1 Corinthians 16:15; 2 Corinthians 9:1; Ephesians 6:21; Colossians 1:7; Colossians 1:25; 2 Corinthians 3:6; 2 Corinthians 3:8; 2 Corinthians 4:1; Acts 6:2-4; 2 Corinthians 5:18; Acts 1:25; Galatians 2:8; Romans 15:16; Acts 1:21; Acts 6:3; 1 Timothy 3:2-7; Titus 1:7-9; 1 Timothy 3:8-13; 1 Timothy 6:11; 1 Timothy 6:11-14; 2 Timothy 4:7; Colossians 4:7; Acts 8:21; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; Acts 1:21-22
* **Associated Articles:** 5932: response (ID: `5932@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7720: elders, in the church (ID: `7720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8206: Christlikeness (ID: `8206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8248: faithfulness (ID: `8248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8265: godliness (ID: `8265@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8420: equipping (ID: `8420@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7948: mission (id: 7948)

Specific actions that bear witness to the good news of what God has done for his people. Israel, Jesus Christ and the church all in their different ways bear witness to the saving acts of God in history. Christian mission is empowered by the Holy Spirit.

* **Associated Articles:** 7949: mission, of Israel (ID: `7949@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7950: mission, of Jesus Christ (ID: `7950@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7951: mission, of Holy Spirit (ID: `7951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7952: mission, and Holy Spirit (ID: `7952@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7953: mission, of the church (ID: `7953@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7949: mission, of Israel (id: 7949)

God chose Israel to be his own people, and bring the good news of his salvation to the world. God's mission through Israel is fulfilled in the mission of his servant and continues through the mission of the church.

Israel as a channel of God's blessing
-------------------------------------

### Blessings for all people through Abraham

**[GEN 18:18](https://ref.ly/Gen18:18)** *See also* [GEN 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:2-Gen12:3), [GEN 22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:18), [GEN 26:4](https://ref.ly/Gen26:4) *God's promise repeated to Isaac*; [GEN 28:13–28:14](https://ref.ly/Gen28:13-Gen28:14) *God's promise repeated to Jacob*; [ACT 3:25](https://ref.ly/Acts3:25); [GAL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gal3:8)

### Israel's call to be a nation of priests

**[EXO 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5-Exod19:6)** *See also* [ISA 61:6](https://ref.ly/Isa61:6)

God's desire to display his glory
---------------------------------

### God's glory is to fill the earth

**[HAB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hab2:14)** *See also* [NUM 14:21](https://ref.ly/Num14:21); [PSA 72:18–72:19](https://ref.ly/Ps72:18-Ps72:19); [ISA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Isa6:3)

### God's people display his glory

**[ISA 60:1–60:3](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1-Isa60:3)** *See also* [ISA 46:13](https://ref.ly/Isa46:13), [ISA 49:3](https://ref.ly/Isa49:3), [ISA 55:5](https://ref.ly/Isa55:5)

### God's people declare his glory among the nations

**[PSA 96:3](https://ref.ly/Ps96:3)** *See also* [PSA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Ps57:9), [PSA 96:10](https://ref.ly/Ps96:10), [PSA 105:1–105:2](https://ref.ly/Ps105:1-Ps105:2) parallel [1CH 16:8–16:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:8-1Chr16:9); [PSA 145:11–145:12](https://ref.ly/Ps145:11-Ps145:12)

The nations will be drawn to God's people
-----------------------------------------

### In recognition of God's presence among his people

**[ZEC 8:20–8:23](https://ref.ly/Zech8:20-Zech8:23)** *See also* [1KI 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1) parallel [2CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1); [ISA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Isa45:14); [JER 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jer16:19); [EZK 37:27–37:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:27-Ezek37:28), [EZK 39:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:7)

### To Jerusalem as the centre of universal worship

**[ISA 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:2-Isa2:4)** parallel [MIC 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Mic4:1-Mic4:3) *See also* [ISA 11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:9), [ISA 25:6–25:8](https://ref.ly/Isa25:6-Isa25:8), [ISA 27:13](https://ref.ly/Isa27:13), [ISA 56:6–56:8](https://ref.ly/Isa56:6-Isa56:8); [ZEC 14:16](https://ref.ly/Zech14:16)

God made known through his mighty acts
--------------------------------------

### God's deliverance of Israel

**[EXO 7:5](https://ref.ly/Exod7:5)** *See also* [PSA 98:1–98:3](https://ref.ly/Ps98:1-Ps98:3), [PSA 102:15–102:16](https://ref.ly/Ps102:15-Ps102:16); [ISA 49:26](https://ref.ly/Isa49:26); [EZK 38:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:23)

### God's action on behalf of Israel

**[EXO 34:10](https://ref.ly/Exod34:10)** *See also* [EXO 8:19](https://ref.ly/Exod8:19); [JOS 4:24](https://ref.ly/Josh4:24); [1KI 8:41–8:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:41-1Kgs8:43) parallel [2CH 6:32–6:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:32-2Chr6:33); [PSA 67:1–67:4](https://ref.ly/Ps67:1-Ps67:4), [PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2)

### God's renewal of Israel

**[EZK 36:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:23)** *See also* [EZK 20:40–20:41](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:40-Ezek20:41), [EZK 28:24–28:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:24-Ezek28:25), [EZK 38:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:16)

Israel as a witness to other nations
------------------------------------

### God's people sent to other nations

**[ISA 66:19](https://ref.ly/Isa66:19)** *See also* [1KI 17:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:9); [JON 1:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:2), [JON 3:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:2); [MAT 23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:15)

### Examples of God's people bearing witness to him

[GEN 41:16](https://ref.ly/Gen41:16) *Joseph*; ; [EXO 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Exod7:16-Exod7:17) *Moses and Aaron*; ; [2KI 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:3) *Naaman's servant girl*; ; [DAN 2:44–2:47](https://ref.ly/Dan2:44-Dan2:47) *Daniel*; ; [DAN 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:16-Dan3:18) *Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego*

Israel's failure in mission
---------------------------

**[ISA 26:18](https://ref.ly/Isa26:18)** *See also* [EZK 36:20–36:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:20-Ezek36:21)

The fulfilment of God's mission through Israel
----------------------------------------------

### The mission of God's Messiah

**[ISA 11:10](https://ref.ly/Isa11:10)** *See also* [ROM 15:12](https://ref.ly/Rom15:12); [PSA 72:17](https://ref.ly/Ps72:17) *God's promise to Abraham is fulfilled through the Messianic king*; [GAL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gal3:16)

### The mission of God's servant

**[ISA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:6)** *See also* [ISA 42:1–42:6](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1-Isa42:6), [ISA 52:15](https://ref.ly/Isa52:15)

### The mission of the church

**[1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)** *See also* [ROM 15:16](https://ref.ly/Rom15:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 18:18; Genesis 12:2-3; Genesis 22:18; Genesis 26:4; Genesis 28:13-14; Acts 3:25; Galatians 3:8; Exodus 19:5-6; Isaiah 61:6; Habakkuk 2:14; Numbers 14:21; Psalms 72:18-19; Isaiah 6:3; Isaiah 60:1-3; Isaiah 46:13; Isaiah 49:3; Isaiah 55:5; Psalms 96:3; Psalms 57:9; Psalms 96:10; Psalms 105:1-2; 1 Chronicles 16:8-9; Psalms 145:11-12; Zechariah 8:20-23; 1 Kings 10:1; 2 Chronicles 9:1; Isaiah 45:14; Jeremiah 16:19; Ezekiel 37:27-28; Ezekiel 39:7; Isaiah 2:2-4; Micah 4:1-3; Isaiah 11:9; Isaiah 25:6-8; Isaiah 27:13; Isaiah 56:6-8; Zechariah 14:16; Exodus 7:5; Psalms 98:1-3; Psalms 102:15-16; Isaiah 49:26; Ezekiel 38:23; Exodus 34:10; Exodus 8:19; Joshua 4:24; 1 Kings 8:41-43; 2 Chronicles 6:32-33; Psalms 67:1-4; Psalms 126:2; Ezekiel 36:23; Ezekiel 20:40-41; Ezekiel 28:24-25; Ezekiel 38:16; Isaiah 66:19; 1 Kings 17:9; Jonah 1:2; Jonah 3:2; Matthew 23:15; Genesis 41:16; Exodus 7:16-17; 2 Kings 5:3; Daniel 2:44-47; Daniel 3:16-18; Isaiah 26:18; Ezekiel 36:20-21; Isaiah 11:10; Romans 15:12; Psalms 72:17; Galatians 3:16; Isaiah 49:6; Isaiah 42:1-6; Isaiah 52:15; 1 Peter 2:9; Romans 15:16
* **Associated Articles:** 1045: God, glory of (ID: `1045@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1190: glory (ID: `1190@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1403: God, revelation of (ID: `1403@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2230: Messiah, coming of (ID: `2230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2327: Jesus Christ, as servant (ID: `2327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5075: Abraham (ID: `5075@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5622: witnesses (ID: `5622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7140: people of God (ID: `7140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7412: priesthood (ID: `7412@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7510: Gentiles (ID: `7510@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7950: mission, of Jesus Christ (id: 7950)

Jesus Christ came to reveal God, to announce the coming of God's kingdom and to redeem a fallen humanity through his death on the cross. Though he came first to the Jews, the scope of Christ's mission includes the whole human race and continues through the Spirit\-empowered witness of the church.

Jesus Christ's mission originated with God
------------------------------------------

### It was purposed by God

**[1PE 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18-1Pet1:20)** *See also* [ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23); [EPH 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Eph1:4-Eph1:5)

### The Father sent the Son

**[GAL 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:5)** *See also* [JHN 5:37–5:38](https://ref.ly/John5:37-John5:38), [JHN 7:29](https://ref.ly/John7:29), [JHN 8:42](https://ref.ly/John8:42), [JHN 10:36](https://ref.ly/John10:36), [JHN 17:8](https://ref.ly/John17:8); [1JN 4:14](https://ref.ly/1John4:14)

### Jesus Christ came in willing obedience to the Father

[JHN 6:38](https://ref.ly/John6:38); ; [PHP 2:5–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:8)

### Jesus Christ's mission was motivated by God's love

**[JHN 3:16](https://ref.ly/John3:16)** *See also* [ROM 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rom5:8); [1JN 4:10](https://ref.ly/1John4:10)

Jesus Christ's mission was to make God known
--------------------------------------------

### He reveals God in his own person

**[JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/John1:18)** *See also* [JHN 14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:9), [JHN 17:26](https://ref.ly/John17:26); [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15); [HEB 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:3)

### He reveals God through his teaching

**[JHN 18:37](https://ref.ly/John18:37)** *See also* [MAT 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28-Matt7:29); [JHN 3:11–3:13](https://ref.ly/John3:11-John3:13)

Jesus Christ's mission was to announce God's kingdom
----------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43)** *See also* [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17); [MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15); [MAT 12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:28) parallel [LUK 11:20](https://ref.ly/Luke11:20) *Jesus Christ's miracles reveal the presence of God's kingdom.*

Jesus Christ's mission was to redeem humanity
---------------------------------------------

### He came to seek and save the lost

**[LUK 19:10](https://ref.ly/Luke19:10)** *See also* [LUK 15:1–15:10](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:10)

### He came to save from sin

**[MAT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:21)** *See also* [ROM 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25-Rom3:26); [1TI 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:15)

### He came to break the devil's power

**[1JN 3:8](https://ref.ly/1John3:8)** *See also* [GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15); [JHN 12:31](https://ref.ly/John12:31); [HEB 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14-Heb2:15)

### He came to bring eternal life

**[JHN 10:10](https://ref.ly/John10:10)** *See also* [JHN 6:38–6:40](https://ref.ly/John6:38-John6:40), [JHN 17:2–17:3](https://ref.ly/John17:2-John17:3)

### He came to give access to God

[EPH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Eph2:18), [EPH 3:12](https://ref.ly/Eph3:12); ; [HEB 10:19–10:20](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:20)

### He came to restore a fallen humanity

[ROM 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Rom5:17-Rom5:18); ; [1CO 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:22)

Jesus Christ's mission was to establish the church
--------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18)** *See also* [EPH 2:17–2:19](https://ref.ly/Eph2:17-Eph2:19), [EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27); [1PE 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:4-1Pet2:5), [1PE 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9-1Pet2:10)

The scope of Jesus Christ's mission
-----------------------------------

### Jesus Christ came first to Israel

[MAT 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:6), [MAT 15:24](https://ref.ly/Matt15:24); ; [JHN 1:11](https://ref.ly/John1:11)

### Jesus Christ's mission extends to all peoples

[ISA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:6); ; [MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11); ; [JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:16); ; [MAT 8:10](https://ref.ly/Matt8:10) parallel [LUK 7:9](https://ref.ly/Luke7:9) *Jesus Christ commends the faith of a Roman centurion*; ; [MAT 15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:29](https://ref.ly/Mark7:29) *Jesus Christ grants the request of a Canaanite woman*; ; [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8) *Jesus Christ sends his disciples out into the whole world*; ; [ACT 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:17-Acts26:18) *Paul's commission to go to the Gentiles is received directly from Jesus Christ.*

Jesus Christ's mission made the cross necessary
-----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:28)** parallel [MRK 10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:45) *See also* [MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21) parallels [MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31), [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22); [LUK 12:50](https://ref.ly/Luke12:50); [COL 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:14-Col2:15); [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)

Jesus Christ's mission will be completed at his return
------------------------------------------------------

**[1JN 3:2](https://ref.ly/1John3:2)** *See also* [MAT 24:31](https://ref.ly/Matt24:31) parallel [MRK 13:27](https://ref.ly/Mark13:27); [COL 3:4](https://ref.ly/Col3:4); [1TH 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:16-1Thess4:17)

Jesus Christ's mission continues through the church
---------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 20:21–20:22](https://ref.ly/John20:21-John20:22)** *See also* [MAT 28:18–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:20); [JHN 15:26–15:27](https://ref.ly/John15:26-John15:27)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Peter 1:18-20; Acts 2:23; Ephesians 1:4-5; Galatians 4:4-5; John 5:37-38; John 7:29; John 8:42; John 10:36; John 17:8; 1 John 4:14; John 6:38; Philippians 2:5-8; John 3:16; Romans 5:8; 1 John 4:10; John 1:18; John 14:9; John 17:26; Colossians 1:15; Hebrews 1:1-3; John 18:37; Matthew 7:28-29; John 3:11-13; Luke 4:43; Matthew 4:17; Mark 1:14-15; Matthew 12:28; Luke 11:20; Luke 19:10; Luke 15:1-10; Matthew 1:21; Romans 3:25-26; 1 Timothy 1:15; 1 John 3:8; Genesis 3:15; John 12:31; Hebrews 2:14-15; John 10:10; John 6:38-40; John 17:2-3; Ephesians 2:18; Ephesians 3:12; Hebrews 10:19-20; Romans 5:17-18; 1 Corinthians 15:21-22; Matthew 16:18; Ephesians 2:17-19; Ephesians 5:25-27; 1 Peter 2:4-5; 1 Peter 2:9-10; Matthew 10:5-6; Matthew 15:24; John 1:11; Isaiah 49:6; Matthew 8:11; John 10:16; Matthew 8:10; Luke 7:9; Matthew 15:28; Mark 7:29; Acts 1:8; Acts 26:17-18; Matthew 20:28; Mark 10:45; Matthew 16:21; Mark 8:31; Luke 9:22; Luke 12:50; Colossians 2:14-15; Hebrews 12:2; 1 John 3:2; Matthew 24:31; Mark 13:27; Colossians 3:4; 1 Thessalonians 4:16-17; John 20:21-22; Matthew 28:18-20; John 15:26-27
* **Associated Articles:** 2321: Jesus Christ, as redeemer (ID: `2321@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2324: Jesus Christ, as Saviour (ID: `2324@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2354: Jesus Christ, mission of (ID: `2354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of (ID: `2363@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2376: kingdom of God, coming of (ID: `2376@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2410: cross, the (ID: `2410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2525: Jesus Christ, cross of (ID: `2525@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6510: salvation (ID: `6510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6720: redemption (ID: `6720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7024: church, nature and foundations of (ID: `7024@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8495: witnessing (ID: `8495@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7951: mission, of Holy Spirit (id: 7951)

See [3257: Holy Spirit, gift of](3257.md)



## 7952: mission, and Holy Spirit (id: 7952)

See [3212: Holy Spirit, and mission](3212.md)



## 7953: mission, of the church (id: 7953)

The continuation of Jesus Christ's mission through his followers. Believers are empowered by the Holy Spirit and sent out by Christ to bear witness to him and to preach, heal, teach, baptise and make disciples of all peoples.

The power and authority of the church's mission
-----------------------------------------------

### Believers are sent out by Jesus Christ

**[JHN 15:16](https://ref.ly/John15:16)** *See also* [MAT 9:37–9:38](https://ref.ly/Matt9:37-Matt9:38); [LUK 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Luke10:1-Luke10:3); [JHN 4:36–4:38](https://ref.ly/John4:36-John4:38)

### Believers are given authority by Jesus Christ

**[LUK 9:1](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1)** *See also* [MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1), [MAT 28:18](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18); [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7), [MRK 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Mark16:17-Mark16:18); [LUK 10:17–10:19](https://ref.ly/Luke10:17-Luke10:19)

### Believers continue Jesus Christ's mission

**[JHN 20:21](https://ref.ly/John20:21)** *See also* [JHN 17:18](https://ref.ly/John17:18)

### Believers are empowered by the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)** *See also* [LUK 24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:49); [JHN 20:22](https://ref.ly/John20:22); [ACT 4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31); [HEB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Heb2:4)

The task of the church in mission
---------------------------------

### Making disciples

**[MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20)** *See also* [ACT 2:41–2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:41-Acts2:42), [ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15), [ACT 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14-Acts16:15), [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8); [ROM 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:14-Rom10:15); [1JN 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:2-1John1:3)

### Preaching and healing

**[LUK 9:2](https://ref.ly/Luke9:2)** *See also* [MAT 10:7–10:8](https://ref.ly/Matt10:7-Matt10:8); [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20); [LUK 9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:6)

### Proclaiming the gospel

**[ACT 20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:24)** *See also* [ACT 8:40](https://ref.ly/Acts8:40); [ROM 1:9](https://ref.ly/Rom1:9), [ROM 15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:20); [2TI 1:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:11)

### Bearing witness to Jesus Christ

**[ACT 5:30–5:32](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30-Acts5:32)** *See also* [LUK 24:48](https://ref.ly/Luke24:48); [JHN 15:26–15:27](https://ref.ly/John15:26-John15:27); [ACT 4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:20)

### Bringing honour to God

**[EPH 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Eph3:10-Eph3:11)** *See also* [JHN 15:8](https://ref.ly/John15:8); [1PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:12)

The universal scope of the church's mission
-------------------------------------------

**[LUK 24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:47)** *See also* [MAT 24:14](https://ref.ly/Matt24:14) parallel [MRK 13:10](https://ref.ly/Mark13:10); [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15)

The church reaching out in mission
----------------------------------

### To the Jews

**[MAT 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:5-Matt10:6)** *The disiples' mission began with the Jews.* *See also* [MAT 10:9–10:15](https://ref.ly/Matt10:9-Matt10:15) parallels [MRK 6:8–6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:8-Mark6:11), [LUK 9:3–9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:3-Luke9:5), [LUK 10:4–10:12](https://ref.ly/Luke10:4-Luke10:12); [ACT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Acts11:19) *The scattered believers at first preached only to Jews.*

### To the Samaritans

**[ACT 8:4–8:8](https://ref.ly/Acts8:4-Acts8:8)** *See also* [ACT 8:14–8:17](https://ref.ly/Acts8:14-Acts8:17), [ACT 8:25](https://ref.ly/Acts8:25)

### To the Gentiles

Paul as the apostle to the Gentiles: [ACT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Acts9:15); ; [ROM 11:13](https://ref.ly/Rom11:13), [ROM 15:16](https://ref.ly/Rom15:16); [ACT 10:34–10:35](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34-Acts10:35) *Peter preaches to Cornelius and his family*; ; [ACT 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/Acts11:20-Acts11:21) *Scattered believers preach to the Gentiles*; ; [ACT 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:1-Acts13:3) *Paul begins his first missionary journey (with Barnabas)*; ; [ACT 15:40–15:41](https://ref.ly/Acts15:40-Acts15:41) *Paul begins his second missionary journey (with Silas)*; ; [ACT 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9-Acts16:10) *Paul is called to preach the gospel in Macedonia*; ; [ACT 18:23](https://ref.ly/Acts18:23) *Paul's third missionary journey*; ; [ACT 28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:31) *Paul preaches the gospel in Rome.*

Missions undertaken by church officials
---------------------------------------

[ACT 11:22–11:23](https://ref.ly/Acts11:22-Acts11:23) *Barnabas is sent to Antioch to strengthen the new church.*; [ACT 11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:30), [ACT 12:25](https://ref.ly/Acts12:25); [ACT 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/Acts15:22-Acts15:23) *Judas and Silas are sent to Antioch with a letter from the apostles and elders.*

* **Associated Passages:** John 15:16; Matthew 9:37-38; Luke 10:1-3; John 4:36-38; Luke 9:1; Matthew 10:1; Matthew 28:18; Mark 6:7; Mark 16:17-18; Luke 10:17-19; John 20:21; John 17:18; Acts 1:8; Luke 24:49; John 20:22; Acts 4:31; Hebrews 2:4; Matthew 28:19-20; Acts 2:41-42; Acts 14:15; Acts 16:14-15; Acts 18:8; Romans 10:14-15; 1 John 1:2-3; Luke 9:2; Matthew 10:7-8; Mark 16:20; Luke 9:6; Acts 20:24; Acts 8:40; Romans 1:9; Romans 15:20; 2 Timothy 1:11; Acts 5:30-32; Luke 24:48; John 15:26-27; Acts 4:20; Ephesians 3:10-11; John 15:8; 1 Peter 2:12; Luke 24:47; Matthew 24:14; Mark 13:10; Mark 16:15; Matthew 10:5-6; Matthew 10:9-15; Mark 6:8-11; Luke 9:3-5; Luke 10:4-12; Acts 11:19; Acts 8:4-8; Acts 8:14-17; Acts 8:25; Acts 9:15; Romans 11:13; Romans 15:16; Acts 10:34-35; Acts 11:20-21; Acts 13:1-3; Acts 15:40-41; Acts 16:9-10; Acts 18:23; Acts 28:31; Acts 11:22-23; Acts 11:30; Acts 12:25; Acts 15:22-23
* **Associated Articles:** 2012: Jesus Christ, authority of (ID: `2012@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2420: gospel (ID: `2420@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3212: Holy Spirit, and mission (ID: `3212@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5622: witnesses (ID: `5622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7027: church, purpose and mission of (ID: `7027@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7620: disciples (ID: `7620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7724: evangelists (ID: `7724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7739: missionaries (ID: `7739@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7754: preaching (ID: `7754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8424: evangelism (ID: `8424@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7957: sacraments (id: 7957)

The sacraments of baptism and the Lord's Supper were instituted by Jesus Christ as signs and seals of the covenant. They explain the basis of this covenant and apply its benefits to believers. They replace circumcision and the Passover which were the sacraments of the old covenant.

The sacraments as signs of the new covenant
-------------------------------------------

### Baptism

**[MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19)** *Baptism is one sign of being a disciple of Jesus Christ.* *See also* [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:41)

### The Lord's Supper

**[1CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:25)** parallels [MAT 26:26–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26-Matt26:28), [MRK 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:17–22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:17-Luke22:20) *The new covenant was sealed by the blood of Jesus Christ.*

The sacraments as a participation in the body of Christ
-------------------------------------------------------

### Baptism

**[GAL 3:27](https://ref.ly/Gal3:27)** *In the NT baptism immediately follows conversion and testifies to the individual's union with Christ.* *See also* [ROM 6:3](https://ref.ly/Rom6:3)

### The Lord's Supper

**[1CO 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16-1Cor10:17)** *The “body of Christ” is a term which goes beyond Jesus Christ to the church. Those who partake of the bread in this way are declaring their membership of the church.* *See also* [MAT 26:26](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26) parallels [MRK 14:22](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22), [LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19), [1CO 11:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24); [JHN 6:32–6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:32-John6:35), [JHN 6:48](https://ref.ly/John6:48) *Jesus Christ likens himself to bread as he is the source of spiritual life and nourishment*; [JHN 6:50–6:58](https://ref.ly/John6:50-John6:58) *The believer who eats the bread of the Lord's Supper is renewing his or her dependence on Jesus Christ for spiritual life.*

The sacraments as a participation in the death of Christ
--------------------------------------------------------

### Baptism

**[ROM 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Rom6:3-Rom6:4)** *Through baptism individuals declare that they have applied to themselves the death of Jesus Christ which atones for sin.* *See also* [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:16) *Baptism does not accomplish the cleansing from sin but it is the outward sign that this has taken place by faith*; [ROM 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Rom6:5-Rom6:7); [COL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:12); [TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5); [1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:21)

### The Lord's Supper

**[1CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:26)** *In taking the emblems of Jesus Christ's broken body and shed blood individuals perpetually apply the merits of Christ's death (i.e., forgiveness of sins) to themselves.* *See also* [MAT 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:27-Matt26:28) parallels [MRK 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:23-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20), [1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25); [JHN 6:53–6:56](https://ref.ly/John6:53-John6:56); [1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16)

The sacraments of the old covenant
----------------------------------

### Circumcision

**[ACT 7:8](https://ref.ly/Acts7:8)** *Circumcision was the initiation rite under the old covenant.* *See also* [GEN 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10-Gen17:14); [ROM 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:28-Rom2:29) *Circumcision was never meant as merely an outward rite. To be effective it required a right attitude of heart*; [ROM 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rom4:10-Rom4:11) *Circumcision was the seal of Abraham's righteousness imputed to him by his faith*; [COL 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:11-Col2:12) *Circumcision has now been superseded by baptism for the Christian as the rite of initiation.*

### The Passover

**[EXO 12:25–12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:25-Exod12:27)** *The Passover meal was instituted as a regular commemoration of Israel's redemption from slavery in Egypt.* *See also* [LUK 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/Luke22:15-Luke22:16) *The death of Jesus Christ is closely associated with the Passover by its timing and meaning. The Lord's Supper has superseded the Passover as the corporate meal commemorating the act of redemption*; [1CO 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 28:19; Acts 2:38; Acts 2:41; 1 Corinthians 11:23-25; Matthew 26:26-28; Mark 14:22-24; Luke 22:17-20; Galatians 3:27; Romans 6:3; 1 Corinthians 10:16-17; Matthew 26:26; Mark 14:22; Luke 22:19; 1 Corinthians 11:24; John 6:32-35; John 6:48; John 6:50-58; Romans 6:3-4; Acts 22:16; Romans 6:5-7; Colossians 2:12; Titus 3:5; 1 Peter 3:21; 1 Corinthians 11:26; Matthew 26:27-28; Mark 14:23-24; Luke 22:20; 1 Corinthians 11:25; John 6:53-56; 1 Corinthians 10:16; Acts 7:8; Genesis 17:10-14; Romans 2:28-29; Romans 4:10-11; Colossians 2:11-12; Exodus 12:25-27; Luke 22:15-16; 1 Corinthians 5:7
* **Associated Articles:** 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2315: Jesus Christ, as Lamb (ID: `2315@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2410: cross, the (ID: `2410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4418: bread (ID: `4418@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6754: union with Christ (ID: `6754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7028: church, life of (ID: `7028@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7110: body of Christ (ID: `7110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7404: ordinances (ID: `7404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7406: Passover (ID: `7406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7933: Lord's Supper (ID: `7933@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7960: singing (id: 7960)

The musical voicing of praise to God. On account of God's majesty and great acts of salvation singing was a regular part of the life of Israel and of the early church. In Israel musicians and singers were set aside solely for this purpose.

The call to sing praise to God
------------------------------

**[PSA 33:1–33:3](https://ref.ly/Ps33:1-Ps33:3), [PSA 96:1–96:2](https://ref.ly/Ps96:1-Ps96:2)** *See also* [PSA 5:11](https://ref.ly/Ps5:11), [PSA 9:11](https://ref.ly/Ps9:11), [PSA 30:4](https://ref.ly/Ps30:4), [PSA 47:6](https://ref.ly/Ps47:6), [PSA 68:4](https://ref.ly/Ps68:4), [PSA 68:32](https://ref.ly/Ps68:32), [PSA 81:1–81:2](https://ref.ly/Ps81:1-Ps81:2), [PSA 95:1](https://ref.ly/Ps95:1), [PSA 98:1](https://ref.ly/Ps98:1), [PSA 98:4–98:5](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4-Ps98:5), [PSA 98:8–98:9](https://ref.ly/Ps98:8-Ps98:9), [PSA 117:1](https://ref.ly/Ps117:1), [PSA 147:7](https://ref.ly/Ps147:7), [PSA 149:1](https://ref.ly/Ps149:1); [ISA 44:23](https://ref.ly/Isa44:23), [ISA 49:13](https://ref.ly/Isa49:13), [ISA 52:9](https://ref.ly/Isa52:9); [ROM 15:11](https://ref.ly/Rom15:11)

The resolve to sing praise to God
---------------------------------

**[PSA 104:33](https://ref.ly/Ps104:33)** *See also* [PSA 7:17](https://ref.ly/Ps7:17), [PSA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Ps9:2), [PSA 13:6](https://ref.ly/Ps13:6), [PSA 57:8–57:9](https://ref.ly/Ps57:8-Ps57:9), [PSA 59:16–59:17](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16-Ps59:17), [PSA 89:1](https://ref.ly/Ps89:1), [PSA 101:1](https://ref.ly/Ps101:1), [PSA 147:1](https://ref.ly/Ps147:1)

Singing in response to God's deliverance from enemies and sin
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:10)** *See also* [PSA 18:47–18:49](https://ref.ly/Ps18:47-Ps18:49), [PSA 71:22–71:24](https://ref.ly/Ps71:22-Ps71:24); [ISA 54:1](https://ref.ly/Isa54:1), [ISA 55:12](https://ref.ly/Isa55:12); [REV 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:11-Rev5:14), [REV 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3-Rev15:4)

God sings of his delight and love for his people
------------------------------------------------

**[ZEP 3:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:17)**

Singing in Israel
-----------------

### Singing accompanied major events

*See also* [1CH 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:27-1Chr15:28) *the bringing of the ark to Jerusalem*; [2CH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12-2Chr5:13) *the dedication of the temple*; [2CH 20:21–20:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:21-2Chr20:22) *a march into battle*; [2CH 23:11–23:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:11-2Chr23:13) *a coronation*; [2CH 29:27–29:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:27-2Chr29:28) *the purification of the temple*[2CH 30:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:21), [2CH 35:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:15)[NEH 12:27–12:29](https://ref.ly/Neh12:27-Neh12:29) *the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem*

### Part of day\-to\-day worship

[2CH 23:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:18); ; [NEH 11:23](https://ref.ly/Neh11:23)

### Accompanied by instruments

**[1CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16)** *See also* [1KI 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:12) parallel [2CH 9:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:11); [PSA 137:1–137:4](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1-Ps137:4)

### Musicians and singers appointed to lead Israel's singing

**[1CH 9:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:33), [1CH 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:22)** *See also* [1CH 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:31-1Chr6:32), [1CH 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6-1Chr25:7); [EZR 2:41](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:41) parallel [NEH 7:44](https://ref.ly/Neh7:44); [EZR 2:65](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:65); [NEH 11:22](https://ref.ly/Neh11:22), [NEH 12:46](https://ref.ly/Neh12:46)

Singing in the NT
-----------------

**[EPH 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19-Eph5:20)**; **[JAS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jas5:13)** *See also* [MAT 26:30](https://ref.ly/Matt26:30) parallel [MRK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Mark14:26); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25); [1CO 14:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:15); [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)

Singing is not always appropriate
---------------------------------

**[PRO 25:20](https://ref.ly/Prov25:20)** *See also* [ECC 7:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:5)**[AMO 5:23](https://ref.ly/Amos5:23)** *God detests the songs of Israel that are not accompanied by obedience and justice.* *See also* [ISA 24:14–24:17](https://ref.ly/Isa24:14-Isa24:17), [ISA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:5); [EZK 26:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:13); [AMO 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Amos6:5-Amos6:7), [AMO 8:3](https://ref.ly/Amos8:3), [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 33:1-3; Psalms 96:1-2; Psalms 5:11; Psalms 9:11; Psalms 30:4; Psalms 47:6; Psalms 68:4; Psalms 68:32; Psalms 81:1-2; Psalms 95:1; Psalms 98:1; Psalms 98:4-5; Psalms 98:8-9; Psalms 117:1; Psalms 147:7; Psalms 149:1; Isaiah 44:23; Isaiah 49:13; Isaiah 52:9; Romans 15:11; Psalms 104:33; Psalms 7:17; Psalms 9:2; Psalms 13:6; Psalms 57:8-9; Psalms 59:16-17; Psalms 89:1; Psalms 101:1; Psalms 147:1; Isaiah 35:10; Psalms 18:47-49; Psalms 71:22-24; Isaiah 54:1; Isaiah 55:12; Revelation 5:11-14; Revelation 15:3-4; Zephaniah 3:17; 1 Chronicles 15:27-28; 2 Chronicles 5:12-13; 2 Chronicles 20:21-22; 2 Chronicles 23:11-13; 2 Chronicles 29:27-28; 2 Chronicles 30:21; 2 Chronicles 35:15; Nehemiah 12:27-29; 2 Chronicles 23:18; Nehemiah 11:23; 1 Chronicles 15:16; 1 Kings 10:12; 2 Chronicles 9:11; Psalms 137:1-4; 1 Chronicles 9:33; 1 Chronicles 15:22; 1 Chronicles 6:31-32; 1 Chronicles 25:6-7; Ezra 2:41; Nehemiah 7:44; Ezra 2:65; Nehemiah 11:22; Nehemiah 12:46; Ephesians 5:19-20; James 5:13; Matthew 26:30; Mark 14:26; Acts 16:25; 1 Corinthians 14:15; Colossians 3:16; Proverbs 25:20; Ecclesiastes 7:5; Amos 5:23; Isaiah 24:14-17; Isaiah 25:5; Ezekiel 26:13; Amos 6:5-7; Amos 8:3; Amos 8:10

## 7963: song (id: 7963)

In Scripture, songs express praise, thanksgiving, joy, victory and love. They also express sadness and derision and can be used to teach and warn. In heaven believers will sing a new song to God.

Different kinds of song
-----------------------

### Songs of praise and thanksgiving

**[2CH 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13)**; **[PSA 69:30](https://ref.ly/Ps69:30)** *See also* [NEH 12:8](https://ref.ly/Neh12:8), [NEH 12:46](https://ref.ly/Neh12:46); [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 95:2](https://ref.ly/Ps95:2); [JER 30:18–30:19](https://ref.ly/Jer30:18-Jer30:19); [JON 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:9); [JAS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jas5:13)

### Songs of joy and celebration

**[PSA 98:4](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4)** *See also* [2SA 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:5) parallel [1CH 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:8); [1CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16); [PSA 100:2](https://ref.ly/Ps100:2), [PSA 107:22](https://ref.ly/Ps107:22), [PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2), [PSA 126:6](https://ref.ly/Ps126:6); [ISA 52:9](https://ref.ly/Isa52:9), [ISA 55:12](https://ref.ly/Isa55:12)

### Songs of victory and deliverance

**[EXO 15:1–15:18](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1-Exod15:18)** *The song celebrating God's spectacular victory over Pharaoh and his army (compare with [Rev 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3)).* *See also* [JDG 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Judg5:1-Judg5:3); [1SA 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:7); [2SA 22:1–22:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:1-2Sam22:4); [PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2), [PSA 32:7](https://ref.ly/Ps32:7), [PSA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Ps42:8), [PSA 118:15](https://ref.ly/Ps118:15); [ISA 26:1](https://ref.ly/Isa26:1), [ISA 44:23](https://ref.ly/Isa44:23), [ISA 49:13](https://ref.ly/Isa49:13)

### Psalms and hymns

**[COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)** *“psalms, hymns and spiritual songs” are not clearly distinguished from each other in Scripture.* *See also* [1CH 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:7); [PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3), [PSA 47:7](https://ref.ly/Ps47:7); [MAT 26:30](https://ref.ly/Matt26:30) parallel [MRK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Mark14:26); [1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25); [EPH 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19)

### Songs of ascent

PSA 122:0 *Psalms 120–134, the so\-called “Songs of Ascent”, are thought to have been sung by pilgrims on their way to Jerusalem.*

### Songs of love

PSA 45:0 *The title to this psalm describes it as “a wedding song”*; ; [SNG 1:1](https://ref.ly/Song1:1); ; [ISA 5:1](https://ref.ly/Isa5:1); ; [EZK 33:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:32)

### Laments

**[2SA 1:17–1:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:17-2Sam1:19)** *See also* [2SA 3:33–3:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:33-2Sam3:34); [2CH 35:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:25); [PSA 102:1–102:2](https://ref.ly/Ps102:1-Ps102:2); [JER 7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:29), [JER 9:10](https://ref.ly/Jer9:10), [JER 9:20](https://ref.ly/Jer9:20); [LAM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Lam2:5) *The book of Lamentations is a series of laments after the fall of Jerusalem*; [EZK 19:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:1), [EZK 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:14), [EZK 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:17-Ezek26:18), [EZK 27:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:2), [EZK 27:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:32), [EZK 28:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:12), [EZK 32:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:16); [AMO 5:1](https://ref.ly/Amos5:1)

### Songs of derision

[JOB 30:9](https://ref.ly/Job30:9); ; [PSA 69:12](https://ref.ly/Ps69:12); ; [LAM 3:14](https://ref.ly/Lam3:14), [LAM 3:63](https://ref.ly/Lam3:63); ; [MIC 2:4](https://ref.ly/Mic2:4)

God is described as the song of believers
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 118:14](https://ref.ly/Ps118:14)** *See also* [EXO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:2); [ISA 12:2](https://ref.ly/Isa12:2)

Songs can teach and warn
------------------------

**[DEU 32:44–32:46](https://ref.ly/Deut32:44-Deut32:46)** *See also* [DEU 31:19](https://ref.ly/Deut31:19), [DEU 31:22](https://ref.ly/Deut31:22), [DEU 31:30](https://ref.ly/Deut31:30); [PSA 119:54](https://ref.ly/Ps119:54); [EPH 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19)

A new song
----------

### Singing a new song to God expresses a renewed desire to praise him

**[PSA 40:1–40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:1-Ps40:3)** *See also* [PSA 33:3](https://ref.ly/Ps33:3), [PSA 98:1](https://ref.ly/Ps98:1), [PSA 144:9](https://ref.ly/Ps144:9), [PSA 149:1](https://ref.ly/Ps149:1); [ISA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Isa42:10)

### In heaven God's people will sing a new song

**[REV 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9-Rev5:10)** *See also* [REV 14:3](https://ref.ly/Rev14:3)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 5:13; Psalms 69:30; Nehemiah 12:8; Nehemiah 12:46; Psalms 28:7; Psalms 95:2; Jeremiah 30:18-19; Jonah 2:9; James 5:13; Psalms 98:4; 2 Samuel 6:5; 1 Chronicles 13:8; 1 Chronicles 15:16; Psalms 100:2; Psalms 107:22; Psalms 126:2; Psalms 126:6; Isaiah 52:9; Isaiah 55:12; Exodus 15:1-18; Revelation 15:3; Judges 5:1-3; 1 Samuel 18:6-7; 2 Samuel 22:1-4; Psalms 18:2; Psalms 32:7; Psalms 42:8; Psalms 118:15; Isaiah 26:1; Isaiah 44:23; Isaiah 49:13; Colossians 3:16; 1 Chronicles 16:7; Psalms 40:3; Psalms 47:7; Matthew 26:30; Mark 14:26; 1 Corinthians 14:26; Acts 16:25; Ephesians 5:19; Song of Songs 1:1; Isaiah 5:1; Ezekiel 33:32; 2 Samuel 1:17-19; 2 Samuel 3:33-34; 2 Chronicles 35:25; Psalms 102:1-2; Jeremiah 7:29; Jeremiah 9:10; Jeremiah 9:20; Lamentations 2:5; Ezekiel 19:1; Ezekiel 19:14; Ezekiel 26:17-18; Ezekiel 27:2; Ezekiel 27:32; Ezekiel 28:12; Ezekiel 32:16; Amos 5:1; Job 30:9; Psalms 69:12; Lamentations 3:14; Lamentations 3:63; Micah 2:4; Psalms 118:14; Exodus 15:2; Isaiah 12:2; Deuteronomy 32:44-46; Deuteronomy 31:19; Deuteronomy 31:22; Deuteronomy 31:30; Psalms 119:54; Psalms 40:1-3; Psalms 33:3; Psalms 98:1; Psalms 144:9; Psalms 149:1; Isaiah 42:10; Revelation 5:9-10; Revelation 14:3

## 7966: spiritual gifts (id: 7966)

Although all believers have received the gift of the Holy Spirit, Scripture points to God giving individuals certain special gifts of a spiritual nature for the fulfilling of specific tasks.

* **Associated Articles:** 7967: spiritual gifts, and responsibility (ID: `7967@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7968: spiritual gifts, nature of (ID: `7968@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7967: spiritual gifts, and responsibility (id: 7967)

A spiritual gift may be intended to equip its recipient for a specific function or appointment. Those who are equipped in this way need the enabling power of God's spirit to carry out their appointed tasks.

OT examples of God's gifts or appointments
------------------------------------------

### God appoints prophets

**[JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5)**

### God appoints Israel's kings

**[1SA 15:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:11)** *See also* [2SA 7:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:8), [2SA 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:7)

### God appoints the Persian king Cyrus

**[ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1)** *See also* [ISA 41:2](https://ref.ly/Isa41:2)

### God empowers leaders and kings with his Spirit

**[NUM 27:18](https://ref.ly/Num27:18)** *See also* [JDG 3:10](https://ref.ly/Judg3:10), [JDG 6:34](https://ref.ly/Judg6:34), [JDG 11:29](https://ref.ly/Judg11:29), [JDG 13:25](https://ref.ly/Judg13:25), [JDG 14:6](https://ref.ly/Judg14:6), [JDG 14:19](https://ref.ly/Judg14:19), [JDG 15:14](https://ref.ly/Judg15:14); [1SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:6), [1SA 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:10), [1SA 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:6), [1SA 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13-1Sam16:14)

### The Spirit is given to the servant of the LORD

**[ISA 42:1](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1)** *See also* [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1)

Jesus Christ is endowed with the Holy Spirit
--------------------------------------------

**[MAT 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:16-Matt3:17)** parallels [MRK 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Mark1:10-Mark1:11), [LUK 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/Luke3:21-Luke3:22) *See also* [JHN 1:32–1:33](https://ref.ly/John1:32-John1:33); [ACT 10:38](https://ref.ly/Acts10:38)

The apostles are endowed with the Holy Spirit
---------------------------------------------

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)**

Spiritual gifts given to the church
-----------------------------------

**[EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11)** *See also* [1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28)

### Many functions within the church yet unity is preserved

**[EPH 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Eph4:4-Eph4:7)** *See also* [1CO 12:4–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:4-1Cor12:11)

### All Christians are appointed to build up the church

**[EPH 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Eph4:15-Eph4:16)** *See also* [1CO 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:12)

The experience of spiritual gifts
---------------------------------

### Associated with the laying on of hands

**[2TI 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:6-2Tim1:7)** *See also* [NUM 8:10](https://ref.ly/Num8:10), [NUM 27:18](https://ref.ly/Num27:18); [DEU 34:9](https://ref.ly/Deut34:9); [ACT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:6); [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14), [1TI 5:22](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:22)

### Associated with prayer and fasting

**[ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23)** *See also* [ACT 13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:3)

Spiritual gifts must not to be neglected
----------------------------------------

**[1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14)** *See also* [LUK 19:11–19:26](https://ref.ly/Luke19:11-Luke19:26)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 1:5; 1 Samuel 15:11; 2 Samuel 7:8; 2 Samuel 12:7; Isaiah 45:1; Isaiah 41:2; Numbers 27:18; Judges 3:10; Judges 6:34; Judges 11:29; Judges 13:25; Judges 14:6; Judges 14:19; Judges 15:14; 1 Samuel 10:6; 1 Samuel 10:10; 1 Samuel 11:6; 1 Samuel 16:13-14; Isaiah 42:1; Isaiah 61:1; Matthew 3:16-17; Mark 1:10-11; Luke 3:21-22; John 1:32-33; Acts 10:38; Acts 1:8; Ephesians 4:11; 1 Corinthians 12:28; Ephesians 4:4-7; 1 Corinthians 12:4-11; Ephesians 4:15-16; 1 Corinthians 12:12; 2 Timothy 1:6-7; Numbers 8:10; Deuteronomy 34:9; Acts 6:6; 1 Timothy 4:14; 1 Timothy 5:22; Acts 14:23; Acts 13:3; Luke 19:11-26
* **Associated Articles:** 3040: Holy Spirit, promise of (ID: `3040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3212: Holy Spirit, and mission (ID: `3212@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3224: Holy Spirit, and preaching (ID: `3224@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ (ID: `3269@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3272: Holy Spirit, in OT (ID: `3272@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church (ID: `3275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5556: stewardship (ID: `5556@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7724: evangelists (ID: `7724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7734: leaders, spiritual (ID: `7734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 7968: spiritual gifts, nature of (id: 7968)

In both the OT and the NT God graciously pours out gifts on his people. They are to be welcomed and used for the good of all.

OT examples of God giving special gifts
---------------------------------------

### God's gracious provision from above

**[JOL 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Joel2:23-Joel2:24)** *This gracious provision of material needs from above can be seen as foreshadowing God's bestowal of spiritual gifts.* *See also* [EXO 16:4](https://ref.ly/Exod16:4), [EXO 16:8](https://ref.ly/Exod16:8), [EXO 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/Exod16:13-Exod16:14); [DEU 11:14](https://ref.ly/Deut11:14); [1KI 17:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:6); [JOB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Job5:10); [ISA 55:10](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10)

### Distribution of land to tribes

**[JOS 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Josh13:6-Josh13:7)** *The Hebrew word for “give” often includes the sense of “apportion” or “assign”.* *See also* [EZK 47:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:21)

### The future promise of spiritual gifts

**[JOL 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28-Joel2:29)** *See also* [ACT 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17-Acts2:18)

In his earthly ministry Jesus Christ offers supernatural gifts
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28)** *See also* [MAT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19); [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Luke10:19); [JHN 4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:14), [JHN 6:51](https://ref.ly/John6:51)

Spiritual gifts linked with grace
---------------------------------

**[ROM 12:6](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6)** *See also* [MAT 10:8](https://ref.ly/Matt10:8); [1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7)

Specific reference to spiritual gifts
-------------------------------------

**[1CO 12:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:1)** *See also* [ROM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Rom1:11); [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1), [1CO 14:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:12), [1CO 14:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:37)

Diverse gifts, one giver
------------------------

**[1CO 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:4-1Cor12:6)** *See also* [ROM 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6-Rom12:8); [1CO 7:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:7), [1CO 7:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:17), [1CO 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:8-1Cor12:11), [1CO 12:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27); [EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11); [HEB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Heb2:4); [1PE 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10-1Pet4:11)

The purpose of spiritual gifts is to build up the church
--------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 14:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:12)** *See also* [1CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:7), [1CO 14:2–14:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:2-1Cor14:5), [1CO 14:17–14:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:17-1Cor14:19), [1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26), [1CO 14:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:31); [EPH 4:16](https://ref.ly/Eph4:16)

### The body of Christ benefits from these varied gifts

**[1CO 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:12)** *See also* [1CO 12:14–12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:14-1Cor12:31); [ROM 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4-Rom12:6)

### Encouragement to aspire to the greater spiritual gifts

[1CO 12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:31), [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1)

The importance of love in exercising spiritual gifts
----------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 13:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:1)** *See also* [ROM 12:5–12:9](https://ref.ly/Rom12:5-Rom12:9); [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1)

Warnings about spiritual gifts
------------------------------

**[1CO 14:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:39)** *See also* [1CO 14:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:37); [1TH 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:19-1Thess5:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Joel 2:23-24; Exodus 16:4; Exodus 16:8; Exodus 16:13-14; Deuteronomy 11:14; 1 Kings 17:6; Job 5:10; Isaiah 55:10; Joshua 13:6-7; Ezekiel 47:21; Joel 2:28-29; Acts 2:17-18; Matthew 11:28; Matthew 16:19; Luke 10:19; John 4:14; John 6:51; Romans 12:6; Matthew 10:8; 1 Corinthians 4:7; 1 Corinthians 12:1; Romans 1:11; 1 Corinthians 14:1; 1 Corinthians 14:12; 1 Corinthians 14:37; 1 Corinthians 12:4-6; Romans 12:6-8; 1 Corinthians 7:7; 1 Corinthians 7:17; 1 Corinthians 12:8-11; 1 Corinthians 12:27; Ephesians 4:11; Hebrews 2:4; 1 Peter 4:10-11; 1 Corinthians 12:7; 1 Corinthians 14:2-5; 1 Corinthians 14:17-19; 1 Corinthians 14:26; 1 Corinthians 14:31; Ephesians 4:16; 1 Corinthians 12:12; 1 Corinthians 12:14-31; Romans 12:4-6; 1 Corinthians 12:31; 1 Corinthians 13:1; Romans 12:5-9; 1 Corinthians 14:39; 1 Thessalonians 5:19-20

## 7972: tongues, gift of (id: 7972)

The divine enabling of a believer to use a language, unknown to the speaker. The gift of tongues may be used to praise God or to utter a message from God that, supplemented by the gift of interpretation of tongues, edifies other believers. Note that “tongues” can simply mean “other languages” at times.

Tongues is a gift of the Holy Spirit
------------------------------------

### It is given to individual believers

**[ACT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:4)** *See also* [1CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:11), [1CO 12:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:30) *it is not universal among Christians*

### It was foretold in the OT

[1CO 14:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:21); ; [ISA 28:11](https://ref.ly/Isa28:11)

### It is a divine gift in response to faith

**[MRK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Mark16:17)**

### It may be given through the laying on of hands

**[ACT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Acts19:6)**

### It is a sign of the presence of the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 10:44–10:46](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:46)**

Tongues in relation to other gifts
----------------------------------

### The need for love

**[1CO 13:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:1)**

### The need for prophecy

**[1CO 14:4–14:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:4-1Cor14:6)**

### The need for intelligibility

*See also* [1CO 14:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:9), [1CO 14:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:13), [1CO 14:16–14:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:16-1Cor14:19), [1CO 14:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:23)

### The need for interpretation

**[1CO 12:8–12:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:8-1Cor12:10), [1CO 14:12–14:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:12-1Cor14:13)**

### The transience of tongues

**[1CO 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:8)**

The use of tongues within the church
------------------------------------

### In prayer and praise

*See also* [ROM 8:26](https://ref.ly/Rom8:26); [1CO 14:14–14:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:14-1Cor14:15), [1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26), [1CO 14:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:28); [EPH 6:18](https://ref.ly/Eph6:18); [JUD 1:20](https://ref.ly/Jude1:20)

### For the benefit of believers

**[1CO 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:7-1Cor12:10)** *See also* [1CO 14:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:5), [1CO 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:16-1Cor14:17), [1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26)

### In public it needs to be augmented by the gift of interpretation

**[1CO 14:26–14:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26-1Cor14:28)** *See also* [1CO 12:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:30), [1CO 14:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:5), [1CO 14:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:13)
